En: We know Ruby was the driver. We know that's your *affiliation* with her. He taps on his notebook. "Detective, you want to deliver the coup de grace?"
Zh: “我们知道露比就是那个司机了，我们知道了那就是你们跟她的*关联*。”他轻敲着笔记本。“警探，你要来给他们致命一击吗？”

En: You sure do love long walks through Theory Town, don't you? the big guy asks, straightening his cap. "Well, I'm thirsty now. Thirsty for beer. Got any less theoretical questions, cop?"
Zh: “你真的很喜欢在理论世界漫步，是不是？”大个子边问边整理着自己的帽子。“行了，我现在渴了，很想喝啤酒。还有什么理论上的问题吗，条子？”

En: And what about this Ruby? Is she the eighth Hardie who runs this thing for you?
Zh: “那个露比是怎么回事？她是替你管理这件事的第八个哈迪兄弟吗？”

En: Okay, maybe it doesn't *feel* like this did anything to them -- but they have to be fretting a *little*. Everyone is afraid of  witnesses. *Witness* is a scary word.
Zh: “好吧，也许这对他们来说并没有什么*感觉*——但肯定会给他们造成*小小的*烦恼。每个人都害怕目击者，*目击者*是一个很可怕的词。”

En: So much bluster to hide the fact that they're uncomfortable with you having this info.
Zh: 所有的虚张声势都是为了掩盖他们对你所掌握的信息而感到不安的事实。

En: They're only pretending to enjoy this. Beneath the act -- they don't like you knowing this.
Zh: 他们只是在假装很享受这一切。在表象之下——他们很不喜欢你知道的东西。

En: Little shit? That's Cuno in the yard.
Zh: 小崽子？那应该是院子里的坤诺。

En: He doesn't let it show, but must be a little impressed. You've put a lot of things together, fast.
Zh: 他没有表现出来，但是肯定有一点被打动了。你整理出了很多线索，而且很快。

En: He's practically saying: yes. A *yes* to everything you said.
Zh: 他实际上就是在说：是的。对你所说的一切，说了一句*是的*。

En: This was good. Every little piece helps put the big puzzle together.
Zh: 这样很好。每一条小线索能把帮助解开那个大谜题。

En: Forget it. I knew they'd never own up to it. (Back off.)
Zh: “算了吧，我知道他们永远不会承认的。”（退后。）

En: I don't know, the big man says, swirling his beer can, "I guess *theoretical* things don't make me emotional. Now, I was sorta going to get my brewskie on."
Zh: “我不知道，”大块头摇晃着啤酒罐，说到，“我猜这种*理论上*的东西不会让我感情用事吧。现在，我有点想喝酒了。”

En: Don't worry, we will figure this out sooner or later. (Conclude.)\n
Zh: “别担心，我们迟早会搞清楚的。”（结束。）

En: Don't worry, I will figure this out sooner or later. (Conclude.)
Zh: “别担心，我迟早会搞清楚的。”（结束。）

En: Why don't you go home and log it into evidence. These men have told you what happened.
Zh: “你为什么不回去把它记录下来作为证据呢？这些人已经把事情经过告诉你了。”

En: Sire, it would be an event most dramatic if you were to produce the bullet and dangle it before their very eyes!
Zh: 大人，如果您能当着他们的面把这颗子弹取出来，这将是一件多么惊人的事情啊！

En: It's not like you blew it *wide* open, but there's a little crack in there, somewhere...
Zh: 并不能说你把它*完全*撬开了，但至少在某处，有一条小裂缝……

En: He gives you an indulgent look. "Well there are so many bullets in the world and so many heads..."
Zh: 他宽容地看了你一眼。“世界上有那么多子弹，那么多脑袋……”

En: I really don't care. He shrugs and looks over his shoulder.
Zh: “我真的不在乎。”他耸耸肩，回头看了一眼。

En: I just found a *bullet* -- in the hanged man's head.
Zh: “我刚刚发现了一颗*子弹*——在那个被吊起来的人的脑袋里。”

En: I told you already, I spoke to Evrart and he said you'd cooperate.
Zh: “我告诉过你，我跟艾弗拉特谈过了，他向我保证你会配合的。”

En: Me and Evrart talked. He promised you'd cooperate.
Zh: “我跟艾弗拉特谈过了，他向我保证你会配合的。”

En: It's bound to happen again, you know. He taps on his right temple. "Just statistically speaking, of course."
Zh: “你知道，这种事一定会再次发生的。”他轻敲着自己的右侧太阳穴。“当然，只是从统计学上讲。”

En: Yeah yeah, I heard him. The fuck do you think I'm doing here? You'd have your ass handed to you if it wasn't for the bossman's word.
Zh: “是的，我听到了。你他妈以为我在这里干什么？如果不是老大发话，我早就给你好看了。”

En: You gonna let him talk to me like that, Titus? He turns his eyes, full of hurt, to the big man.
Zh: “你就放任他这么跟我们说话吗，提图斯？”他用受伤的眼神看向大个子。

En: Truly curious. If you *hanged* him, how did it get there?
Zh: “真是怪了。如果你*吊死*了他，这是怎么进去的？”

En: It would take an army to bend Titus to its will, but having Evrart back you up like that... did seem to have *some* effect.
Zh: 只有动用军队才能让提图斯屈服，不过有艾弗拉特背书……看起来确实有*一些*效果。

En: You still on about that bullet? He pats the back of his head. "A bullet in a hanged man's head. You're right, copper -- that *is* mighty curious."
Zh: “你还在纠结那颗子弹？”他拍拍后脑勺。“一个吊人的脑袋里有颗子弹。你是对的，条子——这可*真是*怪了。”

En: A bullet, you say? He pats the back of his head. "That's mighty curious."
Zh: “你说一颗子弹？”他拍拍后脑勺。“这真是怪了。”

En: Oh, so you went and talked to my mommy -- and now she's making me play with you? He spits. "Is that it, lawman?"
Zh: “哦，所以你去找我妈咪了——而且现在她让我跟你一起玩？”他吐了口唾沫。“是这样吗，执法官？”

En: He turns back to you. "The old man sent word you'd be around again. That's the reason I'm being so forthcoming with ya. Don't wear it out."
Zh: 他转向你。“老头子说你会回来的。这就是我对你如此坦诚的原因，别浪费了。”

En: And what's going to happen if we don't? The little guy leans forward. "You gonna go and *tell* on us?"
Zh: “而且如果我们不愿意会怎么样？”小个子前倾着身体。“你要去*告*我们的状吗？”

En: Typical binoclard. Always a tattletale. He shakes his head resentfully and continues.
Zh: “典型的四眼仔，永远只会搬弄是非。”他愤怒地摇头，继续说道。

En: Evrart sent word you two would be around again. That's the only reason I'm being so forthcoming with ya. Don't wear it out.
Zh: “艾弗拉特告诉我你们俩还会回来的。这就是我对你们如此坦诚的原因，别浪费了。”

En: Very mature, guys. Evrart sent word, right?
Zh: “真成熟啊，伙计们。艾弗拉特放话了，对吧？”

En: Explosive laughter follows. To his men, Titus Hardie is a golden god. They want to laugh at his jokes even before they leave his lips. This guy is a born leader.
Zh: 紧接着是一阵爆炸似的笑声。对他的兄弟们来说，提图斯·哈迪就是神。他们甚至想在他的笑话尚未说出口前就大笑出声，这家伙是个天生的领袖。

En: Curious yes, I guess we'll never know... About something else though?
Zh: “确实很奇怪，也许我们永远不会知道了……还有别的事吗？”

En: I would though, he says, as casually as if he were commenting on the weather.
Zh: “我会的，”他漫不经心地说着，好像是在评价天气。

En: Indeed. *Mighty*. How did it get there? The lieutenant is fixed on Titus.
Zh: “的确，*非常*。它是怎么进去的？”警督盯着提图斯。

En: He sighs deeply. "I guess it's only  logical -- at some point one of them bullets had to end up in one of them heads..."
Zh: 他深深叹了一口气。“我想只能是在某一时刻，其中一颗子弹必须要打在其中一颗脑袋上——才合乎逻辑了……”

En: Why don't you push me some more and find out, gimp.
Zh: “不如你继续逼问我啊，混蛋。”

En: Let's see... He chuckles. "Yeah. I guess I am, you big pussy."
Zh: “我看看……”他咯咯笑着。“没错，我想我会的，你这个大娘炮。”

En: I would *never* tell on someone!
Zh: “我*绝不会*告密的！”

En: Let me state this very clearly, coppo. He clears his throat and declares: "Hello, officer! I'm Titus Hardie and these are my boys. Hardie boys. How may we assist you?"
Zh: “让我跟你说清楚，条子。”他清了清嗓子。“你好，警官！我是提图斯·哈迪。这是我的兄弟们，哈迪兄弟。我们有什么能帮忙的吗？”

En: Well, snitches get stitches and you don't have any stitches, although by the looks of it you could probably use some. He sizes you up.
Zh: “这个嘛，告密者的嘴巴是要被缝上的，而你没有。虽然看起来你可能需要被缝上几针。”他打量着你。

En: This line of questioning is *over*, the woman interrupts. "You got the cause of death already. Hanging."
Zh: “询问已经*结束*了，”那个女人打断了你。“你已经知道死因了，吊死。”

En: There was a bullet in the dead man's brain, the lieutenant checks his notes. "Why was it there, if you hanged him?"
Zh: “死者的脑袋里有一枚子弹，”警督查看着自己的笔记。“如果你是把他吊死的，为什么里面会有子弹？”

En: This again? He looks at you unsurprised and takes a sip of his beer.
Zh: “又来？”他毫不惊讶地看着你，喝了一口啤酒。

En: Huh?! A sip of beer makes the surprise go down easier.
Zh: “哈？！”一口啤酒帮他更好的消化了这个惊喜。

En: How does the *bullet* in his head factor into this?
Zh: “他脑袋里的*子弹*跟这件事有什么关系？”

En: If there's any *post-mortem* trauma, it's your problem. This will not turn into a cross-examination.
Zh: “如果真有什么*死后*创伤，那也是你的问题。别把这个变成盘问。”

En: If you hanged him, why was there a bullet in his head?
Zh: “如果你是把他吊死的，为什么他脑袋里会有子弹？”

En: She cut in so Titus wouldn't have to answer.
Zh: 她插了进来，以免提图斯开口。

En: ...or lie. Something he's probably not very good at.
Zh: ……或者说谎，他可能不太擅长这个。

En: They'll keep blocking this. Return to this subject later, when they've dropped their guard.
Zh: 他们会继续搪塞这个问题的。等他们放松警惕后再谈这个话题。

En: You still haven't explained the bullet in his head.
Zh: “你还是没有解释他脑袋里的子弹是怎么回事。”

En: You know, all this talk about bullets and heads makes me think you want one in *yours*.
Zh: “知道吗，所有这些关于子弹和脑袋的话让我感觉你也想要给*自己的*脑袋来一颗。”

En: Titus has already given you a cause of death. Hanging. Why is it so hard for you to understand? The question is rhetorical.
Zh: “提图斯已经把死因告诉了你们，吊死。这有什么难以理解的吗？”这是一一句反问句。

En: I'm gonna ask you again, why was this... (Show them the bullet) "... in the victim's head?"
Zh: “我再问你一次，为什么这个……”（给他们看子弹）“……会在被害人的脑袋里？”

En: Did you guys shoot him?
Zh: “是你们开的枪吗？”

En: I think the hanging was a cover-up for the shooting.
Zh: “我认为把他吊起来是为了掩盖枪击。”

En: You know what I think? He raises his finger. "I think he was shot in the head as a kid. And his brain grew around the bullet."
Zh: “知道我的想法吗？”他竖起手指。“我认为他小时候头部就中过枪，他的大脑就这么围绕着子弹发育了。”

En: Around the bullet, man... Alain pinches the root of his nose. "That's a good one."
Zh: “围绕着子弹，老兄……”阿兰捏了捏自己的鼻梁。“说得好。”

En: It *is* possible to live with an injury like that. Makes you touch the back of your skull, wondering if there's one in there?
Zh: 带着那种伤口长大*确实*有可能。这让你不禁摸了摸自己的后脑勺，好奇里面会不会也有一个？

En: No. You'd remember that. You've been shot at four times. Hit twice.
Zh: 不对。这样你会有印象的。你被打过四枪，命中两次。

En: Wow. The little man leans in to inspect the leaden blossom. "He's got it in a real evidence bag and all..."
Zh: “哇哦。”小个子男人倾过身子去看那朵浅灰色的花。“他还把它装在一个真正的证物袋里了……”

En: All the goofing around is to avoid lying. It's a technique.
Zh: 所有的虚与委蛇都是为了避免说谎，这是一种手段。

En: Never been worried in my life, lawman. He crosses his hairy arms, having forgotten his beer for a moment.
Zh: “我这辈子从没有担惊受怕过，执法官。”他环抱着自己毛茸茸的胳膊，暂时忘记了自己的啤酒。

En: Shit! He throws his head back. "*I* probably did shoot him. I was drunk last night. You guys know me when I'm drunk..."
Zh: “该死！”他把头往后一仰。“我可能真的朝他开枪了。我昨晚喝醉了，你们知道我喝醉的时候……”

En: Yeah, Glen likes to shoot his guns when he's drunk. He grins at you. "And he's getting drunk right now."
Zh: “是啊，格伦喝醉的时候就喜欢乱开枪。”他对你咧嘴一笑。“他现在已经醉醺醺的了。”

En: Yeah, Glen likes to shoot his guns when he's drunk. The little guy looks you in the eye. "Better hope he stays sober."
Zh: “是啊，格伦喝醉的时候就喜欢乱开枪。”小个子直视着你的眼睛。“最好祈祷他能保持清醒吧。”

En: I meant *before* he was hanged.
Zh: “我是说在他被吊起来*之前*。”

En: Before? After? *During?* She spreads her arms. "This is getting ridiculous. They told you what happened. Stop wasting your time."
Zh: “之前？之后？*正当时*？”她张开双臂。“越来越荒谬了。他们已经告诉了你事情的经过，别再浪费时间了。”

En: No, he meant *before* he was hanged. Did you shoot him *before* you hanged him?
Zh: “不，他的意思是在他被吊起来*之前*。你是在把他吊起来*之前*开的枪吗？”

En: Yes. Death threats, he says calmly. "My partner meant *before* he was hanged."
Zh: “是的，死亡威胁。”他平静地说。“我搭档的意思是在他被吊起来*之前*。”

En: Before? After? *During?* She shakes her head slowly. "I seriously advise you to stop testing these men. They're getting *agitated*."
Zh: “之前？之后？*正当时*？”她慢慢地摇着头。“我郑重地建议你停止揣测这些人，他们开始*焦躁*了。”

En: *They're* no more agitated than they were a minute ago. She is though.
Zh: *他们*并不比一分钟前更焦躁，反倒是她才是焦躁的那个。

En: Oh, they're itching for one... He clacks his tongue. "I listen to Channel 8. These cops have a death wish."
Zh: “哦，他们倒想……”他咂舌。“我听第八频道，这些条子们都有死亡意愿。”

En: Let's change the subject.
Zh: “我们换个话题吧。”

En: Oh no, Shanks... you hurt his feelings. He mocks you. "Please ask your little questions, little guy."
Zh: “哦不，尚克……你伤到他的感情了。”他嘲笑你。“小家伙，请你问你的小问题吧。”

En: The old man sent word you'd would be around. That's the only reason I'm being so forthcoming with ya. Don't wear it out.
Zh: “老头子捎话说你会在附近的。这就是我对你如此坦诚的唯一原因，别浪费了。”

En: Shanky -- it's Shanky, right? He doesn't wait for an answer. "I thought there's something wrong about the lynching story. Now I *know* there was."
Zh: “尚克——是叫尚克，对吗？”他没有等人回答。“我一直觉得私刑的故事有点问题，现在我能*肯定*了。”

En: It's you assholes that feel the need to go around like a fucking brass band -- the Hardie boys are dead silent.
Zh: “是你们这些混蛋觉得有必要像个该死的军乐队一样四处打转的——哈迪兄弟可是一直守口如瓶的。”

En: Yeah! It's like they put cowbells on you before they send you to the streets. He says provokingly. "What's with the cowbells, police man?"
Zh: “没错！就像他们把你送上街之前还给你戴上牛铃一样。”他挑衅地说。“那些牛铃是怎么回事，条子？”

En: Vapour, huh? He glances at Alain. "Well, that checks out at least."
Zh: “蒸汽，嗯？”他瞥了一眼阿兰。“嗯，至少那是真的。”

En: My witness isn't a local.
Zh: “我的目击者不是本地人。”

En: Those flaccid MI cocks with their *culture* language. The Mesque spits on the ground angrily. "Everything's a *surreal play* or a *sublime whatever*. Doesn't mean anything."
Zh: “那些用自己*文化*语言来讽刺我们的混蛋。”梅斯克人愤怒地唾了一口。“一切都是*超现实主义*或是*崇高的什么*，没有任何意义。”

En: That means absolutely nothing to me, he says taking a sip from his can. "Sounds like some made-up horse shit."
Zh: “那对我来说一点意义都没有。”他从罐子里喝了一口啤酒。“听上去就像胡编乱造的说法。”

En: I found someone who saw the hanging. A witness.
Zh: “我发现有人看到了他被吊起来的过程，一位目击者。”

En: Areopagite? He starts laughing. "Boss, I think he's trying to say me and Theo."
Zh: “艾瑞奥帕吉特人？”他笑了起来。“老大，我想他是想说我和特奥。”

En: Well yeah... Titus nods. "What is confusing you? Eugene, Theo and Alain were there too. I already told you -- we were all there."
Zh: “是啊……”提图斯点点头。“你有什么困惑吗？尤金、特奥和阿兰也去过那里。我已经告诉过你了——我们都在现场。”

En: It means the whole scene was long and drawn out. Like a movie.
Zh: “这意味着整个场景都被拉长了，就像电影一样。”

En: A witness? The tall man crosses his arms on his chest. "You ain't got shit. The locals would never come to you with this."
Zh: “一个目击者？”高个子男人双臂交叉放在胸前。“你根本没有什么目击者，当地人绝不会来找你的。”

En: That's just cop-tactics, Titus. Next he's gonna tell you one of us already rolled on the others -- and is in witness protection.
Zh: “那只是条子的战术而已，提图斯。接下来他会告诉你，我们中有一个人已经出卖了大家——而且受到证人保护了。”

En: It means the whole scene was long and drawn out. Like it was from a *film*.
Zh: “这意味着整个场景都被拉长了，就像*电影*里演得一样。”

En: What is this fella's problem?! The little guy addresses everyone and no one. "Sorry, we didn't make it more action-packed. It wasn't the first thing on our minds you see."
Zh: “这家伙有什么毛病？”小个子对着空气问到。“抱歉我们没能把它演绎得更精彩，但这并不是我们首先要考虑的问题。”

En: Actually nothing.
Zh: “其实也没什么。”

En: You can almost see the blood curdle in  the lieutenant's veins. Miraculously he remains silent.
Zh: 你几乎可以看到警督血管里的血液凝结成块。他竟然奇迹般地保持着沉默。

En: I've seen this shit a million times, Titus. Flyfishing -- they're desperate. He turns to you. "Tell us, copper, what wacky claims did he make?"
Zh: “我已经看过一百万次了，提图斯。钓鱼执法——他们绝望了。”他转向你。“告诉我们吧，条子。他说了什么乱七八糟的？”

En: You didn't break this wagon fort, but you did manage to rattle the people inside a bit.
Zh: 你并没有攻破这个堡垒，但着实把里面的人吓了一跳。

En: Let's just drop this.
Zh: “别说这个了。”

En: Charles Villedrouin, a high ranking government official from Rue de Saint Ghislaine 33-B.
Zh: “查尔斯·维尔德鲁安，一位住在圣吉莱纳路33-B的高级政府官员。”

En: The witness said the end is here, the great undoing. We will all be vapour.
Zh: “目击者说终结已经来临，大毁灭。我们都会变成蒸汽。”

En: Yeah, right... like we wouldn't know if an MI plant was in our town. Titus looks over his shoulder to his men. "Nice try, right?"
Zh: “好吧……说得好像我们不知道镇上有没有MI卧底似的。”提图斯回头看着他的兄弟。“做得还不错，对吧？”

En: It's like you said, Al. He turns to the man on his left. "Copper's coming up with this on the spot. There is no witness."
Zh: “正如你所说的，兰。”他转向左边那个人。“条子是当场乱编的，根本没有什么目击者。”

En: The witness said it all looked like a *surreal play*.
Zh: “目击者说一切看起来都非常*超现实主义*。”

En: Who he *is* is irrelevant.
Zh: “他是*谁*根本无关紧要。”

En: Let's just say he's a high ranking government official -- and leave it at that.
Zh: “姑且说他是个高级政府官员——就这样吧。”

En: Not only his name, but his address... You are a goddamn menace.
Zh: 不仅说了他的名字，连他的地址也……你真是个该死的威胁。

En: The witness said the hanging went down very quietly. No shouting, no commotion.
Zh: “目击者说整个吊起来的过程非常安静，没有叫喊，也没有任何骚动。”

En: The witness said he saw two people of Areopagite descent. And one Mesque.
Zh: “目击者说他看到两名艾瑞奥帕吉特血统的人，还有一个梅斯克人。”

En: So some Moralintern cronie, he says with great reproach. "What did he tell you? I bet he had a dead hooker situation, if he came to Martinaise."
Zh: “所以是国际道德伦理委员会的人，”他深恶痛绝地说。“他是怎么跟你说的？我敢打赌，如果他来马丁内斯，肯定是碰到了妓女死掉的问题。”

En: Titus scratches the back of his head and asks: "Well, let's hear it then. Who is your mystery fella?"
Zh: 提图斯挠了挠后脑勺说：“行啊，我们来听听看吧。你那个神秘的伙伴是谁？”

En: He's not alarmed by the sudden appearance of a witness. But he is surprised.
Zh: 突然出现的目击者并没有让他多惊慌，反倒是很惊讶。

En: Your fake witness? Titus rubs his chin. "Yeah, let's do that before this gets embarrassing."
Zh: “你乱编的目击者？”提图斯摸着下巴。“行啊，在越来越尬尴之前我们就这么办吧。”

En: MI is short for Moralintern. Moralintern is short for Moralist International, the coalition that runs Revachol.
Zh: MI是国际道德伦理委员会的简称。国际道德伦理委员会则是国际道德伦理主义者委员协会的简称，他们负责管理瑞瓦肖。

En: This goes without saying, but nonetheless -- don't give out his name.
Zh: 这是不言而喻的，尽管如此——不要说出他的名字。

En: Enough about the witness.
Zh: “别再打听目击者了。”

En: For the record -- which I have to type up later tonight -- no apocalypse related testimony was provided to us by the witness.
Zh: “先说一句——今天晚些时候我会打印出来——目击者没有向我们提供任何有关世界末日的证词。”

En: Check out the stand-up comedians the RCM has. When are we gonna get funnymen like that?
Zh: “来看看RCM的喜剧演员吧，我们什么时候才能找到这样的滑稽演员？”

En: You don't know shiiiiiit!
Zh: “你什么都不知道！”

En: I know you're lying, Shanky. He writes in his notebook.
Zh: “我知道你说谎了，尚克。”他在笔记本上写着。

En: They're avoiding having to answer this question.
Zh: 他们避免了正面回答这个问题。

En: I'd imagined you guys drinking and singing lynching songs. What's with the funeral silence?
Zh: “我还以为你们会喝着酒，唱着私刑的小曲呢。这沉默的葬礼是怎么回事？”

En: Never mind.
Zh: 算了。

En: We were drinking! He looks around. "Weren't we guys? I hit the bottle hard. I was drunk as fuck."
Zh: “我们是在喝酒！”他四处看了看。“是不是兄弟们？我拼命地喝酒，喝得烂醉如泥。”

En: Right. I'm convinced, Glen. He nods sarcastically. "Nothing off here. Just a regular hanging."
Zh: “好吧，我信了，格伦。”他讽刺地点点头。“没有任何异常，只是个普通的绞死事件。”

En: Bullseye! Glen looks around uncomfortably. The lieutenant hit a nerve.
Zh: 正中靶心！格伦不安地环顾四周，警督触及他的要害了。

En: Yes. Of course. Be *polite* with the suspects...
Zh: 是的，当然。对待嫌犯要有*礼貌*……

En: Damn, they wormed their way out of it. Somehow... try something else if you can.
Zh: 该死，他们狡辩脱身了。无论如何……如果可以的话，试试别的办法。

En: Yes, enough of Charles Villedrouin from Rue de Saint Ghislaine 33-B.
Zh: “是的，别再说住在圣吉莱纳路33-B的查尔斯·维尔德鲁安了。”

En: Some witness... I pulled the same shit. Came up with some shit -- then went and said it to people. Get a reaction. Don't have to go to cop school to try that.
Zh: “某个目击者……我也干过这种事。胡编乱造一番——然后去告诉别人，观察他们的反应。不用去条子学校都能学会。”

En: Great witness! He must be really parched, because he takes a giant gulp of beer.
Zh: “好棒的目击者！”他一定相当的渴，因为他喝了一大口啤酒。

En: You don't have to go to school for shit -- I never went to school and I'm doing great!
Zh: “根本不用去学校学任何东西——我从没去过学校，现在也很好啊！”

En: You doing a hell of a job, man! Hell of a job!
Zh: “你干得真棒，老兄！干得真棒！”

En: An almost inaudible whisper from the lieutenant: "Damn..."
Zh: 警督用几乎听不见的声音说道：“该死……”

En: Whatever, man. You're really wasting our time with this shit.
Zh: “随便吧，老兄。你这是在浪费我们的时间。”

En: Surprise, Titus! I've connected you to the local drug trade.
Zh: “惊喜吧，提图斯！我把你和本地的毒品交易联系起来了。”

En: Like hell you have. He leans back, unruffled. "There is no 'local drug trade'. This place is as clean as a rifle. Go back to Jamrock and ask the local junkies how clean *your* streets are in Precinct '41-Kilos'."
Zh: “你可真行。”他不慌不忙地往后一靠。“根本没有‘本地毒品交易’，这地方干净的不得了。回去加姆洛克问问那儿的瘾君子，*你*所在的‘41公斤’分局有多干净吧！”

En: We'll do that. In the meantime, he points south, "did you know that there's an abandoned lorry at the intersection that was used to move raw ingredients for drugs from Terminal B to Jamrock?"
Zh: “我们会的。与此同时，”他指向南方，“你知道在十字路口有一辆废弃的货車吗，就是曾经用来把毒品原材料从B中转站运到加姆洛克的那个？”

En: You're right, there is no local drug trade -- except you. (Point at Titus.)
Zh: “你说得对，本地没有什么毒品交易——除了你自己。”（指向提图斯。）

En: That's a mighty interesting theory. He rubs his jaw, smiling. "I guess that's what you would need to do, theoretically. A big, strong, state run monopoly *would* out-compete the runts on the street."
Zh: “非常有趣的推理。”他摩挲着下巴笑了。“理论上，我想这就是你需要做的。一个强大的国有垄断企业*将会*胜过那些满大街乱窜的小鬼们。”

En: Yeah, man. *Theoretically* that's what you would do -- to get rid of the gangs, the dealers, even some of the junkies.
Zh: “没错，老兄。*理论上*这就是你必须要做的。扫除黑帮成员和毒贩，甚至一些吸毒者。”

En: You would need good, trustworthy people to take their place, of course. *Hardy* men to run such a monopoly. He grins. "For the good of the community, of course."
Zh: “你肯定需要优秀、值得信赖的人来接替他们的位置，需要*吃苦耐劳*的人来经营这样一个垄断企业。”他笑着说。“这当然是为了社区的利益。”

En: Good idea. People are always going to do drugs. At least this way you have some control over it.
Zh: “好主意。人们总是会去吸毒的。至少这样你会拥有一些控制权。”

En: Yeah, keep telling yourself that. I know for a fact there's still plenty of drugs out there.
Zh: “是啊，你就一直这么跟自己说吧。我只知道一个事实，外面还流通着大量毒品。”

En: There's still plenty of drugs out there. In fact, I have some in my bloodstream right now.
Zh: “外面还流通着大量毒品。事实上，现在我的血液里可能就有一些。”

En: Enjoy your shitty ride, junkie-cop. We still have it under control. All you're doing is contributing to the local economy. It's just a little hitch.
Zh: “好好放飞自我吧，毒鬼警察。我们还是能控制的。你所做的是在为当地的经济发展做贡献。这只是个小问题。”

En: The person driving it was present at the hanging. It was one of you. We've connected the footprints. He taps on his notebook, then turns to you. "Detective, do you want to deliver the coup de grace?"
Zh: “开車的人当晚就在案发现场，就是你们其中一人。我们已经匹配了脚印。”他轻敲着笔记本，然后转向你。“警探，你要来给他们致命一击吗？”

En: Forget it. I knew you'd never own up to it. (Back off.)
Zh: “算了吧。我知道你永远不会承认的。”（退后。）

En: Own up to what? he shrugs. "Why don't you limp out of here, little detective, and keep your nose off my intersection."
Zh: “承认什么？”他耸耸肩。“为什么不滚开呢，小警探，从我面前消失吧。”

En: The thought forms in your head, like a lightning strike branching in the sky.
Zh: 这个想法在你脑中形成，如同闪电划过了天空。

En: No, do the honour, you've earned it. (Nod at the lieutenant.)
Zh: “不，还是你请吧，这是你应得的。”（向警督点点头。）

En: Thank you. He turns to face the man. "You're right, Titus. There is no local drug trade, because it's all controlled by *you*. *You're* the drug trade."
Zh: “谢谢你。”他转身面对那个男人。“你说得对，提图斯。本地没有毒品交易，因为一切都是由*你*控制的。*你们*就在贩毒。”

En: No, the thunder is his. Leave it to the lieutenant.
Zh: 不，这个想法是他的，交给警督把。

En: Yeah, there's always gonna be little sprouts. People find a way to make coin. But that's the little picture... Hardie boys are taking care of the big picture!
Zh: “是啊，总会有些小苗头。大家都想找路子赚点小钱。但这只是其中的一小部分……哈迪兄弟会负责大局！”

En: No, there aren't. Some little shit and his dad are doing speed. Boo-fucking-hoo. The stuff's probably from Jamrock.
Zh: “才没有。只是某个小崽子和他老爸在嗑药，大概是从加姆洛克搞来的吧。”

En: Whatever you've seen is peanuts. Look at the big picture, man. The place is a paradise -- and all thanks to Hardie boys!
Zh: “你所看到的不过是冰山一角，看看大局吧，老兄。这地方就是一座天堂——都要归功于哈迪兄弟！”

En: You've just diverted the flow to Jamrock. You've made it *our* problem.
Zh: “你只是把祸水都引到了加姆洛克，让它变成了*我们*的问题。”

En: This is disgusting. You're admitting to profiting off of poisoning your own people.
Zh: “真恶心。你承认通过毒害自己人而获利了。”

En: Yes, but that just sends everyone else to Jamrock, doesn't it?
Zh: 是的，但那只会把其他所有人都送到加姆洛克去，不是吗？

En: We haven't *done* anything. But theoretically, it has to be someone's problem. So it might as well be yours. He takes a sip of his beer and smiles.
Zh: “我们什么都没*做*。但理论上讲，这肯定会是某个人的问题，所以也有可能是你的问题。”他抿了一口啤酒，微笑着。

En: Titus, I'm amazed. Where is the same professionalism when it comes to *other* topics? You're doing great. I hardly have to interfere at all.
Zh: “提图斯，我很惊讶。涉及到*其他*话题时你那份专业性到哪儿去了？你做的很好，我几乎不用插手了。”

En: I don't know, Lizzie. I guess *theoretical* things don't make me emotional. He turns to you. "Now was there anything else? I was sorta going to get my brewskie on."
Zh: “我不知道，莉兹。也许*理论上*的东西不会让我情绪化吧。”他转向你。“现在还有别的问题吗？我想喝点小酒了。”

En: Thank you. Theoretically, of course. Thank you -- *theoretically*. He takes a sip of his beer and smiles.
Zh: “谢谢你。理论上来说，当然。谢谢你——*理论上*。”他抿了一口啤酒笑了。

En: Boo-fucking-hoo. People will always be taking drugs. Might as well do it clean and organized.
Zh: “呜呜。人们总是要吸毒的，还不如让一切干脆利落，有组织有纪律起来。”

En: He seems genuinely surprised at your response. In a good way.
Zh: 他似乎真的对你的反应有些惊喜了。

En: Yeah, that's what the labour movement is *all* about. Clean and organized. And the Hardie boys are runnin' it.
Zh: “没错，这就是劳工运动的*意义*所在。干脆利落，有组织有纪律。哈迪兄弟们正是这么办的。”

En: Theoretically, of course. We're just talking politics here. My answer to your drug accusation is: *How dare you? Go fuck yourself!*
Zh: “理论上说，当然。我们只是聊了几句政治问题。我对于贩毒指控的回答是：*你居然敢这么说？滚你妈的蛋！*”

En: Not quite yet, Mr. Hardie. There were eight sets of prints on the crime scene. There are only seven Hardie boys here.
Zh: “还没完呢，哈迪先生。犯罪现场有八组脚印，这里只有七个哈迪兄弟。”

En: Aaaand here we go. Back to the usual. The woman sighs.
Zh: “来吧，恢复如常。”女人叹了口气。

En: I know, I know! The little man raises his index finger excitedly. "Fattie walked on all fours. He's so fucking fat he left two sets of footprints."
Zh: “我知道，我知道。”小个子男人兴奋地举起了食指。“胖子是用四肢爬行的。他太他妈的胖了，所以留下了两组脚印。”

En: That's more like it, boys. She turns to you. "You heard him, it was Angus on all fours. Anything else you need to know?"
Zh: “这才像话，兄弟们。”她转向你。“你听到他说的了，那是用四肢走路的安格斯留下的。还有什么想问的吗？”

En: Go fuck your mom, Dennis.
Zh: “干你老母，丹尼斯。”

En: Finally, you got something out of him. This could prove useful in the future.
Zh: 终于，你从他嘴里套出了点东西，这在以后是能派上用场的。

En: Despite everything, the little man is quick on his feet.
Zh: 不论发生了什么，那个小个子男人总能很快振作起来。

En: Fine. I had other questions.
Zh: “行吧，我还有一个问题。”

En: What fucking questions?
Zh: “他妈的还有什么问题？”

En: Oh, he's itching for one... He clacks his tongue. "I listen to Channel 8. This cop has a death wish."
Zh: “哦，他们倒想……”他咂舌。“我听第八频道，这些条子们都有死亡意愿。”

En: The eighth Hardie -- the one who's missing. She runs the thing, right?
Zh: “第八个哈迪兄弟——消失的那个。是她在掌管一切，对吗？”

En: My answer is: *fuck off*! He takes a step closer. "Mind your own business. There is no *eighth Hardie*. I run this goddamn scene!"
Zh: “我的答案是：*滚开*！”他走近了一步。“少管闲事。根本就没有*第八个*哈迪兄弟，我才是这里的掌管者！”

En: That was an unsure phrasing.
Zh: 那是个不确定的措辞。

En: He's really prepped himself up for this. He has to. To keep the fear in check.
Zh: 他真的已经做好准备了。他必须这样。控制住自己的恐惧。

En: I talked to Joyce. The merc you hanged -- his friends are coming for you.
Zh: “我跟乔伊斯谈过了。你吊起来的那个雇佣兵——他的朋友要来找你了。”

En: Yeah? He doesn't seem worried. "By friends you mean his squadmates from Krenel?"
Zh: “是吗？”他似乎不怎么担心。“你说的朋友，指的是他在科奈尔的战友？”

En: Big fucking surprise... He mutters. "They hire psycho scum, arm them to the teeth and let them loose in the city. What do you think is gonna happen?"
Zh: “真他妈的惊喜……”他喃喃自语着。“他们雇了一群心理变态的人渣，让他们全副武装，放他们在城市里乱逛。你觉得会发生什么？”

En: Let them come! Blondie yells across the cafeteria. "The Hardie boys are right fucking here!"
Zh: “让他们放马过来好了！”金发男的叫喊响彻餐厅。“哈迪兄弟他妈的就在这里！”

En: The mercenaries are armed with automatic weapons.
Zh: “那些雇佣兵们都装备着自动武器。”

En: This Krenel is bad news. You know that, right?
Zh: “这个科奈尔很棘手。你知道的，对吧？”

En: Yes, they are forming some kind of  *tribunal* -- and they're coming for you.
Zh: “是的，他们正在准备*宣判*——而且是冲你们来的。”

En: This is what happens if you take the law into your own hands. Other people start doing it too.
Zh: “这就是你们自己掌握执法权的结果，别人都纷纷效仿了。”

En: You heard the man -- right here. He points to the ground. "We're armed, we got the whole district behind us and Glen... Glen is fucking *crazy*."
Zh: “你听到他说的了——就在这里。”他指着地面。“我们也有武装，整个地区的人都支持着我们，还有格伦……格伦就他妈是个*疯子*。”

En: *Nervous* snickering. There's a rush of adrenaline present.
Zh: *紧张*的窃笑。现在肾上腺素正在激增。

En: Wouldn't wanna beat up his grandma. There's snickering in the room. Some of the men put their beers down.
Zh: “真不想揍他的奶奶。”房间里有人在窃笑，有些人放下了啤酒。

En: Titus did his best, but his men are a bit unsettled.
Zh: 提图斯尽力了，但他的兄弟们都有些不心神不宁。

En: Forget I mentioned it, it was probably nothing.
Zh: “忘了我说的话吧，可能根本没什么。”

En: Goddamn right it's nothing. The fuck are they gonna do? We got ten thousand men in the Union.
Zh: “当然没什么了！他们他妈的能怎么样？我们工会里有一万人呢。“

En: Evrart said there were two thousand three hundred and seventy two Union members. But whatever keeps their spirits up.
Zh: 艾弗拉特说工会一共有2372名成员，但无论什么都能让他们振奋起来。

En: It's probably more like two or three thousand. But whatever keeps their spirits up.
Zh: 可能更像是两三千人吧。不过无论什么都能让他们振奋起来。

En: Yeah, a well oiled murder-machine! He punches blondie on the shoulder.
Zh: “没错，一台上了油的杀戮机器！”他猛锤了一下金发男子的肩膀。

En: The mood is on the rise. They're feeling confident -- ready to punch out the whole merc platoon.
Zh: 他们情绪高涨，非常自信——准备好要干掉整排雇佣兵了。

En: Do you know that a single Seraise Giant Hornet can kill forty bees a *minute*?
Zh: “你知道一只塞拉斯大黄蜂*一分钟*能杀死四十只蜜蜂吗？”

En: ... the fuck is that supposed to mean?
Zh: “……那他妈是什么意思？”

En: You're the bees; they're the hornet.
Zh: “你们是蜜蜂；他们是大黄蜂。”

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: We're not bees, we're men. We're socialists!
Zh: “我们不是蜜蜂，我们是人类，是工人主义者。”

En: Easy, E, he's tryna' faze you. He turns to you. "What are you trying to do? Scare my men?"
Zh: “冷静，尤金，他是在吓唬你。”他转向你。“你想干什么？吓坏我的兄弟们？”

En: Goddamn right it's nothing. This isn't a fucking terrarium. This is Martinaise.
Zh: “当然没什么了！这里可不是什么生态箱，这里是马丁内斯。”

En: Terrarium? He's really trying to salvage something out of it.
Zh: 生态箱？看来真的想挽回点什么。

En: Okay. (Conclude with a shrug.)
Zh: “好吧。”（以耸肩结束。）

En: We got weapons of our own. He cracks open his vest to give you a glimpse of his holster. "We got Ister 50s, Zielegers, Glen's got a nock-cannon at home..."
Zh: “我们自己也有武器。”他拉开背心，让你看了一眼他的枪套。“我们有伊斯特50s，齐格勒，格伦家里还有把七管大抬枪……”

En: Will they pierce ceramic armour?
Zh: “它们能穿透陶瓷盔甲吗？”

En: I guess we're gonna see, aren't we?
Zh: “我们只能拭目以待了，不是吗？”

En: What do you mean *okay*? He jerks forward a bit.
Zh: “你说*好吧*是什么意思？”他猛地朝前上了一步。

En: I mean, 'Okay, they're going to wipe you the fuck out, Titus.'
Zh: “我的意思是，‘好吧，他们会把你们干掉的，提图斯。’”

En: I mean 'Okay, you got this' -- you got the numbers.
Zh: “我的意思是，‘好吧，看来你能搞定——人多势众。”

En: Joyce said they've gone rogue. Nobody is controlling them.
Zh: “乔伊斯说他们已经失控了，没人能制得住他们。”

En: I mean, 'Okay, I'll be on your side when they come.'
Zh: “我的意思是，‘好吧，他们过来的话我会站在你们这边的。’”

En: Goddamn right we do. He nods. "We've done it before. Ain't that right, fellas?!"
Zh: “这是当然。”他点了点头。“我们以前也经历过。是不是，伙计们？！”

En: Yeah... he snorts. "Just try not to get in the way -- when the whoop-ass flies open!"
Zh: “是啊……”他嗤之以鼻。“当屁股开花的时候——尽量别碍事！”

En: I mean, 'Okay, you got one more gun on your side.' Once I find mine. I lost it.
Zh: “我的意思是，‘好吧，还有一把枪会站在你这边。’等我找到枪之后。我把它弄丢了。”

En: I mean, 'Okay, I guess I'll be gunning you down right alongside them.'
Zh: “我的意思是，‘好吧，我只好把你和他们一并干掉了。’”

En: We don't need you or your lost gun. We all *have* our guns. He almost splashes his beer at you. "We'll just use those if it's okay with you."
Zh: “我们不需要你还有你弄丢的枪。我们都*有*自己的枪。”他差点把啤酒溅到你身上。“如果你没意见的话，我们就用这些就行了。”

En: Funny. Don't get hit by a stray bullet.
Zh: “有意思，别被流弹击中了。”

En: No wonder you cops get shot to shit every day -- can't go to war with an attitude like *that*.
Zh: “难怪你们这些条子每天都被枪击——可不能带着*那种*态度参战争。”

En: Pft! A spray of beer. "So were the local gangs. The fuckin' *Barmy Army* and the Madre scum. You've been out there. Seen any around?"
Zh: “呸！”喷出一口啤酒。“本地帮派也是一样。该死的*傻逼军团*和马德雷的渣滓们。你出去时没见到他们吗？”

En: Yeah? Where are they now, huh? He points South. "Sent back to Madre in an airtight cargo crate."
Zh: “是吗？他们现在在哪儿，嗯？”他指着南边。“打包进密封的集装箱里送回马德雷那里去了。”

En: See what? That they don't?
Zh: “待什么？打不穿吗？”

En: For your sake I hope you're right.
Zh: “为了你自己，希望你是对的。”

En: Yeah, like you've been up against ceramic armour... He takes a sip of beer to bide his time, then tries to get the last word in.
Zh: “是啊，说得好像你直面过陶瓷盔甲似的……”他抿了一小口啤酒来打发时间，在最后的时候插了进来。

En: Right about what? He looks at you defiantly. "That a 50 calibre bullet goes through shit? Yeah. I am."
Zh: “什么对的？”他挑衅地看着你。“一颗50口径的子弹能穿过去吗？是啊，我太对了。”

En: No he won't. The lieutenant adjusts his glasses. "This is probably some kind of a joke."
Zh: “不，他不会。”警督调整着他的眼镜。“这可能就是个玩笑。”

En: Fuck yeah we have! Battle-hardened urban soldiers, man. We got the guns *and* the reserves.
Zh: “他妈的当然有！我们可是久经沙场的城市战士，我们有枪*和*预备人员。”

En: Yeah... we got reserves. He nods nervously. "We can call in reinforcements."
Zh: “是啊……我们有预备人员。”他紧张地点头。“我们可以请求增援。”

En: This guy will be the first one to bail.
Zh: 这家伙一定会第一个逃跑。

En: I think he meant a *can* of whoop-ass?
Zh: 我想他的意思是要*打得*他们屁股开花？

En: Beneath the *whoop-assery* it's clear that he appreciates all the help he can get.
Zh: 在*开花的屁股*背后，他显然很感激所有他能得到的帮助。

En: Lost gun or not, you're going to need help. The lieutenant looks Titus in the eye. "We're offering it."
Zh: “不管有没有丢枪，你都需要帮助。”警督直视着提图斯的眼睛。“而我们向你伸出了手。”

En: Okay, I accept the offer. The man cracks his shoulder. "But I'll believe it when I see it."
Zh: “好吧，我接受你的援手。”那人扭了扭肩膀。“但我要亲眼看到才会相信。”

En: He appreciates it, but can only accept it *conditionally*.
Zh: 他很感激，但只愿*有条件的*接受。

En: No, they won't. He shakes his beer at you. "Get out of here with your negative energy."
Zh: “不，他们不会的。”他朝着你摇晃着啤酒。“带着你的负能量滚出去。”

En: He really doesn't like you ruffling their feathers like that -- on what might be the eve of battle.
Zh: 他真的不喜欢你这般招惹他们——在一个随时可能发生战争的前夕。

En: All he means is that the situation is serious.
Zh: “他的意思是情况很严重。”

En: These people are trained military professionals. Special forces, as you said. They're not a gang, or a *Barmy Army*.
Zh: “这些人都是训练有素的军事专业人员。就像你说的，特种部队。他们不是什么帮派，或者*傻逼军团*。”

En: These people are trained military professionals. With decades of combat experience. They're not a gang, or a *Barmy Army*.
Zh: “这些人都是训练有素的军事专业人员。拥有几十年的战斗经验。他们不是什么帮派，或者*傻逼军团*。”

En: No, they're not. They're un-coordinated and drunk. Don't forget: we're listening in on their communications. Their shit is *way* apart.
Zh: “不，他们不是。他们是一群无组织的醉汉。别忘了：我们在监听他们的通话。他们就是一盘*散沙*。”

En: No, they're not. They're un-coordinated and drunk. We know more about them than you think.
Zh: “不，不是，他们是一群无组织的醉汉。我们比你想象的更了解他们。”

En: Well, if Glen has a *nock-cannon* at home I guess you'll be alright.
Zh: “好吧，如果格伦家里有台*七管大抬枪*的话，我想你会没事的。”

En: That thing has seven barrels. One trigger. You do the math.
Zh: “那东西有七个枪管，一个扳机，你自己算算。”

En: So that's *one* dead and a looooong reload. But whatever.
Zh: 所以那意味着*一个*死人和巨久无比的每一次装弹都极其不便，需要很长时间。

En: You haven't even seen the whole suit, right? I've seen the whole fucking thing and it didn't make him immortal.
Zh: “你还没见过全套装备，对吧？我可看到了整套东西，但也没能让他成长生不死啊。”

En: Yup. Her.
Zh: “是的。她。”

En: He pushes up his cap. "God, poor lady. Don't worry. We'll handle this. I think she's got some family in Couron or something... Bastards left her alone when she got sick, we've been getting complaints."
Zh: 他把帽子往上推了推。“天啦，可怜的老太太。别担心。我们会处理的。我记得她在科戎好像有家人之类的……她病了之后，那帮杂种就把她一个人留下了，我们收到过很多投诉。”

En: The children kept her together.
Zh: 孩子能让她保持镇静。

En: Police paraphernalia doesn't just wash up on the coast, people. It's not like we dump it at night. She bought it, collected it.
Zh: “警察的随身用品是不会被冲上海岸的。又不是说我们会在半夜把东西偷偷倒了。都是她买来的，收集的。”

En: Was? Like before? Just an old lady. Her kids moved away years ago. Never come to visit, never took her calls. She gets... out every now and then. He swirls his beer. "She did right by lots of us when we were kids. Always was a little off, but still."
Zh: “是谁？以前吗？她就是个老太太。她的孩子几年前就搬走了。从不来看她，也不接她电话。她时不时……会出去溜达。”他摇晃着手中的啤酒。“在我们还是孩子的时候，她对我们很好。虽然总是有点不太对劲，不过还是很好。”

En: Us kids? That must have been ages ago. She was better then.
Zh: 还是孩子？那一定是很久之前的事了。那时候她的状态要好一些。

En: Someone whose only connection to the outside world is Channel 8 and it's cop programs?
Zh: “和外界唯一的联系就是第八频道的警察节目的人？”

En: Oh, don't even get me started on that Gareth Morrand crap! The Mesque gets visibly agitated. "That racist fucking fuck... motherfucker."
Zh: “哦，别跟我提加雷斯·莫兰德那个垃圾！”梅斯克人明显激动起来了。“那个该死的……种族主义混蛋！”

En: Easy, Al... He glances over his shoulder. "We all hate him. The man's all cock and no balls. Can't trust a fella like that."
Zh: “冷静，兰……”他回头看了看。“我们都很讨厌他。那家伙有胆没种。不能信任那种人。”

En: Must be a local saying.
Zh: 一定是句当地俗语。

En: The big man laughs, nearly spitting out his beer. "What's that, copper? You want us to help little old ladies now?"
Zh: 大块头笑了，几乎要把啤酒喷出来。“你说什么，条子？你现在想让我们去帮小老太太了？”

En: There's an old catatonic lady in the old fish market, on the other side of the bay. She needs help.
Zh: “旧鱼市里有个患有紧张性精神症的老太太，就在海湾的另一边。她需要帮助。”

En: She had my gun.
Zh: “她拿了我的枪。”

En: Nope! She didn't have anything of mine.
Zh: “没有！她没有我的任何东西。”

En: No? Well, whatever then, copper. Titus chugs his beer down and wipes his mouth.
Zh: “没有？好吧，管它呢，条子。”提图斯咕嘟咕嘟灌着啤酒，擦了擦嘴。

En: You're *local law enforcement* aren't you? The lieutenant looks Titus in the eye. "Helping troubled civilians should fall under your jurisdiction."
Zh: “你是*本地执法人员*，不是吗？”警督直视着提图斯的眼睛。“帮助陷入困境的民众应该是你的分内事。”

En: You're fucking with me. The big man slams his beer onto the table. "Your gun! She had your fucking gun!"
Zh: 大块头男人把啤酒砰地一声摔在桌上。“你的枪！她拿了你该死的枪！”

En: Sure did, boss! The rat-man's face is twisted with a smile. "Heard him loud and clear -- the pig's lost his gun to The Pigs!"
Zh: “当然了，老大！”老鼠脸男人扭曲的微笑着。“听得可清楚了——条子把枪搞丢了，落到猪猪女侠手里了！”

En: How did she get like this?
Zh: “她是怎么变成这样的？”

En: Who was she?
Zh: “她是谁？”

En: Boy howdy. He sips his beer, recovering. "Well. We'll get her help. You just stay out of the way. Way far out of the way."
Zh: “真有你的。”他喝着啤酒，慢慢平复下来。“可以，我们会去帮她的。只要你不挡道，离得远远的。”

En: What's her name? Her real name.
Zh: “她叫什么名字？她的真名。”

En: Where'd she get all her cop gear?
Zh: “她的警用装备是哪里来的？”

En: Thanks for helping out with this one, Titus. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢你的配合，提图斯。”[离开。]

En: Hey, Eugene interjects. "Wasn't Evrart's B Team looking for her the other day? They said something about her... I dunno, finding something?"
Zh: “嘿，”尤金插了一句。“那天艾弗拉特的B小队不是还在找她吗？他们说她……我也不知道，发现了什么东西？”

En: Yeah, I think you're right, Gene. Titus gives him a beer salute and turns to you. "She have something of yours, pig?"
Zh: “是啊，我想你说得对，尤金。”提图斯举起酒杯向他致敬，然后转向你。“她那儿有你的东西吗，条子？”

En: No, they totally said what it was... What was it... He's still thinking it over in the corner. "Anyone remember?"
Zh: “不，他们绝对说了那是……什么来着……”他留在角落里思索着。“有谁记得吗？”

En: The tattooed man scratches his head with his knuckles. "I don't remember, I was fuckin' drunk."
Zh: 纹身男用指关节挠挠头。“我记不清了，我他妈的喝醉了。”

En: Let me know if you figure it out back there, the big man says, turning to you. "Now, anything else we can help you with?"
Zh: “等你在那儿想明白了再告诉我，”大个子男人转身对你说。“还有什么需要我们帮忙的吗？”

En: No problemo, cop-man. We take care of our mentally ill here in Martinaise. Ain't that right, boys?
Zh: “小事一桩，条子。我们会照顾马丁内斯的精神病患者。是不是，兄弟们？”

En: Sure enough! Eugene raises his glass in toast. "We're the *real* heroes on these streets!"
Zh: “毫无疑问！”尤金举起酒杯致敬。“我们才是这条街上*真正的*英雄！”

En: The tattooed Mesque sinks into his seat, unsure how to respond. "Yeah... you should be."
Zh: 有纹身的梅斯克人陷在自己的椅子上，不确定该如何回应。“是啊……应该的。”

En: Get wanting to be a cop, you mean? Well, she... He furrows his brow in thought. "Shit, I don't actually know. Anyone know why she started acting like a pig?"
Zh: “你是说想成为一个条子？那个嘛，她……”他皱着眉头沉思起来。“妈的，我真的不知道。有人知道她为什么开始表现得像个条子一样吗？”

En: No fuckin' clue, the tattooed man shouts. "It's gotta be the crazy. Who'd *want* to be a pig?"
Zh: “他妈的不知道，”纹身男喊道。“一定是疯了吧。谁会真的*想*当条子？”

En: Someone who wanted to set the world right before it ends. Ever think of that?
Zh: “那种想在世界末日来临之前让世界恢复秩序的人，有想过吗？”

En: Fuck you, pig! He stands. "What the fuck you playing at, huh?"
Zh: “去死吧，条子！”他站起来。“你他妈在搞什么鬼，嗯？”

En: More like who *wouldn't*! Cool uniforms, fast machines, packing heat? Sign me up!
Zh: “不如说是谁*不想*吧！超酷的制服，超快的汽車，还能持枪？算上我一个！”

En: I didn't want it. Kim has it.
Zh: “我不想要了，金还有一把。”

En: Law enforcement is a respectable occupation, sir.
Zh: “执法人员是一份体面的工作，先生。”

En: It's been taken care of, the lieutenant replies laconically. "But she hasn't been."
Zh: “已经解决了，”警督简洁地回答。“但她没有。”

En: I can't but feel partially responsible for the unhealthy culture that poor woman latched onto. I'm sorry.
Zh: “我忍不住觉得自己应该为那个可怜女人不健康的文化负部分责，我很抱歉。”

En: He takes off his cap and scratches his head. "Auntie LePlante, we always called her. Something LePlante?"
Zh: 他摘下帽子挠挠头。“普兰特阿姨，我们总是这么叫她。普兰特什么的？”

En: Marianne, *amigo*, he says pouring himself another drink.
Zh: “玛丽安，*朋友*，”他说着又给自己倒了一杯酒。

En: Probably for the best. At least it's not going to do harm there... He sips his beer, recovering. "Well. We'll get her help. You just stay out of the way. Way far out of the way."
Zh: “也许这样最好，至少没有任何坏处……”他喝着啤酒，慢慢平复下来。“可以，我们会去帮她的。只要你不挡道，离得远远的。”

En: Marianne Amigo LePlante.
Zh: “玛丽安·朋友·普兰特。“

En: At least it's in good hands. He sips his beer, recovering. "Well. We'll get her help. You just stay out of the way. Way far out of the way."
Zh: “至少它没有所托非人。”他喝着啤酒，慢慢平复下来。“可以，我们会去帮她的。只要你不挡道，离得远远的。”

En: The big man stares at you, still stunned. Too stunned to even drink his beer. "Where is it now," he asks slowly. "I don't see it..."
Zh: 大块头盯着你，仍处于震惊之中，震惊到啤酒都不喝了。“它现在在哪儿？”他慢吞吞地问到。“我反正没看见……”

En: Yeah, cop, that's some pretty fucking weird shit to say. *The Pigs* level weird. The big man shrugs. "But whatever gets you through the day."
Zh: “是啊，条子，这话说得真他妈太奇怪了。*猪猪女侠*级别的奇怪。”大个子耸耸肩。“但能让你混过今天就行。”

En: He blinks. "No, I didn't. Because I'm not fucking stupid, *polizonte*."
Zh: 他眨眨眼。“不，我没有，因为我他妈的不是傻逼，*警察*。”

En: It fell into the water.
Zh: “它掉进水里了。”

En: You're already signed up, *cerdo*. The man shakes his head. "This guy's seriously damaged, T."
Zh: “你已经是了，*猪仔*。”男人摇摇头。“这家伙真是病得不轻，提。”

En: Respectable professions don't shoot kids, *cerdo*! He stands, hands wrapped tight around the back of his chair. "They don't beat confessions out of prisoners!"
Zh: “体面的职业不会向孩子开枪的，*猪仔*！”他站起来，双手紧紧抓着椅背。“也不会屈打成招。”

En: You absolute fool.
Zh: 你绝对是个傻瓜。

En: Marianne LePlante.
Zh: “玛丽安·普兰特。”

En: Yeah, yeah. We'll send someone out. Who is -- wait... It's *The Pigs*, isn't it?
Zh: “好吧好吧。我们会派人过去，谁是——等等……是*猪猪女侠*，对吗？”

En: If you wanna be the local law enforcement, these kind of things fall on your shoulders.
Zh: “如果你想成为本地的执法人员，这些事情就会落在你的肩头。”

En: Easy, Al. The man says soothingly. "Don't let him get to you. Have one for Auntie LePlante, huh?"
Zh: “冷静，兰。”那人安慰道。“别让他影响到你，就算是为了普兰特阿姨，嗯？”

En: The big guy nods, drinking his toast. "Anything else you wanted to know?"
Zh: 大个子点点头，干掉了他的酒。“你还想知道什么吗？”

En: The big man stares at you, still stunned. Too stunned to even drink his beer. "Well," he says after a moment. "At least I can see you got it back. If you have any questions..." He waves in circles, still searching for words.
Zh: 大块头盯着你，仍处于震惊之中，震惊到啤酒都不喝了。“好吧，”过了一会儿他说。“至少我看见你把它拿回来了。如果你还有任何问题……”他挥舞着双手，还在寻找适合的词汇。

En: Don't disrespect Miss LePlante, copper. The big man finishes off a can. "She's good people. We all get a little crazy sometimes."
Zh: “别不尊重普兰特小姐，条子。”大个子男人喝完了一罐啤酒。“她是个好人。有时候我们都会有点小疯狂。”

En: Ah, don't worry about it, Al. The big man smiles, pushing up his cap. "The cops wanna dress up and play policeman. Let 'em. We'll keep doing the real work."
Zh: “啊别担心，兰。”大个子男人笑了，把帽子向上推了推。“条子们想要打扮成警察的样子，随他们去吧。我们会继续做真正的工作的。”

En: Whatever you do, do not admit to these punks you lost your gun. You will suffer if you do.
Zh: 不管你要做什么，都别向这群混蛋承认你弄丢了自己的枪，否则你会吃苦头的。

En: The big man shrugs. "Whatever, cop. Don't worry about it. We'll take care of our own."
Zh: 大个子耸耸肩。“随便吧，条子。别担心。我们会顾好自己人。”

En: Easy there, Al. He turns to the tattooed man, who is taking heaving breaths. "Sit down. We've got one of our own to take care of. So let's not get worked up."
Zh: “冷静，兰。”他转向那个纹身男，他正大口喘着粗气。“坐下。我们的人我们自己照顾。我们还有自己人要照顾呢。所以别太激动了。”

En: Dunno, Titus says, shrugging. "She lives by the water. Shit washes up all the time on the beach."
Zh: “不知道。”提图斯耸耸肩说。“她住在海边，总是有东西被冲到海滩上来。”

En: Station 41. A man carries a crate of rusted, unused badges. "You think these would net us something at the annual auction?" A balding detective drags a comb futilely across his head. "Shit, who'd want those? Just dump them into the river."
Zh: 41分局，一名男子搬着一箱生锈的、没用过的警徽。“你觉得这些能在年度拍卖上为我们捞点什么吗？”一个秃头警探徒劳地用梳子刮着自己的头顶。“妈的，谁会想要这些？把它们扔进河里去吧。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: ... no worse than the bangers on Boogie Street.
Zh: “……不会比布吉街的那些帮派分子糟糕。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: *This* is gonna make a hell of a sad tattoo...
Zh: “*这*将会是一个非常悲伤的纹身……”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: Theo went like he lived -- fast and hard. Tough old man.
Zh: “特奥走的时候跟他生前一样——又快又狠。是条汉子。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: The Hardie boys are *unfuckable* now.
Zh: “哈迪兄弟已经*无人能敌*了，我们可以接管整座岛屿。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: We could take over the entire city.
Zh: “我们可以接管整座城市。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: Like a fucking *cobarde*... bitchman-Alain, shit...
Zh: “像个该死的*胆小鬼*……弱鸡阿兰，狗屎……”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / Alain 2 barks post tribunal
En: Alain is a fucking roach name anyway... So *me*.

En: The elevator stands open, inviting you to step inside.
Zh: 电梯门大敞着，邀请你走进去。

En: This small elevator is dimly lit by a bulb that's been glowing for ages. The latticed cage is open, inviting you to step inside.
Zh: 小电梯里的老旧灯泡散发出微弱的光芒，格状铁笼大敞着，邀请你走进去。

En: (Close the doors and go up.)
Zh: （关门上楼。）

En: Go down.
Zh: 下楼。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The elevator screeches and rattles, like the belly of some ancient beast, as it carries you upward...
Zh: 电梯嘎吱作响，仿佛某种古代巨兽的肚子，正带着你朝上走……

En: The elevator lurches and squeaks, like the belly of some ancient beast, as you descend....
Zh: 电梯摇摆不定，仿佛某种古代巨兽的肚子，正带着你朝下走…….

En: Look in.
Zh: 往里看。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Smells of nougat and sweat. Your head brushes up against the ceiling. There is a control panel to your right -- and just enough room for two people to fit in.
Zh: 伴随着一股牛轧糖和汗水的味道，你的头碰到了天花板。你的右手边有个控制面板——正好只够容纳两个人。

En: The maintenance card under the control panel reads: 'Last Maintenance: 10 July '88'.
Zh: 控制面板下方的维修卡上写着：*上次维护时间：88年，7月10日*。

En: Look at the elevator controls.
Zh: 看着电梯的控制面板。

En: There are large rectangular buttons: 'Monter', 'Descendre', and an international: 'Call for Emergency Assistance'. That third one appears to be broken.
Zh: 上面有巨大的矩形按钮：“上升”，“下降”，以及一句国际通用的：“请求紧急援助。”第三个按钮似乎坏掉了。

En: It says the last maintenance was in '88.
Zh: “上面说最后一次维护是在88年。”

En: That it does. The lieutenant peeks in. "I say -- let's brave it."
Zh: “的确如此。”警督往里瞄了一眼。“要我说——勇敢一点吧。”

En: This elevator was last maintained in the *future*?
Zh: “这部电梯最后一次维护是在*未来*？”

En: That means it's not really that safe, is it.
Zh: “这意味着它不是非常安全，对吧？”

En: Look on the bright side: if it fails, we will only sustain minor injuries. I'm talking three, maybe four months in the hospital. Maximum five.
Zh: “往好的方面想：如果它坏掉了，也不过是受点轻伤而已。我是指在医院住上三、四个月，或者五个月不会再多了。”

En: No, it was maintained in '88 of the *previous* century.
Zh: “不是，这是在*上个*世纪的88年维护的。”

En: So it's *not* a message from the future?
Zh: “所以这不是来自未来的信息咯？”

En: That's disappointing.
Zh: “真令人失望。”

En: No, I think the bureaucrats just forgot about this back-room elevator after the Revolution.
Zh: “不，我认为官僚们只是在大革命后忘记了这部秘密电梯。”

En:
If some form of time travel becomes possible in the future, I doubt its inventors will use it to repair elevators in Martinaise,” the lieutenant responds with a grave voice.	警督严肃地答道：“如果未来真有可能实现某种时间旅行，我怀疑它的发明者也不会用它来修理马丁内斯的电梯。”
0x0100005800009EE6	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F1 / ELEVATOR	You	What were you used for, elevator?	你是做什么用的，电梯？
0x0100005800009EF3	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F1 / ELEVATOR	Elevator	The deep scratches on the floor suggest it was used as a freight elevator -- for transporting heavy machinery. And one man.	地板上深深的划痕表明它曾被当做货梯使用——用于运输重型机械，和一个人。
0x0100005800009EF9	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F1 / ELEVATOR	Kim Kitsuragi	Seems so, although it's pretty... quaint for a freight elevator." He taps on the guttering light bulb -- it's golden in the dark.
Zh: “看上去确实如此，尽管它很漂亮……用于运货也太古怪了。”他轻敲着忽明忽暗的灯泡——它在黑暗中闪着金色的光芒。

En: Seems like a small freight elevator for transporting machinery. For that -- it's pretty quaint. He taps the on the guttering light bulb -- it's golden in the dark.
Zh: “看着像部运送机械的小货梯。这么看来——挺古怪的。”他轻敲着忽明忽暗的灯泡——它在黑暗中闪着金色的光芒。

En: This elevator must have been used to transport pinball machines to and from the upstairs workshop.
Zh: 这部电梯一定是用来把弹球机从楼上的車间运进运出的。

En: I wonder what this elevator was used for.
Zh: “我想知道这部电梯是做什么用的。”

En: So it was mostly pinball machines riding this elevator.
Zh: “所以大部分情况下都是弹球机在乘坐这部电梯。”

En: This elevator must have been needed to transport the pinball machines up and down.
Zh: “这部电梯一定是用来楼上楼下运送弹球机的。”

En: Would a pinball machine really fit in here?
Zh: 这真能塞得下一台弹球机吗？

En: Could this have been for moving pinball machines up and down?
Zh: 这有可能是用来楼上楼下运送弹球机的吗？

En: Just about. If tilted precisely. For a small elevator this one is surprisingly nice. The ancient golden bulb gutters in the dark.
Zh: 差不多，如果找准角度的话。对于一部小电梯来说，它出乎意料的精妙。破旧的金色灯泡在黑暗中忽明忽暗着。

En: Looks like one would fit in there, yes. Although it's also surprisingly human -- in the golden light of that ancient bulb.
Zh: 看起来确实能装下一台，没错。虽然它意外地像人——在破旧灯泡的金色光芒下。

En: A small steel plaque reads 'Halter 800'. Halter is a Königsteiner lift company who went out of business a long, long time ago.
Zh: 一小块钢匾上写着‘霍尔特800’。霍尔特是一家很早很早之前就倒闭了的柯尼希斯电梯公司。

En: Eighty-eight... This elevator was maintained a long time ago.
Zh: “88年……这部电梯是很久以前维护的。”

En: At the end of the last century. He nods. "Look on the bright side. If it fails, we will only sustain minor injuries -- I'm talking three, maybe four months in the hospital. maximum five."
Zh: “上个世纪末。”他点点头。“往好的方面想：如果它坏掉了，也不过是受点轻伤而已。我是指在医院住上三、四个月，或者五个月不会再多了。”

En: It appears his whole enthusiasm is sarcastic.
Zh: 看来他毕生的热爱就是讽刺。

En: I suppose, he continues, his voice now calm, "I should at least consider sticking around. If I'm not here, it's all over."
Zh: “或许吧，”他继续说，现在他的声音平静了下来，“我至少应该考虑留下来。如果我不在这里，那一切都结束了。”

En: Let's not get ahead of ourselves. He frowns. "We'll see what he does with the whole Shanky business."
Zh: “不要操之过急。”他皱着眉头。“我们等着看他会如何处理尚克的事吧。”

En: Yeah, man, you know... He slaps his hand on the table in a sudden burst of drunk enthusiasm. "You know -- you've got this. You got this!"
Zh: “是啊，老兄，你知道……”酒精的兴奋劲一上来，他突然拍了拍桌子。“你知道——你曾经做过。你可以的！”

En: He looks off to the side, eyes filled with worry. "I haven't *got* shit, Al."
Zh: 他把目光移到一边，眼睛里满是忧虑。“我从没有*做过*任何事，阿兰。”

En: There's something there. A germ of determination. He knows he has no choice but to rise to the occasion.
Zh: 那里有点什么。决心在悄悄萌芽。他知道自己别无选择，只能随机应变。

En: If *any* of them had leadership capacity beside Titus, it's him. Keep encouraging him.
Zh: 除了提图斯之外，如果说*还有谁*有这个领导才能，那就只能是他。继续鼓励他。

En: He's not a drinker. Another good thing about him.
Zh: 他不是个酒鬼。这又是他的一个优点。

En: This is not *completely* ludicrous. Him and Titus were the only detectives of the bunch. Titus more, but Eugene at least knew how to think along.
Zh: 这并非*完全*的荒唐。他和提图斯是这群人里唯一的侦探。提图斯更厉害一些，但是至少尤金知道怎么思考问题。

En: Damn songbird of misery that one... The man shakes his head.
Zh: “该死的痛苦夜莺……”男人摇摇头。

En: And do you have any information on this 'songbird of misery'?
Zh: “关于这个‘痛苦夜莺’，你知道什么信息吗？”

En: And do you have any information on her whereabouts?
Zh: “你知道她的去向吗？”

En: She's gone. Face it.
Zh: 她走了。面对现实吧。

En: Move on. There's no catching her this time around.
Zh: 向前看吧。这次是抓不到她了。

En: Yeah, you too. It's a rough world out there... He shakes his head. "It's not easy being a cop. We were too hard on you..."
Zh: “是啊，你也是。外面的世道很艰难……”他摇摇头。“当警察可不容易。我们对你太苛刻了……”

En: ...both of you, he turns to the lieutenant. "We shouldn't have fucked with you like we did. You got between us and a lot of bullets in that fight. Martinaise owes you one."
Zh: “……对你们俩都是，”他转向警督。“我们不应该那样欺骗那你们的。在那场战争之间，你站在我们中间，挡了不少子弹。马丁内斯欠你一个人情。”

En: That's kind of you to say. The lieutenant closes his notes. "Take care of your friend, okay?"
Zh: “你能这么说真是太好了，”警督合上笔记本。“照顾好你的朋友，好吗？”

En: I will. You take care of yours, he nods to you.
Zh: “我会的。你们也要照顾好你们的朋友，”他朝你点点头。

En: We shouldn't have fucked with you like we did. You got between us and a lot of bullets in that fight -- Martinaise owes you one.
Zh: “我们不应该那样欺骗那你们的。在那场战争之间，你站在我们中间，挡了不少子弹。马丁内斯欠你一个人情。”

En: Hear that, detective Pig? You're a fuckin' hero now.
Zh: “听见了吗，猪头警探？现在你他妈是个英雄了。”

En: I wouldn't go that far -- but you have a home here if you need it. And two busted up Union men who are grateful to you. So take care, okay?
Zh: “我倒是不会说得那么夸张——但是如果你需要的话，这里有你一个家。连带两个向你表示感激的工会成员。所以要照顾好自己，好吗？”

En: He really means it.
Zh: 他是认真的。

En: A sharp pain shoots up your side and into your stomach. You must not look too good. Luckily it passes...
Zh: 一阵尖锐的痛楚窜上你的一侧，直深入你的胃部。你肯定看起来不太好。幸运的是，它过去了……

En: He's not gonna get over it.
Zh: 他挺不过来了。

En: Yes. People have called you that before. You didn't care.
Zh: 是的。以前的确有人这么叫过你，但你不在乎。

En: Oh, it's you... Didn't think we'd see you walking any time soon. He looks to the man behind him. "Alain, look, it's the rat cop."
Zh: “哦，是你啊……想不到你这么快就能行走自如了。”他看向自己身后的男人。“阿兰你看，叛徒警察来了。”

En: Uh... what? He looks up, his eyes full of confusion, as if he'd just woken from a deep sleep.
Zh: “呃……什么？”他抬起头，眼睛里充满了困惑，好像刚从睡梦中醒来。

En: Oh, it's you... Didn't think we'd see you walking any time soon. He looks to the man sitting behind him. "Alain, look, it's the cowardly cop."
Zh: “哦，是你啊……想不到你这么快就能行走自如了。”他看向坐在自己身后的男人。“阿兰你看，胆小鬼警察来了。”

En: Oh, it's you... Didn't think we'd see you walking any time soon. He looks to the man sitting behind him. "Alain, look, it's the killer cop."
Zh: “哦，是你啊……想不到你这么快就能行走自如了。”他看向坐在自己身后的男人。“阿兰你看，杀手警察来了。”

En: Our condolences for your losses, he says with a little nod, but the remaining Hardie boys don't seem to register his words.
Zh: “我们对你们的损失表示哀悼，”他说话时微微点头，但其余的哈迪男孩似乎没有注意到他说了什么。

En: He's very, *very* drunk.
Zh: 他喝得非常，*非常*醉了。

En: What's gonna happen to the Hardie boys now that Titus is dead?
Zh: “提图斯走后，哈迪兄弟们要怎么办呢？”

En: Any idea what happened to Klaasje?
Zh: “你知道卡拉洁怎么样了吗？”

En: Could you guys help me get in to the harbour?
Zh: “你们能帮我进入港口吗？”

En: Take care guys. [Leave.]
Zh: “照顾好自己，伙计们。”[离开。]

En: Is he okay? (Point to Alain.)
Zh: “他还好吗？”（指向阿兰。）

En: Does he look like he's okay?
Zh: “他看着像好的样子吗？”

En: He does not. His unshaven face is almost grey, and he reeks of piss, sweat, and booze.
Zh: 并不像。他胡子拉碴的脸几乎灰败了，浑身散发着小便、汗水和酒精的味道。

En: How badly was he hurt?
Zh: “他伤得有多严重？”

En: Yeah, I suppose that was a dumb question.
Zh: “好吧，这的确是个蠢问题。”

En: No shit, Mullen.
Zh: “没什么，马伦。”

En: While still alive, he has abandoned his own body to decomposition.
Zh: 即便他还活着，却已把自己活成了一具行尸走肉。

En: He wasn't, that's the thing. He sighs heavily. "Titus, Theo, Dennis, Angie, Lizzie... They're all gone, but he got away without a scratch."
Zh: “他没有，就是这样。”他重重地叹了口气。“提图斯、特奥、丹尼斯、安吉、莉兹……他们都死了，他却毫发无伤地逃走了。”

En: He wasn't, that's the thing. He sighs heavily. "Titus, Theo, Angie, Elizabeth... They're all gone, but he got away without a scratch."
Zh: “他没有，就是这样。”他重重地叹了口气。“提图斯、特奥、安吉、伊丽莎白……他们都死了，他却毫发无伤地逃走了。”

En: He wasn't, that's the thing. He sighs heavily. "Titus, Theo, Dennis, Angie... they're all gone, but he got away without a scratch."
Zh: “他没有，就是这样。”他重重地叹了口气。“提图斯、特奥、丹尼斯、安吉……他们都死了，他却毫发无伤地逃走了。”

En: I just... He seems barely able to keep his head up. "...there's nothing left. NOTHING!"
Zh: “我只是……”他似乎连头都抬不起来了。“……什么都没有了，没有了！”

En: He has lost the will to live.
Zh: 他失去了活下去的意愿。

En: It's survivor's guilt. He feels like he should have died with the others. He may kill himself if he doesn't find a new purpose -- or else go on in this way, not dead, but not really living either.
Zh: 这是幸存者的负罪感。他觉得自己应该跟其他人一同死去。如果找不到新目标，他很可能会选择自杀——或是一直像这样下去，虽然没有死，但也不算真正的活着。

En: You'll get over it.
Zh: “你会挺过去的。”

En: This is terrible. I don't think I could go on living after this...
Zh: “这可真是太糟了。我觉得自己应该是没办法独活的……”

En: This is a broken man, he whispers to you. "He probably feels that he should have been able to protect the others somehow. And now he feels guilty for not dying with them, or instead of them."
Zh: “他已经崩溃了，”他低声对你说。“他大概认为自己应该保护好别人。现在他因为没有同他们一起战死，或是没有代替他们死去而心怀愧疚。”

En: Shit is tough but you need to get off your ass and finish this.
Zh: “这种破事真的很难，但你还是要振作起来，了结这一切。”

En: The eyes looking back at you from the narrow face are those of a dead man.
Zh: 那个窄脸男人往回看你的眼神就好像死人一样。

En: The eyes looking back to you from the narrow face momentarily light up and his lips move without sound.
Zh: 那个窄脸男人往回看你的眼神亮了那么一瞬间，他的嘴唇无声地动了动。

En: He said 'thank you'.
Zh: 他说‘谢谢你’。

En: That shit ain't helping anyone, asshole. The fuck do you want with us anyway?
Zh: “那玩意帮不了任何人，混蛋。你他妈的到底想让我们干什么？”

En: (Whisper.) "What are you suggesting, Kim?"
Zh: （低语。）“你是在暗示什么吗，金？”

En: I'm not suggesting much. All I'm saying is -- he lost everything.
Zh: “我没有在暗示什么。我想说的只是——他失去了一切。”

En: The dull eyes looking back to you from the narrow face momentarily light up and he mumbles: "What...?"
Zh: 那个窄脸男人往回看你的眼神亮了那么一瞬间，他喃喃着：“什么……？”

En: The little guy who ran -- Shanky -- you're just going to leave it like that?
Zh: “那个逃跑的小个子——尚克——你就打算放着不管了？”

En: Krenel, Wild Pines... All the people responsible for the deaths of your friends. You're just going to leave it like that?
Zh: “科奈尔，野松公司……这些人都要为你死去的朋友负责。你打算就这样放着不管了？”

En: Nothing, just thinking out loud here.
Zh: “没什么，只是在自言自语罢了。”

En: The flicker of light in his eyes goes out and he collapses back on the table.
Zh: 他眼中闪烁的光芒熄灭了，随即瘫倒在桌子上。

En: Sons of bitches... He mumbles something in Mesque. When he sinks down on the table again, a malevolent smile spreading across his face.
Zh: “一群混蛋……”他用梅斯克语嘟囔了几句。当他再次瘫倒在桌子上时，一抹恶意的微笑掠过他的脸庞。

En: Was this really the best idea? The lieutenant pauses to think for a moment. "I guess you may have prolonged his existence a bit. If he can still remember this when he sobers up."
Zh: “这是最好的主意吗？”警督停下来思索了一会儿。“我想你也算延长了他的生命，如果他酒醒之后还能记得的话。”

En: If you think this will keep him from offin' himself, I'll make sure to remind him... so far I've just been pouring whiskey in him to keep him sedated. He nods.
Zh: “如果你以为这样能阻止他自杀，我保证会提醒他的……目前为止我只是一直在给他灌威士忌，让他能够安静。”他点点头。

En: His eyes, which remain fixed on yours, become somewhat clearer, and he smiles a malevolent drunken smile. "Dennis is a dead man... just doesn't know it yet..."
Zh: 他那双一直紧盯着你不放的眼睛，变得有几分清澈了，他露出了一个恶意与醉意满满的微笑。“丹尼斯已经是个死人了……只是还不知道而已……”

En: Couldn't help you even if I wanted to. He shakes his head. "The whole harbour is in lockdown and Evrart sent word to sit tight and wait for instructions."
Zh: “即使我想帮你，也无能为力。”他摇摇头。“整个港口都被封锁了，艾弗拉特让我们在这里等待指示。”

En: T... Ti... Tit... The spit flying out of his mouth is accompanied by a painful grimace. "Tit... Titus's fuckin' dead..."
Zh: “提……提……提图……”他的口水伴随着痛苦的表情飞了出来。“提图……提图斯他妈的死了……”

En: What's gonna happen to the Hardie boys? Look around you, man. This shit is done. There are no more Hardie boys!
Zh: “哈迪兄弟会怎么样？自己看吧，兄弟。一切都结束了，不会再有哈迪兄弟了！”

En: Oh yeah... that makes sense. Never mind.
Zh: “是吗……有道理，算了。”

En: You two are just going to go back to hauling containers in the harbour?
Zh: “你们俩准备回去港口拖集装箱吗？”

En: Look, what do you want from us, man? Things are pretty tough right now.
Zh: “听着，你到底想从我们这里得到什么？现在真的很困难。”

En: No, man. I don't know... I guess the Union will still be policing the neighbourhood, but... He looks at his dazed companion. "Who's gonna do it now?"
Zh: “不，老兄。我不知道……我猜工会还是会维持周边的治安，但是……”他看着那位晕乎乎的同伴。“现在交给谁来做呢？”

En: I am.
You, Eugene.
Zh: “你，尤金。”

En: He will. (Point to Alain.) "You just need to sober him up, that's all."
Zh: “他。”（指向阿兰。）“只要让他清醒过来，那就够了。”

En: You should just disband. Let other, better people take over.
Zh: “你们应该原地解散，让其他更优秀的人来接手吧。”

En: Anyone ever tell you you're a total dickhead? His voice is quiet and resigned. "Well... you are, man. Just fuck off and leave us be."
Zh: “有人说过你就是个彻头彻尾的白痴吗？”他的声音毫无波澜。“那好……你就是。滚吧，别来烦我们了。”

En: Alain? He won't be leading anything. He turns to his friend. "I mean, look at him -- the man's broken through and through."
Zh: “阿兰？他什么都领导不了。”他面向他的朋友。“我是说，你看他——这人完全垮了。”

En: That wouldn't be funny even if we weren't surrounded by the ghosts of our fallen friends.
Zh: “即便我们身边没有围绕着逝去朋友的鬼魂，这也一点也不好笑。”

En: Me? No, man... I'm not a leader. He smiles suddenly. "You know Titus only recruited me 'cause I play the guitar? Said the team could use the morale boost."
Zh: “我？算了吧……我不是一个领导者。”他突然笑了。“你知道提图斯招募我过来仅仅是因为我会弹吉他吗？说需要鼓舞大家的士气。”

En: Didn't Titus have a brother? Tibbs?
Zh: “提图斯没有任何兄弟吗？提布斯？”

En: Tiberius? He doesn't wanna get mixed up in our shit. Titus has been buttering him for years on the count of all this being *fun as fuck*.
Zh: “提比乌斯？他可不愿意跟我们扯上关系。提图斯因为*这档事*巴结他好多年了。”

En: But he's got his window fitting business, a wife with two kids and the third one on the way... No way he's gonna leave all that.
Zh: “但他已经有了自己的橱窗装修生意，妻子和两个孩子，第三个小孩也即将出生……他是不可能抛下那些的。”

En: You can do it. *Eugenie Boys* has quite the ring, doesn't it?
Zh: “你可以的。*尤金兄弟*听起来挺不错的，不是吗？”

En: Sounds like you gotta go with Mr. Outside Hire, then.
Zh: “那听起来你要跟外部招聘先生一起走了。”

En: I ain't gonna stick around for that. He shakes his head resolutely. "I only stayed because of Titus. *There* was a man born to lead."
Zh: “我不会为了这事留下来的。”他坚决地摇头。“我是为了提图斯才留下来的，*他*是个天生的领袖。”

En: What? Fuck no! *If* I'd stay we'd keep the name to honour Titus.
Zh: “什么？当然不行！*就算*我留下来，也会留用这个名字来纪念提图斯。”

En: You're right, Eugene Hardie doesn't sound bad either.
Zh: “你说得对，尤金·哈迪听起来也不错。”

En: That's a noble thought. You're going to do alright. No one is born to lead.
Zh: “非常高尚的想法。你会做得很好的。没有人天生就是领导者。”

En: Titus was... His eyes meet yours. "But thanks for the vote of confidence. If I don't stick around, it's all over. So I guess sorta I have to now."
Zh: “提图斯就是……”他对上了你的眼睛。“但是，感谢你投上了信任的一票。如果我不留下来，那一切都结束了。所以现在，我猜我必须这么做。”

En: Man... you have no idea how this works do you? He looks at the glass of milk on the table. "But yes... If I don't stick around then it's all over. So I guess I sorta have to now."
Zh: “老兄……你根本不明白事情是怎么回事，对吗？”他看着桌子上的那杯牛奶。“但是，没错……如果我不留下来，那一切都结束了。所以现在，我猜我必须这么做。”

En: Hardie boys without a Hardie? That's just stupid.
Zh: “哈迪兄弟里没有一个哈迪？真是够傻的。”

En: Cop, you just don't understand how things work. He exhales sharply in frustration, but then quickly collects himself.
Zh: “条子，你根本不明白事情是怎么回事。”他沮丧地喘了口气，但又很快恢复了镇静。

En: Nah. Garte said she took off some time before the firefight, that's all. Trying to remember if there was anything... oh, yeah, he said she seemed to be in real hurry.
Zh: “不。加尔特说她在交火之前就离开了，就这些。我想想还有没有别的……哦，有了，他说她好像真的很匆忙。”

En: Oh no... He winces. "You fly solo on this one."
Zh: “哦不……”他的面部抽搐着。“这次你得自己上了。”

En: Seeing you approach, the bruised man clenches his fists.
Zh: 看到你走近，那个受伤的男人攥紧了拳头。

En: Seeing you approach, the bruised man raises his beer can in welcome.
Zh: 看到你走近，那个受伤的男人举起了啤酒表示欢迎。

En: Those guys have been going around town, he continues in a whisper, "Crying about their new asshole. Cuno can't handle this emotional shit when he's off the lightning."
Zh: “那些家伙一直在城里转悠，”他继续小声说道。“因为他们的新混蛋哭泣。坤诺戒掉闪电丸之后就无法处理这种情感问题了。”

En: He probably means they've been mourning their lost friends.
Zh: 他可能是在说他们一直在哀悼逝去的朋友。

En: The man stares at you with spite in his eyes.
Zh: 那人用满怀恶意的目光盯着你。

En: The man stares at you through the haze in his eyes.
Zh: 那人醉意朦胧地盯着你。

En: What a fucking pussy... Cuno whispers.
Zh: “真他妈是个胆小鬼……”坤诺小声说到。

En: (Sings.) "Our bar, our bar, Hardie bar!"
Zh: （歌唱。）“去酒吧，我们的酒吧，哈迪酒吧！”

En: So which one was it, man?
Zh: “究竟是哪个，老兄？”

En: Didn't Monica have giant titties?
Zh: “莫妮卡的咪咪不是超级大吗？”

En: I don't do karaoke, man. I'm too good.
Zh: “我不唱卡拉OK的，老兄。我的水平太高了。”

En: We need some *real* music in here.
Zh: “我们需要一些*真正*的音乐。”

En: Yeah, what's up with that?
Zh: “是啊，那是怎么回事？”

En: Yeah, like that changes anything.
Zh: “是啊，好像能有什么改变似的。”

En: Wish I had my guitar...
Zh: “真希望我的吉他还在身边……”

En: Goddamn Shanky... never liked that rat-faced fuck.
Zh: “该死的尚可……我从来都不是很喜欢那个老鼠脸的混账。”

En: One day I'll write a song about it.
Zh: “总有一天，我会为此写一首歌。”

En: Strange... I even miss Angie's loud stomach...
Zh: “奇怪……我甚至有点想念安吉那个咕噜的很大声的肚子了……”

En: Think Mañana could make a good Hardie boy?
Zh: “你觉得马列拉会成为一个优秀的哈迪兄弟吗？”

En: Shit like that really makes you think.
Zh: “遇到那种破事真的会让你开始好好思考。”

En: Hardie boys are local legends now.
Zh: “现在哈迪兄弟成为本地的传说了。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / EUGENE barks post tribunal

Zh: “现在哈迪兄弟成为本地的传说了。”
