En: Word on the street is: 'Outgunned, the Hardie boys kill twenty Krenel mercs...'
Zh: “坊间传言是：‘尽管火力被压制，哈迪兄弟杀了二十个科奈尔雇佣兵……'”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / EUGENE barks post tribunal
En: Never thought I'd miss the smell of Angie's rotting feet...
Zh: “从未想过有一天我竟然会想念安吉的臭脚味……”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / EUGENE barks post tribunal
En: What about Mañana? Think he'd make a good Hardie boy?
Zh: “马列拉呢？他能成为一个优秀的哈迪兄弟吗？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / EUGENE barks post tribunal
En: Shit like that can turn a man into pacifist... Not me, but someone...
Zh: “那种事足以把一个人变成和平主义者……不是我，而是别人……”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / EUGENE barks post tribunal
En: I wrote a song about the fight, anybody wanna hear it, or...? Too soon, I get it.
Zh: “我写了一首关于这场斗争的歌，有人想听听吗，还是……？太快了，我明白。”

En: Hold up, piggo. There's something you gotta understand about the Cuno first. He drags his sleeve across his nose. "Cuno's gotta protect the Cuno name."
Zh: “慢着猪头，坤诺有件事得先让你知道。”他用袖子擦了擦鼻子。“坤诺得保护坤诺的名声才行。”

En: The gimps out there ain't gonna think we a thing. Cuno will follow you, but don't expect anything *extra* okay, pig? Cuno's silent, like a fucking vegetable.
Zh: “不能被外面的人知道我们是一伙的。坤诺会跟着你，但别指望*更多*了，知道吗猪头？坤诺什么都不会说的，就像植物人那样。”

En: Listen up, pig! Cuno's gonna scram now. He rubs his hands together.
Zh: “听好了猪头！坤诺现在得走了。”他搓着手。

En: See you on the seafort island -- try not to get shot again or drunk or shit. Cuno's counting on you.
Zh: “海堡岛上见——尽量别再挨枪子儿或是喝醉了，坤诺还指望着你呢。”

En: And don't forget about that bitch's window. Scope that shit. Cop Cuno's telling you, that's the break in the case.
Zh: “而且别忘了那个贱人的窗户，盯着点儿。坤诺警官告诉你，那是破案的关键。”

En: Cuno will rejoin your party later.
Zh: 坤诺稍后会重新加入你的队伍。

En: Cuno's gonna scram now. He rubs his hands together. "Can't be seen with pigs. Can't shit on Cuno's good name."
Zh: “坤诺得走了。”他搓着手。“不能被人看到坤诺和猪头们在一起，不能破坏坤诺的好名声。”

En: So yeah -- see you on the seafort island. Try not to get shot or drunk again. Cuno's counting on your ass.
Zh: “那就这样吧——海堡岛上见。尽量别再挨枪子儿或是喝醉了，坤诺还指望着你呢。”

En: As you approach the door leading out of the hostel, the bespectacled man in an orange bomber jacket turns to you:
Zh: 正当你靠近通向旅馆外的大门时，那个戴着眼镜、穿着橙色短夹克的男人转向你：

En: Excuse me... I think you may be the person I've been waiting for. He narrows his eyes and extends his hand in greeting.
Zh: “打扰了……我想你可能就是我在等的人。”他眯起眼睛，伸出手来打招呼。

En: As you approach the door leading out of the hostel, the bespectacled man in a black bomber jacket turns to you:
As you approach the door leading out of the hostel, the bespectacled man in an orange bomber jacket turns to you:
*This* is what I studied law for?
Zh: “我学法律就是为了*这个*？”

En: Titus, you were told to keep a low profile.
Zh: “提图斯，告诉过你要低调一点。”

En: Can't wait to change out of these rags.
Zh: “等不及要换掉这身破衣服了。”

En: Babysitting imbeciles... what the heck, Liz?
Zh: “照顾这群蠢货……搞什么鬼，莉兹？”

En: Evrart isn't paying me enough for this.
Zh: “艾弗拉特付的钱不够。”

En: Unbelievable...
Zh: “难以置信……”

En: The IIR or interisolary reál is the global reserve currency. Whatever part of the world you're in right now, it's safe to assume he means you owe him some money.
Zh: IIR或者跨岛雷亚尔是全球储备的货币。不论你现在身处世界的那个角落，可以肯定他的意思是你欠他钱。

En: I don't owe you shit.
Zh: “我不欠你什么。”

En: What's *real*?
Zh: “*瑞亚尔*是什么？”

En: Oh, excuse me. You owe me 130 *reál*. He pronounces the 'r' with a mock aristocratic accent.
Zh: “哦，不好意思。你还欠我130*雷亚尔*。”他模仿着贵族的腔调发出了“雷”的音。

En: By *real* do you mean some form of street cred?
Zh: “你口中的*瑞亚尔*是指某种街头信誉点吗？”

En: Oh, I understand, you mean I owe you money.
Zh: “哦，我明白了，你的意思是我欠你钱。”

En: *Reál* sounds like some fancy skin care product... but why would I owe you one hundred and thirty of them?
Zh: “*雷亚尔*听上去像是某种高档护肤产品……但我怎么会欠你一百三十个呢？”

En: You're under arrest.
Zh: “你被逮捕了。”

En: Did I just hear my tie speak to me *twice*?
Zh: “我刚才是听到我的领带*两次*对我说话了吗？”

En: No one is saying that. No one is saying the tie can talk -- that would be ludicrous. It's just that... you should arrest him. It would be *wiser*. He wants to take your money.
Zh: 没人这么说，没人说过领带会说话——不然也太可笑了。只是……你应该逮捕他，这样*比较好*，他想拿你的钱。

En: I'm not comfortable with this. (Try to somehow tuck the tie away.)
Zh: 我不喜欢这样。（设法把领带塞到别处。）

En: Great advice, I'm considering it. (Give the tie an affirmative tug.)
Zh: 好主意，我会考虑的。（用力扯一下领带。）

En: No way, I have duties. I'm a cop, I'm not talking to a tie. (Take the tie off.)
Zh: 不可能，我有职责在身。我是个警察，是不会跟领带对话的。（取下领带。）

En: A man in his late twenties stands behind the counter, inspecting a stuffed seabird. As you approach, he gives you a sideways glance, then looks down again.
Zh: 一名年近三十的男子站在柜台后面，打量着一只海鸟标本。当你靠近时，他瞥了你一眼，很快又收回了视线。

En: That was disdain in his eyes. Even now he's purposefully ignoring you.
Zh: 他的眼里满是轻蔑，即便是现在他也在故意无视你。

En: That was disdain in his eyes. Even now he is purposefully ignoring you. Looks like he's not a fan.
Zh: 他的眼里满是轻蔑，即便是现在他也在故意无视你。看来他不怎么喜欢你。

En: Look at the stuffed bird.
Zh: 看着海鸟标本。

En: Are you the bartender?
Zh: “你是这里的酒保吗？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Something tells me you don't like me.
Zh: “看来你不太喜欢我。”

En: What's wrong? Don't you like The Officer?
Zh: “怎么？你不喜欢警官吗？”

En: I sense you're not a fan of mine.
Zh: “我觉得你不怎么喜欢我。”

En: Oh no, he says without looking up. "You're a hero. A real hero cop."
Zh: “哦，不是的，”他头也不抬地说。“你是个英雄，真正的英雄警察。”

En: So not only am I a cop, but I'm also a hero?
Zh: “所以我不仅是个警察，还是个英雄？”

En: Could the massive property damage upstairs have anything to do with this?
Zh: 楼上的巨大财产损失与此有关吗？

En: So it's true, I really *am* a cop?
Zh: “所以这都是真的，*我*真是一个警察？”

En: Wait, did you just say I was a *cop*?
Zh: “等等，你刚才是不是说我是个*警察*？”

En: Yes, you are! A real decorated hero.
Zh: “是啊，没错！一个真正的英雄。”

En: What did I do?
Zh: “我都做了什么？”

En: You're being sarcastic.
Zh: “你在讽刺我。”

En: What did you *not* do? First you took the body down, then you solved the murder, then you *didn't* trash my hostel room. Maybe you even negotiated the strike...
Zh: “应该是你*没有*做什么？首先是把尸体放下来，解决谋杀案，之后也*没有*破坏我的旅社。甚至还有参加罢工谈判……”

En: Ah, the dead body. I didn't actually get it down, did I?
Zh: “啊，那具尸体。我其实并没有把他放下来，对吧？”

En: I'm guessing I didn't do any of those things?
Zh: “我猜这些事我都没做过吧？”

En: Oh my god, I did all those things?
Zh: “我的天，我做了那么多事？”

En: I do not appreciate your tone. This is no way to talk to an officer.
Zh: “我不喜欢你说话的语气，这不是跟警察说话的方式。”

En: No -- you see -- actually you didn't. You haven't done anything even remotely useful since you got here.
Zh: “不是——明白吗——其实你没有。你到这儿来以后，一件有用的事情都没干过。”

En: You're right, you didn't. And it's only taken you *three days* not to.
Zh: “答对了，你没有。不过是整整*三天*一事无成而已。”

En: Oh, it's not?! He raises his shoulders like he's about to say something more, but then gets a hold of himself.
Zh: “哦，不是吗？！”他耸了耸肩，好像还想说些什么，但很快又克制住了。

En: What have I been doing all that time? Have you seen me around?
Zh: “这段时间以来我都在干什么？你看到过我吗？”

En: No, I haven't *seen you around*. I'm not always here. He looks down again and keeps plucking at the bird.
Zh: “不，我没有*看到过你*。我也没有一直待在这里。”他又低下了头，继续修整那只鸟的羽毛。

En: He has no respect for you personally, but this man sees himself as a law-abiding citizen -- and you a representative of that law. He tries to avoid outright conflict.
Zh: 他对你没有丝毫尊重的意思，但这个男人认为自己是个守法公民——而你是法律的代表。他想试图避免直接冲突。

En: What have I been doing then? Have you seen me around?
Zh: “那我一直在干什么呢？你看到过我吗？”

En: No, I'm not the *bartender*. I'm the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “不，我不是*酒保*。我是这里的经理。”

En: What kind of question is that?
Zh: “这算什么问题？”

En: Am I? Or did you ride in, take the body down, solve the murder, and *not* trash my hostel room?
Zh: “我有吗？还是你确实来到这里，放下了尸体，解决了谋杀案，*没有*破坏我的旅社？”

En: The man plucks at the seabird, frowning.
Zh: 那人皱着眉头，拨弄着海鸟。

En: I didn't know I was a cop. Are you sure?
Zh: “我都不知道自己是个警察，你确定吗？”

En: That makes sense. Ever since I got up, I've felt something in me... (Look at your hands and feel it flowing through your veins.) "Now I know it's *The Law*."
Zh: “那就说得通了。从我起床到现在，就一直感觉到我身体里有某种东西……”（看着自己的手，感觉它流过你的血管。）“现在我明白那是*法律*了。”

En: I can't be a police officer... This is insane.
Zh: “我不可能是警察的……这也太荒谬了。”

En: Nothing. I just wanted to hear you say it.
Zh: “没什么，我只是想听你说出来。”

En: I don't know. Am I? He looks at you, taking stock of your cop-ness and finding it lacking.
Zh: “不知道，我有吗？”他打量着你，评估着你身上的警察气息，发现它严重不足。

En: Not far from here a 34-year-old man called Jean Vicquemare reluctantly picks his facial hair while a dark-haired woman in an officer's suit waits for an answer. A typical police station morning hums in the background: telephones ringing, the whirr of a roll'o'dex...
Zh: 离这里不远处，一名叫做让·维克玛的三十四岁男子不情愿地修整着自己脸上的毛发，而一位西装革履的黑发女士正等待着他的答案。一个典型的警局清晨在背后嗡嗡作响：电话铃声，翻阅联络簿的声音……

En: Have you seen the way he dresses? The man asks. "It's like he's not even a cop anymore. Shows up in here looking like a clown, reeking of alcohol. Screams at everyone... I'm done with that guy."
Zh: “你看到他穿的衣服了吗？”男人问。“就好像他甚至都不再是个警察了。像个小丑一样出现，浑身散发着酒气。冲每个人大喊大叫……我受够那家伙了。”

En: Let him sort it out for himself.
Zh: “让他自己去解决吧。”

En: Are you serious right now?
Zh: “你现在是认真的吗？”

En: It is, isn't it? He looks at you, shaking his head in disbelief.
Zh: “没错，不是吗？”他看着你，难以置信地摇着头。

En: The responsibility has taken its toll on my body, but I will take up the mantle once more. This is who I am.
Zh: “责任已经让我的身体付出了代价，但我仍然会再次承担起来。我就是这样的人。”

En: Of course -- if by The Law" you mean *blood alcohol level*."
Zh: “当然——如果你口中的*法律*指的是*血液酒精含量*的话。”

En: Of course I'm not serious.
Zh: “我当然不是认真的啦。”

En: I really don't know what to say to that. He looks down at the seabird.
Zh: “我真的无言以对了。”他低着头看海鸟。

En: What's the difference?
Zh: “有什么区别吗？”

En: You look like a bartender.
Zh: “你看着很像酒保。”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: That period of my life is over. Not everyone who stands behind a counter is a bartender, okay? I'm the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “我生命中的那个阶段已经结束了。不是所有站在柜台后面的人都是酒保，明白吗？我是餐厅的经理。”

En: I have three cafeterias to manage. *Three*. Sylvie tends the bar here, not me, I'm only standing in.
Zh: “我有三家餐厅要管理。*三家*。塞尔维才是这里的酒保，不是我，我只是站在这里而已。”

En: Where did this Sylvie go?
Zh: “那这个塞尔维去哪儿了？”

En: Mhm.
She just, you know... He shrugs.
Zh: “她就，你懂的……”他耸耸肩。

En: He rolls his eyes.
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。

En: His eyes dart from left to right. This man isn't lying, but he *is* hiding something.
Zh: 他的眼神左右游移着。这个男人并非在说谎，而是*有*所隐瞒。

En: So *now* you're a cop? Forget it.
Zh: “*现在*你又是警察了？得了吧。”

En: The man with the unimpressive beard notices you approaching. He drops the ledger he was holding and turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: 一个留着稀疏胡子的男人注意到了你的到来。他放下手中的账本，转向警督。

En: The cafeteria manager sees you approaching. He drops the ledger he was holding and turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: 餐厅经理看到你走过来。他放下手中的账本，转向警督。

En: A man in his late twenties with a thin, unimpressive beard notices you approaching. He drops the ledger he was holding and turns to the lieutenant.
Zh: 一个年近三十，留着稀疏胡子的男人注意到了你的到来。他放下手中的账本，转向警督。

En: Mr... Garte, right? The lieutenant glances into his little notebook. "You run this place?"
Zh: “是……加尔特先生吗？”金瞄了一眼他的小笔记本。“你是这里的店主吗？”

En: I am Kim Kitsuragi from Precinct 57. This is an inter-district investigation, so joining me from Precinct 41... He looks to you, realizing he still doesn't know your name.
Zh: “我是57分局的金·曷城。这次是跨区域办案，所以跟我一起来的是41分局的……”他看着你，发现他还不知道你的名字。

En: Yes, he responds tersely.
Zh: “是的，”他简短地答道。

En: The harbinger of ruin.
Zh: “毁灭的先驱。”

En: As I said, Detective Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: “就像我说的，拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托警探。”

En: It's probably Harry.
Zh: “大概是哈里吧。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Nothing. I just wanted to see what happens if I say that. Now I do.
Zh: “没什么。只是想看看如果我这么说了会有什么反应，所以就这么说了。”

En: The gloaming. I *am* it.
Zh: “我的名字*叫*黄昏。”

En: Never mind.
Zh: “别放在心上。”

En: I have *no idea* what that means.
Zh: “我*完全*不知道那是什么意思。”

En: I have been to the precipice, Cafeteria Manager. I have seen the end.
Zh: “我站在了悬崖边缘，餐厅经理，我看到了终结。”

En: I have been to the precipice. I have seen the end.
Zh: “我站在了悬崖边缘，我看到了终结。”

En: The human experiment will soon be over, *Cafeteria Manager*. It doesn't matter if you understand.
Zh: “人类实验很快就会结束了，*餐厅经理*。你明白与否无足轻重。”

En: This duskland will be gone soon. It makes no difference if you understand or not.
Zh: “这片幽暗之地很快就会消失，无论你明白与否。”

En: What is this, a joke to you? Is *this* what you get when you call the police now? *This* guy? He points at you. "We've been waiting for a week here!"
Zh: “这算什么，你觉得这很好笑吗？现在大家报警的结果就是*这样*？*这种人*？”他指着你。“我们都在这等了一星期了！”

En: Never mind.
Zh: “别放在心上。”

En: Sir, I understand your concern, but we're here to do a job, and for us to do it, I need you to stay calm.
Zh: “先生，我能理解你的担心，但我们就是来履行职责查案的。因此，我需要你保持冷静。”

En: Never mind? *Gloaming*? What is this? Is *this* what you get when you call the police now? He points at you. "*This* guy? Lieutenant, we've been waiting for a week here!"
Zh: “别放在心上？*黄昏*？什么东西？现在大家报警的结果就是*这样*？”他指着你。“*这种人*？警督，我们已经在这等了一星期了！”

En: What is this, a joke to you? Is *this* what you get when you call the police now? *This* guy? He turns to the lieutenant. "We've been waiting for a week here!"
Zh: “这算什么，你觉得这很好笑吗？现在大家报警的结果就是*这样*？*这种人*？”他转向警督。“我们都在这等了一星期了！”

En: Could he be irritated with you? He avoids eye contact altogether. Like you're not even there.
Zh: 他是被你激怒了吗？他完全避开了眼神交流，仿佛你根本不存在一样。

En: Right... Now, I know it took us a while to arrive at the scene. It also took you a while to call us and report the dead body -- it *was* you who placed the call, yes?
Zh: “好吧……我们确实花了一段时间才到现场，但你也花了一段时间才打电话通报尸体——*是*你报的警，对吗？”

En: I'm currently in-between names.
Zh: “目前我还没想好我的名字。”

En: Fantastic.
Zh: “太棒了。”

En: It's like when you've left your band, but you haven't decided on a solo artist name yet.
Zh: “这就像你离开了乐队，但还没想好一个单飞艺术家的名字。”

En: Kim is about to say something. Let him.
Zh: 金正打算说些什么，让他说。

En: That's wonderful! Really. I think so. But there's a dead body out back. No one wants to work in these conditions! It's been a week!
Zh: “那可真是太棒了！真的，我真心的。但还有一具尸体在后面，没人愿意在这种情形下工作！已经挂了一个星期了！”

En: What's that supposed to mean -- probably?
Zh: “那是什么意思——大概？”

En: No, I only just got here. It was probably Sylvie who called you. She usually works the bar here. I'm only temporarily taking over her duties.
Zh: “不，我也是刚到。大概是塞尔维打的电话吧，她通常在这里的酒吧工作。我只是临时来接替她的。”

En: Do you have her number?
Zh: “你有她的号码吗？”

En: As a matter of fact, I do. He looks behind a pile of coasters, finds a slip of paper, and hands it to the lieutenant.
Zh: “我正好有。”他在一堆杯垫后面翻了翻，找到一张纸条递给了警督。

En: You said you just got here -- from where? Are you a local?
Zh: “你说自己刚到——从哪里过来的？你是本地人吗？”

En: Where exactly is the body?
Zh: “尸体到底在哪儿？”

En: Who killed him?
Zh: “是谁杀了他？”

En: What is your problem with me?
Zh: “你对我有什么意见吗？”

En: She just... what?
Zh: “她就……什么？”

En: Okay, then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: Mhm.
He looks down at the stuffed bird, quietly shaking his head.
Zh: 他低头看着那只海鸟标本，沉默地摇头。

En: What? Of Martinaise -- no. I live in Jamrock. I only *sometimes* come here to keep an eye on the place. This is just one of the many, many cafeterias I manage.
Zh: “什么？你问我是不是马丁内斯人？不，我住加姆洛克，我只是*时不时*来这里看看。这里只是我管理的众多餐厅之一。”

En: But you still know your way around, yes? In case we need directions.
Zh: “但这一片你还是很熟悉的，是吗？以防我们需要指路。”

En: Yes, I know where *some* things are. But, as I said, I don't live here. I just used to work here. And I'm not going to start working here again, if that's what you think.
Zh: “没错，我知道*一些*地方。但就像我说的，我不住这儿。我以前在这里工作过，但之后都不打算再来了。如果你想暗示我什么的话，不必了。”

En: I didn't imply that. He turns to you. "Detective?"
Zh: “我没暗示什么。”他转向你。“警探你呢？”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: (Play the fool.) "Who, me?"
Zh: （装傻。）“谁？我吗？”

En: Who, me?
Zh: “谁？我吗？”

En: Yes?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: Yes, Detective Costeau. He says the name with a purposefully straight face. "I have everything. You?"
Zh: “是的，库斯托警探。”他刻意板着脸说出了你的名字。“我没什么想问的了。你呢？”

En: Mhm. He checks his little notebook. "I have everything. You?"
Zh: “嗯。”他翻看着自己的小笔记本。“我没什么想问的了。你呢？”

En: I have everything. You? He looks in his little notebook.
Zh: 他翻看着自己的小笔记本。“我没什么想问的了。你呢？”

En: It's your turn. He takes a respectful little step back.
Zh: 他礼貌地后退了一小步。“该你了。”

En: No, I'm good. (Pass on the questions.)
Zh: “不了，我不用。”（不问问题。）

En: Oh, you mean, do I have *questions*? (Ask them.)
Zh: “哦，你是说，我还有没有*问题*了？”（问问题）

En: Oh, you mean questions?
Zh: “哦，你是说问题吗？”

En: Yes, yes, he means do you have questions for me. Like a police officer would. The cafeteria manager is clearly agitated again.
Zh: “是啊，是啊，他问你还有没有别的问题想问我。像个警官一样。”餐厅经理显然又激动起来了。

En: He probably means this is where you step in and ask your questions.
Zh: 他的意思大概是现在你应该介入并提出疑问。

En: The lieutenant nods at you affirmatively, then glances at the exit. "The body was behind the building, right -- how do we get there?"
Zh: 警督朝你肯定地点头，然后看了一眼出口。“尸体在大楼后面，是吗——我们要怎么过去？”

En: Right then. Questions. I got this.
Zh: “好吧。问题，我有一个。”

En: His face expresses profound doubt in your *having this*.
Zh: 他的脸上流露出对于你的深切怀疑。

En: Why did Sylvie go away?
Zh: “塞尔维为什么离开了？”

En: You know, I actually can't think of a single thing.
Zh: “实际上，我什么都想不出来。”

En: That's all.
Zh: “就这样。”

En: No, before I asked you *where*. Now I'm asking you *why* she left.
Zh: “不，之前我问的是*哪里*。现在我问的是她*为什么*要离开。”

En: You're right.
Zh: “你说的对。”

En: Thank you, the lieutenant says. He opens his little notebook at the cover. The number is safely tucked away in a small pocket.
Zh: 警督翻开他的小笔记本扉页，号码被安全地塞在一个小口袋里。“谢谢。”他说。

En: I asked an employee out. She didn't want to come, but felt obliged to. It was a bad idea. Now what is so god damn fascinating about that for you? It's got nothing to do with the lynching.
Zh: “我约了一名员工出去。她不愿意，却又觉得不得不来。这是个坏主意，但这到底有什么地方如此吸引你？跟私刑一点关系都没有。”

En: Everything has something to do with everything.
Zh: “每件事与每件事都是息息相关的。”

En: You're right. I probably shouldn't have asked.
Zh: “你说的对，或许我不该问。”

En: That's good. But there is a timetable for this investigation, dictated to us by the stages of decomposition. And it says we need to move on.
Zh: “那很好。但这次调查是有时间要求的，由尸体腐败的阶段而定。而这意味着我们得继续了。”

En: Is that so? Mr. Feminist... He catches the rest before it leaves his mouth, probably thinking it's best not to say anything.
Zh: “这样的吗？女权先生……”还未将剩下的话说出口他便闭上了嘴，可能觉得还是不说为好。

En: Not so fast Mr. Feminist. He points to you. "You owe me 130 reál."
Zh: “别急啊，女权先生。”他指着你。“你还欠我130雷亚尔。”

En: Not so fast. He points to you. "You owe me 130 reál."
Zh: “别急啊。”他指着你。“你还欠我130雷亚尔。”

En: Slip away unnoticed.
Zh: 悄悄溜走。

En: No, you don't owe me street cred. You owe me money. You owe this establishment 130 reál. He points to the red ledger on the counter.
Zh: “不，你欠我的不是街头信誉点。你欠我钱，欠这里130雷亚尔。”他指着柜台上面红色的账本。

En: Wow, you're a genius! Yes, that's right -- money. You owe this establishment 130 reál. He points to the red ledger on the counter.
Zh: “哇哦，你可真是个天才！是的，没错——钱。你欠这里130雷亚尔。”他指着柜台上面红色的账本。

En: You owe me *money*, not a skin care product. Money. You owe this establishment 130 reál. He points to the red ledger on the counter.
Zh: “你欠我的是钱，不是什么护肤品。钱，你欠这里130雷亚尔。”他指着柜台上面红色的账本。

En: You can't arrest me, I haven't done anything.
Zh: “你不能抓我，我什么都没做。”

En: No one is saying the multi-patterned necktie you found tied to the ceiling fan can *talk*. No one. It must be merely *imagination*, but...
Zh: 没人说过你在吊扇上发现的那条混合纹领带会*说话*，没有人。这一定只是你的*想象*，但……

En: Let's bail! Time to push the EJECT button. Sounds like a *responsibility*. You don't like those.
Zh: 我们快溜吧！是时候按下脱出按钮了。听起来像是一份*责任*，你不喜欢这种东西。

En: Real mature, man! Real mature.
Zh: “真成熟，老兄！真成熟。”

En: One moment you're running like the wind, then you've suddenly turned around and are giving him the finger. Furiously, with both hands. Why?!
Zh: 上一秒你还在像风一样狂奔着，下一刻就突然转身朝他竖起了中指。那么愤怒，还用了两只手。为什么？！

En: The lady in the wheel-chair is right behind me, isn't she?
Zh: 那位坐着轮椅的女士就在我后面，是吗？

En: Fuck you, asshole!
Zh: “去死吧，混蛋！”

En: WATCH OUT!!
Why did I do this? Why did I have use *both* my hands to flip him off? Why both...
Zh: 我为什么要这么做？为什么我要用*两只*手朝他竖中指？为什么是两只……

En: Who does that CLOWN think he is? Arrest him!
Zh: 那个小丑以为自己是什么东西？逮捕他！

En: You know, I get this all the time. Some hobo comes in here, or some backpacking asshole off the boat. The next day when they can't make their fees they get *aggressive*. I just never thought I'd be in this shit with a cop. Please, just give me the money...
Zh: “知道吗，我经常听到这种话。几个流浪汉，或是几个混蛋背包客从船上下来。到第二天他们掏不出钱来的时候，就会变得*咄咄逼人*起来。不过我从未想过这会发生在警察身上。拜托了，把钱给我就好……”

En: The lieutenant watches you fiddle with your horrific tie, sweat stains forming under your armpits. He puts his hand on your shoulder.
Zh: 警督看着你摆弄那条可怕的领带，你的腋下开始出汗。他把手放在你的肩膀上。

En: What do I owe this place for?
Zh: “我欠这里多少？”

En: But what exactly *is* money?
Zh: “但究竟什么*是*钱呢？”

En: But what exactly *is* money?
Zh: “但究竟什么*是*钱呢？”

En: Somehow the knot is stuck so you can't get it off. The lieutenant watches you struggle with it, the sweat dripping from your brow. He puts his hand on your shoulder:
Zh: 不知怎的，这个结系得太死，你无法解开。警督看着你与它之间的斗争，汗水从你的额头上滴落。他把手放在你的肩膀上：

En: The lieutenant looks at you tugging on your garish tie. He puts his hand on your shoulder:
Zh: 警督看着你扯着那条花哨的领带，他把手放在你的肩膀上：

En: If you don't have the money, it's okay. None of us are in this for the wages.
Zh: “如果你实在没钱也没关系，我们都不是为了工资才干这个的。”

En: Put this man in his place.
Zh: 让这个男人摆正位置。

En: Lost your nerve before.
Zh: 你以前失去了勇气。

En: Really lost your nerve before.
Zh: 你以前真的失去了勇气。

En: Sylvie incident known.
Zh: 知晓塞尔维事件。

En: Inexplicable Feminist Agenda engaged!
Zh: 参与令人费解的女权主义议程！

En: The words leave your mouth as if walking up the stairs -- everything is going fine until you reach the end and take one step too many:
Zh: 脱口而出的这些话，就仿佛上楼梯一般——一切都很好，直至你步子迈得太大，一脚跨过了终点：

En: I fucking well can, *bitch*!
Zh: “我他妈当然可以，*贱人*！”

En: Okay, you're not under arrest.
Zh: “好吧，你没有被捕。”

En: I don't know why I said that. I thought it's something a cop would say.
Zh: “我不知道自己为什么会这么说，还以为这是警察会说的话呢。”

En: I don't really need you to *say* anything, I just need the money.
Zh: “我不需要你*说*任何东西，把钱给我就行。”

En: Tsk... He rolls his eyes.
Zh: “啧……”他翻了个白眼。

En: Oh, so apparently I'm a *bitch* now. Okay, I'll be the 'bitch', and you'll be the broke cop who owes me money, and no one will arrest anyone because that's insane. How's that?
Zh: “哦，所以现在我又变成*贱人*了。行吧，我可以当‘贱人’，那你就是欠我钱的破产警察，而且没有人会逮捕任何人，因为那样就太疯狂了。怎么样？”

En: That's cop for: "I haven't offered to pay because I don't have any money either."
Zh: 意思是说：“我没有主动提出要付钱，因为我也没有钱。”

En: Let's see. He dramatically turns a page in the ledger. "Three nights at a tariff of 20 reál comes to 60 reál. Then there's the window you *annihilated* -- the hole in the window was the first thing I saw when I came to work, so don't try to tell me you didn't! That will be 40 reál in damages."
Zh: “我看看。”他做作地翻开开了一页账本。20雷亚尔一晚的房间，三天就是60雷亚尔。还有你*报废*的那扇窗户——我一来上班就看到窗户上的洞了，所以别想狡辩。一共是40雷亚尔的赔偿费用。”

En: Another thing you've annihilated is half the bar -- you've run a tab of 30 reál. Actually more, but we'll round it down to 30 for your hard work maintaining the stability and order of Revachol. That's 60 + 40 + 30 = 130 reál. And yes, reál is still money.
Zh: “你报废的另一样东西是半个吧台——这是30雷亚尔。实际上还有更多，但因为你努力工作、维护瑞瓦肖的稳定与秩序，我们为你四舍五入到30。一共是60+40+30=130雷亚尔。而且没错，雷亚尔仍然是钱。”

En: Money is what grown up people use to pay for things. Things like this hostel room or... He peeks into the ledger: "...or eight bottles of 'Potent Blend' and nine packs of 'Royal Extra'. We use it for everything, really."
Zh: “钱是成年人用来交易的东西，比如这间旅社的房间或是……”他瞄了一眼账本。“……八瓶*混合烈酒*和九包*精装皇家*。说真的，我们用它完成日常的一切。”

En: (Proceed, but don't show him the coins. They're *yours*.)
Zh: （继续，但别让他看到硬币。它们是*你的*。）

En: (Proceed and show him the coins you found.) "Is this money?"
Zh: （继续，给他看你找到的硬币）“这是钱吗？”

En: What are you, brain-damaged?
Zh: “你是怎么回事，脑残？”

En: Actually, I might be.
Zh: “老实说，有可能。”

En: Since I woke up I have trouble remembering even the most basic concepts of reality.
Zh: “自从醒来之后，我甚至连最基本的生活常识都想不起来了。”

En: No, I'm just getting my bearings.
Zh: “不，我只是想弄清其中的关联。”

En: Yes. It is.
Zh: “是的。”

En: (Count them and give them to him.) "That's 10 plus 10 plus 20 equals 40. I'm now down to 90, right?"
Zh: （数一数，交给他。）这里有10加10加20一共40。现在我只欠你90了，对吗？”

En: No, you see... There's a tinge of sadness in his voice. "That's 40 *cents*. Cents are a form of currency one hundred times smaller than the reál. I'm not even going to take this. Come back when you have 130 reál."
Zh: “不，你看……”他的声音里有一丝悲哀。“那是40*分*。分是一种比雷亚尔小一百倍的货币，我甚至都不会要这个。等你凑够130雷亚尔的时候再来吧。”

En: One *hundred* times smaller?
Zh: “小*一百*倍？”

En: But that's horrible.
Zh: “这也太可怕了。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: It is. He stands silently looking at the coppers on the counter.
Zh: “没错。”他静静地站在那里，看着柜面上的硬币。

En: Isn't it... evil? The order of magnitude between what is asked of a person -- and what they have?
Zh: 是不是很……邪恶？一个人被索取——以及他所拥有的的东西之间的数量级差。

En: Darkness rides. (Pick up the coins.)
Zh: “黑暗笼罩一切。”（收起硬币。）

En: For a moment, his antipathy is overcome by compassion for the sheer smallness of the money.
Zh: 一瞬间，他的反感被对于那丁点儿硬币的同情所压倒了。

En: It does, doesn't it.
Zh: “确实，不是吗？”

En: The cafeteria manager stands silently looking at the coppers on the counter before him.
Zh: 餐厅经理静静地站在那里，看着柜面上的硬币。

En: He stands silently looking at the coppers on the counter.
Zh: 他静静地站在那里，看着柜面上的硬币。

En: Keep it to yourself and pick up the coins.
Zh: 自己留着，把硬币收起来。

En: There's a shuffle of nylon as Lieutenant Kitsuragi looks for something in the pockets of his orange bomber.
Zh: 曷城警督在他的橙色夹克口袋里翻找时，传出一阵尼龙摩擦声。

En: Keep the money.
Zh: 留下钱。

En: What happens now?
Zh: “现在要怎么办？”

En: Interesting. Where do I get it from?
Zh: “有意思，哪里才能拿到它呢？”

En: Where can I get money?
Zh: “我去哪里能弄到钱呢？”

En: Why do I need it?
Zh: “为什么我会需要钱呢？”

En: Are you serious? From your work, I don't know... you can take bribes, I guess. He looks at the lieutenant. "I'm sorry. I don't think cops take bribes."
Zh: “你认真的吗？工作啊，我也说不好……还可以受贿，我猜。”他看着警督。“抱歉，我不觉得警察会收受贿赂。”

En: Some do take recompense. The lieutenant is dead serious. "But only to survive."
Zh: “有些人的确会收取酬金。”警督非常严肃。“但也只是为了生存。”

En: For survival, to pay *me*. Unless you want to become a *hobo*? Do you want to become a hobo? There's nowhere else to stay in Martinaise and it's a cold spring outside. Money doesn't make you happy but it lets you be *un*happy for a while longer.
Zh: “为了生存，为了付*我*钱。除非你想变成*流浪汉*？你想当流浪汉吗？在马丁内斯你无处可去，外面就是寒春。金钱不会让你快乐，却能让你*不*快乐更久一点。”

En: If you run out of money, you die. It's like that for all of us -- me too. That's why I need you to pay me. He stops and says, mostly to himself: "I'm not an asshole."
Zh: “如果没有钱，只有死路一条。大家都是一样——包括我。所以我需要你向我付钱。”他停下来，自言自语道：“我不是个混蛋。”

En: Back so soon?
Zh: 这么快就回来了？

En: I had an accident.
Zh: 我出了点意外。

En: So worth it.
Zh: 相当值得。

En: This did not need to happen. I gave no such commands to my body.
Zh: 这种情况完全没必要发生，我没对自己的身体下达这样的指令。

En: Those are the mysteries of the Spinal Cord. The Spinal Cord has yet to reveal itself to you -- its mysteries are unholy mysteries.
Zh: 那就是脊髓的奥秘。脊髓还未向你揭示全部的自己——它那极其不洁的奥秘。

En: I don't know... that just comes off as a lazy effort at re-conceptualizing the antics of a shambling drunk.
Zh: 我不明白……那给人的感觉只是简单地重新定义了一个蹒跚的醉汉的滑稽表演。

En: If it comes off like that, it's because it *is*. And you *are*.
Zh: 感觉像这样，是因为事实就是*如此*，你就是*如此*。

En: It was no accident. Those were *disco moves* from your Spinal Cord. The Spinal Cord has yet to reveal itself to you -- its mysteries are unholy mysteries.
Zh: 那不是意外。那些是来自你脊髓的*迪斯科舞步*。脊髓还未向你揭示全部的自己——它那极其不洁的奥秘。

En: How's it going out there? Are the tiny apes screaming about money yet?
Zh: 外面怎么样了？那些小猩猩还在尖叫着要钱吗？

En: I was wrong to let you go. I should have kept you here... is it bright where you are? Is it TERRIFYING? Have you felt THE LOVE?!
Zh: 我不该让你离开的，应该把你留在这儿……你所在的地方光明吗？可怕吗？你感受到爱了吗？

En: Admit it, you just missed the quarter-light. The darkness on the edge of town.
Zh: 承认吧，你只是想念边窗了。城市边缘的黑暗。

En: Wow, there's more of you? Hidden? I'm so cool.
Zh: 哇哦，还有更多的你在这里吗？藏起来了？我可真是太酷了。

En: You are *way* cool, cooler than the bottom of the sea. Too cool for this world.
Zh: 你*当然*很酷，比最深的海底还要酷，对这个世界来说酷过头了。

En: I sure as hell haven't felt no love!
Zh: “我肯定是没有感受过缺爱的！”

En: It's okay. It's going fine.
Zh: “没关系，会好的。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: It's not going very well.
Zh: “不是很顺利。”

En: I'm doing fine.
Zh: “我还不错。”

En: I think the tiny apes have started screaming, yes.
Zh: “我想那些小猩猩已经开始尖叫了，是的。”

En: What are you, a philosopher?
Zh: “你是什么哲学家吗？”

En: He turns to the lieutenant. "I'm sorry, but he has to pay, I can't let him stay here any longer if he doesn't. If he doesn't have the money by tonight, then..." He shrugs.
Zh: 他转向警督。“我很抱歉，但他必须得付钱，否则我不会让他继续住在这里了。如果他今晚还是付不出钱，那……”他耸耸肩。

En: I don't have a home.
Zh: “我没有家。”

En: I don't remember where my home is.
Zh: “我不记得我的家在哪儿了。”

En: Isn't there somewhere else I can stay around here?
Zh: “这附近没有别的地方可以住了吗？”

En: You mean somewhere else to run up a huge debt? I don't think so. The Union squeezed most places out of business to fund the strike. You're better off home.
Zh: “你说另一个让你继续欠钱的地方？我不觉得。工会为了给罢工集资把大多数地方压榨一空，你最好还是回家去吧。”

En: I'll see what I can do.
Zh: “我看看能做什么。”

En: Fuck this place. I'll take my chances on the streets.
Zh: “去他妈的吧，我要去街上碰碰运气。”

En: Officer... A pattern of creases appears on his forehead. "You really need to take this up with your station, I have a shortwave radio in my car. Call them, ask for assistance. We have to get this investigation started now."
Zh: “警官……”他的前额出现了几道皱纹。“你真的应该向局里汇报当前的状况，我車里就有短波电台。打给他们，请求支援。我们必须现在就开始调查。”

En: No, don't, there's no need... A pattern of creases appears on his forehead. "You really need to take this up with your station, I have a shortwave radio in my car. Call them, ask for assistance. We have to get this investigation started now."
Zh: “不，不用，没必要……”他的前额出现了几道皱纹。“你真的应该向局里汇报当前的状况，我車里就有短波电台。打给他们，请求支援。我们必须现在就开始调查。”

En: I'm sorry I couldn't help more. Creases line his forehead, above the spectacles. "You should take this up with your station, I have a shortwave radio in my car, okay? We have to get this investigation started now."
Zh: “抱歉我帮不上什么忙。”他的前额，眼镜上方出现了几道皱纹。“你真的应该向局里汇报当前的状况，我車里就有短波电台，好吗？我们必须现在就开始调查。”

En: Sir, leash in your tongue. The lieutenant's voice is controlled, yet threatening. Like it's coming from a different person.
Zh: “先生，注意措辞。”警督的声音很克制，但很有威胁性，仿佛是从另一个人的口中说出的。

En: The words nail the Cafeteria Manager to his place. Unmoving he stares down at the counter with his cheeks reddish.
Zh: 这句话让餐厅经理定在了原地，他一动不动地盯着柜台，双颊通红。

En: The words nail the man to his place. Unmoving, he stares down at the counter with his cheeks reddish.
Zh: 这句话让那个男人定在了原地，他一动不动地盯着柜台，双颊通红。

En: Officer, he turns to you. "You need to contact your station. Ask your people for assistance with the money you owe this place. There is a shortwave radio in my car, when you get the time, use it. We need to get this investigation started now."
Zh: “警官，”他转向你。“你需要联系局里，请同事帮你结清这里的账单。我車里就有短波电台，有空就用一下吧。我们必须现在就开始调查。”

En: I'm sorry, but I can't let him stay here if he doesn't pay... He's three days on the slip. If he doesn't have the money by tonight, he'll have to work this from home.
Zh: “我很抱歉，但如果他付不出钱，我就不能让他继续住在这里……已经三天了，如果今晚还是没钱，他就只能在家工作了。”

En: Isn't there somewhere else I can stay around here?
Zh: “这附近没有别的地方可以住了吗？”

En: You can look, but... the Union squeezed most people out of business to fund the strike. So, I don't know... your best shot is just going home.
Zh: “你可以找找，但……工会为了给罢工集资把大多数人压榨一空。所以，我不知道……你最好还是回家去吧。”

En: Okay... The lieutenant lets out a heavy sigh. "Let's go. Goodbye, sir."
Zh: “好的……”警督长叹一口气。“我们走吧。再见，先生。”

En: I'll see what I can do.
Zh: “我看看能做什么。”

En: I don't remember where my home is.
Zh: “我不记得我的家在哪儿了。”

En: No, there's no need... The lieutenant is silent for a moment. "Let's go. Goodbye, sir."
Zh: “不，没这个必要……”警督沉默了一会儿。“我们走吧。再见，先生。”

En: I don't have a home.
Zh: “我没有家。”

En: Fuck this place. I'll take my chances on the streets.
Zh: “去他妈的吧，我要去街上碰碰运气。”

En: Good luck.
Zh: “祝你好运。”

En: The Cafeteria Manager stands silently behind the counter. His eyes fixed on the broken taxidermy, then on the ledger. He does not know what to say.
Zh: 餐厅经理安静地站在柜台后面，眼睛死死地盯着那件破损的标本，然后又看向账本。他不知道该说什么好了。

En: The man stands silently behind the counter, with his eyes fixed on the broken taxidermy, then on the ledger. He does not know what to say anymore.
Zh: 那人安静地站在柜台后面，眼睛死死地盯着那件破损的标本，然后又看向账本。他不知道该说什么好了。

En: The man starts to say something, but then thinks better of it. "Good luck."
Zh: 那人开口说了几句，但很快止住了。“祝你好运。”

En: The man wants to say something, then thinks better of it. "Good luck."
Zh: 那人准备说点什么，但很快止住了。“祝你好运。”

En: Did someone order a vague, farcical reading of *The Law*? More than one pops into your head:
Zh: 有人想听对于*法律*含糊而滑稽的解读吗？你的脑海中浮现了不止一个：

En: An entire array of vague, farcical readings of *The Law* pop into your head:
Zh: 你的脑海中浮现出一大堆含糊、滑稽的法律解读：

En: From the bottom of your blue soul, a term emerges:
Zh: 在你忧郁的灵魂深处，一个术语浮现出来：

En: 'Refusal to aid an officer of the peace.' You are impeding me from carrying out a murder investigation with your inane requests for money.
Zh: “拒绝帮助治安官。你在用疯狂的讨钱诉求妨碍我正在进行的谋杀调查。”

En: But it's not inane, I just... you owe me money.
Zh: “但这并不疯狂，我只是……你欠我钱。”

En: It is crude of you. A man has been killed. File a request for compensation with Station 41. We need to work now.
Zh: “你太鲁莽了。有人在这里被杀了，去向41分局申请补偿金吧。我们现在得去工作了。”

En: Okay, you'll only have to pay for the window. And the drinks. That's 70. I'll get the rest from your stupid station.
Zh: “好吧，那你只要付窗户修理费用，还有酒钱，一共70。剩下的我就找你的分局要了。”

En: I have no memory of drinking them. I'm not paying for the drinks.
Zh: “我不记得我喝过了，我是不会付酒钱的。”

En: I won't pay for the window.
Zh: “我不会付窗户钱的。”

En: Cool. [Leave.]
Zh: “没问题。”[离开。]

En: Goodbye and thank you for your cooperation. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见，感谢你的配合。”[离开。]

En: Fine, I'll take it off the bill! You want to sleep in a post-apocalyptic shit-hole, go ahead! But I'm not giving you another room.
Zh: “行吧，我把它从账单上划掉！你想睡在末日的狗窝里，随你去吧！我不会再给你另外的房间了。”

En: The ledger does! The ledger has a memory! He points to the book. "The drinks are non-negotiable, there's no way your station will pay for them."
Zh: “账本记得啊！账本上有记录！”他指着账本。“酒钱没得商量，你的警局绝对不可能买单的。”

En: No, not *cool*. You still owe me 30 for the drinks. If you don't have it by tonight I'm not letting you back up there... He points upstairs.
Zh: “不，很有*问题*。你还欠我30块酒钱呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上。

En: You still owe me 70. If you don't have it by tonight I'm not letting you back up there, he points upstairs.
Zh: “你还欠我70块呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的。”他指着楼上。

En: Don't thank me. You still owe me 70. If you don't have it by tonight I'm not letting you back up there, he points upstairs.
Zh: “先别谢我。你还欠我70块呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的。”他指着楼上。

En: Don't thank me. You still owe me 30 for the drinks! If you don't have it by tonight I'm not letting you back up there... He points upstairs.
Zh: “先别谢我。你还欠我30块酒钱呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上。

En: I wonder what Sylvie has to say about that?
Zh: “我很好奇塞尔维会怎么说？”

En: You have admitted to *sexual* crimes. You will be taken to *sex* prison.
Zh: “你已经承认了*性*犯罪，你要被关进*性*监狱了。”

En: He laughs. "What in the name of God..."
Zh: 他笑了出来。“看在上帝份上……”

En: Sounds like you think *sex* is more than a social construct. Tell me, do you think women are mentally inferior to men?
Zh: “听起来你认为*性*不仅仅是社会组成部分。告诉我，你觉得女人的智力比男人低下吗？”

En: Are you trying to frighten me now? He turns to the lieutenant: "What is this?"
Zh: “你是想恐吓我吗？”他转向警督：“这是怎么回事？”

En: You tell me what this is? Did you *rape* her?
Zh: “你告诉我这是怎么回事？你*强暴*过她吗？”

En: Fine, fuck this. I'll do it myself, I'll call your station... what was it, 41? I'll ask them to pay for the room. The drinks and the window you still have to pay for. That's 70. Are we done, officers?
Zh: “行了，妈的。我自己来，我会打给你的警局……41，是不是？我会问他们要房费。但是酒和窗户的钱你还是得给我，一共是70块。完了吗，警官们？”

En: Okay, okay... He settles down. "I know what is going on -- and okay. I'll call your station... what did you say it was?"
Zh: “好吧，好吧……”他安静下来。“我知道是怎么回事了——那好吧。我会打给你的警局……你刚刚说多少来着？”

En: Forty one, the lieutenant steps in.
Zh: “41，”警督插了一句。

En: I'll call them for recompensation for the room. But you still owe me 70 for the drinks and the window. And I do *not* think women are mentally inferior to men.
Zh: “我会打给他们要求补偿房费。但你还欠我70块的酒和窗户费用，而且我并*不*认为女性在智力上不如男性。”

En: This is me threatening you with excruciating legal procedures, because you're impeding my investigation.
Zh: “是我在用磨人的法律程序威胁你，因为你妨碍了我办案。”

En: It doesn't look like it is, sweetie. Are you okay? An old woman appears out of the blackness.
Zh: “看起来不像啊，亲爱的。你还好吗？”一位老妇人出现在黑暗中。

En: It doesn't look so, sweetie. Are you okay? An old woman appears out of the blackness.
Zh: “看起来不像啊，亲爱的。你还好吗？”一位老妇人出现在黑暗中。

En: Did one of these tiny apes" have a pale green face?" An old woman appears out of the blackness.
Zh: “这些*小猩猩*中有没有一只浅绿色脸的？”一位老妇人出现在黑暗中。

En: Yes!
Zh: “是的！”

En: It's a joke, sweetie. I didn't actually think you saw the Kind Green Ape of South Safre in a hostel in Martinaise. That would be ridiculous. Are you okay?
Zh: “这是个玩笑，甜心。我不认为你会在马丁内斯的一家旅社里看到南萨弗里的善良绿人猿。不然就太荒谬了。你还好吗？”

En: That is too bad. An old woman appears out of the blackness. "We all need a little love. Are you okay?"
Zh: “那太糟了。”一位老妇人出现在黑暗中。“我们都需要一点爱。你还好吗？”

En: What do you mean what do I mean? An old woman appears out of the blackness. "Are you okay?"
Zh: “你说的我是什么意思，是什么意思？”一位老妇人出现在黑暗中。“你还好吗？”

En: No, I don't think so.
Zh: “不，我不那么觉得。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: With your back against the cold mosaic floor you feel the pain recede. You just need to get up and dust yourself off...
Zh: 当你把背部贴在冰冷的地砖上时，疼痛消失了。你只需站起来，掸掸身上的灰尘……

En: No. You have sustained a trauma to your lower neck. In addition, you have strained your left trapezius muscle. Pain surges down your back when you move.
Zh: 不，你的下颈部受伤了。另外，你还拉伤了左斜方肌。当你移动时，疼痛就会涌向你的背部。

En: No, are *you* okay?
Zh: “不，*你*还好吗？”

En: Ouch.
Zh: “哎哟。”

En: I'm not very okay.
Zh: “我不是很好。”

En: The chair took the brunt of it. Don't worry.
Zh: “有椅子挡着，别担心。”

En: Sir, I didn't mean for this to happen. I'm sorry, this has always been a cop-friendly place... The man seems shaken by the incident.
Zh: “先生，我不是故意的。抱歉，这里一直对警察很友好……”那人似乎被这个事故吓坏了。

En: Officer, maybe you're better off working this from home for now? You live in Jamrock, right? It's not that far away.
Zh: “警官，或许你还是从家里来办案比较好？你住在加姆洛克，是吗？也不是很远。”

En: Not *entirely* cool. You still owe me 60 reál for three nights stay. If you don't have it by tonight, I can't let you back up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “并不是*完全*没问题。你仍然欠我60雷亚尔的房费。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: Not *entirely* cool. You still owe me 100 reál. If you don't have it by tonight, I can't let you back up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “并不是*完全*没问题。你仍然欠我100雷亚尔。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: I won't pay for that window... ouch. (Grab your side.)
Zh: “我不会付窗户钱的……哎呦。”（扶着你的老腰。）

En: Cool.
Zh: “没问题。”

En: Thank you for your cooperation.
Zh: “感谢你的配合。”

En: Okay, fine. I'll take it off your bill! Sleep in a post-apocalyptic hell-hole if you want to. Just know I won't give you another room.
Zh: “行吧，我把它从账单上划掉！你想睡在末日的狗窝里就去吧！但记住，我不会再给你另外的房间了。”

En: Don't thank me yet. You still owe me 100 reál. If you don't have it by tonight, I just can't let you up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “先别谢我。你还欠我100雷亚尔呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: Don't thank me yet. You still owe me 60 reál for three nights stay. If you don't have it by tonight, I just can't let you up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “先别谢我。你还欠我60雷亚尔的房费呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: And for god's sake watch out for yourself.
Zh: “看在神明的份上，小心点儿。”

En: And how do we get there?
Zh: “我们要怎么过去呢？”

En: See that door there? He points west, toward the pinball table. "Go out and turn right. There's a..." He pauses and sighs. "There's a big hole in the fence. No need for a key, just go through that hole and you'll be in the courtyard."
Zh: “看到那边的门了吗？”他朝西指着弹球桌。“出门右转。那里有……”他停下叹了一口气。“围栏上有个大洞。不用钥匙，穿过那个洞就能进去院子了。”

En: Are you supposed to feel guilty about the hole? His sigh tells you: yes.
Zh: 你应该为那个洞感到愧疚吗？他的叹息声告诉你：是的。

En: Can I have a drink?
Zh: “能给我来杯喝的吗？”

En: No, he leans in for emphasis, "you cannot."
Zh: “不，”他倾身强调，“不可以。”

En: Officer... I was thinking more along the lines of your name.
Zh: “警官……我想的更多的是你的名字。”

En: But you can still pour me a drink, right?
Zh: “但你还是可以给我倒杯酒的，对吧？”

En: I could if I were a bartender. But I'm not, I'm the cafeteria manager. So I won't.
Zh: “如果我是酒保的话，确实可以。但我不是，我是餐厅的经理。所以我不会。”

En: There may be some way to get a drink out of him (even for you) but this is not it.
Zh: 也许是有办法让他倒杯酒（甚至是给你）的，但并不是这回事

En: As you blow this joint -- behind you a whiny voice shouts:
Zh: 在你匆忙离开之前——身后传来一声暴躁的喊叫：

En: I don't think I have *any* money. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我想我*没有*钱。”（继续。）

En: No, I'm not the *bartender*. I'm the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “不，我不是*酒保*。我是这里的经理。”

En: What's the difference?
Zh: “有什么区别吗？”

En: You look like a bartender.
Zh: “你看着很像酒保。”

En: That period of my life is over. Not everyone who stands behind a counter is a bartender, okay? I'm the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “我生命中的那个阶段已经结束了。不是所有站在柜台后面的人都是酒保，明白吗？我是餐厅的经理。”

En: I have *three* cafeterias I manage. Three. Get over it.
Zh: “我有*三家*餐厅要管理。三家。得了吧。”

En: He's very animated all of a sudden. This is clearly a touchy subject for him.
Zh: 他突然变得活跃起来，这对他来说显然是个敏感的话题。

En: I hope it is.
Zh: “希望如此。”

En: But you can still pour me a drink, right?
Zh: “但你还是可以给我倒杯酒的，对吧？”

En: I could if I were a bartender. But I'm not, I'm the cafeteria manager. So I won't.
Zh: “如果我是酒保的话，确实可以。但我不是，我是餐厅的经理。所以我不会。”

En: There may be some way to get a drink out of him (even for you) but this is not it.
Zh: 也许是有办法让他倒杯酒（甚至是给你）的，但并不是这回事

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: So not only am I a cop, but I'm also a hero?
Zh: “所以我不仅是个警察，还是个英雄？”

En: He has no respect for you personally, but this man sees himself as a law-abiding citizen -- and you a representative of that law.
Zh: 他对你没有丝毫尊重的意思，但这个男人认为自己是个守法公民——而你是法律的代表。

En: Yes, you are! He looks at you with mock admiration. "A real decorated hero."
Zh: “是啊，没错！”他带着嘲弄的赞赏看着你。“一个真正的英雄。”

En: What did I do?
Zh: “我都做了什么？”

En: You're being sarcastic.
Zh: “你在讽刺我。”

En: I do not appreciate your tone. This is no way to talk to an officer.
Zh: “我不喜欢你说话的语气，这不是跟警察说话的方式。”

En: What have I been doing all that time? Have you seen me around?
Zh: “那我一直在干什么呢？你看到过我吗？”

En: Am I? Or did you ride in, take the body down, solve the murder, and *not* trash my hostel room?
Zh: “我有吗？还是你确实来到这里，放下了尸体，解决了谋杀案，*没有*破坏我的旅社？”

En: What did you *not* do? First you took the body down, then you solved the murder, then you *didn't* trash my hostel room. Maybe you even negotiated the strike?
Zh: “应该是你*没有*做什么？首先是把尸体放下来，解决谋杀案，之后也*没有*破坏我的旅社。也许你还去参与了罢工谈判？”

En: Ah, the dead body. I didn't actually get it down, did I?
Zh: “啊，那具尸体。我其实并没有把他放下来，对吧？”

En: I'm guessing I didn't do any of those things?
Zh: “我猜这些事我都没做过吧？”

En: Oh my god, I did all those things?
Zh: “我的天，我做了那么多事？”

En: What have I been doing then? Have you seen me around?
Zh: “那我一直在干什么呢？你看到过我吗？”

En: No -- you see -- actually you didn't. You haven't done anything even remotely useful since you got here.
Zh: “不是——明白吗——其实你没有。你到这儿来以后，一件有用的事情都没干过。”

En: No, I haven't *seen you around*. I just got here, remember? He looks at you, then at the lieutenant. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: ”不，我并没有*看到过你*。我也是刚到，记得吗？”他看了看你，然后又看向警督。“还有什么事吗？”

En: Oh, it's not?! He raises his shoulders like he's about to say something more, but then gets a hold of himself.
Zh: “哦，不是吗？！”他耸了耸肩，好像还想说些什么，但很快又克制住了。

En: You're right. It's not.
Zh: “你是对的，不是。”

En: You're right, you didn't. And it's only taken you *three days* not to.
Zh: “答对了，你没有。不过是整整*三天*一事无成而已。”

En: This is it! He said *they* hoisted him up on a tree -- who is this *they* if he doesn't know?
Zh: 这就对了！他说是*他们*把他吊在了树上——如果他什么都不知道，那*他们*是谁？

En: Do you have a fridge?
Zh: “你这有冰箱吗？”

En: What?!
Khm... The lieutenant coughs in your direction. "Not now."
Zh: “咳……”警督冲你咳了几声。“不是现在。”

En: Now is not a good time to ask him about the corpse-fridge.
Zh: 现在不是问他尸体冰箱的时候。

En: This sounds like something you can *use* -- to call this Sylvie later...
Zh: 似乎是你可以*用到*的东西——之后可以打给这个塞尔维……

En: No, don't listen to him. It's madness -- just *talk*.
Zh: 不，别听他的。这太疯狂了——只是*聊聊*而已。

En: There's something there and it's not good. That's all you know for now.
Zh: 那里有个不太好的东西，你现在只知道这么多。

En: You've gained a Thought! When this dialogue is over, go to your THOUGHT CABINET and INTERNALIZE it for special bonuses and effects.
Zh: 你获得了一项思维！在本次对话结束后，前往你的思维阁并开始内化新思维吧，这将给你带来加成效果。

En: Everything is cool between you and this guy. He's a big fan. Make some small talk.
Zh: 你跟这家伙之间的所有事都解决了。他是我的超级粉丝。跟他闲聊一下吧。

En: Reál? Must be cosmetics. Probably a body milk.
Zh: 雷亚尔？肯定是化妆品。可能是身体乳。

En: Oh! He puffs with indignation. "You don't owe me *shit*? Well, officer, you're right -- you *don't* owe me shit, you owe me MONEY."
Zh: “哦！”他愤怒地喘着粗气。“你不欠我*什么*？好吧，警官，你说对了——你不欠我什么，你欠我钱。”

En: He turns to you, calmly. "We do not need to assert ourselves here. We only need instructions from him. This is the proprietor, remember?"
Zh: 他平静地转向你。“没必要在这里表明我们的主张。我们只需要他的说明，他是这里的店主，记得吗？”

En: No! He called me a f****t!
Zh: “不行！他骂我是个混蛋！”

En: Sounded like extortion to me.
Zh: “我听着像是敲诈啊。”

En: Alright, calm down Cafeteria Manager, what is this about?
Zh: “好吧，冷静点，餐厅经理。这到底是怎么回事？”

En: Alright, calm down. What is this about?
Zh: “好吧，冷静一点。这到底是怎么回事？”

En: But, you see, he didn't.
Zh: “可是，你看，他并没有。”

En: A competent work of taxidermy, the white and brown seabird lies among piles of coasters and drying mugs, one of its wings broken. The man is trying to mend it.
Zh: 这只白褐相间的海鸟是一件极其出色的动物标本作品，它躺在成堆的杯垫和晒干的马克杯之中，一只翅膀折断了。这人正在设法修理它。

En: Looks like the bird was ripped off the shield that was used to mount it -- most likely on a wall.
Zh: 看上去这只鸟是从用来镶嵌它的盾牌上扯下来的——很可能是在墙上。

En: A small engraved steel tag says: "The Great Skua (Stercorarius skua)."
Zh: 一个小小的雕花钢铁铭牌上写着：“大贼鸥（北贼鸥）。”

En: Something about it makes you feel bitter...
Zh: 关于它的某些东西让你觉得苦涩……

En: What happened to the bird?
Zh: “这只鸟怎么了？”

En: Can I help you with that?
Zh: “我能帮你做些什么吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Look, your *buddy* is over there. He looks at the doors, where a man in a bomber jacket is tapping his foot on the floor.
Zh: “听着，你的*伙伴*在那边呢。”他看向门口，一个穿着短夹克的男人正用脚轻敲着地板。

En: This is a great skua. The seabird is the symbol for the discovery of the Insulindian isola, the part of the world you are in right now.
Zh: 这是一只大贼鸥。这种海鸟是发现伊苏林迪洲的象征，就是现在这个世界的一部分。

En: The small steel tag says as much: "The Great Skua (Stercorarius skua)."
Zh: 小小的钢铁铭牌也说明了这一点：“大贼鸥（北贼鸥）。”

En: That's the great skua, right?
Zh: “那是大贼鸥，对吗？”

En: Why don't you go and talk to him, okay?
Zh: “你去跟他说话吧，行吗？”

En: What do you mean, 'my *buddy*'?
Zh: “‘我的*伙伴*’，你是什么意思？”

En: Again with that insubordinate tone!
Zh: “又是这种不服从的口气！”

En: I'm a cop and I talk to whomever I please.
Zh: “我是警察，警察爱跟谁说话就跟谁说话。”

En: I talk to whomever I please.
Zh: “我爱跟谁说话就跟谁说话。”

En: He pretends not to hear you, concentrating on the bird instead.
Zh: 他假装没听见你的话，反而把注意力集中到了那只鸟身上。

En: For a moment, the Cafeteria Manager fidgets under the lieutenant's gaze. Then he gives in.
Zh: 有那么一会儿，餐厅经理在警督的注视下有些烦躁。然后他放弃挣扎了。

En: For a moment, the man fidgets under the lieutenant's gaze. Then he gives in.
Zh: 有那么一会儿，男人在警督的注视下有些烦躁。然后他放弃挣扎了。

En: Yes, of course. He takes a step back.
Zh: “好的，应该的。”他退后了一步。

En: I know it took us a while to arrive at the scene. But it also took you a while to call us. It was you who placed the call, correct?
Zh: “我们确实花了一段时间才到现场，但你也花了一段时间才给我们打电话。是你报的警，对吗？”

En: It means I'm not ruling out Harry" at this juncture."
Zh: “意思是现在我还不能排除*哈里*的可能性。”

En: I have forgotten my name.
Zh: “我忘记自己的名字了。”

En: I don't like your tone.
Zh: “我不喜欢你说话的语气。”

En: What is this, a joke to you? There's a dead body out back, and it's been there for a week now! No one wants to work in these conditions!
Zh: “这算什么？你觉得这很好笑吗？外面的尸体已经挂了一星期了！没人愿意在这种情形下工作！”

En: And I don't like the dead body that's been hanging out there for a week! He gestures somewhere outside. "No one wants to work in these conditions!"
Zh: “而且我不想再看到那个挂了一个星期的死尸了！”他冲外面比划了一下。“没人愿意在这种情形下工作！”

En: Behind this building, there's a courtyard. He points to the kitchen behind him. "They hoisted him up on a tree there."
Zh: “大楼后面有个院子。”他指着身后的厨房。“他们把他吊在那边的树上了。”

En: And how do we get there then?
Zh: “那我们要怎么过去呢？”

En: This man means the heavy cavalry of Innocence Franconegro, sweeping over the plains and nations of the enemies of mankind, 5th Century style. Unified currency and the concept of *cool* came in their wake. They wore lamellar and carried guns. But first and foremost, Franconigerian heavy cavalry was really, really wide. That hole in the fence must be *enormous*.
Zh: 这人指的是无罪之王弗兰考格斯的重骑兵，以第五世纪的风姿扫荡了平原以及其上的人类公敌之国。他们的觉醒统一了货币以及带来了种种被称为*酷*的东西。他们身披薄甲，手持尖枪。但最重要的是，弗兰考格斯的重骑兵团极其之浩大，围栏上的那个洞一定*硕大无比*。

En: That's easy! See that door there? He points to the west. "First you exit through that. Then to your right you should see a big hole in the fence -- a really big one. You can get to the courtyard through there. No need for the keys. The hole is big enough for the Franconigerian cavalry to fit through."
Zh: “很简单！看到那边的门了吗？”他指着西边。“先从那里出去。然后在你右边，应该能看到围栏上有个大洞——非常大的洞，可以穿过它到院子里去。根本不用钥匙，那个洞很大，大到弗兰考格斯骑兵都能穿过去。”

En: Does he want you to feel *guilty* of making that hole? It's implied in his voice.
Zh: 他是想让你因为那个洞而感到*愧疚*吗？他的声音暗示了这一点。

En: I don't know who killed him, I'm not the police. That's your job.
Zh: “我不知道是谁杀了他，我又不是警察。那是你的工作。”

En: Did you kill him?
Zh: “是你杀的吗？”

En: Before you said *they* hoisted him up on a tree. Who did you mean by *they*?
Zh: “你之前说过*他们*把他吊在树上，*他们*指的是谁？”

En: What are you -- crazy? Of course I didn't *kill* him.
Zh: “你是怎么回事——疯了吗？我当然没有*杀*他啊！”

En: Did you kill him, Garte?
Zh: “是你杀的吗，加尔特？”

En: No, I didn't.
Zh: “不，不是我。”

En: You can tell me, Garte. You killed him, right?
Zh: “你可以跟我说实话，加尔特。是你杀的人，对吗？”

En: I didn't. He turns to the lieutenant: "I don't appreciate this. What is this?"
Zh: “我没有。”他转向警督：“我很不喜欢这样，这算什么？”

En: Why did you have to kill him?
Zh: “你为什么要杀他？”

En: Do I have to answer him? He asks the lieutenant. "Is this mandatory?"
Zh: “我一定得回答他吗？”他问警督。“这是强制性的吗？”

En: The lieutenant stands motionless, his expression unreadable.
Zh: 警督一动不动地站在那里，面上的表情难以捉摸。

En: Did he hurt you? Is that why? Did you get some kind of sick *kick* out of killing him?
Zh: “他伤害过你吗？这就是动机？杀人过后你有觉得很*畅快*吗？”

En: What are you, an idiot?! I told you I haven't killed anyone!
Zh: “你是白痴吗？！我说了我没杀过任何人！”

En: Anyone? Have you killed someone else then?
Zh: “任何人？那你杀过别人吗？”

En: He ignores you.
Zh: 他无视了你。

En: Did you kill Sylvie?
Zh: “你杀了塞尔维吗？”

En: Fuck you, man.
Zh: “去死吧你。”

En: That's enough. You can't tell who the remark is aimed at, you or Garte.
Zh: “够了。”你听不出这句话在针对谁，你还是加尔特。

En: Oh. He's a bit surprised you caught that. "People are saying it was the Union dockworkers. That it was a lynching."
Zh: “哦。”他有些惊讶你竟然捕捉到了这一点。“大家都说是码头工会的人干的，是一场私刑。”

En: Who exactly is saying that?
Zh: “具体是谁说的呢？”

En: The locals. Customers, the people who eat here. A lot of dockworkers eat here. Sylvie told me everyone knows the dockworkers did it.
Zh: “当地人，客人，来用餐的人。很多码头工人都会来这里用餐。塞尔维跟我说大家都知道是那些码头工人干的。”

En: Why would the dockworkers lynch this man?
Zh: “为什么码头工人要对这个男人动用私刑呢？”

En: Did the Débardeurs themselves tell her this, or is it a rumour?
Zh: “是装卸工们自己跟她说的，还是只是谣言？”

En: I don't really know. You'll have to ask her.
Zh: “我也不是很清楚，得去问本人了。”

En: I would suppose it's because they have nothing better to do.
Zh: “我估计是因为他们也没有别的事可做了。”

En: You mean the strike?
Zh: “你是说罢工？”

En: Yes, the *strike*, he makes little quotation marks with his fingers. "The man they hanged was a security guard for the harbour company, I hear -- a mercenary. The unionistas probably thought they'd send a message."
Zh: “是啊，*罢工*，”他用手指比划着引号。“他们吊死的那个人是港口公司的安保人员，我听说——是个雇佣兵。那些工会分子大概觉得这能传递出一种讯号吧。”

En: A very silent wave comes from the lieutenant, like the distant echo of a drum-roll. It's approval.
Zh: 警督微微挥手，仿若远处鼓点的回声。这是批准的意思。

En: I have another question.
Zh: “我还有一个问题。”

En: The lieutenant turns the page in the little notebook he's been scribbling in.
Zh: 警督把他一直在记录的小笔记本翻了页。

En: Okay. I believe you.
Zh: “好吧，我相信你。”

En: You have not entirely convinced me.
Zh: “你并没有完全说服我。”

En: Thank you ever so much.
Zh: “那可真是太谢谢你了。”

En: That's a real pity. He stares angrily at you.
Zh: 他愤怒地盯着你。“那真的太遗憾了。”

En: Haven't you asked me that already? What is it with you and this woman? She has nothing to do with this.
Zh: “你不是已经问过了吗？你跟这个女人之间有什么问题？她与此事无关。”

En: She went away because *none of your business*.
Zh: “她离开是因为*不关你的事*。”

En: Okay, you got me. She went away because of the dead body out back -- and because I asked for her number. That's why Sylvie went away. I hope you appreciate that...
Zh: “好吧，被你发现了。她离开是因为外面那具尸体——以及我问她要了电话。所以塞尔维离开了，希望你能理解……”

En: Mystery solved!
Zh: “谜题解开了！”

En: Didn't go well?
Zh: “不顺利吗？”

En: Suddenly you tense up, blood is being pushed to your muscles. You should hound him on this, hound him *hard*, the prey drive says.
Zh: 你瞬间紧张起来，血液在你的肌肉里奔腾。你猎食者的直觉告诉你自己，你应该在这个问题上抓住他，*使劲*纠缠他。

En: Okay, I'm not gonna hound you on this.
Zh: “好吧，我就不过多纠缠了。”

En: Oh, thank you.
Zh: “哦，谢了。”

En: More. Press on, push through...
Zh: 更多，用力，施压……

En: Oh. He's a bit surprised you caught that. "I meant the Union dockworkers. You know, the guys who *everyone* is saying killed him. Which no one is saying about me -- because I didn't."
Zh: “哦。”他有些惊讶你竟然捕捉到了这一点。“我是指码头工人工会，*大家*都说凶手是他们。但都没有人提到我——因为我没有杀人。”

En: Who is this everyone?
Zh: “大家指的是谁？”

En: She pertains to the coming apocalypse.
Zh: “她与即将到来的世界末日有关。”

En: Am I not a cop? Everything is my business.
Zh: “我不是警察吗？每件事都与我有关。”

En: Fine.
Zh: “很好。”

En: Have they not been telling you you're a cop?
Zh: 他们没说你是个警察吗？

En: Extra fine.
Zh: “特别好。”

En: Does she? Maybe she does... maybe she *pertains* to the apocalypse. He snorts. "Sylvie is not here because I asked for her number. The dead body out back didn't help either, but it was mostly me. I hope you appreciate that..."
Zh: “是吗？或许吧……或许她跟世界末日*有关*。”他嗤之以鼻。“塞尔维不在这儿是因为我问她要了电话。或多或少也是因为外面的尸体，但主要是因为我。希望你能理解……”

En: Yes, I am.
Zh: “是的，没错。”

En: I don't know. I just went for it.
Zh: “不知道，我只是想争取一下。”

En: Is it? Because I thought you were supposed to be investigating the lynching, not my employer conduct.
Zh: “是吗？因为我以为你应该去调查私刑，而不是来管该我怎么当老板的。”

En: This stuff gets on my nerves. I am a feminist.
Zh: “这些事让我很烦躁，我是个女权主义者。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: That's right. If there's one thing I know about myself it's that I'm a *total*, raging feminist.
Zh: “没错。如果说我对自己有什么了解的话，那就是我是个*彻头彻尾的*、狂热女权主义者。”

En: Do you have a phallus in your ear? I said I'm a feminist.
Zh: “你的耳朵里有生殖器吗？我说我是女权主义者。”

En: You heard me.
Zh: “你听到我说的了。”

En: Forget it.
Zh: “算了吧。”

En: I guess I like to be thorough.
Zh: “我想我就是喜欢面面俱到。”

En: Good for you. Was there something else? I'd like to get back to what I was doing.
Zh: “祝贺你。还有别的事吗？我想回去做自己的事了。”

En: Why would I have a problem with you? You're a hero cop.
Zh: “我为什么要对你有意见呢？你可是个英雄警察。”

En: Are you the bartender?
Zh: “你是这里的酒保吗？”

En: I have *three* cafeterias I manage. Three. Get over it.
Zh: “我有*三家*餐厅要管理。三家。得了吧。”

En: Let's go.
Zh: “我们走吧。”

En: I knew it.
Zh: “我知道。”

En: But what if I don't have any? (Proceed.)
Zh: “但如果我没有呢？”（继续。）

En: (Play the fool.) "Who, me?"
Zh: （装傻。）“谁？我吗？”

En: Who, me?
Zh: “谁？我吗？”

En: That's cop for: "I haven't offered to pay because I don't have any money either."
Zh: 警察这么说的意思就是：“我没有主动提出要付钱，因为我也没有钱。”

En: Everything goes dark.
Zh: 一切都变黑了。

En: I'm not a feminist, I just wanted to see the look on your face.
Zh: “我不是女权主义者，只是想看看你的脸上的表情而已。”

En: What are you, a chauvinist then? Hah!
Zh: “那你是个沙文主义者咯？哈！”

En: He smiles until he realizes his comeback wasn't very good. Then he frowns again.
Zh: 他笑着，直到他意识到自己的回击并不漂亮。于是又皱起了眉头。

En: He's very animated all of a sudden. This seems like a touchy subject.
Zh: 他突然变得活跃起来。这对他来说显然是个敏感的话题。

En: This is the second time you've avoided the subject.
Zh: “这是你第二次回避这个话题了。”

En: Oh my god! What is your *obsession* with this Sylvie person? Get over it!
Zh: “我的天呐！你对塞尔维这个人究竟有什么*执念*？算了吧你！”

En: Maybe it's he who's obsessed with "this Sylvie person"? Certainly sounds like it.
Zh: 或许真正对“塞尔维这个人”有执念的是他？听上去确实如此。

En: What is gold and orange like a forest fire, but smells like liquor?
Zh: “什么东西是如同森林大火一般金橙相间，但闻上去像是烈酒？”

En: Are you kidding me?
Zh: “你在逗我？”

En: No, man. Help me out: what is golden-orange and smells like liquor?
Zh: “不，老兄。帮帮我吧：什么东西是金橙相间，但闻上去像是烈酒？”

En: Kim is about to say something. Let him.
Zh: 金正打算说些什么，让他说。

En: It's you, it's obviously you. *You* smell like liquor. And you're orange.
Zh: “是你，明显是你。*你*闻上去一股酒味，而且是橙色的。”

En: That's enough now, sir. Good day.
Zh: “够了，先生。再见。”

En: See -- everyone agrees it's your colour scheme. We're on the right track with this name thing.
Zh: 看见了吗——大家都认为这是你的配色。关于名字的问题我们是正确的。

En: He turns to the lieutenant. "Is this what you get when you call the police now? We've been waiting for a week here!"
Zh: 他转向警督。“现在报警的结果就是*这样*？我们都在这等了一星期了！”

En: Ask him about the body's location *before* asking if he killed him. People give up information in the more innocuous questions, which you can later use in the more sinister ones, not vice versa.
Zh: *先*问他尸体的位置，再问是不是他干的。人们往往会在一些无关痛痒的问题中泄露某些信息，你可以在之后更加严肃的提问中利用起来，反之亦然。

En: He thinks you're stupid, sire.
Zh: 他认为您很蠢，大人。

En: He doesn't know? He just said "they" hoisted him up on a tree -- who is this they?
Zh: 他不知道？他刚刚才说是*他们*把他吊在树上的——他们是谁？

En: That's true, yes. And it's worrying. But let's not *fixate* on it. Look at that stupid bartender instead -- he has no idea what's going on! You and your fuck-you-fingers floating in the air. Barkeep's got his mouth agape like an idiot. You showed him.
Zh: 没错，是的。而且很令人担忧，但我们不要过于*纠结*。看看那个愚蠢的酒保吧——根本不知道发生了什么！你的中指竖在半空，酒吧老板像个白痴似的张着嘴巴。给他点颜色看看。

En: The drinks are on the house, okay? There were *a lot* of drinks on the tab. I still have to charge you for three nights and the broken window, though -- that's 100 square.
Zh: “酒算我请你的，好吗？账单上记了*很多*杯。不过我还是得收你三个晚上的住宿费，还有窗户修理费——整好100块。”

En: He's shaken. Barter now and he'll cave in.
Zh: 他动摇了。现在去讨价还价吧，他会屈服的。

En: Isn't it a bit too late for that?
Zh: “现在是不是有点太晚了？”

En: Okay, I'm not gonna hound you on this.
Zh: “好吧，我就不过多纠缠了。”

En: You're right. It's not.
Zh: “你是对的，不是。”

En: Pick up the coins.
Zh: 收起硬币。

En: He demonstratively suppresses a world-weary sigh.
Zh: 他明显抑制住了一声厌世的叹息。

En: Ma'am, are you alright?
Zh: “夫人，你没事吧？”

En: Are you sure, ma'am?
Zh: “你确定吗，夫人？”

En: Yes, yes -- check on him!
Zh: “是的，是的——去看看他吧！”

En: Oh, I'm fine. He just tripped over my chair -- check on him! She looks at you with concern.
Zh: “哦，我没事。但他被我的轮椅绊倒了——去看看他吧！”她关切地看着你。

En: There's a shuffle of nylon as Lieutenant Kitsuragi looks for something in the pockets of his black bomber.
There's a shuffle of nylon as Lieutenant Kitsuragi looks for something in the pockets of his orange bomber.
Yeah, it will be another week before the phone company gets around to sending a repair guy.
Zh: “是啊，电话公司下个星期才会派维修工过来。”

En: Seeing the disappointment in your face, he relents. "Oh, fine, hero-cop. Lena left a forwarding address. 1113 Tabernacle Road, in Jamrock. If you see them again... do give them my best."
Zh: 看到你脸上失望的表情，他心软了。“哦好吧，英雄警察。莉娜留下了一个转寄地址。1113圣堂街，加姆洛克。如果你再见到他们……请代我向他们问好。”

En: But we weren't done looking for the phasmid...
Zh: “但我们还没找到竹节虫呢……”

En: Especially when you're so busy doing *nothing* to stop psychopaths. How could you just stand there? Still, you did risk your life...
Zh: “特别是你当你忙着*什么都不做*来阻止变态狂的时候，你怎么能光站在那里？尽管如此，你还是冒了生命危险……”

En: 'Defended' may be putting it a bit generously, though. I had a great view of you doing *nothing* to stop those psychopaths. Then I crawled inside. Bullets started flying. Anyway... He clears his throat.
Zh: “不过，‘捍卫’这个词或许有点过于慷慨了。我明明白白地看到你对于那些变态狂*无动于衷*，然后我就爬进了屋。枪火开始纷飞。无论如何……”他清了清嗓子。

En: I found the victim's jeans in the trash container out back.
Zh: “我在外面的垃圾箱里发现了被害人的牛仔裤。”

En: I found the victim's clothes in the trash container out back.
Zh: “我在外面的垃圾箱里发现了被害人的衣服。”

En: I need a drink. Can you pour me one?
Zh: “我得喝点东西。你能给我倒杯酒吗？”

En: Is the trash container out back yours?
Zh: “外面的垃圾箱是你们的吗？”

En: Good bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: Can I help you? He arches an eyebrow.
Zh: “需要帮忙吗？”他挑起眉毛。

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “那我今晚的账单……”

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “那我今晚的账单……”

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “那我今晚的账单……”

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “那我今晚的账单……”

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “那我今晚的账单……”

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “那我今晚的账单……”

En: Yes, yes. For the dead body. You want to put a dead *corpse* into my fridge, right?
Zh: “是的，是的。为了那具尸体，你想把*尸体*塞进我的冰箱，对吧？”

En: Uhm... no.
No. We want to put a *very important* dead body into your fridge.
Zh: “不。我们是想把一具*非常重要*的尸体放进你的冰箱。”

En: Yes. That's the gist of it.
Zh: “没错，正有此意。”

En: How did you know?
Zh: “你是怎么知道的？”

En: Because you haven't taken it away yet, and it must be stinking *hard* about now. Well -- you're not putting it here. Rest assured.
Zh: “因为你还没有把他拖走，现在他一定臭得*不得了*。不过——你不能把它放在这里。放心。”

En: Well -- I *have* a fridge and you're not putting it here.
Zh: “不过——我确实*有*个冰箱，但你不能把它放进里面。”

En: I don't care. You're not putting a dead body into my fridge.
Zh: “我不在乎。你不能把尸体放进我的冰箱。”

En: You're lying. He shakes his head. "You're *not* putting a dead body into my fridge."
Zh: “你在说谎。”他摇摇头。“你*不能*把尸体放进我的冰箱。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Because this is a *culinary* establishment, not a morgue. I can't believe you even asked me.
Zh: “因为这里是*做饭*的地方，不是停尸房。真不敢相信你竟然问得出口。”

En: I'm not gonna even ask why. You're just gonna say something mean.
Zh: “我甚至都不会问为什么，你也只会说些刻薄的话。”

En: This is a *culinary* establishment, not a morgue -- you don't have to ask me *why* you can't store a dead body in my kitchen.
Zh: “这里是*做饭*的地方，不是停尸房——你不用问我*为什么*不能在我的厨房里放尸体了。”

En: Hmm... kitchen. It's right nearby. What if you asked the cook?
Zh: 哼……厨房。就在旁边，如果你去问厨师呢？

En: It would only be for a...
Zh: “只需要放……”

En: Lieutenant -- you too? He can't believe it. "*You're* asking too?! No. The answer is no. I will not turn this place into some kind of *macabre* circus."
Zh: “警督——你也来？”他有些不敢相信。“*你*也要问吗？！不，答案是不行。我绝不会把这里变成一个*死亡*马戏团。”

En: Hmm... the kitchen is close by -- maybe the cook will be more forthcoming?
Zh: 哼……厨房就在旁边——或许厨师会更加乐于助人一些？

En: Wow. Turned the lieutenant off like he was a busted old radio. He really is the *lord* of his realm.
Zh: 哇哦，让警督像台坏掉的老收音机一样打住了，不愧是自己主场的*主人*。

En: Seems we're just going to have to keep looking around.
Zh: “看来我们只能继续观望了。”

En: Seems we have no choice but to trust in Cuno now.
Zh: “看来我们只能选择相信坤诺了。”

En: He sighs. "Let's go talk to the Frittte clerk."
