En: And there we have it. Isn't that beautiful? Another situation fixed by Doctor Love.
Zh: 结果就是这样。这难道不美好吗？爱情医生的又一次胜利。

En: Yeah... I do have a lot of cafeterias to manage. And a lot of employees there. I can't make this part of my work practice, it was... unprofessional of me.
Zh: “是啊……我确实有很多餐厅要管理，也有很多员工。我不能把这当成我工作的一部分，这样……很不专业。”

En: Anyway -- thank you for clearing it up. Now, if there isn't anything else... He does not seem to be overjoyed about his new-found professionalism.
Zh: 他似乎并不为自己新获得的专业精神而感到雀跃。“总而言之——谢谢你清理这个烂摊子。现在，如果没有别的事……”

En: I assure you, it was him.
Zh: “我向你保证，就是他。”

En: ...or can you give him a moment? Somehow you realize this is not going to net you any *professional discounts*. Already he's reverting back to defensive.
Zh: ……或许你能给他一点时间？不知为何，你意识到这不会给你带来任何*专业折扣*。他已经重新设起心防了。

En: What -- the interior-decorating kind? He inspects the bird, somewhat suspiciously -- then mellows. "You know -- I'm sorry. This is actually a nice bird. A competent piece of taxidermy."
Zh: “什么——室内装饰用的？”他怀疑地端详着那只鸟——然后柔和了下来。“那个——我很抱歉。这是一只很漂亮的鸟，一件出色的标本。”

En: I can fix it to the plaque and have a... new bird in the establishment I guess? He hesitates. "So, I don't know... thank you? I'm gonna go with *thank you*."
Zh: “我想可以把它固定在装饰板上，然后为这里……做一个新的鸟类标本？”他犹豫了。“那个……谢谢你？我还是要*谢谢你*的。”

En: I feel good about our work here today. The lieutenant nods. "It's all about the little things -- like bringing people random stuffed animals."
Zh: “我对我们今天的工作很满意。”警督点点头。“全都是一些琐事——比如给人们送去各种各样的毛绒动物。”

En: It's.... not actually about that. But he liked it.
Zh: 这个吧……其实不是那么回事。但他很喜欢。

En: People just don't know how to accept gifts, especially taxidermy. He likes it. He likes the bird. It solves his broken bird problem.
Zh: 人们只是不知道该如何接受礼物，尤其是动物标本之类的礼物。他喜欢这个，很喜欢这只鸟，这解决了他坏掉的标本问题。

En: It was actually me who broke the great skua. I wanted to apologize by bringing you this ruffed grouse.
Zh: “其实弄坏大贼鸥的人是我才对，我带给你这只流苏松鸡是想向你道歉的。”

En: Cool. Okay. He inspects the bird. "I was really hoping *not* to think about the cock carousel any longer, because it made me feel like *shit* -- but this *is* a competent piece of taxidermy."
Zh: “哦，好吧。”他检查着这只鸟。“我真希望自己*不要*再想起鸡鸡轮盘这回事了，让我感觉*糟透了*——但这*是*一件出色的标本。”

En: I should have known Sylvie wouldn't *accidentally* break things here... He inspects the bird, somewhat suspiciously -- then mellows. "Okay. This is actually a nice bird. A competent piece of taxidermy."
Zh: “我早该知道塞尔维不会*不小心*弄坏这里的东西的……”他怀疑地端详着那只鸟——然后柔和了下来。“好吧。这是一只很漂亮的鸟，一件出色的标本。”

En: Okay... He inspects the bird, somewhat suspiciously -- then mellows. "Okay. Well. This is actually a nice bird. A competent piece of taxidermy."
Zh: “好吧……”他怀疑地端详着那只鸟——然后柔和了下来。“那个，好吧。这是一只很漂亮的鸟，一件出色的标本。”

En: He tries not to look at you -- it's dangerous to *acknowledge* the karaoke man.
Zh: 他尽量不去看你——跟手持话筒的人*对上视线*是很危险的。

En: It's for the... He begins confidently, but then stumbles on his own words. "It's for no one. It's a prop. I'm not letting anyone use it after the Great Karaoke Catastrophe of '44."
Zh: “这是给……”他开口时很自信，之后却结结巴巴地说不出话来了。“这不是给谁用的，只是道具而已。自44年卡拉OK大灾难之后，我不会再让任何人用它了。”

En: What happened in '44?
Zh: “44年发生了什么事？”

En: A lot of people got killed because some asshole wanted to sing karaoke.
Zh: “因为某个混蛋想唱卡拉OK，导致很多人被杀了。”

En: I'm having it uninstalled, he mumbles to himself.
Zh: “我要把它拆了，”他喃喃自语道。

En: Oh, that's so sad to hear, he says with a barely disguised grin. "I'd *totally* let you sing karaoke, if you only had a tape -- *and* you'd paid your bills."
Zh: “哦，那可真遗憾。”他几乎不加掩饰地笑着说。“如果你有磁带——*并且*付钱之后，我*绝对*会让你去唱歌。”

En: I have the tape now. You *have* to let me sing karaoke. (Give him the tape)
Zh: “我现在有磁带了，你*得*让我唱歌了。”（把磁带给他）

En: Fine. Okay. The kitchen is closed until 13:00 because the cook is working. You can snoop around after that -- if you must.
Zh: “行了，好吧。因为厨师在工作，厨房会一直关闭到13:00。之后你就可以进去调查了——如果你非得去的话。”

En: Fucker? He shakes his head. "Lay off the swear words, they don't make you *cool*. This isn't kindergarten."
Zh: “混蛋？”他摇摇头。“别再说脏话了，它们不会让你变*酷*的。这里可不是幼儿园。”

En: Okay then, never mind.
Zh: “那好吧，没关系。”

En: Yes. What else?
Zh: “是的。还有什么事？”

En: It wouldn't hurt to get a little life in here. Other than the hellish karaoke machine. That one's always *causing trouble*.
Zh: “在这里稍微添点生机也无妨，总比那台该死的卡拉OK机器要好。那东西总是会*惹麻烦*。”

En: Yeah, those numbers he's adding up must be making good sense to him right now.
Zh: 是啊，他累计起来的那些数字现在对他来说一定很有意义。

En: Garte, what if I told you I got into the back room -- behind the *blue steel door*
Zh: “加尔特，如果我告诉你我进去了后面的房间呢——*蓝色铁门*后面。”

En: Garte, what if I told I got into the back room? Behind the blue door in the kitchen.
Zh: “加尔特，如果我告诉你我进去了后面的房间呢？厨房的蓝门里面。”

En: It takes a lot of willpower not to ask. Obviously he's been wanting to know what's behind the door...
Zh: 这需要很强大的意志力才能忍住好奇，显然他想知道门背后的东西很久了……

En: Aren't you gonna ask me what's back there?
Zh: “你不打算问我门背后是什么吗？”

En: Okay -- what *is* back there?
Zh: “好吧——背后*是*什么？”

En: Pinball machines. A pinball workshop.
Zh: “弹球机，一个弹球机工坊。”

En: Skeletons. A mausoleum of the dead.
Zh: “骷髅，是一座死者的陵墓。”

En: Nothing. The black gaping maw at the end of Time.
Zh: “什么都没有，仿佛在时间尽头大张着的嘴。”

En: He shakes his head. "There is no gaping maw. If you don't want to tell me you don't have to. It's okay."
Zh: 他摇头。“根本就没有大嘴。如果你不想告诉我就算了，没关系。”

En: Yes. Ha ha. What's *actually* behind there?
Zh: “是吗，哈哈。里面*到底*有什么？”

En: Hah! I knew it. I've always wondered where those machines by the door came from -- *and* they told me there was some kind of pinball thing here too...
Zh: “哈！我就知道。我一直很想知道门边的那些机器是从哪儿来的——*而且*他们告诉我这里曾经也有些弹球机之类的东西……”

En: I knew it. He repeats. "Were there any back there? In working order I mean?"
Zh: “我就知道。”他重复道。“后面还有吗？我是说还能正常运作的那种？”

En: *But please do still tell me!*
Zh: *但请务必告诉我！*

En: Sounds like he cares about the place. He's not going to be overjoyed to hear that it's part of the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: 听起来他很关心这个地方。如果他知道这里是诅咒商业区的一部分，一定很不开心。

En: He should still know. You have to be *forewarned* about these things.
Zh: 他还是应该知道，这类事必须*提前警告*一下才行。

En: So he knows of the Doomed Commercial Area. *And* its address. He's thought about this.
Zh: 所以他是知道诅咒商业区*以及*它的地址的，他考虑过这个问题。

En: There is an acrimonious note. It's clear he's doing the real work around here.
Zh: 这种尖酸刻薄的语气，显然他才是这里真正做事的人。

En: For 14 years, man -- that's how long I've worked here. I've kept this place up through hail and through sleet. Fuck me, if some Doom Ghost... he steadies his voice.
Zh: “14年，老兄——这就是我在这里工作过的时间。无论冰雹还是雨夹雪，我一直都在守护着这个地方。去他的吧，如果真有什么诅咒恶灵……”他的声音愈发坚定。

En: You're guessing there aren't *that* many others.
Zh: 你想也不会有*多少*其它了。

En: And who named it Whirling-in-Rags?
Zh: “谁给这里起名褴褛飞旋的？”

En: Well, it sure as hell wasn't the real estate company.
Zh: “这个嘛，肯定不是房地产公司。”

En: You did?
Zh: “是你吗？”

En: It was you?
Zh: “是你吗？”

En: You look surprised? What? It's a great name, I know. Cafeteria managers come up with great names too. It's from a song.
Zh: “你看起来很惊讶？怎么？我知道，这是个好名字，餐厅经理也是能想出好名字的。它来自于一首歌。”

En: A song?
Zh: “一首歌？”

En: *Hail Holy Queen* by The Etenniers. 'Hail holy queen of the sea,' he quotes. "'You're whirling in rags -- you're vast and you're sad."
Zh: “不朽者*向神圣的女王致敬*。‘向海中的神圣女王致敬，’”他引用道。“‘你衣衫褴褛，不停旋转——浩瀚又悲伤。’”

En: Good pick, the lieutenant nods.
Zh: “不错的选择。”警督点点头。

En: Good pick.
Zh: 不错的选择。

En: Well -- too bad it's all doomed then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧——可惜都被诅咒了。”（总结。）

En: Luck has got nothing to do with it. He looks to where the hidden room is. "I need to think about where I'm gonna place those pinballs -- I have a feeling they're gonna help."
Zh: “这与运气无关。”他看着密室的方向。“我得想想要把这些弹球机放在哪里——我有种感觉它们会派上用场的。”

En: Against the Doom, it's implied.
Zh: 这是反抗诅咒的暗示。

En: So if you didn't have anything else to tell me -- about my establishment -- can we, you know, wrap it up?
Zh: “如果你没有别的事情——关于这里的事情要告诉我了——我们能不能，那个，结束了？”

En: Wait -- so you *know* of the curse!
Zh: “等等——所以你是*知道*诅咒的！”

En: Everyone *knows* of it.
Zh: “每个人都*知道*。”

En: Klaasje. In Room #3. She nicked the phone line.
Zh: “卡拉洁，3号房间的那个。她把电话线切断了。”

En: Why? He stops himself. "No. Fuck it. I don't wanna know. I don't want to know why these degenerates do what they do. I thought we had *one* good guest in the building..."
Zh: “为什么？”他自己闭嘴了。“不，去他的吧，我不想知道，不想知道那些道德沦丧的家伙这么做的原因。我还以为旅社里有*一个*好客人呢……”

En: Yeah, I bet you didn't want it to be her.
Zh: “是啊，我敢说你肯定不希望是她。”

En: Well, anyway, mystery solved. I don't like loose ends.
Zh: “好吧，反正谜底也揭开了。我不喜欢虎头蛇尾。”

En: You have Lena. Lena is a good customer.
Zh: “你有莉娜呀，她是个好客人。”

En: He really doesn't like it being Klaasje.
Zh: 他真的不希望是卡拉洁。

En: What's that supposed to mean? Fuck you, buddy -- she's not my taste. Good thing she pays for her stuff on time, I'll forward her the bill and be done with it.
Zh: “什么意思？去你的吧，伙计——她不合我的口味。还好她会按时付钱，我会把账单给她，然后就完事了。”

En: Now was there anything else? I have, like, four thousand coasters to stack and then I need to wash dishes because it turns out I have to do that too now.
Zh: “还有什么事吗？我还有，大概4000个杯垫要叠，然后还要去洗碗，因为现在这个也该我做了。”

En: Hmm... wow. She really isn't his taste. Her spell hasn't spread here.
Zh: 嗯……哇哦。她是真的不合他的口味，她的魅力还没有延伸到这里。

En: Yeah, neither do I. Thank you for telling me I guess. Good thing she pays for her stuff on time -- I'll forward her the bill and be done with it.
Zh: “是啊，我也不喜欢，谢谢你告诉我这个。还好她会按时付钱——我会把账单给她，然后就完事了。”

En: Hmm... sounds like she has money tucked away somewhere. Enough to be a stable paying guest.
Zh: 嗯……听上去她好像还藏着钱，足以让她成为一个按时付费的客人。

En: Yeah, well -- she's not a guest, is she?
Zh: “是啊，那个——她其实不是客人，对吧？”

En: What? the woman chimes in. "Did someone mention my name?"
Zh: “什么？”那个女人插了进来。“有人提到我的名字了吗？”

En: We were just paying you a compliment. And finding out a *degenerate* from room 3 nicked the phone line. Everything is okay here! he yells, then turns to you.
Zh: “我们是在夸你呢，而且发现了3号房间的*堕落者*切断了电话线。这里一切正常！”他喊道，然后转向你。

En: Good thing that guest pays for her stuff on time -- I'll forward her the bill and be done with it. Was there anything else?
Zh: “幸好那个客人会按时付钱——我会把账单给她，然后就完事了。你还有什么事吗？”

En: Anyway, I'll forward that woman her bill and be done with it... Now, was there anything else?
Zh: “总之，我会把账单给那个女人，然后就完事了……你还有什么事吗？”

En: Sir, he was only asking a question. It's his job -- and mine. See that it's covered.
Zh: “先生，他只是问了个问题罢了。这就是他——和我的工作。就当它已经解决了。”

En: Yes. I absolutely will. He calms his breathing. "Sorry. It's been a bit of a day -- and now a hole in the wall."
Zh: “好的，我绝对会的。”他平静了下来。“抱歉，我今天有点不对劲儿——现在墙上还被开了个洞。”

En: In your defence -- neither do I.
Zh: “为你说一句——我也不知道。”

En: Damn you fat Evrart, your novelty check is useless!
Zh: 去死吧肥猪艾弗拉特，你的兑奖支票根本没用！

En: Like what? I was really enjoying talking about the *money* you owe me...
Zh: “比如呢？我真的很喜欢说起你欠我的*钱*……”

En: Draconian measures...
Zh: 严厉的措施……

En: I *really* didn't want to resort to this, the man is thinking.
Zh: 我*真的*不想求助于他，这个男人思索着。

En: You can cash it in at the local Frittte, near the harbour gates -- though it might not be enough to cover it...
Zh: “你可以去本地的弗利多兑换，就在港口大门附近——虽然可能还是不够……”

En: Literary demotion! That's the worst...
Zh: 文化降级！这是最糟糕的……

En: Oh, lieutenant -- we're *done* here.
Zh: “哦，警督——我们已经*结束*了。”

En: Frittte. Near the gates. The lieutenant sounds tired. "They'll exchange it."
Zh: “弗利多。大门附近。”警督听上去很疲惫。“他们会兑奖的。”

En: How could ANYONE forget, asshole?!
Zh: 怎么会有人忘记呢，混蛋？！

En: Maybe I have a vendetta against it?
Zh: “也许我跟它有仇吧？”

En: We were sort of hoping there would be a gun, an expensive jewel, or at least a *sword* in it for you -- if you deliver the message. Oh well...
Zh: 我们或多或少希望里面会有一把枪、一件昂贵的珠宝、或者至少是一柄*剑*——如果你传达到了的话。哦好吧……

En: Garte, I found a new bird... dammit, I FORGOT to take it with me!
Zh: “加尔特，我找到了一只新鸟……该死，我忘记带来了！”

En: I have no idea what you're talking about.
Zh: “我完全听不懂你在说什么。”

En: It's a long *healthy* trek back to the village.
Zh: 要经过很长一段艰苦*锻炼*才能回到村子里。

En: Back to the village -- then back here -- then back to the coast *again*... That must be 3 kilometres in total. Okay...
Zh: 回去村子——再回到这里——然后*再*回去岸边……加起来得有三公里了。好吧……

En: This was mostly about the fucking *cardio*. Massive cardio here. You'll live 'til 90! *Or* you'll get a heart attack from running.
Zh: 基本上就是跟该死的*有氧运动*有关。大量的有氧运动。你会活到90岁的！*或是*因为跑步而心脏病发作。

En: *Or* you broke the bird. That's why you brought the replacement. Out of guilt...
Zh: *或者*是你弄坏了那只鸟，所以你才带了替代品，处于内疚……

En: Okay, *fuck* me then! Just FUCK me!
Zh: “好吧，那就*去他妈*的吧！去他妈的！”

En: Stop yelling.
Zh: “别再大声嚷嚷了。”

En: This is the look of a man who's *defeated*. He knows he's out of excuses.
Zh: 这是一副*失败者*的表情，他知道自己找不出任何理由了。

En: By the way, I'm going to sing karaoke here.
Zh: “顺便一提，我打算在这里唱卡拉OK。”

En: Absolutely out of the question.
Zh: “绝对没这个可能。”

En: You wait and see, cafeteria manager!
Zh: 你就等着瞧吧，餐厅经理！

En: Absolutely *in* the question! First we find a *sad* banger, then we sing this place to SHIT.
Zh: 绝对*有*可能！首先我们要找到一首*悲伤*的金曲，然后一起嗨翻全场。

En: Your body is ready, sire.
Zh: 您的身体已经准备好了，大人。

En: Garte, I saw another *thing* at the Whirling...
Zh: “加尔特，我在飞旋旅社还看到了另一样*东西*……”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: By the way -- you should come back to this *thing*-based questionnaire if you see anything interesting in the Whirling later!
Zh: 顺便一提——如果之后你在飞旋旅社发现了任何有趣的东西，最好回来重新展开关于这些*东西*的调查。

En: Hmm... you feel like you should come back to this *thing*-based questionnaire if you see anything interesting in the Whirling later.
Zh: 嗯……你觉得如果自己之后在飞旋旅社发现了任何有趣的东西，回来重新展开关于这些*东西*的调查。

En: So, just to be clear -- you don't know how to open it?
Zh: “所以，先说清楚——你不知道该怎么打开它？”

En: Nope. No idea -- don't *want* to either.
Zh: “不，我不知道——也*不想*。”

En: What a shame. To fix such a good peephole.
Zh: 真遗憾，要堵上那么好的一个窥视孔。

En: I feel a capitalist plot coming up.
Zh: “我感觉到了一个资本主义陷阱正在酝酿当中。”

En: Capitalist *plot*. He rolls his eyes. "The pinball we have in the corner now is broken -- I want to diversify the entertainment options."
Zh: “资本主义*陷阱*。”他翻了个白眼。“现在我们放在角落里的那台弹球机坏掉了——我想让娱乐的方式更多样化一些。”

En: Damn! Your feet thought we got away!
Zh: 该死！你的脚以为我们逃脱了！

En: Maybe I *am* a cook?
Zh: “或许我*就是*个厨师呢？”

En: You're not. You're a menace... He gets a hold of himself. "Fine, okay, the kitchen is closed until 13:00 because the *real* cook is working. You can snoop around after that -- if you must."
Zh: “你不是，你就是个威胁……”他克制住了自己。因为*真正的*厨师在工作，厨房会一直关闭到13:00。之后你就可以进去调查了——如果你非得去的话。”

En: ... and, come to think of it, Frittte's probably closed at this hour. They're open from seven to eleven.
Zh: “……另外，仔细想想，弗利多这个时候可能也关门了。他们的营业时间是七点到十一点。”

En: Correct him!
Zh: 纠正他！

En: Flattery is the way.
Zh: 恭维他。

En: Yeah. We've heard you have, like, a *huge* fridge. Colossal. Practically a mausoleum.
Zh: “没错。我们听说有一个，*巨型*冰箱。可以说是个帝王陵了。”

En: You've heard wrong. And you're *not* putting a dead body into my fridge.
Zh: “你们听错了。而且你们也*不能*把尸体放进我的冰箱。”

En: Damn, I thought the size thing would work...
Zh: 该死，我还以为这尺寸可以呢……

En: That bird ain't no symbol of hope. It's a menace and a traitor!
Zh: 那只鸟可不是希望的象征，而是个威胁，一个叛徒！

En: Didn't check. Pinball is stupid.
Zh: “我没去检查，弹球机也太蠢了。”

En: The lieutenant nods in agreeance.
Zh: 警督点头表示同意。

En: Take it easy on him. Deep down he really hates being the guy who has to remind you.
Zh: 别对他太苛刻了。在内心深处，他也不想成为那个不得不提醒你的人。

En: You know, there *might* be other places to sleep on this coast... you'll find one eventually.
Zh: 知道吗，这片海岸上*或许*会有别的地方可以睡觉……你最终会找到的。

En: It's safe to say this is about your karaoke performance.
Zh: 可以肯定的说这跟你的卡拉OK表演有关。

En: It really took courage. Don't pick at him.
Zh: 这真的需要勇气。不要指责他。

En: He's not too offended. The experience has changed something in him.
Zh: 他并没有太生气。这次经历让他发生了一些变化。

En: Do I have a shaker in my hand? Is this... He points to his empty hand. "Is this a shaker?"
Zh: “我手里有摇酒器吗？这是……”他指着自己空空的手。“这是摇酒器吗？”

En: I can pay it off with my net worth if that's cool?
Zh: “如果可以的话，我可以用我的净资产来支付。”

En: No, of course it's not *cool*. He shoots you a look of disbelief. "What am I supposed to do with your *net worth*? I need cash, moron."
Zh: “不，当然不*可以*。”他怀疑地看了你一眼。“我拿你的*净资产*有什么用？我需要现金，白痴。”

En: But I paid for the work on that monument out there. Does that mean nothing to you?
Zh: “但是我出钱修了那座纪念碑。难道那对你来说没意义吗？”

En: That was *you*? Of course it was. The man behind every new misery in my life. He looks you right in the eyes. "But let's get back to the topic of the money you owe me."
Zh: “原来是*你*啊？当然有意义了。我生命中的每一个不幸都是他造成的。”他直瞪瞪地看着你。“不过还是回到你欠我钱的事情上吧。”

En: Why? Do you want to play? Because I might be up for a game...
Zh: “怎么？你想试试吗？因为我还挺想玩的……”

En: Didn't check. Pinball isn't relevant to the investigation.
Zh: “我没去检查，弹球机跟本次调查无关。”

En: No, I was... just wondering. He appears to be making a calculation in his head. "If you found pinball machines there..."
Zh: “不，我只是……想想而已。”他似乎在盘算着什么。“如果你在那里发现了弹球机……”

En: I was... just wondering. He appears to be making a calculation in his head. "If you found pinball machines there..."
Zh: “我只是……想想而已。”他似乎在盘算着什么。“如果你在那里发现了弹球机……”

En: He was *wondering* about something business-related. About how much money he could make off one.
Zh: 他在*想着*什么生意经呢，能从一台弹球机上赚多少钱之类的。

En: He's really, really holding himself back here.
Zh: 他是真的在克制自己。

En: Oh? Okay. Well. He controls his excitement well. "I did hear you make noise back there. So -- good for you."
Zh: “哦？那好吧。”他很好地控制住了自己的激动心情。“我确实听到你在后面吵吵嚷嚷的，这样——挺不错的。”

En: Did you see the HUGE graffito outside?
Zh: “你看到外面那个巨大的涂鸦了吗？”

En: Did you see the HUGE BURNING graffito outside?
Zh: “你看到外面那个燃烧中的巨大涂鸦了吗？”

En: Yes. Very *responsible*.
Zh: “是的。真是太*负责*了。”

En: C'mon, you've gotta admit -- it was pretty cool.
Zh: “得了吧，你得承认——真的挺酷的。”

En: I guess you're right, it was pretty irresponsible of me.
Zh: “我想你是对的，我真是太不负责任了。”

En: Yes, well... He sighs. "At least nothing bad happened -- yet."
Zh: “是啊……”他叹了口气。“至少目前——还没有什么坏事发生。”

En: He's so exhausted following recent events, he doesn't even have the energy to really get on your case.
Zh: 近期发生的事件让他精疲力尽了，甚至都没有精力处理你这档事。

En: Looked pretty hot to me... He stops, realizing that this is also a compliment. "At least nothing bad actually happened," he adds with a sigh.
Zh: “我觉得挺火辣的……”他停了下来，意识到这也是一种赞美。“至少没有什么坏事发生。”他叹了口气补充道。

En: No, I *completely* failed to notice that. It's only in front of the entrance to the establishment I manage. Though, maybe, he continues reluctantly, "it's still better than just the blood."
Zh: “不，我*完全*没注意到呢。它就在我管理的餐厅正门口呢。不过，也许，”他不情愿地继续道，“它还是比血迹好多了。”

En: Do you think it's connected to The Return?
Zh: “你认为这跟归来有关吗？”

En: He rolls his eyes. "The Return of what? Some commie bullshit? The Return of the king? I don't concern myself with paranoid political rumours. I have a *real* place to run."
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。“什么归来？康米主义之类的玩意吗？国王的归来？我不关心那些偏执的政治谣言。我有*真正的*生意要做呢。”

En: This again? He stares at the novelty cheque. "You just won't let it go, will you? Real funny."
Zh: 他盯着那张兑奖支票。“你是不会放弃的，是吧？真有意思。”

En: You must be kidding, right? He stares at the large novelty cheque, baffled. "Yeah, good one, officer. Real funny."
Zh: “你一定是在开玩笑吧？”他盯着那张兑奖支票，迷惑了。“是啊，干得漂亮，警官。真有意思。”

En: But this establishment only takes cash. Now, do you have that cash, Mr. Novelty Cheque Man?
Zh: “但这家店只收现金。那么，你有现金吗，兑奖支票先生？”

En: I don't think I have *any* money. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我觉得我好像*没钱*。”（继续。）

En: Time stops advancing after 02.00 in the morning. If you haven't paid for your room by then, it's game over. Don't leave finding money to the last minute, however -- it's harder to make cash after nightfall, when the shops are closed and the streets empty.
Zh: 凌晨2点之后，时间就会停止前进。如果那个时候你还没有支付房费，游戏就结束了。不过，不要把找钱留到最后一刻——夜幕降临之后，商店关门，街道空无一人，那时候就很难弄到钱了。

En: He's right, the lieutenant whispers, "you *really* have to sort out your money problems, starting tomorrow. I can't help you again."
Zh: “他说得对，”警督小声说到，“你*真的*应该解决自己的金钱问题，就从明天开始。我不能再帮你了。”

En: He's right. You can't depend on Kim helping you out again.
Zh: 他说得对。你不能再指望金帮你了。

En: How many times do I have to tell you about the trash bin idea?! Let's get climbing already! Into trash bins!
Zh: 关于垃圾箱的主意，我还要告诉你多少次？！走啊，现在就爬起来！爬到垃圾箱里去！

En: You could try climbing into that trash bin in the yard, hobocop style...
Zh: 你可以试着爬进院子里的垃圾箱，这是流浪警探的风格……

En: You could try climbing into that trash bin in the yard, hobocop style... after you've gotten open the lid.
Zh: 你可以试着爬进院子里的垃圾箱，这是流浪警探的风格……在你打开盖子之后。

En: Yes, have you got it?
Zh: “是的，你拿到了吗？”

En: Here's 130 reál.
Zh: “这里是130雷亚尔。”

En: Here's 100 reál.
Zh: “这里是100雷亚尔。”

En: Here's 70 reál.
Zh: “这里是70雷亚尔。”

En: Here's 60 reál.
Zh: “这里是60雷亚尔。”

En: Here's 30 reál.
Zh: “这里是30雷亚尔。”

En: Actually, let's talk about something else.
Zh: “实际上，我们来聊点别的吧。”

En: About my bill for tonight...
Zh: “关于我今晚的账单……”

En: Yeah, 20 reál for the night.
Zh: “是的，住宿费20雷亚尔。”

En: Not yet...
Good. You got the room for the night, but remember -- you'll need *another* 20 reál tomorrow.
Zh: “很好。你今晚可以住这里，但要记住——明天还得付*另外*20雷亚尔。”

En: He rolls his eyes. "Then why are you wasting my time... and yours?"
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。“那你为什么还在浪费我的……和你自己的时间？”

En: They come here in the evenings. Dumb, unruly types. Think they're Big Shit. But they're good customers -- they place big orders, and always pay *on time*.
Zh: “他们晚上会过来，不守规矩的傻瓜们，自以为是什么大人物。但他们是不错的客人——舍得花钱，*按时*付款。”

En: We should find out who this loud faction is, occupying the booth. Loudness means talkative -- and we need info. He gives you a meaningful nod.
Zh: 他意味深长地点点头。“我们得找出那帮吵吵嚷嚷的，霸占了卡座的家伙是谁。吵闹意味着健谈——而我们正好需要讯息。”

En: Am I wearing a little bow tie? Am I wearing a bow tie and doing this? He shakes the imaginary shaker, furiously.
Zh: “我打着小领结吗？我打着领结然后在做这个吗？”他愤怒地摇着想象中的摇酒器。

En: Am I smiling? Do you see me smiling and shaking my little shaker? No? Do you know *why?*
Zh: “我在笑吗？你看到我笑着摇我的小摇酒器了吗？没有？你知道*为什么*吗？”

En: He sounds irritated.
Zh: 他听起来很生气。

En: You sound irritated -- why? All I want is a drink.
Zh: “你好像生气了——为什么？我只是想要杯喝的而已。”

En: No. There's nothing there.
Zh: “没，什么也没有。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Because he's not a bartender.
Zh: 因为他不是酒保。

En: Because you're not a bartender?
Zh: “因为你不是酒保吗？”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: I feel like you've given me a hint previously, but it isn't connecting...
Zh: “我觉得你之前好像给过我提示，但它们没有关联啊……”

En: That's right -- I'm THE CAFETERIA MANAGER. He calms his breath. "I'm glad we cleared that. Was there anything else?"
Zh: “没错——我是餐厅的经理。”他平复了一下呼吸。“很高兴我们能达成共识，还有别的事情吗？”

En: Because I'm not a bartender. I'm a cafeteria manager. Is there anything else you wanted?
Zh: “因为我不是酒保，我是餐厅经理。还有什么需要吗？”

En: You don't understand the seriousness of the situation. I'm an alcoholic. I need my fix.
Zh: “你不明白事件的严重性，我是个酒鬼，我需要解药。”

En: Please make an exception. I got the shakes bad, man. I need my pacifier.
Zh: “请破例一次吧。我在浑身发抖，老兄。我需要一杯镇静剂。”

En: I want a fucking drink, asshole!
Zh: “我他妈的想喝一杯，混蛋！”

En: What you want is a GOD DAMN DRINK. How is that so hard to understand?
Zh: 你想要的只是一杯该死的酒！有什么难以理解的呢？

En: I am an alcohol-operated detective. If you want me to clean up the dead body and solve the case then you need to insert alcohol into my mouth.
Zh: “我是个被酒精驱动的警探。如果你想让去我清理死尸或侦破案件，就得把酒精灌进我的嘴里。”

En: I am an alcohol-operated detective. If you want me to solve crimes or do whatever it is I'm here to do you need to insert alcohol into my mouth.
Zh: “我是个被酒精驱动的警探。如果你想让去我破案或做其它任何事，就得把酒精灌进我的嘴里。”

En: Mine? No, it belongs to the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “我的？不，那是褴褛飞旋的。”

En: Thank you for clearing that up. Why do you keep the container locked?
Zh: “感谢你澄清这一点。可是你为什么要把垃圾箱锁起来呢？”

En: Don't get smart with me.
Zh: “别跟我耍小聪明。”

En: I need the keys to the container.
Zh: “我需要垃圾箱的钥匙。”

En: I was merely clearing up the difference. What did you want with it?
Zh: “只是一点小小的清理工作而已，你要它做什么？”

En: That's it. There was something else I wanted.
Zh: “就这样。我还需要些别的东西。”

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么？”

En: May I ask why?
Zh: “可以问为什么吗？”

En: It's part of the case. There may be evidence in there.
Zh: “这是调查的一部分，那里面可能会有证据。”

En: I'm the police. That's why. Give me the keys.
Zh: “我是警察，这就是为什么。把钥匙给我。”

En: It's a personal matter.
Zh: “这是件私事。”

En: I had a *premonition*. And *omen* even. Call it what you want... I need to see what's in that container.
Zh: “我有一种*预感*，或者说是*预兆*，随你怎么说……我得看看那个垃圾箱里装的是什么。”

En: An omen? He looks at you, his neck crooked and his brow furrowed, then says shakes his head: "Whatever..."
Zh: “*预兆*？”他弯着脖子，皱着眉头看看你，然后摇头说：“随便吧……”

En: He takes the keys from under the counter and hands them to you: "Just bring them back once you're done, please."
Zh: 他从柜台下面拿出钥匙递给你：“用完之后请把它还回来。”

En: If it's a personal matter, why in the name of god should I... He stops mid-sentence. "Okay, whatever."
Zh: “如果是私事，看在老天爷的份上为什么我……”话说到一半他停了下来。“行吧，随便你。”

En: Why? To keep the hobos and drunks out, that's why. And the neighbours too. They put their trash there and they don't pay for the garbage company.
Zh: “为什么？为了不让流浪汉和醉鬼进来，这就是为什么。还有邻居们，他们会把垃圾扔在里面，也不付钱给垃圾处理公司。”

En: *Callous*? What are you, Kras Mazov? Almost all establishments in Revachol keep their trash locked. The Whirling-in-Rags is not special in that regard.
Zh: “*冷酷无情*？你是卡拉斯·马佐夫吗？几乎所有的餐厅都会把垃圾箱锁起来，这方面褴褛飞旋并没有什么特别的。”

En: What if I told you *I* am a hobo. Or soon to be one, rummaging through that trash. Will you keep me out too?
Zh: “如果我告诉你*我*就是个流浪汉呢，或者很快就会成为流浪汉了，翻找着那些垃圾。你也不让我进来了？”

En: Sir, I'm sorry the RCM is having financial trouble, but it's not this establishment's problem.
Zh: “先生，我很抱歉RCM正在经历财务问题，但这不是这里的问题。”

En: He looks at you with his head tilted and his brow furrowed: "Fine."
Zh: 他歪斜着脑袋皱眉看你：“好吧。”

En: I thought as much -- and are you the only party with access to the trash container?
Zh: “我也是这么想的——你是唯一能打开垃圾箱的人吗？”

En: Well, yes, us and the garbage disposal company.
Zh: “是的，没错，我们和垃圾处理公司。”

En: We need those keys.
Zh: “我们需要那些钥匙。”

En: Doesn't it seem callous to you -- guarding even your *leftovers* from the poor?
Zh: “你不觉得这太冷酷无情了吗——连你们的*剩饭*都不准穷人碰？”

En: Well, you're not getting it! He slams his hand on the counter, "*Asshole*!"
Zh: “那好吧，我是不会给你的！”他把手重重地放在柜台上。“*混蛋*！”

En: This is a thoughtful exchange, but can we move on please?
Zh: “嗯，这是一段有营养的交流，不过我们可以继续了吗？”

En: Now -- was there anything else you needed?
Zh: “现在——你还有什么需要吗？”

En: Even more reason for me not to serve you, sir. Was there anything else you needed -- other than alcohol?
Zh: “我又有更多理由拒绝为您效劳了，先生。您还有什么需要吗——除了酒之外？”

En: Oh, well, in that case, let me pour you a nice big refreshing Marinella. Do you want that out of a glass or a pineapple?
Zh: “哦好吧，既然如此，让我给你倒一杯爽口的马瑞艾拉吧。你想用杯子还是菠萝喝酒呢？”

En: Glass.
Zh: “杯子。”

En: Pineapple.
Zh: “菠萝。”

En: You do *not* want to taunt me -- not when it comes to Marinellas!
Zh: “你*不会*想要戏弄我的——尤其是马瑞艾拉相关的事情。

En: Don't be an imbecile, I'm not going to serve you a Marinella. I have work to do -- and broken things to fix -- if that was all, I'd like to return to it.
Zh: “别犯傻了，我不会给你倒马瑞艾拉的。我还有工作要做——破坏的东西要修理——如果没有其它事，我想回去工作了。”

En: Play it calm. This man needs to understand you need a drink to help the community deal with police-stuff.
Zh: 冷静。这个人需要明白你需要一杯酒才能处理警察事务，帮助这个社区。

En: Fine, then. I had something else I needed.
Zh: “那好吧，我还有一件事。”

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如呢？”

En: But I need those leftovers to survive!
Zh: “但我需要那些剩饭才能活下去！”

En: Good call. So would I. Teach a man to fish.
Zh: “好主意，我也会这么做的。授人以鱼不如授人以渔。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I'm not a fishing-teacher. He spreads his hands. "That is not why we keep our trash locked. Any place with a kitchen does it, it's not *political*, I assure you."
Zh: “我不是什么钓鱼老师。”他摊开手。“那不是我们锁上垃圾箱的原因。任何有厨房的地方都是这么做的，不带任何*政治色彩*，我向跟保证。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Everything is political.
Zh: 所有事都有政治色彩。

En: Everything is political.
Zh: “所有事都有政治色彩。“

En: Fine. Was there something you wanted?
Zh: “好吧。你还有什么需要吗？”

En: He stares at you, beady-eyed...
Zh: 他瞪大了眼睛，盯着你……

En: Kras Mazov (nom de guerre), was an economist and a historical materialist. He was a leading figure on the Graad side of the Centennial Revolution, where he headed the Eleven Day Government. Mazov is considered the father of scientific communism (Mazovian thought or Mazovianism).
Zh: 卡拉斯·马佐夫（笔名），是一名经济学家以及历史唯物主义者。他是世纪之交的那场大革命中格拉德一方的领导人，他在那里主导了十一日政府。马佐夫被认为是科学康米主义（又称为马佐夫思想或马佐夫主义）之父。

En: Yum yum, tell me more.
Zh: 嗯嗯，还有呢。

En: Maybe I *am* Kras Mazov?
Zh: “也许我*就是*卡拉斯·马佐夫呢？”

En: I'm no Kras Mazov.
Zh: “我可不是卡拉斯·马佐夫。”

En: He killed himself.
Zh: 他自杀了。

En: No one was implying you were, officer. Where were we?
Zh: “没人说你是，警官。我们说到哪儿了？”

En: What are we talking about? Was this not about the trash container?
Zh: “我们到底在说什么？不是在谈垃圾箱的事情吗？”

En: No wait... (Whisper and point to the back of your head.) "What if I *am* Kras Mazov."
Zh: “不，等等……”（小声说着，指着你的后脑勺。）“如果我*就是*卡拉斯·马佐夫呢。”

En: Yes, let's return to that.
Zh: “是的，我们回到这个话题吧。”

En: We should return to the case of your *mistaken identity* at a later time, officer. This was about the *trash container*.
Zh: “我们还是晚些再来调查你的*身份错误*吧，警官。现在在说*垃圾箱*的事。”

En: Was there something you *wanted*?
Zh: “你*想要*什么？”

En: What for, Mazov? Are you planning to nationalize my trash container?
Zh: “为什么，马佐夫？你打算把我的垃圾箱国有化吗？”

En: What do you need them for?
Zh: “你们要它们做什么？”

En: It concerns the case. The lieutenant's voice is harsh and sudden. "Please cooperate."
Zh: “它与本案有关。”警督的声音刺耳又突兀。“请配合。”

En: We should return to this theory at a later time, officer. This here was about the *trash container*.
Zh: “我们之后再讨论这个假设，警官。现在在说*垃圾箱*的事。”

En: Okay, the stupid drinks are on the house, Mr. Athlete. I still have to charge you for three nights and the broken window -- that's 100 square.
Zh: “好吧，运动员先生，那些酒水就算我请你的。我还是得收你三晚的住宿费和窗户修理费——整好100块。”

En: Oh, well, if that was *Sam Bo* style, then let me wipe that debt, sir! he snorts sarcastically, then suddenly changes tone, probably realizing it *was* pretty cool.

En: I wish Kim could have been here. He really liked these guys.
Zh: “要是金在这儿就好了，他可是铁粉。”

En: His face moves between a sympathetic smile and a sincere frown. Unsure what to do, he covers his mouth with his hand and pretends to cough.
Zh: 他的脸上一会露出同情的笑容，一会又真诚地皱着眉。不知道该怎么办的他只能用手捂住嘴，假装咳嗽。

En: After a moment, he speaks. "Maybe you could send the shoes to him? In the hospital. Maybe it'd cheer him up. Do you know where he is?"
Zh: 片刻之后，他说道。“你没准可以把这双鞋寄给他？他正在住院。说不定能鼓励一下他。你知道他在哪一间医院吗？”

En: Yeah. Totally. I totally know. Of course.
Zh: “嗯，知道，我当然知道。”

En: I... I don't know what hospital he's in. I don't know where Kim is.
Zh: “我……我不知道他在哪家医院。我不知道金在哪儿。”

En: Sounds good. Send it to Kim.
Zh: “把鞋寄给金，好像挺不错的。”

En: The lieutenant lies in recovery. Where he does so, however, is unknown.
Zh: 警督正在医院里康复。但是你却不知道他在哪。

En: Right... He looks pained on your behalf. "I'll tell you what. I'll find out if you like, and can send them on to him. What do you think?"
Zh: 他露出难过的表情。“好吧……那这样吧，如果你愿意，我可以帮你找出他在哪家医院，然后把鞋寄给他。你觉得呢？”

En: He looks pained on your behalf.
Zh: 他露出难过的表情。

En: Your liessss, sire! He seesss through them with a dissscerning eye of truth!
Zh: 您的谎言，大人！他用敏锐的真理之眼看穿了您的谎言！

En: Right... I'll tell you what. I'll find out if you like, and can send them on to him. What do you think?
Zh: “好吧……那这样吧，如果你愿意，我可以帮你找出他在哪家医院，然后把鞋寄给他。你觉得呢？”

En: Happy to help, officer. Anything else I can help you with? He puts the package back under the counter.
Zh: 他点点头，然后把包裹放回柜台底下。“乐意效劳，警官。还有什么可以帮你的吗？”

En: Jealous, Kim?
Zh: “嫉妒吗，金？”

En: It's not something I'd wear on the job. He smiles slightly.
Zh: 他微微笑了笑。“我可不会在工作的时候穿这个。”

En: You can have them if you want, Kim.
Zh: “你想要的话，就拿去吧，金。”

En: Good. Because it's finders keepers, sucker.
Zh: “很好，因为谁拿到就是谁的，傻子。”

En: A thought flickers in his eyes, and is then extinguished. He shakes his head. "Keep them, detective. You earned it."
Zh: 他的眼中闪过一丝光芒，然后便消失无踪。他摇了摇头。“你自己留着吧，警探。这是你争取来的。”

En: Fair enough. Now if we're finished discussing this, can we return to solving the murder? He struggles to keep his smile.
Zh: 他勉强地笑了笑。“很好。要是这件事说完了，我们能继续解决谋杀案了吗？”

En: Sounds good! (Take the shoes.)
Zh: “行啊！”（收起鞋子。）

En: Thank you. (Take the shoes.)
Zh: “谢谢。”（收起鞋子。）

En: Anything else I can do for you, officers? The man smiles at you both.
Zh: 那个人对你们笑了笑。“还有什么可以效劳的吗，警官们？”

En: I'm sorry, Kim. You can have them if you want.
Zh: “抱歉，金。如果你想要的话，就拿去吧。”

En: You sure? Seriously, you can have them if you want.
Zh: “你确定？说真的，如果你想要的话，就拿去吧。”

En: I am sure, he says, smiling at the plastic-wrapped shoes in their neon glory.
Zh: “确定。”他一边回答，一边对着塑料袋里霓虹色的鞋笑了笑。

En: So, about that money I owe...
Zh: “那个，我欠的那些钱……”

En: That was roughly it.
Zh: “大概就是这样了。”

En: You don't want to know.
Zh: “你不会想要知道的。”

En: The man leans his hands on the corner and sighs. His head drops between the shoulders, heavy and defeated.
Zh: 男人把手靠在柜台的角落，叹了口气。他的头沉重又沮丧地垂在两肩之间。

En: She broke the bird, you know. The great skua, not the grouse you brought. I found it on the ground with a broken wing. On the morning she left.
Zh: “她把鸟弄坏了，知道吗。这只大贼鸥可不像你带来的松鸡。离开的那天早上，我发现它掉在地上，翅膀折断了。”

En: She broke the bird, you know. The great skua. I found it on the ground with a broken wing. On the morning she left.
Zh: “她把鸟弄坏了，知道吗，这只大贼鸥。她离开的那天早上，我发现它掉在地上，翅膀折断了。”

En: I talked to Sylvie. She left because of me, not you.
Zh: “我跟塞尔维谈过了。她是因为我才离开的，不是你。”

En: Garte, I found a new bird for the Whirling. (Give him the ruffed grouse.)
Zh: “加尔特，我为飞旋旅社找了只新的鸟。”（把流苏松鸡给他。）

En: What is this thing? The man takes the stuffed bird.
Zh: “这是什么东西？”男人接过了毛绒鸟。

En: I wanted to apologize for breaking the great skua -- by bringing you this ruffed grouse.
Zh: “我想为弄坏了那只大贼鸥而道歉——所以把这只流苏松鸡带给你。”

En: It's no biggie. I just thought it would look nice on the wall. I'm that kind of cop.
Zh: “没什么。就是觉得这个挂在墙上会很好看，我就是这种警察。”

En: This is the ruffed grouse. It symbolizes the might of male courtship rituals. May it serve as a reminder -- to keep an eye on the cock-carouseliers.
Zh: “这是一只流苏松鸡，它象征着男性求爱仪式。它可以一直提醒你——注意周围的潜在对象。”

En: Wait, what? He looks up, surprised. "But what about the bird?"
Zh: “等等，什么？”他惊讶地抬起头。“那只鸟呢？”

En: She didn't break it, I did. I threw it against the wall. That's why I brought the grouse.
Zh: “不是她弄坏的，是我。我把它扔到墙上去了，所以才带了这只松鸡过来。”

En: She didn't break it, I did. I threw it against the wall.
Zh: “不是她弄坏的，是我。我把它扔到墙上去了。”

En: She said it was a just an accident.
Zh: “她说那只是个意外。”

En: Why didn't she just *tell* me that?! I don't care about stuffed birds, I care about my employees... He stumbles with words, shaking his head.
Zh: “为什么她不直接*告诉*我呢？！我才不在乎什么鸟类标本，我在乎的是我的员工……”他晃着脑袋，结结巴巴地说不出话来。

En: Alright. He calms himself. "Did she say anything else? About me, you know." He repeats: "Did she say anything about *me*?"
Zh: “好吧。”他冷静了下来。“她还说了什么吗？关于我的，你知道。”他重复着：“她提到*我*了吗？”

En: She said she was flattered -- it was just a bad timing.
Zh: “她说自己很受宠若惊——只是时机不对。”

En: She said you're nice. But also her employer. I mean, c'mon. The power vertical here... (Shake your head.)
Zh: “她说你人很好，但也是她的老板。我的意思是，拜托，你们是上下级关系……”摇摇头。

En: No, we didn't really talk about you.
Zh: “没有，我们没怎么提到你。”

En: *You* broke the skua?! His face is flushed with emotions. A rash covers his neck.
Zh: “*你*弄坏了大贼鸥？！”他的脸因为激动而涨的通红，脖子上一片潮红。

En: I see. It's clear he's disappointed. "Well, thank you for the intel anyway. Shame about the bird."
Zh: “我明白了。”他的失望显而易见。“总之，还是谢谢你告诉我这些。你真该为那只鸟的事感到羞愧。”

En: It's as if he can't decide whether to be angry or relieved that it was you -- again.
Zh: 就好像他还无法决定究竟是生气，还是——因为又是你而松了一口气。

En: Why on Earth did you have to break the skua?!
Zh: “你究竟为什么要摔坏一只贼鸥？”

En: It's a mystery.
Zh: “这是一个谜。”

En: You tell me.
Zh: “你告诉我啊。”

En: I can't believe it... I was so sure it was Sylvie. Even worse, I thought she was trying to send me a *message*. Symbol of hope and all...
Zh: “真不敢相信……我是如此确定是塞尔维做的。更糟糕的是，我以为那是她给我的*暗示*。希望的象征之类的……”

En: A tender type of hope. Something stirs in you... perhaps this is why *you* broke it?
Zh: 一种温柔的希望。你的内心有某种东西在骚动……也许这就*你*把它弄坏的原因？

En: Really? I should... I should give her a call then. The man doesn't know what to say. He wipes his brow and stares at the counter.
Zh: “真的吗？”男人不知该说些什么了。他擦了擦额头，盯着柜台。“我应该……应该给她打个电话。”

En: Thanks, I guess... He gives you a short nod. "Was there anything else you wanted, or..."
Zh: “谢了，我想……”他向你微微点头。“你还有别的需要吗，或者……”

En: Garte, I need to sing karaoke now.
Zh: “加尔特，我现在想去唱卡拉OK了。”

En: No, you *don't*. It's not happening.
Zh: “不，你*不想*。这不可能的。”

En: No, you *don't*.
Zh: “不，你*不想*。”

En: Yeah, but... (Look him in the eye) "Johnny Law is about to tear it up sad-style."
Zh: “是啊，可是……”（看着他的眼睛）“警官会用悲伤的风格引爆全场的。”

En: Why do you even have the PA system if no one's going to use it?
Zh: “既然没人能用，你当初又为什么要装广播系统呢？”

En: It's part of my quest of self-discovery. Help me.
Zh: “这是我的自我探索之旅的一部分。帮帮我。”

En: The Whirling doesn't need more *sad style*. That's one of the styles it can do without right now.
Zh: “飞旋旅社不需要再多*悲伤风格*了，这是它目前不需要的风格之一。”

En: The Whirling's not a charity or a musical therapy clinic, it's a commercial establishment. You're going to scare away the customers.
Zh: “飞旋旅社不是慈善机构，也不是音乐疗养所，而是一家商业机构。你会把顾客吓跑的。”

En: This is my way of apologizing for the trouble I've caused. Please let me say I'm sorry.
Zh: “这是我为自己造成的不便而道歉的方式，请让我说声抱歉吧。”

En: By causing more trouble? I think we're good.
Zh: “通过制造更多的不便？我想还是算了吧。”

En: It's not a prop. It's for your clients. I know it's used.
Zh: “它不是道具。这是为你的客人准备的。我知道它是用过的。”

En: When was the last time you had a band play here?
Zh: “你们最后一次有乐队在这里演出是什么时候？”

En: It's not for bands, it's for clients. *Some* clients, okay? Not you.
Zh: “这不是为乐队准备的，是为了客人。*某些*客人，明白吗？不是你。”

En: I'm a real client, I've paid my bills and I have the right to use the karaoke machine.
Zh: “我就是真正的客人。我已经付清了账单，我有权使用卡拉OK机。”

En: I'm a client. Why can't I use it?
Zh: “我就是客人，为什么不能用它？”

En: You're a *bad* client. You haven't paid your bills yet.
Zh: “你是个*糟糕的*客人，你还没付钱呢。”

En: Okay, yes, it's for *some* clients, he admits reluctantly.
Zh: “好吧，没错，这是为了*某些*客人准备的。”他不情愿地承认了。

En: Hah! Well! He comes up with a counterargument. "We don't have any tapes. They all got stolen."
Zh: “哈！好吧！”他强词夺理起来。“我们没有磁带。全被偷走了。”

En: So -- after I've paid my bills, can I use the karaoke carousel?
Zh: “那么——我付钱之后，就能用点唱机了吗？”

En: Technically -- okay, yes. But we don't have any tapes. They all got stolen.
Zh: “理论上来说——好吧，是的。但我们没有任何磁带了，它们被人偷了。”

En: You need to approach this situation logically. Ask him why he has the PA system installed, if you can't use it.
Zh: 你需要理性地处理这一状况。问问他为什么装了广播又不让你用。

En: He's lying. But whatever, you don't really *need* his lame tracks.
Zh: 他在说谎。但无论如何，您其实真的不*需要*他那些蹩脚的音乐。

En: It's all right, I have my own song with me. (Give him the tape for "Smallest Church in Saint-Saëns".)
Zh: “没关系，我带着自己的歌呢。”（把‘圣桑小教堂’的磁带交给他。）

En: The man in the vest and the violet shirt stares at the tape you've just given him. He begins to frown. Hard.
Zh: 身穿背心和紫罗兰衬衣的男人盯着你刚刚递给他的磁带，深深地皱起了眉头。

En: Fine. Fine. Climb on that stage and do your thing, just get out of my hair. He shakes the tape at you. "I'll plug it in for you. Damn this karaoke machine..."
Zh: “好吧。好吧。上台去做你该做的事吧，别再烦我了。”他朝你挥舞着磁带。“我会帮你接上电源的。该死的卡拉OK机……”

En: Fine. Fine. *After* you've paid your bills you can climb that stage and do whatever the hell you need to do. *After*. He shakes the tape at you. "Damn this karaoke machine..."
Zh: “好吧，好吧。你付清账单*之后*，随便你想爬上台干什么都行。是*之后，”他朝你挥舞着磁带。“该死的卡拉OK机……”

En: Oh yeah. Time to do the damage.
Zh: 噢耶。是时候搞点破坏了。

En: He's lying... But there's no way to call him out on it.
Zh: 他在说谎……但也没办法揭穿他。

En: Oh no.... I don't have my tape with me either.
Zh: “哦不……我身上也没有磁带。”

En: Oh, that's so sad to hear, he says with a barely disguised grin. "I'd *totally* let you sing karaoke, if you only had a tape."
Zh: “哦，那可真遗憾。”他几乎不加掩饰地笑着说。“如果你有磁带的话，我*绝对*会让你去唱歌的。”

En: Wait, why don't you have a song?! Way to fuck it up! Better to try this line of questioning again after you've found the tape.
Zh: 等等，你为什么不唱首歌呢？！在你找到磁带之后，最好再试着问一次这个问题。

En: Real mature, man. What exactly were you trying to accomplish? He crosses his arms. "You do understand you still owe me money, right?"
Zh: “真成熟，老兄。你究竟有什么目的？”他抱起双臂。“你知道自己还欠我钱吗？”

En: That doesn't seem fair. You demonstrated some serious skills there, slipping away Sam Bo-style.
Zh: 这似乎有些不公平。你展现了一些厉害的技巧，萨拳式退场。

En: By the way, Sam Bo or Samaran boxing is an eloquently violent set of one-on-one fighting moves originating from the Samaran isola. Sam Bo used to incorporate a wide array of martial disciplines from archery to mounted combat, but mainly means aesthetically pleasing single combat nowadays. *Sam Bo style* implies stealth, cleverness, and cool.
Zh: 顺带一提，萨拳或者萨马拉拳击是起源于萨马拉洲的一种极富表现力的一对一格斗运动。从射箭到坐骑格斗，萨搏曾是武术训练集大成者。但如今主要是一种赏心悦目的单人格斗表演。*萨拳式*隐喻着隐秘、聪明和冷静。

En: Don't I get something for effort?
Zh: “我的努力没有结果吗？”

En: I'm sorry, I know it was irresponsible of me to run. You have to understand -- I was desperate.
Zh: “抱歉，我知道逃走是不负责任的表现。但你要明白——我已经绝望了。”

En: You didn't appreciate my moves?
Zh: “你不喜欢我的招式嘛？”

En: You know what? The stupid drinks you've had are on the house. You know why? Because I *know* you can't pay for them. *Not* because you ran away.
Zh: “知道吗？你喝的那些该死的酒我请了。知道为什么吗？因为我*知道*你付不起，并*不是*因为你逃走了。”

En: Don't thank me yet. You still owe me 100 reál. If you don't have it by tonight, I can't let you up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “先别谢我，你还欠我100雷亚尔呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: So we're cool?
Zh: “那我们是两清了？”

En: Fine, I'll take it off the bill! Sleep in a post-apocalyptic hell-hole if you want to. Just know I won't give you another room.
Zh: “行吧，我把它从账单上划掉！你想睡在末日的狗窝里就去吧！但记住，我不会再给你另外的房间了。”

En: Don't thank me yet. You still owe me 60 reál for three nights stay. If you don't have it by tonight, I can't let you up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “先别谢我。你还欠我60雷亚尔的房费呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: Not *entirely* cool. You still owe me 100 reál. If you don't have it by tonight, I can't let you back up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “也不是*彻底地*两清，你还欠我100雷亚尔呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: Not *entirely* cool. You still owe me 60 reál for three nights stay. If you don't have it by tonight, I can't let you back up there... He points upstairs, toward your room.
Zh: “也不是*彻底地*两清，你还欠我60雷亚尔的房费呢。如果你今晚还拿不出钱来，我是不会让你上去的……”他指着楼上，你房间的位置。

En: Thank you for your cooperation.
Zh: “感谢你的配合。”

En: That window was just an unfortunate casualty of me practising my moves. I won't pay for it.
Zh: “那扇窗户只是我在练习招式时不幸的牺牲品，我是不会付钱的。”

En: I was wondering if we could come to some sort of arrangement for tonight.
Zh: “我在想我们今晚能否达成某种协议。”

En: Does that arrangement include... you paying me *what we already agreed* you owe me?
Zh: “那份协议包括……你要付我们*已经达成共识*的那部分钱了吗？”

En: I understand your predicament as the manager. However... He adjusts his glasses.
Zh: 他调整了一下眼镜。“我理解你作为管理人的困境，不过……”

En: ...I feel I must remind you that we are here to conduct an important investigation that also affects *your* business.
Zh: “……我感觉有必要提醒你，我们正在这里进行的重要调查同样也会影响你的*生意*。”

En: Forgive me for saying this, but your colleague seems more committed to drinking and... He stops mid-sentence.
Zh: “请原谅我这么说，但你的同事似乎更喜欢喝酒……”他话说到一半停了下来。

En: I mean no offence. It's really nothing personal. I just *have* to protect the interests of this establishment.
Zh: “无意冒犯，我真的不是在针对谁。只是我*必须*维护这里的利益。”

En: He shrinks back a bit under the lieutenant's severe gaze.
Zh: 在警督严厉的注视下，他往后退了一些。

En: I will pay you back tomorrow. I'm an honest cop.
Zh: “我明天就能还你钱，我是个诚实的警察。”

En: C'mon man. After everything I've been through...
Zh: “拜托，在我遭了那么多罪之后……”

En: Hold on, I still have my key, you know.
Zh: “等等，你要知道钥匙还在我身上呢。”

En: This conversation isn't going anywhere, is it?
Zh: “这次谈话是不会有结果的，是不是？”

En: I'll find a way in. I'll *break* the door down.
Zh: “我会想办法进去的，我要把门*砸开*。”

En: All the locks have an electronic component. They have to be unlocked down here -- with a *master* key before your *guest* key will open the lock.
Zh: “所有的锁里都有个电子元件，必须在这里用*万能钥匙*解锁之后——才能用*客用钥匙*打开。”

En: Wonderful. *Try* to do that. I'll have to call you an ambulance.
Zh: “太棒了。去*试一试*吧，我会为你叫辆救护車的。”

En: Not until you bring me the money.
Zh: “除非你把钱带来。”

En: You know I still have my key. Your cheap-ass lock seems to be stuck, but I'll get it open.
Zh: “你要知道钥匙还在我身上。你这把廉价的锁好像卡住了，但我仍然可以把它打开。”

En: Yeah, good luck with that. He taps his foot against a metal box installed in the back of the counter.
Zh: “好啊，祝你好运。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。

En: What about what *I* have been through, huh? You're not the goddamn *protagonist* of this story -- I am. This is *my* hostel-cafeteria and I need my money.
Zh: “那*我*遭的罪呢，嗯？你不是这个故事的*主演*——我才是。这里是我的旅社，而我需要我的钱。”

En: As a police officer, you must understand that I cannot take you at your word -- without *evidence*.
Zh: “身为警察，你肯定明白我无法相信你的话——在没有*证据*的情况下。”

En: He pauses, realizing the contradiction in his statement. "Evidence being money. You can't stay here without *money*."
Zh: 他停顿了一下，意识到自己发言中的自相矛盾。“证据指的是钱，没有*钱*你就不能待在这里。”

En: Okay... He turns to you with a heavy sigh. "I *might* have something in my motor carriage we can use. When you're done here."
Zh: “好吧……”他重重地叹了一口气，转向你。“我的車里*可能*会有能用得上的东西，等你把这里的事办完。”

En: Great, thank you officer, that's *all* I wanted -- payment for services rendered. If you continue to stay here, I just ask that you please pay your nightly bill in advance, starting tomorrow.
Zh: “很好，谢谢你警官，这*正是*我想要的——我提供服务，收到报酬。如果你想继续住在这里，我只要求你从明天开始提前支付住宿费用。”

En: Kim is about to say something. Let him.
Zh: 金正打算说些什么，让他说。

En: He's not sorry about his behaviour for your sake. Now that you have money, he really wants to make sure you're not angry with him.
Zh: 因为你的缘故，他对自己的行为并不感到抱歉。现在你有钱了，他是真心想确保你不会生他的气。

En: I'll take a room here too. He opens his wallet.
Zh: 他打开了钱包。“我也要开一间房。”

En: Of course. He takes the money and hands him a keyring. "Always happy to have officers from the RCM as guests. Anything else I can do for you?"
Zh: “当然。”他拿了钱，递给他一个钥匙圈。“永远欢迎RCM警官到来做客，我还能为你做什么吗？”

En: They're probably getting drunk. Or *protesting* something somewhere. Or laying low after the, you know, lynching.
Zh: “他们大概是喝醉了，或者是去*抗议*什么了。要么就是在那个，私刑之后低调做人了。”

En: Whatever he may feel about you, he can't miss the opportunity to throw you a look of what he assumes is shared understanding. Go with it.
Zh: 无论他对你是何种印象，他都不会错过这个让你了解他认为是共识的东西的机会。见机行事。

En: He rolls his eyes. "What *thing*?"
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。“什么*事*？”

En: I saw a sign that said the mess hall is reserved for the Union.
Zh: “我看到一个告示牌上写着包厢被工会预定了。”

En: Garte, I saw a sign that said I couldn't go into the kitchen. Why can't I go into the kitchen?
Zh: “加尔特，我看到一个告示牌上写着我不能进入厨房。为什么我不能进厨房？”

En: There's a mysterious *blue steel door* in the back of the kitchen.
Zh: “厨房后面有一扇神秘的*蓝色铁门*。”

En: Yes -- not the whole damn Union, thank god. Just the nastiest and *loudest* faction. He tosses his head in disdain.
Zh: “没错——幸好不是整个工会，只有最下流*最吵闹*的那帮人。”他轻蔑地摇了摇头。

En: He hates the Union, but grudgingly recognizes its power over him -- so he's directing his frustration at you instead. Retaliate!
Zh: 他讨厌工会，但勉强认可工会对他的控制——所以他把自己的挫败感转移到了你身上，报复！

En: It's a shame you've gotta suck up to the Union to stay afloat.
Zh: “你得巴结工会才能维持下去，太悲哀了。”

En: There's something else I want to ask about.
Zh: “我还有一件事想问。”

En: I don't. I'm simply providing a service -- or, really, facilitating the offering of services to *paying* customers, and... He runs out of steam. "It doesn't matter. I don't have to explain myself to you."
Zh: “我没有。我只是单纯地提供服务——或者，老实说，是为*付费*的顾客提供便利的服务，以及……”他叹了一口气。“无所谓了，我没必要跟你解释那么多。”

En: (Nod sagely.) "Yes, I think I see..."
Zh: （审慎地点头。）“是的，我想我明白了……”

En: Why's that?
Zh: “那是为什么呢？”

En: They probably fucking killed that guy or something, that's why.
Zh: “他们可能把那家伙杀了吧，这就是为什么。”

En: The Union guys think they're untouchable. They probably fucking killed that guy or something... and now think they can hide out till it all blows over and it's fair weather again in Martinaise.
Zh: “工会的人觉得自己无人敢惹，他们很可能把那家伙杀了……认为他们可以躲过这阵风头，等马丁内斯恢复平静了再出来。”

En: What are you, a cook now? That's none of your business.
Zh: “你现在又变成厨师了吗？那跟你没有关系。”

En: I have a search warrant.
Zh: “我有搜查令。”

En: But I'm really hungry.
Zh: “但我真的好饿。”

En: And want me to make an exception for you? You're not even a hostel guest till you pay your outstanding bills... He changes his mind.
Zh: “然后想让我为你破例一次吗？在你付清账单之前，你甚至都不是旅社的客人……”他改变了主意。

En: No, you don't.
Zh: “不，你没有。”

En: You better believe it, fucker.
Zh: “你最好相信，混蛋。”

En: How did you know?
Zh: “你是怎么知道的？”

En: If you had a warrant, you'd be shoving it in my face right now. He crosses his arms.
Zh: “如果你真有搜查令，现在就会把它举到我面前了。”他抱起双臂。

En: Besides, the RCM doesn't *do* search warrants. I know the law. You'll just have to wait for the kitchen to open -- if you *have* to get in there. Which you don't.
Zh: “另外，RCM是不*负责*搜查令的。我懂法律。等着厨房开门吧——如果你*非要*进去的话，当然你不需要。

En: I have to warn you -- I may have discovered that the Whirling is part of the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: “我得警告你——我可能已经发现飞旋旅社是诅咒商业区的一部分了。”

En: Oh, yes, that door, sure. There's nothing *mysterious* about it. It's just a door. He shrugs.
Zh: “哦，是的，那扇门，当然。那没有什么*神秘*的，只是一扇门而已。”他耸耸肩。

En: Do you know what's behind it? Do you have...
Zh: “你知道门后面有什么吗？你有……”

En: No, I don't have a key -- I don't know how to get there. And I don't *care* either. It's not like I've been *wondering* about it for ten years. It's just the Frittte warehouse probably.
Zh: “不，我没有钥匙——也不知道怎么进去，而且我也不*在乎*。我又不是*好奇*这件事十多年了。大概只是弗利多的仓库吧。”

En: Or some boring storage space with a bunch of old junk... and dust. Junk and dust. He runs his finger across the counter to check for dirt.
Zh: “或者只是存放着一堆旧杂物……和灰尘的地方吧。杂物和灰尘。”他用手指在柜台上摸了摸，检查着脏东西。

En: He's attempting to maintain an air of indifference. It's absolutely not convincing.
Zh: 他试图维持着一副漠不关心的态度，但这绝对说服不了任何人。

En: Thinking of turning this place back into a pinball arcade?
Zh: “想把这里变回弹球游乐室吗？”

En: If you're thinking of selling those pinball machines, I want a fat cut. C-Suite shit. (Beat your chest.) "I'm a disruptor."
Zh: “如果你想把那些弹球机卖掉，我也想分一杯羹。最高管理层之类的。”（拍拍你的胸膛。）“我是个破坏分子。”

En: He scowls. "No. But we could... *diversify* the entertainment options. Seeing as you've opened the door back there... The machine we have in the corner now is broken."
Zh: 他皱着眉头。“不。但我们可以……*丰富*娱乐的选项。既然你已经打开了后面的门……我们放在角落的那台机器坏掉了。”

En: He rolls his eyes. "Those machines are Whirling property. But, if it makes you *feel* any better, I'm *not* planning to sell them."
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。“那些机器是飞旋旅社的财产。但如果这样能让你*感觉*好一些的话，我并*没有*打算卖掉它们。”

En: I think you'd like to know what's back there.
Zh: “我想你会想知道后面有什么的。”

En: Fine, okay. A little. He shrugs. "But my job doesn't leave me time for wondering about *one* locked door in *one* of the cafeterias I manage..."
Zh: “行吧，有一点。”他耸耸肩。“但我的工作并没有给我时间去好奇我管理的*一家*餐厅里的*一扇*上锁的门……”

En: So I haven't opened it. I *have* cleaned the whole place a hundred times over, though -- after the *animals*. And I haven't found a key. So good luck with that.
Zh: “我还没有打开它。不过，我*已经*把整个地方打扫过一百遍了——在某些*动物*捣乱之后。我也没找到钥匙。所以祝你好运了。”

En: What? He looks mildly startled. "Why would you say that? We're at a *completely* different address from that whole thing."
Zh: “什么？”他看起来有点吃惊。“你怎么会这么说？我们和那里根本不在一个地方。”

En: The Whirling is adjacent to them. And there's also the pinball workshop. The Whirling hasn't always been a hostel...
Zh: “飞旋旅社就在它们旁边。那里也有一个弹球工坊。褴褛飞旋也不是一直都是旅社……”

En: Relax, Mr. Garte. I'm sure there is no *Doom*. He simply wants to share his discoveries with you.
Zh: “冷静，加尔特先生。我相信根本没有什么*诅咒*。他只是想把自己的发现与你分享而已。”

En: It's cold out. I'll freeze to death because I'm losing the stupid money-game.
Zh: “外面太冷了。我会被冻死的，就因为我输了这场愚蠢的金钱游戏。”

En: Game? *Everything* is just a game to you, isn't it?
Zh: “游戏？*一切*对你来说都只是游戏，不是吗？”

En: How much do I owe you again?
Zh: “我还欠你多少钱？”

En: A lot. A lot-lot. For the room, drinks, and broken window -- 130 reál.
Zh: “很多，很多很多。住宿、酒水和一扇破碎的窗户——一共130雷亚尔。”

En: A lot. For the room and broken window -- 100 reál.
Zh: “很多。住宿和一扇破碎的窗户——一共100雷亚尔。”

En: A lot. For the drinks and broken window -- 70 reál.
Zh: “很多。酒水和一扇破碎的窗户——一共70雷亚尔。”

En: For the room -- 60 reál.
Zh: “住宿费用——一共60雷亚尔。”

En: For the drinks -- 30 reál.
Zh: “酒水费用——一共30雷亚尔。”

En: Great, thank you officer, that's *all* I wanted, payment for services rendered.
Zh: “太好了，谢谢你警官，这就是我所需的*全部*服务酬劳。”

En: It's good you paid before 21.00, or your door would have been locked electronically. He taps his foot against a metal box installed in the back of the bar counter.
Zh: “幸好你是在21:00前付的钱，否则你的房门就会被电子门锁锁上了。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。

En: I'll unlock the electronic lock to your room. He taps his foot against a metal box installed in the back of the bar counter. "All the doors lock automatically at 21.00."
Zh: “我会打开你房间的电子门锁。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。“所有房间都会在21:00时自动上锁。”

En: He's not sorry about his behaviour for your sake. Now that you have money, he really wants to make sure you're not angry with him.
Zh: 因为你的缘故，他对自己的行为并不感到抱歉。现在你有钱了，他是真心想确保你不会生他的气。

En: He takes out his key chain and fiddles with the mechanism behind the counter. "The electronic lock to your room has been disabled till 21.00 tomorrow."
Zh: 他掏出钥匙串，拨弄着柜台后面的机械装置。“你房间的电子门锁已经取消，直到明天21:00重新上锁。”

En: He takes out his key chain and fiddles with the mechanism behind the counter. "The electronic lock to your room will remain disabled till 21.00 tomorrow."
Zh: 他掏出钥匙串，拨弄着柜台后面的机械装置。“你房间的电子门锁在明天21:00前会一直禁用。”

En: Anything else I can do for you?
Zh: “我还能为你做什么吗？”

En: Good luck trying to use it after 21.00. He taps his foot against a metal box installed in the back of the counter.
Zh: “祝你在21:00后试用成功。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。

En: Good luck trying to use it. He taps his foot against a metal box installed in the back of the counter.
Zh: “祝你试用成功。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。

En: Let it go. You're above gratuitous baiting.
Zh: 随他去吧，你是不会在这无理取闹的。

En: We should find out who this loud faction is, occupying the booth. Loudness means talkative -- and we need info. The lieutenant gives you a meaningful nod.
Zh: 警督朝你意味深长地点点头。“我们得找出那帮吵吵嚷嚷的，霸占了卡座的家伙是谁。吵闹意味着健谈——而我们正好需要讯息。”

En: Hmh. The lieutenant thinks. "I have a feeling we'll make their acquaintance sooner or later."
Zh: “嗯。”警督思索着。“我有预感我们迟早会认识他们。”

En: You do realize you can just go into the kitchen at 13:00? No need for superstar charm or Sam Bo style antics here.
Zh: 你有没有意识到自己可以在13:00时进入厨房？不需要什么超级明星的魅力，或者是滑稽的萨拳风格。

En: But there may be something pertinent to the investigation in the kitchen.
Zh: “但厨房里可能会有与调查相关的东西。”

En: He rolls his eyes. "He wasn't pan-fried. He was *lynched*. What could the kitchen *possibly* have to do with..." He changes his mind.
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。“他又不是被煎熟的，他被*私刑处置*了。厨房怎么*可能*跟这有关系……”他改变了主意。

En: Sam Bo is an acronym for Samaran boxing. Graceful martial arts stuff. *Sam Bo style* implies stealth, cleverness, and cool. Just letting you know.
Zh: 萨拳是萨马拉拳击的简称。一项优雅的武术运动。*萨拳式*意味着隐秘、聪明与冷静。只是想让你了解一下。

En: Here it is. Sorry for the trouble.
Zh: “在这，抱歉给你添麻烦了。”

En: (Slam the bills down on the counter.) "I hope you choke on it."
Zh: （把钞票甩在柜台上。）“希望你被它噎死。”

En: Here it is. Sorry for the trouble.
Zh: “在这，抱歉给你添麻烦了。”

En: (Slam the bills down on the counter.) "I hope you choke on it."
Zh: （把钞票甩在柜台上。）“希望你被它噎死。”

En: Here it is. Sorry for the trouble.
Zh: “给你，抱歉给你添麻烦了。”

En: (Slam the bills down on the counter.)
Zh: （把钞票甩在柜台上。）

En: Here it is. Sorry for the trouble.
Zh: “给你，抱歉给你添麻烦了。”

En: (Slam the bills down on the counter.) "I hope you choke on it."
Zh: （把钞票甩在柜台上。）“希望你被它噎死。”

En: Here it is. Sorry for the trouble.
Zh: “给你，抱歉给你添麻烦了。”

En: (Slam the bills down on the counter.) "I hope you choke on it."
Zh: （把钞票甩在柜台上。）“希望你被它噎死。”

En: That fucking smug asshole, he knows you've been locked out of your room. He's probably laughing at you now on the inside.
Zh: 那个自以为是的混蛋，他知道你被锁在房间外面了。他说不定正在心里嘲笑你呢。

En: Are you laughing at me?
Zh: “你在嘲笑我吗？”

En: ...what?
Good. He's getting scared now. Pretending he doesn't know what you're talking about. Don't let him get away with it.
Zh: 很好，他现在开始害怕了。假装不知道他在说什么，别让他得逞。

En: You smarmy asshole, you're not going to get away with this. No one laughs at The Law!
Zh: “你这个马屁精，你逃不掉的。没有人能嘲笑法律！”

En: Do I *look* like I'm laughing? Do you see a *smile* on my face? I know what this is about. This is about the electronic lock not letting you in your room -- and it's anything but *funny* to me.
Zh: “我*看着*像在笑吗？你在我脸上看到*笑意*了吗？我知道这是怎么回事了。因为电子门锁把你关在了房间外——但这对我来说一点都不*有趣*。”

En: Look at that cum-stain son of a bitch. He's just begging to be laid out on the floor of this *establishment*.
Zh: 看看那个饥渴的混蛋吧，渴求着有人能把他扑倒在这个*餐厅*的地板上。

En: You know why? Because *unpaid bills* are not funny, when you have to run *four* service establishments. He folds his arms in a huff. "Now. We can talk about a *solution* to this problem."
Zh: “你知道为什么吗？因为当你必须要管理*四家*服务机构时，*未付账单*一点都不有趣。”他气呼呼地抱着双臂。“现在我们可以谈谈这个问题的*解决方案*了。”

En: He trembled just a little as he assumed this position. You've made him nervous, though he doesn't want to show it.
Zh: 他摆出这个姿势时微微颤抖了一下。虽然他不想表现出来，但你让他紧张了。

En: Relax. Knocking out the cafeteria manager is not going to solve your lodging problem, not in the long run.
Zh: 冷静。从长远来看，打倒餐厅经理并不能解决你的住宿问题。

En: Great, perfect, I hope you enjoy your freezing cold room with the window you broke *yourself*.
Zh: “太棒了，简直完美，希望你会喜欢那间被*你自己*打破玻璃的冰冷房间。”

En: Great, perfect, I hope you enjoy your freezing cold room with the window you broke *yourself*.
Zh: “太棒了，简直完美，希望你会喜欢那间被*你自己*打破玻璃的冰冷房间。”

En: Please pay for each night in advance starting tomorrow. Twenty reál per night.
Zh: “从明天开始，*请*预付每晚的房费。每晚20雷亚尔。”

En: I'll unlock the electronic lock to your room. He taps his foot against a metal box at the bar counter. "All the doors lock automatically at 21.00."
Zh: “我会打开你房间的电子门锁。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。“所有房间都会在21:00时自动上锁。”

En: I'll take a room here too. He opens his wallet.
Zh: 他打开了钱包。“我也要开一间房。”

En: Anything *else* I can do for you, officer?
Zh: “还有*其它*需要吗，警官？”

En: By the way, if you hadn't paid before 21.00, your door would have been locked electronically. He taps his foot against a metal box installed in the back of the bar counter.
Zh: “顺便一提，如果你没能在21:00前付款，你的门就会自动上锁。”他用脚踢着一个装在柜台后面的金属盒子。

En: Always happy to have officers from the RCM as guests. He takes the money and hands him a keyring. "Anything else I can do for you?" He looks at the lieutenant, avoiding your gaze.
Zh: 他拿了警督的钱并递给他一个钥匙圈。“永远欢迎RCM警官到来做客，我还能为你做什么吗？”他看着金，避开了你的视线。

En: He takes out his key chain and fiddles with the mechanism behind the counter. "The electronic lock to your room has been disabled till 21.00 tomorrow."
Zh: 他掏出钥匙串，拨弄着柜台后面的机械装置。“你房间的电子门锁已经取消，明天21:00重新上锁。”

En: He takes out his key chain and fiddles with the mechanism behind the counter. "The electronic lock to your room will remain disabled till 21.00 tomorrow."
Zh: 他掏出钥匙串，拨弄着柜台后面的机械装置。“你房间的电子门锁在明天21:00前会一直禁用。”

En: Starting tomorrow, *please* pay for each night in advance. Twenty reál per night. If you don't have the money it's over for you, got it? You've got nowhere else to stay.
Zh: “从明天开始，*请*提前预付每晚的房费。每晚20雷亚尔。如果没钱，那你就完蛋了，明白吗？你会无处可去。”

En: You've really worn down his patience. Even paying him didn't help!
Zh: 你真的耗尽了他的耐心，甚至连付钱都没用了。

En: You've really worn down his patience. Even paying him didn't help!
Zh: 你真的耗尽了他的耐心，甚至连付钱都没用了。

En: He's done a fine job too. Though he's spoken of the place dismissively before, the hostel is actually very important to him.
Zh: 他做的也不错。虽然他以前提起这个地方时总是语带不屑，但旅社对他来说其实非常重要。

En: I've been working here for a *long* time, and that intercom has *never* been used by the Whirling.
Zh: “我在这里工作了*很长*时间，但是飞旋旅社*从来没有*使用过那个对讲机。”

En: The Whirling was once the East Delta Pinball Arcade, before it failed. It's only a matter of time before the Whirling fails too!
Zh: “飞旋旅社曾经是东德尔塔的弹球游乐室，但经营失败了。飞旋旅社的失败也只是时间问题了！”

En: The Whirling was once the East Delta Pinball Arcade, which failed. Though perhaps the Whirling will *escape* the curse...
Zh: “飞旋旅社曾经是东德尔塔的弹球游乐室，但经营失败了。不过飞旋旅社或许有可能*逃脱*诅咒……”

En: The Whirling is listed on the intercom outside. As one of the businesses in Building B. You should get your wiring fixed -- I tried to call and couldn't reach you.
Zh: “外面的对讲机上有飞旋旅社的名字。是B栋的商家之一。你应该把电话线路修好——我试着给你打过电话，但是没打通。“

En: The Whirling is listed on the intercom outside. As one of the businesses in Building B -- of *The Doomed Commercial Area*.
Zh: “外面的对讲机上有飞旋旅社的名字。B栋的一个商家——属于*诅咒商业区*。”

En: Obviously it hasn't. He makes a sweeping gesture with his arm. "Look at the pre-revolutionary tile work. This is an old, proud building. So what if there was a little pinball hall here before?"
Zh: “当然没有。”他用胳膊做了一个清扫的姿势。“你看外面革命前风格的瓷砖，这是座有些年头的建筑了。万一这里以前就有一个弹球室呢？”

En: Does this *look* like part of a *Doomed* Commercial Area? He makes a sweeping gesture. "This pre-revolutionary tile work? These high ceilings? The nice rooms? Well, *most* of the rooms..."
Zh: “这里*看着*像是*诅咒商业区*的一部分吗？”他挥了挥手。“这些革命前风格的瓷砖？这高耸的天花板？这些漂亮的房间？好吧，*大部分*房间……”

En: Hold on -- not once? Isn't that *already* a sign of decline?
Zh: “等等——一次都没有？这难道不*就是*衰败的证明吗？”

En: It's a *sign* of the Whirling-in-Rags not being part of the Doomed Commercial Area. If anything -- we're *revitalizing* this neighbourhood.
Zh: “这是褴褛飞旋不属于诅咒商业区的*证明*。非要说的话——我们正在*振兴*这个街区。”

En: You really care about the Whirling, huh.
Zh: “你真的很在意飞旋旅社，嗯。”

En: You must really care about this place, since you've worked here for so long.
Zh: “既然你在这里工作了这么久，一定很在乎这个地方。”

En: Yeah. He sighs. "It's *slowly* growing on me again. It's beautiful, in its own way -- especially for this neighbourhood. I've been trying to keep it that way..."
Zh: “是啊。”他叹了口气。“我*慢慢地*喜欢上这里了。它有自己独特的美丽之处——尤其是在这个街区。我想一直保持这种状态…….”

En: Who *owns* this place?
Zh: “这里的*主人*是谁？”

En: Some real estate management company. They never come around here, just collect money from afar. Honestly, I think *some* money laundering might be involved.
Zh: “某个房地产管理公司吧。他们从没来过这儿，只负责远程收钱。老实说，我感觉这其中可能涉及*一些*洗钱活动。”

En: Well -- good luck to you with this place then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧——祝你生意兴隆。”（总结。）

En: Whatever. He attempts a scowl, but he's too absorbed in his thoughts to finish it. "Was there anything else? Because I need to *think* -- about how I'm gonna get those pinballs in here..."
Zh: “随便吧。”他试图板起脸来，但他过于沉浸在自己的想法之中，所以没能完成。“还有别的事吗？我得*想想*——怎么把那些弹球机弄进来……”

En: Even if it is part of the damn Doomed Commercial Area...
Zh: “即使它是诅咒商业区的一部分……”

En: Aha! So you finally admit it?
Zh: “啊哈！你终于承认了？”

En: You shouldn't be so worried about that label, you know.
Zh: “你不用如此担心那个标签的，知道吗？”

En: Yes, your police skills have delivered. I'm sure you'll get a commendation. Maybe even a promotion.
Zh: “是啊，你的警察技巧终于派上用场了。我相信你会得到嘉奖，甚至有可能升职的。”

En: *I* don't place much stock in the curse and so on, but the label frightens the clientèle. Who wants to stay at a *doomed* hostel? Everything's doomed enough without that...
Zh: “*我*不太相信诅咒之类的东西，但这个标签会吓跑顾客。谁想住在一个被诅咒的旅社里呢？即使没有这诅咒一切就已经够丧了……”

En: What about those other cafeterias you manage?
Zh: “那你管理的其它餐厅呢？”

En: What about them? He shrugs. "One is a basement dive frequented by chain-smoking communists. I can't *tell* you how sick I am of Kras Mazov and Ignus Nilsen and all those old ghosts..."
Zh: “怎么了？”他耸耸肩。“其中一家是经常有康米主义吸烟者出没的地下酒吧。我都无法*告诉*你我有多讨厌卡拉斯·马佐夫、伊格纳斯·尼尔森那群老不死的……”

En: The *other* is a kebab cart. It's very *successful* in its way, but... it's nothing like the Whirling.
Zh: “*另外*有一辆烤肉餐車。它在某种程度上非常*成功*，可是……它一点也不像飞旋旅社。”

En: He's hesitating, not sure if he should share this information with you. Encourage him.
Zh: 他在犹豫，不确定是否要跟你分享这个信息。鼓励他。

En: And the others?
Zh: “其他的呢？”

En: He doesn't have to tell you anything he doesn't want to.
Zh: 他不必告诉你任何他不想说的东西。

En: Anyway, I thought we were talking about the Whirling.
Zh: “总之，我以为我们就在说飞旋旅社的事呢。”

En: Garte, I saw another *thing* at the Whirling...
Zh: “加尔特，我在飞旋旅社还看到了另一件*事*……”

En: I've seen something here at the Whirling, Garte. A thing I need to talk about.
Zh: “我在飞旋旅社看到了一件事，加尔特。一件我们必须要谈谈的事。”

En: There's a peephole in the wall.
Zh: “墙上有个窥视孔。”

En: He startles. "What wall?"
Zh: 他诧异道。“什么墙？”

En: Upstairs in the secret back room -- right next to Klaasje's bedroom. I found it when I found the pinball machines.
Zh: “楼上的密室里——就在卡拉洁的卧室旁边。我发现弹球机时一并看到的。”

En: Upstairs in the secret back room -- right next to Klaasje's bedroom. I think it's used for *looking in* on guests.
Zh: “楼上的密室里——就在卡拉洁的卧室旁边。我觉得它是用来*看望*客人们的。”

En: I'll have it fixed at once. Thank you for letting me know. I assure you -- the Whirling does *not* abide spying on its guests.
Zh: “我马上就去修理。谢谢你告诉我，我保证——飞旋旅社*绝不*容忍偷窥客人的事情发生。”

En: Absolutely not. He breathes in and out. "Fuck you for even implying it. It wasn't me, it wasn't my staff. The establishment will look at it and ascertain what it was."
Zh: “绝对不可能。”他呼吸了一口气。“你竟然还敢有所暗示。那不是我，也不会是我的员工。我们会调查清楚的。”

En: The colour has drained from his face.
Zh: 他脸上的血色消失了。

En: Are you sure *you* haven't been spying on your guests?
Zh: “你确定*自己*没有偷窥过客人吗？”

En: Couldn't you *keep* the hole there? What if there are some hotties staying in that room... (Wink.)
Zh: “你不能把那个洞*留下*吗？万一有个辣妹住在那里呢……”（眨眼。）

En: Alright -- you've been notified. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧——我已经通知你了。（总结。）

En: Well, he's definitely not lying -- he wouldn't endanger this business like that.
Zh: 好吧，他绝对没有说谎——他不会那样危及自己的生意的。

En: Absolutely not. That would be idiotic. I have no idea why you're even saying this right now...
Zh: “绝对不可能。那也太蠢了，我甚至不知道你为什么会这么说……”

En: Thank you, I'll patch it up personally. Was there something else about the establishment? I hope not...
Zh: “谢谢你，我会亲自把它修补好的。这里还有什么问题吗？希望没有了……”

En: Maybe someone on the staff was *curious*...
Zh: “或许员工中有个人很有*好奇心*……”

En: No, we don't hire staff like that. Thank you for letting me know -- and it will be taken care of as soon as possible.
Zh: “不，我们不会雇佣那样的员工。谢谢你告诉我——我们会尽快处理的。”

En: So the phone line is dead?
Zh: “所以电话线被切断了？”

En: Yes, and the phone company is taking its sweet time sending someone to fix it. He shakes his head and adds: "Losers."
Zh: “是的，但电话公司会抓紧时间派人来修理的。”他摇着头说：“一群屌丝。”

En: Is it true that there was foul play?
Zh: “其中真的有什么内幕吗？”

En: He wrinkles his nose. "Who told you that?"
Zh: 他皱着鼻子。“谁告诉你的？”

En: That's pretty strange.
Zh: “那倒怪了。”

En: It's not *strange*, it's inconvenient.
Zh: “不是*奇怪*，是不方便。”

En: No, no, no. Something nefarious is definitely afoot.
Zh: “不，不，不。肯定有什么邪恶的东西在酝酿中。”

En: He rolls his eyes. "If you say so."
Zh: 他翻了个白眼。“如果你这么说的话。”

En: Lena.
Zh: “莉娜。”

En: It doesn't matter. I just want to know who you suspect.
Zh: “这都没有关系，我只想知道你的怀疑对象。”

En: I would never disclose my sources. That would be dishonourable.
Zh: “我是永远不会透露自己的消息来源的，那样非常不光彩。”

En: Fine, yeah, it looked like someone had messed with the wiring. It was shortly after the hanging, but I don't know if it's at all related... Plenty of assholes around here who aren't murderers.
Zh: “行吧，看来是有人把线路弄乱了。就在绞死事件发生不久后，但我不确定是否有关系……这里有很多没有杀过人的混蛋。”

En: If you do find out who cut the line, though, let me know so I can forward them the repair bill.
Zh: “不过，如果你找到了切断线路的人，请告诉我，这样我就可以把修理账单寄给他们了。”

En: He's exhausted from years of dealing with the *assholes*. That's why he's trying so hard not to be one too...
Zh: 多年来与那些*混蛋*打交道已经让他精疲力尽了，所以他才会如此努力地不成为其中之一……

En: Here's your trash container key.
Zh: “这是你的垃圾箱钥匙。”

En: Thanks. I hope you found what you were looking for.
Zh: “谢了。希望你已经找到想找的东西了。”

En: I found the victim's jeans.
Zh: “我找到了受害者的牛仔裤。”

En: Just trash.
Zh: “只有垃圾而已。”

En: As it should be. Anyway. Thank you for returning my key.
Zh: “本就该是如此。无论如何，谢谢你把钥匙还给我。”

En: I found the victim's clothes.
Zh: “我找到了受害者的衣服。”

En: How... strange. *I* certainly didn't put them there.
Zh: “真是……怪了。肯定不是*我*把它们放进去的。”

En: Maybe you don't remember putting them there. Maybe you were sleepwalking or... in a fugue state...
Zh: “或许你是不记得把它们放进去了，或许你梦游了……或许处于神游状态……”

En: Could someone on your staff have put them there?
Zh: “会不会是你的员工把它们放在那里的？”

En: Who else has keys to the trash container?
Zh: “还有谁有垃圾箱的钥匙？”

En: Thanks. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “谢了。我们再来谈点别的吧。”

En: He grimaces. "I think *fugue states* are more your *forte*, officer."
Zh: 他苦着脸。“我认为*神游状态*更*适用于*你，警官。”

En: Sylvie had the keys before I got here, and I can vouch for her. I can vouch for all my staff. None of us would tamper with a crime scene.
Zh: “在我来之前钥匙在塞尔维手上，但我可以为她担保，我可以为我所有的员工担保。我们中没有人会破坏犯罪现场的。”

En: I vouch for my staff as I would for myself... and I certainly wasn't the one making holes in our walls. He folds his arms and glares at you defiantly.
Zh: “我为我的员工担保，就像为自己担保一样……而且我也绝对不是那个在墙上凿洞的人。”他抱起双臂，挑衅地瞪着你。

En: Sylvie had the keys before I got here, and, as I've said before -- I can vouch for all my staff. None of us would tamper with a crime scene.
Zh: “在我来之前钥匙在塞尔维手上，但，正如我之前说的——我可以为我所有的员工担保。我们中没有人会破坏犯罪现场的。”

En: The Trash Collection Service? CS Municipal. I don't see why they would *put* anything in the trash, though.
Zh: ”垃圾回收服务公司？CS市政公司。虽然我不懂他们为什么会把东西*放进*垃圾箱。”

En: Do you know these guys *personally*?
Zh: “你跟这些人有*私交*吗？”

En: Ah, the illusive CS Municipal. I doubt we'll be able to track down who was sent here last and when. This will have to be one of those *little* threads that solves itself -- down the road.
Zh: “啊，魔幻的CS市政公司……我都怀疑能否查出他们最后派来的人员和时间。这将会是其中一个到了桥头自然直的——*小*船。”

En: Nope. There's nothing more mysterious than the Martinaise Trash Collection Service -- never know who's coming, or when...
Zh: “没有。没有什么比马丁内斯的垃圾回收服务公司更神秘的了——永远不知道谁会在什么时间过来……”

En: On Clinton Street, near the Boogie Street Diamond, a small truck rattles down an alleyway. It stops by a rusted trash container. Two men, their faces rough and swollen, jump out. They look at the container, despondently. CSM is written on their backs...
Zh: 克林顿街，布吉街广场附近，一辆小货車隆隆驶过小巷，停在一个生锈的垃圾箱旁边。两个面部肿胀的男人跳了出来，他们沮丧地看着垃圾箱，背上印着CSM的字样……

En: I have your money.
Zh: “我把钱带来了。”

En: He glares at you suspiciously. "Well?"
Zh: 他怀疑地瞪着你。“嗯？”

En: He refuses to reply. Only his breathing quickens with badly-concealed irritation.
Zh: 他不愿回答。但隐藏不住的怒火让他的呼吸更加急促。

En: It seems a little *callous*, doesn't it? Something stirs in you...
Zh: 这感觉有点*冷酷无情*了，不是吗？你的内心有某种东西在骚动……

En: ... and it's not a shaker. It's nothing. He's holding nothing.
Zh: ……那不是摇酒器。什么都没有，他手里什么都没拿。

En: It's not a shaker. It's nothing. He's holding nothing. It is but an imitation.
Zh: 那不是摇酒器。什么都没有，他手里什么都没拿，只是模仿而已。

En: I have this giant novelty cheque.
Zh: “我有一张超大的兑奖支票。”

En: About my bill...
Zh: “那我的账单……”

En: Got the 20 reál?
Zh: “拿到20雷亚尔了吗？”

En: I don't have any money. I spent all I had on other things. I had the urge to buy things. With *all* of the money!
Zh: “我没钱了，我把所有的钱都花在了别的东西上面。我有一种购物的冲动，挥霍掉*所有*的钱！”

En: He stares at you in disbelief. "Sure. That's it then. You're not getting into the room. Sort your shit out elsewhere."
Zh: 他难以置信地盯着你。“当然，那就这样吧。你不能进房间，去别的地方收拾自己吧。”

En: But I don't have anywhere to go...
Zh: “但我无处可去了……”

En: Yeah... that's not my problem anymore.
Zh: “是啊……但那不是我的问题了。”

En: I have your money.
Zh: “我把钱带来了。”

En: I have your money.
Zh: “我把钱带来了。”

En: I have your money.
Zh: “我把钱带来了。”

En: I have your money.
Zh: “我把钱带来了。”

En: He glares at you suspiciously. "Well?"
Zh: 他怀疑地瞪着你。“嗯？”

En: He glares at you suspiciously. "Well?"
Zh: 他怀疑地瞪着你。“嗯？”

En: He glares at you suspiciously. "Well?"
Zh: 他怀疑地瞪着你。“嗯？”

En: He glares at you suspiciously. "Well?"
Zh: 他怀疑地瞪着你。“嗯？”

En: Can I pay with this cheque? (Show him the giant novelty cheque.)
Zh: “我能用这张支票付款吗？”（给他看那张巨大的兑奖支票。）

En: You must be joking, right? You come here, trash everything -- and now you try to pay with a novelty cheque? The cafeteria manager stares at you, baffled.
Zh: 餐厅经理不解地盯着你。“你一定是在开玩笑吧？你到这儿来，搞砸了一切——现在想用一张兑奖支票付钱？”

En: This isn't a game show. I'm not going to accept it. You owe me cold hard money... I want to see that money in the palm of my hand. Every single cent.
Zh: “这可不是游戏节目，我才不要这个呢。你欠我一大笔钱……我要看到你手里的钱，一分都不能少。”

En: But you must have appreciated my Sam Bo style stealth.
Zh: “但你一定很欣赏我的萨拳式的步法吧。”

En: Hold on -- what exactly *is* money?
Zh: “等等——究竟什么*是*钱？”

En: Hold on -- what is this *money* anyway?
Zh: “等等——话说什么是*钱*？”

En: Money is what grown up people use to pay for things. Things like this hostel room or... He peeks into the ledger: "...or eight bottles of 'Potent Blend' and nine packs of 'Royal Extra'. We use it for everything, really."
Zh: “钱是成年人用来交易的东西，比如这间旅社的房间或是……”他瞄了一眼账本。“……八瓶*混合烈酒*和九包*精装皇家*。说真的，我们用它完成日常的一切。”

En: (Proceed -- don't show him the coins you found.)
Zh: （继续——不把你发现的硬币给他看。）

En: (Proceed -- show him the coins you found) "Is this money?"
Zh: （继续——给他看你找到的硬币）“这是钱吗？”

En: What are you, brain-damaged?
Zh: “你是怎么回事，脑残？”

En: Actually, I might be.
Zh: “老实说，有可能。”

En: Since I woke up I have trouble remembering even the most basic concepts of reality.
Zh: “自从醒来之后，我甚至连最基本的生活常识都想不起来了。”

En: Are you serious? From your work, I don't know... you can take bribes, I guess. He looks at lieutenant. "I'm sorry. I don't think cops take bribes."
Zh: “你认真的吗？工作啊，我也说不好……还可以受贿，我猜。”他看着警督。“抱歉，我不觉得警察会收受贿赂。”

En: No, I'm just getting my bearings.
Zh: “不，我只是想弄清其中的关联。”

En: (Proceed -- show him the coins you found) "I think I might have some."
Zh: （继续——给他看你找到的硬币）“我想我可能有一些。”

En: For a moment, his antipathy is overcome by compassion for the sheer smallness of the money.
Zh: 一瞬间，他的反感被对于那丁点儿硬币的同情所压倒了。

En: Yes. It is.
Zh: “是的，这就是。”

En: Yeah, that's not enough.
Zh: “是啊，但还不够。”

En: (Count them and give them to him.) "That's 10 plus 10 plus 20 equals 40. I'm now down by 40, right?"
Zh: （数一数，交给他。）这里有10加10加20一共40。现在我只欠你40了，对吗？”

En: But what if I don't have any?
Zh: “但如果我没有呢？”

En: No, you see... There's a tinge of sadness in his voice. "That's 40 *cents*. Cents are a form of currency one hundred times smaller than the reál. I'm not even going to take this. Come back when you have 130 reál."
Zh: “不，你看……”他的声音里有一丝悲哀。“那是40*分*。分是一种比雷亚尔小一百倍的货币，我甚至都不会要这个。等你凑够130雷亚尔的时候再来吧。”

En: One *hundred* times smaller?
Zh: “小*一百*倍？”

En: The cafeteria manager stands silently looking at the coins on the counter before him.
Zh: 餐厅经理静静地站在那里，看着柜面上的硬币。

En: He stands silently looking at the coins on the counter.
Zh: 他静静地站在那里，看着柜面上的硬币。

En: But that's horrible.
Zh: “但那也太可怕了。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: That's cop for: "I haven't offered to pay because I don't have any money either."
Zh: 警察这么说的意思就是：“我没有主动提出要付钱，因为我也没有钱。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: It is. He stands silently looking at the coins on the counter.
Zh: “是的。”他静静地站在那里，看着柜面上的硬币。

En: Isn't it... evil? The order of magnitude between what is asked of a person -- and what they have?
Zh: 这不是很……邪恶吗？一个人被索取——以及他所拥有的东西之间的数量差？

En: Darkness rides. (Pick up the coins.)
Zh: “黑暗乘骑。”（收起硬币。）

En: What are you, a philosopher?
Zh: “你是什么哲学家吗？”

En: Keep it to yourself and pick up the coins.
Zh: 自己留着，把硬币收起来。

En: If you run out of money, you die. It's like that for all of us -- me too. That's why I need you to pay me. He stops and says, mostly to himself: "I'm not an asshole."
Zh: “如果没有钱，只有死路一条。大家都是一样——包括我。所以你得付钱。”他停下来，自言自语道：“我不是个混蛋。”

En: It does, doesn't it.
Zh: “确实，不是吗？”

En: Some do take recompense. The lieutenant is dead serious. "But only to survive."
Zh: “有些人的确会收取酬金。”警督非常严肃。“但也只是为了生存。”

En: For survival, to pay *me*. Unless you want to become a *hobo*? Do you want to become a hobo? There's nowhere else to stay in Martinaise and it's a cold spring outside. Money doesn't make you happy but it lets you be *un*happy for a bit longer.
Zh: “为了生存，那就付*我*的钱。除非你想变成*流浪汉*？你想当流浪汉吗？在马丁内斯你无处可去，外面就是寒春。金钱不会让你快乐，却能让你*不*快乐更久一点。”

En: Interesting. Where do I get it from?
Zh: “有意思，哪里才能拿到它呢？”

En: Where can I get money?
Zh: “我去哪里能弄到钱呢？”

En: How so, isn't this the IIR? There's like forty of them! (Give them to him.)
Zh: “怎么会，这不就是IIR吗？差不多有四十个呢！”（把硬币给他。）

En: (Keep the money.)
Zh: （留下钱。）

En: Keep the money.
Zh: 留下钱。

En: Why do I need it?
Zh: “为什么我会需要它呢？”

En: Ah, 'Smallest Church in Saint-Saëns,' right? The cafeteria manager is waiting for you to acknowledge that he recognized the song.
Zh: “啊，‘圣桑小教堂’，对吧？”餐厅经理正在等你意识过来他听过这首歌。

En: Wow. He starts a slow clap. "Just wow. I hope you really enjoyed yourself up there -- had a *cathartic* experience. Because for the rest of us..."
Zh: “哇哦。”他慢慢鼓起掌来。“我只能惊叹了，希望你在上面玩的开心——好好*宣泄*了一番。因为对于我们其余的人来说……”

En: Is there something I can do for you?
Zh: “我能帮你做些什么吗？”

En: Hey. He nods in greeting. "Was there something you needed?"
Zh: “嘿。”他点头示意。“你有什么需要吗？”

En: Well, well, bringing him that new bird sure made a difference in his attitude.
Zh: 哎呀，送给他的那只全新的鸟类标本，绝对改变了他的态度。

En: I fucking rocked that shit.
Zh: “我他妈嗨翻了全场。”

En: Yeah. The Church is actually my *past*.
Zh: “没错，教堂的确是我的*过去*。”

En: It was all right. He doesn't sound like he thinks you *rocked* it. "Subdued. I might start letting people up there again... Now, what can I do for you?"
Zh: “还行吧。”他听起来不像是认同你*嗨翻了*全场的意思。“有点冷清，但我可能会重新开始让人上去表演……现在，我能为你做什么吗？”

En: Cool. He really does sound like he thinks it's a little *cool*. "Now, what can I do for you?"
Zh: “挺酷的。”听起来他是真觉得有那么一点*酷*。“现在，我能为你做什么吗？”

En: ...that really *sucked*.
Zh: “……还真是*烂透了*。”

En: Oh, you're up. He looks you over. "It's good to see you back on your feet. Did you like your room? I cleaned it for you."
Zh: “哦，你来了。”他看了你一眼。“看到你重新振作起来真是太好了。你喜欢自己的房间吗？我帮你清理过了。”

En: I did, thank you. Big improvement.
Zh: “是的，谢谢你。变化很大。”

En: You did? I was too distracted to notice...
Zh: “有吗？我甚至都没注意到……”

En: Pick up the coins.
Zh: 收起硬币。

En: He demonstratively suppresses a world-weary sigh.
Zh: 他明显抑制住了一声厌世的叹息。

En: Another thing -- great. I love those.
Zh: “还有别的东西——太好了，我真喜欢。”

En: Wondering about it is, of course, exactly what he's been doing. For ten years.
Zh: 空想，当然，这正是他一直在做的事情，整整十年了。

En: Let him get to pretend he's indifferent.
Zh: 让他继续假装自己漠不关心吧。

En: He looks at the dust on his finger and wipes it off.
Zh: 他看着手指上的灰尘，把它擦掉了。

En: How do we find them?
Zh: “我们怎么找到他们？”

En: We don't. We have to wait. They'll show up sooner or later. He looks toward the booth. "Men get hungry. Even men on strike."
Zh: “不用找。只要等待就好。他们迟早会出现的。”他朝卡座望去。“人都是会饿的，就连罢工的工人也一样。”

En: There are these things called *days*. You sleep between them. He's saying they'll come *after* you've slept. (Just making clear you got that.)
Zh: 有一种东西叫做*天数*。你可以在其间休息。他是说他们会在你睡醒*之后*来到。（只是想确认一下你是否明白。）

En: We don't. We have to wait. They'll show up sooner or later. He looks toward the booth. "Men get hungry. Even striking men. If not today, then they'll be here tomorrow."
Zh: “不用找。只要等待就好。他们迟早会出现的。”他朝卡座望去。“人都是会饿的，就连罢工的人也不会幸免。如果不是今天，明天他们也会过来。”

En: It's after 16.00. The sign said *reserved starting 16.00*. Why isn't anyone in the mess hall?
Zh: “已经过了16:00了。告示牌上写着*预定从16:00开始*。为什么食堂里一个人也没有？”

En: Good question. He turns to the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “问得好。”他转向餐厅经理。

En: You glance at the clock on the wall behind the manager. Hah!
Zh: 你瞥了一眼经理身后墙上的钟，哈！

En: Hold your peace -- best not to nitpick.
Zh: 保持和平——还是不要吹毛求疵了。

En: Was there something else? Because I have coasters to stack. And glasses to clean. *Apparently* that's what I do now.
Zh: “还有别的事吗？我还有杯垫要叠，杯子要洗呢。*显然*我已经开始做了。”

En: It could have used more work. I just took two bullets for this place.
Zh: “的确还有很多提升的空间。我为这个地方挨了两枪呢。”

En: It could have used more work. After all, I just took a bullet for this place.
Zh: “的确还有很多提升的空间。毕竟，我为这个地方挨了一枪。”

En: Really *cool* man -- yeah. His posture changes immediately. "Entitlement. Everybody likes that -- everybody likes an entitled person who doesn't know how to say thank you."
Zh: “真是*太酷了*老兄——没错。”他的姿势立刻变了。“名不虚传。大家都喜欢——都很喜欢有权有势还不懂得说谢谢的人。”

En: You're welcome. I thought it would be nice for you to wake up in a clean place after you -- let's be fair -- defended this establishment and its clientèle from gunfire.
Zh: “不用谢。我想在你从枪火中保护了这个地方和它的客人们之后——公平地说——如果能在一个干净的地方醒来那就再好不过了。”

En: Where did everyone go?
Zh: “大家都去哪儿了？”

En: Oh, you know... he looks around at the empty place. "People don't tend to stick around after shoot-outs. Turns out they're not good for business."
Zh: “哦，你也知道……”他环视了一圈空荡荡的餐厅。“人们不会在枪击之后继续逗留的。事实证明，它们对生意没有好处。”

En: Are Lena and Morell still in town?
Zh: “莉娜和莫雷尔还在城里吗？”

En: What happened to the man with the sunglasses?
Zh: “那个戴墨镜的人怎么样了？”

En: Where did the horse-faced woman go?
Zh: “那个马脸女去哪了？”

En: Ah, them! Nice people -- but no. Lena said they were going back to Jamrock. I saw them pass by, outside. He waves at the windows. "This was *before* the fight started."
Zh: “啊，他们啊！很好的人——但是没有。莉娜说他们要回加姆洛克去。我看到他们从外面经过了。”他朝着窗户挥挥手。“这是战斗开始*之前*的事了。”

En: Okay. Yes. That was for the best.
Zh: “好吧，没错。这样最好。”

En: But we weren't done looking for the phasmid...
Zh: “但我们还没找到竹节虫呢……”

En: I mean, you're a detective, perhaps you can track them down? Shouldn't be that hard...
Zh: “我是说，你是个警探，也许你能找到他们？应该没有那么难的……”

En: He nods. "Yes. You know. And I went outside and took care of it. Everybody calmed down. Felt like the right thing to do."
Zh: 他点点头。“是的，我出去处理好了。大家都冷静了下来。感觉这是我应该做的。”

En: I don't remember *everyone* who comes here. He shakes his head. "And -- many people wear sunglasses inside lately. Must be a fad."
Zh: 他摇摇头。“我不记得*每个*来过的客人，而且——最近很多人都会在室内戴墨镜。一定是某种时尚吧。”

En: I don't really use words like that to describe my clients, and maybe you shouldn't either… He purses his lips. "Seeing as you're a cop and all."
Zh: 他撅起嘴巴。“我真不会用这种词去描述自己的客人，也许你也不应该——好歹你也是个警察。”

En: All right. Thanks. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，谢谢你。”（总结。）

En: No problem. He looks around at the empty establishment. "They'll come back. They always do."
Zh: “没关系。”他环视着空荡荡的餐厅。“他们会回来的，他们总是这样。”

En: You're already set for the night. Your door is unlocked.
Zh: “你今晚已经有着落了，你的门已经解锁了。”

En: You can get that big shiny fucking plate... He points at something. "...and draw Cuno a line."
Zh: “你可以用那个该死的大盘子……”他指着什么东西。“……给坤诺划清界限。”

En: I'll have a big fat one, bartender.
Zh: “给我来一大杯，酒保。”

En: He keeps his eyes on you, ignoring the boy.
Zh: 他一直盯着你，不理睬那个男孩。
