En: You still haven't drawn Cuno a line, the fuck's up with that?
Zh: “你还没给坤诺划清界限，这他妈是怎么回事？”

En: He doesn't say what's up with that.
Zh: 他没有说这是怎么回事。

En: He doesn't take the bait.
Zh: 他没有上钩。

En: He's used to this sort of thing.
Zh: 他已经习惯了这种事情。

En: Fucking cleaned it... He hisses something under his breath.
Zh: “该死的清理过了……”他在呼吸之间嘟囔着什么。

En: What a dick... dancing around in his maid uniform...
Zh: “这是个混蛋……穿着女仆制服跳舞……”

En: Hold up. What was that, Cuno?
Zh: “等等。那是什么，坤诺？”

En: Cuno forces an unnatural smile.
Zh: 坤诺挤出一个不自然的微笑。

En: You're right, it's not the right time yet.
Zh: “你说得对，时机未到。”

En: There never *will* be a right time, believe me. That karaoke system is only there for show. I'm not going to let *anyone* use it anymore.
Zh: “时机*永远*不会到的，相信我。那套卡拉OK设备只是摆摆样子，我不会再让*任何人*使用它了。”

En: There's a shuffle of nylon as Lieutenant Kitsuragi looks for something in the pockets of his black bomber.
There's a shuffle of nylon as Lieutenant Kitsuragi looks for something in the pockets of his orange bomber.
Bust out the big guns.
Zh: “干掉那些大人物。”

En: C'mon guys, you know me.
Zh: “拜托伙计们，你们都知道我的。”

En: Two in the chest and down he goes.
Zh: “胸部中了两枪，他倒下了。”

En: Blackout drunk, here I come!
Zh: “酩酊大醉，我来了！”

En: Like hell I didn't!
Zh: “我才没有呢！”

En: Monica's titties are big.
Zh: “莫妮卡的咪咪可大了。”

En: Wanna see my knuckle dusters?
Zh: “想看看我的指虎吗？”

En: I got balls. That's it.
Zh: “我很有胆量，就是这样。”

En: He's from Graad. People from Graad don't mind if they're called by their national derogatives. It's okay to call him a *Kojko*.
Zh: 他来自格拉德，那里的人即使被用国家的蔑称称呼也不甚在意，你完全可以喊他*克吉克*。

En: How do you know? Maybe it's a small body.
Zh: “你怎么知道？说不定尸体也小。”

En: No, no. He nods confidently. "I know."
Zh: “不，不。”他自信地点点头。“我知道。”

En: Listen to him. This is a man who clearly knows about bodies and fridges -- and bodies fitting into fridges.
Zh: 你就听他的吧。这人显然很懂冰箱和尸体——也很懂冰箱适不适合放尸体。

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: Mhm. He nods confidently. "I know."
Zh: “嗯。”他自信地点点头。“我知道。”

En: Okay, we have our answer. It won't fit. We need a bigger fridge.
Zh: “好，我们找到答案了。这个装不下。我们得找个更大得冰箱。”

En: The man ponders his cooking utensils and gives you a little nod, acknowledging your presence.
Zh: 这人正对着他的厨具沉思，发现你来之后，他朝你稍微点了点头。

En: The man nods at you, checks his watch and sighs, "Ai-ai-ai..."
Zh: 这人朝你点了点头，看了看表，又叹了口气。“唉——唉——唉……”

En: Shrugging is an international sign for: 'No, I don't know what's behind that door'.
Zh: 耸肩动作有个全世界通用的意思：不，我不知道那扇门后是什么。

En: They're friends.
Zh: 他们是朋友。

En: Just one word. Sounds quizzical. Then he awaits your answer with his brow arched.
Zh: 只说了一个词。听起来充满困惑。他皱着眉头，等待着你的回应。

En: Turning it up seems like a dangerous idea, honestly. The place is a powder keg.
Zh: 说实在的，多加这个似乎是相当危险的主意。这地方就像火药桶一样。

En: He's happy for some reason.
Zh: 由于某种原因，他非常高兴。

En: With the mention of the word *Kojko* the cook starts talking excessively, his hands up in the air again.
Zh: 当提到*克吉克*这个词时，厨师开始夸张地说起话来，再次举起了双手。

En: He *knows* correctly. It is too small.
Zh: 他确实*很懂*。这台冰箱太小了。

En: You wouldn't mind if I were to boil some boots here, would you?
Zh: “你不介意我在这儿煮一双靴子吧，不介意吧？”

En: The man takes another drag of his cigarette and shakes his head, smiling widely. It's not clear whether he understood your question.
Zh: 这人又吸了一口烟，摇了摇头，笑容灿烂。看不出他是否理解了你的问题。

En: It doesn't matter -- you're a police officer, you can do anything you want.
Zh: 无所谓——你才是警察，你应该随心所欲。

En: You can always talk your way out if things go sour.
Zh: 就算出了差错，你也可以凭三寸不烂之舌轻松脱身。

En: As you step in, he nods towards the table and says something in a completely foreign language. The only words you can make out are 'gorący' and 'kubek'.
Zh: 你走进房间时，他朝桌子点了点头，用完全陌生的语言说了句话。你只能听懂“格瑞希”和“库贝克”两个词。

En: Ah, Gorący! I have some questions to ask!
Zh: “啊，格瑞希！我有几个问题想问！”

En: Mr. Kubek? I'm here on official police business.
Zh: “库贝克先生？我是警察，正在办案。”

En: Ignore him. [Leave.]
Zh: 无视他。[离开。]

En: The man puts his cup down and replies something, his left hand drawing arcs in the air.
Zh: 那人放下杯子并回复了几句，他的左手在空中画着弧线。

En: I need a fridge to store a corpse.
Zh: “我需要一台冰箱来存放尸体。”

En: I don't think I need anything else. Stay masculine! [Leave.]
Zh: “我什么都不需要了。保持阳刚之气吧！”

En: The man keeps shaking his head. His eyes seem sad. Then he starts swiping crumbs off the counter top.
Zh: 这人不断地摇着头，眼神充满悲伤。接着便着手清理起柜台上的食物碎屑。

En: I don't think he understands, officer... We need to find some other way to make ourselves clear.
Zh: “我觉得他听不懂你说的话，警官……咱们得想个别的办法表明来意。”

En: It must be his name. Gorący, Gorący Kubek... Sounds representative.
Zh: 那一定是他的名字。格瑞希，格瑞希·库贝克……相当有代表性的名字。

En: A thin man is smoking below an exhaust hood, occasionally sipping from his mug. This must be the Whirling's cook.
Zh: 一个消瘦的男人正在抽油烟机下抽烟，不时端起杯子喝上一口。这位一定就是褴褛飞旋的厨师。

En: Okay, it's definitely not his name. Whatever you do, please don't call him Gorący Kubek! Please. It's not funny.
Zh: 嘿，这绝对不是他的名字。不管你想怎么着，请千万别叫他格瑞希·库贝克！拜托。这一点都不好玩。

En: It's late. He's working.
Zh: 很晚了，他正在工作。

En: By the way, we speak Revacholian in Revachol. (Get your Revacholian Nationhood on.)
Zh: “对了，在瑞瓦肖我们说瑞瓦肖语。”（摆出瑞瓦肖国民的姿态。）

En: Speak Revacholian? The lieutenant raises his eyebrows.
Zh: “会说瑞瓦肖语吗？”警督扬起眉毛。

En: I know, Kim... I know. (Make stabbing motions at the meat on the cutting board.)
Zh: “知道了，金……知道了……”（冲着砧板上的肉做出捅刀子的动作。）

En: I need to get the corpse... (Point to Kim) "... into the fridge!" (Point to the fridge.)
Zh: “我需要把尸体……”（指向金）……“塞到冰箱里！”（指向冰箱。）

En: Wait, what language is he speaking?
Zh: “等等，他说的是什么语言？”

En: What are you doing?!
Zh: “你这是干嘛？！”

En: Why am *I* the corpse?! He turns his eyes to the cook, who gives you both a big bright smile.
Zh: “为什么把*我*当尸体？！”他望向厨师，厨师对你俩回以灿烂的笑容。

En: Doesn't matter... I don't think he understood us anyway. He wouldn't be smiling if he did.
Zh: “无所谓了……反正我也不认为他能理解我们的意思。如果他理解了，就不会那么笑了。”

En: He's from Graad -- a Kojko, the lieutenant informs you.
Zh: “他是格拉德人——一个克吉克，”警督告知了你。

En: The cook looks at you doing the charade and smiles.
Zh: 厨师看着你打哑谜的样子，露出了微笑。

En: Point towards the fridge.
Zh: 指向冰箱。

En: The cook says something. It's quite animated, but you still don't understand.
Zh: 厨师冲你说了什么，还配合着生动的手势，可惜你仍然无法理解。

En: This man is a foreigner!
Zh: “这是个外国人！”

En: Speak Revacholian, yes. Not this funky thing he's spurting out here.
Zh: “说瑞瓦肖语，可以吗。别再满嘴秃噜顺口溜了。”

En: We speak *Suresne*. There is no such thing as the *Revacholian language*, he sighs. "But don't worry, your message remained clear."
Zh: “我们说的是*苏瑞恩语*，并没有所谓的*瑞瓦肖语*，”他叹了口气。“但别担心，你的意思很明确。”

En: The cook is still smoking, ash falling from between his fingers. His hands are large and clean.
Zh: 厨师仍在抽烟，烟灰从他夹烟的指间滑落。他的手掌宽大，相当干净。

En: Good thing he didn't understand a word of your stupidity, he concludes.
Zh: “幸好他听不懂你的傻话。”他如此总结。

En: Leo said you're friends with Mañana. Is that true?
Zh: “利奥说你是马列拉的朋友，是吗？”

En: The mention of 'Mañana' gets his attention. He smiles and delivers a whole slew of unfamiliar words and lively gestures. Then he falls silent again.
Zh: 马列拉的名字吸引了他的注意。配合着生动的手势，他微笑着道出了一长串你从未听过的词。之后又陷入了沉默。

En: Hello, sir. Got time for a few questions?
Zh: “你好，先生。能问你几个问题吗？”

En: You've got some impressive pots there.
Zh: “你这些锅相当吸引眼球啊。”

En: Do you know what's behind that door? (Point to the blue door.)
Zh: “你知道那扇门后面是什么吗？”（指向蓝色的门。）

En: He looks up at you, then looks away quickly, shrugging and muttering something to himself.
Zh: 他抬头看了看你，很快又移开了视线，耸了耸肩，喃喃自语着你听不懂的话。

En: He smiles and bangs his ladle against each of his pots in turn.
Zh: 他微笑着用长勺依次敲打着每一个锅子。

En: It's almost like music, especially with the sounds of assorted dishes boiling and simmering on the stove.
Zh: 这几乎就是一篇乐章了，尤其是伴随着各种菜肴在炉子上煎炒煨炖的声音。

En: I need to put a dead body in this fridge. (Point to the fridge.)
Zh: “我得把尸体塞进这个冰箱里。”（指着冰箱。）

En: Ooh! His eyes light up with understanding, then instantly grow dark again. "No, no," he shakes his head, "Body no fit fridge. Fridge small."
Zh: “哦！”他双眼放光，终于明白了你的意思，但目光很快又黯淡了。“不，不，”他摇着头，“冰箱不放尸体，冰箱小。”

En: What is in that borscht you're making there? (Point to the large pot.)
Zh: “你往罗宋汤里放什么了？”（指着大锅。）

En: The man says a couple of sentences in that strange language of his and then seems to wait for you to speak.
Zh: 这人用你听不懂的语言说了几句话，之后似乎是在等你发言。

En: He smiles, nodding vigorously, then pours half a bottle of vodka into the pot. With a whistle he stirs the brew.
Zh: 他微笑着，用力点了点头，接着把半瓶伏特加全倒进了锅里。吹了一声口哨之后，便专注于搅拌这锅汤。

En: Gorący, could I have some of that brew?
Zh: “格瑞希，能给我来点汤吗？”

En: He smiles and nods enthusiastically, and, chattering away in his language, ladles some brew into a small thermal cup. Then hands it to you.
Zh: 他微笑着，热情地点点头，然后用他的语言喋喋不休地说着什么，并舀了一些汤倒进一个小保温杯里。将它递给了你。

En: The cook shakes his head and rattles off a sequence of undecipherable words. He then returns to his smoking, ash falling from between his fingers.
Zh: 厨师摇了摇头，一口气说了一长串你听不懂的话。接着又继续抽他的烟了，烟灰从夹烟的指间滑落。

En: I'm pretty sure he asked you a question.
Zh: 我非常确定，他是在向你提问。

En: Yes. (Nod.)
Zh: “是的。”（点头。）

En: No. (Shake your head.)
Zh: “不是。”（摇头。）

En: Mercury rising. (Run your fingers through your hair.)
Zh: “水银蒸发令。”（用手指梳理头发。）

En: Sorry, I don't understand you.
Zh: “抱歉，我不明白你的意思。”

En: He doesn't know your language, you can just say something cool in return.
Zh: 他不懂你的语言，你只要随便说两句很酷的话就行。

En: *Barszcz* need… He looks at the soup, then at you. "More *wódka*?" He picks up a bottle from the shelf.
Zh: 这人看了看你，又看了看汤。他露出得意的神色。从架子上拿起一个瓶子。“*裸送汤*需要……多放*夫忑加*？”

En: Yes. (Nod and turn your fingers clockwise.) "Turn the *wódka* up."
Zh: “对。”（点头，沿顺时针比划手指。）“多放*夫忑加*。”

En: No! No *wódka*! (Turn your fingers counter-clockwise.) "Cut it."
Zh: “不对！别放*夫忑加*！”（逆时针比划手指。）“别放。”

En: I'll leave the cooking to you. (Shrug.) "I have business."
Zh: “厨房的事就交给你吧。”（耸肩。）“我还有工作。”

En: Of course! Vodka! Now that makes it a very very special borscht indeed! Turn it up and then ask for some yourself!
Zh: 对啦！就是伏特加！那玩意确实能做出特别的特制罗宋汤！多放点，然后你也来一壶吧！

En: Vodka-borscht! I love it, *bratan*! Turn it the fuck up and then ask for some yourself.
Zh: 伏特加——罗宋汤！我爱死这玩意了，*哥们儿*！赶紧多倒点进去，然后你也来一壶吧。

En: Okay, so it's vodka that keeps the men happy and in good spirits. He nods. "Clever move by the Union."
Zh: “好吧，原来是伏特加让那些人保持好心情和斗志昂扬的。”他点了点头。“工会的明智之举。”

En: The cook gives you a long disappointed look, then turns the stove off and seems to wait for you to speak.
Zh: 厨师满脸失望地凝视着你，接着关上了炉火，似乎是在等你发言。

En: The cook gives you a long inspecting look, then shrugs too and seems to wait for you to speak.
Zh: 厨师打量了你很久，又耸了耸肩，似乎是在等你发言。

En: No. How about you keep sticking around? Look around -- find the company representative, see what they have to say? Maybe the company will help? Then get back to this.
Zh: 不，你继续留在这里如何？四处看看——找到公司代表，看看他们有什么要说的？也许公司会出面帮忙？然后再回来。

En: I think we'll keep sticking around, Titus. We haven't even talked to the company rep yet -- we should do that, he turns to you. "She's on the pier I believe... northwest."
Zh: “我想我们会继续待在这儿的，提图斯。我们甚至还没跟公司代表谈过话呢——这很有必要，”他转向你。“我想她应该在码头……西北方。”

En: Maybe the Wild Pines has help to offer if they won't? Maybe not. Either way -- we'll have a case against your Ruby soon enough.
Zh: “如果他们不愿意的话，或许野松公司能帮上忙？也许都不行。但无论如何——我们很快就能对你的露比提出诉讼了。”

En: Your room had one, or maybe it's too broken...
Zh: 你房间里就有一个，但它或许太破了……

En: Because she's your real leader.
Zh: “因为她是你真正的领导。”

En: You're coo-coo, cop. His smile hides a flash of anger. "I'm the *only* man responsible for this unit -- get with it, or fuck off."
Zh: “你疯了吧，条子。”他的微笑里隐藏着一丝怒火。“我是这个组织*唯一*的负责人——要么接受，要么滚蛋。”

En: Yeah, fuck off!
Zh: “没错，滚蛋！”

En: How many times must I tell you...
Zh: 我得告诉你多少次……

En: From the corner of your eye you see a little bird fly into the bush -- right behind the window; behind Titus' back.
Zh: 通过眼角的余光，你看见一只小鸟飞进了灌木丛——就在窗户后面；提图斯的背后。

En: I think we'll *keep* sticking around, Titus. You'll be surprised at how quickly a theory presents itself if you keep looking.
Zh: “我想我们会继续待在这儿的，提图斯。如果你继续追寻，你会很惊讶一个推论竟能出现得如此之快。”

En: The *boys* are too eager to please him to keep their mouths shut. You're gonna get a good head count here, just wait...
Zh: 那些*兄弟们*太过急于取悦他，以至于很难让他们闭上嘴巴。你很快就能点清人数的，等着吧……

En: Neither one of us will ever get it off completely.
Zh: 我们谁也不能完全摆脱此事。

En: ...hit you -- duck!
Zh: ……撞上你——躲开！

En: Someone has to rush in to break the tension. The second in command.
Zh: 必须有人过去打破这种紧张的气氛了，二把手。

En: Another apocalyptic actor in town?
Zh: 城里又来了个末日传教的演员吗？

En: A door-gun is a gun mounted to the door of a transport airship. They have a habit of mowing down civilians. Farmers mostly.
Zh: 门枪是一种安装在运输飞艇舱门上的枪支。它们有射杀平民的习惯，主要是农民。

En: There's an old catatonic lady in the old fish market, on the other side of the bay. She needs help.
Zh: “旧鱼市里有个患有紧张性精神症的老太太，就在海湾的另一边。她需要帮助。”

En: Hey, about that *complex operation*  out of Rubys lorry -- I think that's tied to another case I'm working.
Zh: “喂，关于露比的货車司机之外的*复杂业务*——我想它跟我调查的另外一起案件有关。”

En: Nope. That's all he says. He doesn't even do anything.
Zh: “不。”他只说了这一句。他甚至一动不动。

En: Next question.
Zh: “下一个问题。”

En: He's used to giving orders and having them obeyed immediately. You should not indulge him.
Zh: 他已经习惯了发号施令，并看着自己的命令立刻执行下去。你不应该纵容他的。

En: Cross your arms.
Zh: 抱起你的胳膊。

En: We need to talk about the man hanged out back.
Zh: “我们得谈谈那个吊人。”

En: First, we need to talk about your attitude.
Zh: “首先，我们得谈谈你的态度问题。”

En: Looks like the circus left town, but the clowns are still here. Titus does not look particularly happy to see you.
Zh: “看来马戏团离开了镇子，但小丑们还留在这里。”提图斯看到你不是很高兴的样子。

En: You hear that, boys? The man looks around. "Looks like the RCM sent us a big dick cop."
Zh: “听到了吗，兄弟们？”男人环视了一圈。“看来RCM派了个狠角色过来。”

En: *You don't get to tell me what to do*. He impersonates you. The crowd laughs again...
Zh: “*轮不到你来告诉我怎么做*。”他模仿着你，众人又笑了起来……

En: What, is he fucking kidding...? This guy high or something? The little guy looks at his mates in disbelief.
Zh: “什么，他是在开玩笑吗……？这家伙嗑嗨了还是怎么的？”小个子难以置信地看着他的同伴们。

En: Hey asshole, up here! We're talking to you!
Zh: “嘿，混蛋，看这里！我们跟你说话呢！”

En: We're looking for Titus Hardie. The lieutenant turns to the broad-shouldered man at the end of the table.
Zh: “我们在找提图斯·哈迪。”警督转向桌子尽头那个肩膀宽阔的男人。

En: Don't say anything yet.
Zh: 先不说话。

En: Hey... HEY, DIPSHIT!!! You hard of hearing or something?! The bossman is talking to you!
Zh: “嘿……嘿，笨蛋！！！你听力不好还是怎么的？！老大在跟你说话呢！”

En: Scan the room.
Zh: 审视房间。

En: Take an aggressive step closer.
Zh: 向前逼近一步。

En: I'm going to take off now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我现在要走了。”[离开。]

En: Detective. The lieutenant acknowledges you with a sharp note. He's leaving it to you.
Zh: “警探。”警督高声喊了你一声，他把任务交给你了。

En: The room is silent. The leader does not flinch.
Zh: 房间里一片寂静，领头人并没有退步。

En: Present a solid theory about why Ruby could've done it.
Zh: 提出一个关于露比为什么能做到这一点的可靠理论。

En: Pin it on Ruby?
Zh: 栽赃给露比？

En: Ruby is a drug trader established.
Zh: 确定了露比是个毒贩。

En: Found key in Union box.
Zh: 在工会包厢里找到钥匙。

En: Saw the winch outside.
Zh: 看到外面的起吊机。

En: Staged like a play.
Zh: 上演了一出戏。

En: Reconstructed murder scene.
Zh: 重建的谋杀现场。

En: Found antique rifle.
Zh: 发现古董步枪。

En: Klaasje gave motive.
Zh: 卡拉洁提供了动机。

En: Secret route found.
Zh: 发现秘密通道。

En: HAS NOT MET COMPANY REP!
Zh: 没有见到公司代表！

En: Okay, I talked to Klaasje. Now I need to talk to Ruby.
Zh: “好吧，我跟卡拉洁谈过了。现在我需要和露比谈谈。”

En: Why? He leans back and regards you with curiosity.
Zh: “为什么？”他靠在椅背上，好奇地看着你。

En: I'm going to level with you. She's the next link in the chain that leads me to Lely's killer.
Zh: “我跟你说实话吧，她就是调查杀害莱利的凶手的下一个突破口。”

En: I'm going to level with you. She's the next link in the chain that leads me to the mercenary's killer.
Zh: “我跟你说实话吧，她就是调查杀害雇佣兵的凶手的下一个突破口。”

En: Hey, I don't... oh, okay... Glen bursts out laughing. "Yeah, take me!"
Zh: “嘿，我不……哦，好吧……”格伦突然大笑起来。“好啊，带我去吧。”

En: Sounds like you're *making* her a suspect in this. Not on my watch you're not.
Zh: “听起来你是准备把她*当做*嫌疑人了。在我的地盘上，休想。”

En: Ruby's one of us. We're not gonna throw her under your Moralintern steamroller, fuck that shit!
Zh: “露比是我们的人，我们是不会把她推到国际道德伦理委员会的車轮下的。去你的吧！”

En: And fuck you too, moral-f*g! He throws a glance at Titus as the last syllable leaves his lips. The big guy sighs.
Zh: “也去你的吧，道德婊兵！”当他说出最后一个音节时，他瞥了提图斯一眼。那个大个子叹了一口气。

En: That's *nothing*, that's just legalese. You don't even have a *sound theory*. He crosses his arms. "I don't wanna be rude, but we're trying to get some R&R here. Think you could fuck off now?"
Zh: “那*没什么*，就是个法律术语。你甚至都没有*合理的推理*。”他抱起双臂。“我不想太失礼，但我们是来这里放松的。可以麻烦你滚蛋了吗？”

En: He and his men carry themselves like giants. You'll need to prove your mettle to be taken seriously.
Zh: 他和他的兄弟们表现得像是某种大人物，你得证明一下自己的勇气才能被认真对待。

En: Let's go over the rape victim again.
Zh: “我们再回顾一下强暴受害人的事情。”

En: Look, I need to pin this on someone and I've decided to pin this on her.
Zh: “听着，我得把这件事归咎于某个人，而我已经决定就是她了。”

En: I have seen her in a laudanum-induced delirium. The paranormal instinct whispers her name into my ear.
Zh: “我见过她吸鸦片酊吸得神志不清的样子，超自然的预感也在我耳边说出了她的名字。”

En: I suspect she killed the victim -- that's why.
Zh: “我怀疑是她杀了被害人——这就是为什么。”

En: That's not nearly enough. I don't wanna be *rude*, but we kinda have our own thing going here. Unless you got a *sound theory* implicating Ruby I think you should just take off.
Zh: “这远远不够。我不想太失礼，但我们现在正忙着呢。除非你有能证明露比有罪的*有力证据*，不然就滚开吧。”

En: A civil war is brewing and the only way to prevent it is to solve the murder. That means talking to Ruby.
Zh: “一场内战正在酝酿当中，阻止它的唯一方法就是解决这起谋杀案。这意味着要跟露比谈谈。”

En: She is a suspect in another case I'm investigating. A drug smuggling.
Zh: “她是我正在调查的另一起案件的嫌疑人，一起毒品走私案。”

En: Ruby is missing. If you hide from the police in a murder investigation you become a suspect. You know how it works, guys.
Zh: “露比失踪了。如果你在谋杀案的调查过程中试图躲避警察，你就会成为嫌疑人。伙计们，你们很清楚这是怎么回事。”

En: She is missing, and if you hide from the police in a murder investigation you become a suspect. That's how it works, guys.
Zh: “她失踪了。如果你在谋杀案的调查过程中试图躲避警察，你就会成为嫌疑人。伙计们，你们很清楚这是怎么回事。”

En: Just dragged her away, huh? Stone cold... He's still looking at the front door. "Didn't think you had it in you..."
Zh: “把她拖走了，是吗？真是冷酷啊……”他看在看着前门。“没想到你还有这个本事……”

En: The chatter is too inane to reach your ears: Raise your chin and gaze into the distance like a prince.
Zh: 这段喋喋不休简直毫无意义：扬起下巴，像王子一样凝视远方。

En: Do not let their squeals disturb your serenity. These are but simple peasants, sire.
Zh: 不要让他们的噪音打扰到您的平静，那不过是些平民罢了，大人。

En: Again? Seriously, man... He shakes his head. "Fine, fire away."
Zh: “又来？拜托，老兄……”他摇摇头。“行，说吧。”

En: Fuck! The big man's eyes and veins bulge. "I knew that fucking whore couldn't be trusted!"
Zh: “该死！”那个大个子的眼睛和血管都暴涨起来。“我就知道不能相信那个该死的婊子！”

En: For the record... she steps in, forcefully. "Titus Hardie did not explicitly specify the *victim* as a whore. Nor did he say anything about trusting her."
Zh: “先说一句……”她强行插了进来。“提图斯·哈迪并没有明确指出*被害人*是淫妇，也没有说过要相信她。”

En: Klaasje says she wasn't raped.
Zh: “卡拉洁说自己没有被强暴。”

En: She's just in denial, asshole. You don't understand the *traumatic experience*. She's shutting down. And she doesn't fucking trust you.
Zh: “她只是不愿承认而已，混蛋，你根本不了解*创伤经历*。她封闭了自己，又他妈不信任你。”

En: Yes, she's crazy, you know, the rat-faced man says carefully. "A crazy-bitch -- you know the type. She's fucked up."
Zh: “是啊，她就是个疯子，知道吗，”老鼠脸的男人小心翼翼地说。“一个疯婆子——你知道那种人，乱七八糟的。”

En: Oh, shut up and stay out of this, Liz! He turns back to you. "He raped her. He was out of his fucking mind. You have no idea!"
Zh: “闭上嘴巴，别掺和进来，莉兹！”他转向你。“他强暴了她。你根本想象不到他有多疯狂。”

En: Listen. She wasn't raped. The lieutenant's voice is beginning to betray his agitation. "The witness' statements were very clear."
Zh: “听着，她没有被强暴。”警督的声音开始流露出他的焦躁。“证人的陈述非常明了。”

En: There's *a lot* of questions. If you ask them now they'll just keep bringing up the tape. Listen to it and they'll have nothing to hide behind.
Zh: 目前的问题有*很多*。如果你现在开口，他们只会一直提起那盘磁带。去听一次，他们就没有什么能隐瞒的了。

En: Where did you get this tape?
Zh: “你从哪里弄到的这盘带子？”

En: Here, jerkwad! He slams an audiotape on the table. "Listen to this shit, and then come back and tell me the *Soldier of the Apocalypse* was an innocent man."
Zh: “就在这儿，混球！”他把录音带砰的一声甩到桌子上。“听完这个再来告诉我*末日战士*是无辜的。”

En: So what's on this tape?
Zh: “这盘带子上有什么？”

En: Show it to him, T. He turns to Titus, who's still breathing heavily. "What's the harm, right?"
Zh: “给他看看，提。”他转向提图斯，后者还在喘着粗气。“有什么害处呢，是吧？”

En: Look, copper. The tattooed man snaps his fingers to get your attention. "We know the dead fuck was a rapist and a killer. We got him confessing to it on tape."
Zh: “听着，条子。”那个纹身男打个响指吸引了你的注意。“我们知道那个死掉的混蛋是个强奸杀人犯，我们录下了他的忏悔。”

En: Frankly it's a bit terrifying.
Zh: 坦白说，还是有点吓人的。

En: A few more things about the tape before I go listen to it...
Zh: “在听录音之前，还有几件事……”

En: I'm sure we can find a tape player, the lieutenant whispers.
Zh: “我们肯定能找到一台录音机的。”警督低声说。

En: When she's angry, she emphasizes the *s*. It gives her voice a strangely hypnotic quality. Her lips barely move as she speaks.
Zh: 当她生气的时候，她会加重*尾音*。这就赋予了她的声音一种奇怪的催眠效果。她说话的时候嘴唇几乎不动。

En: What's on it? We call it the Doorgunner Megamix. You'll know why, once you listen to it.
Zh: “上面有什么？我们称之为门炮手经典串烧。听过之后你就知道为什么了。”

En: Where can I listen to this?
Zh: “我要去哪里听呢？”

En: You've hit a nerve. Titus is furious -- no, more than that -- the loyal Titus feels *betrayed*.
Zh: 你说到点子上了。提图斯非常愤怒——不，不仅于此——忠诚的提图斯感觉被*背叛*了。

En: 'Where can I listen to this?' he mocks you. "Why don't you try shoving it up your ass, genius?!"
Zh: “我要去哪里听呢？”他嘲笑着你。“不如试着把它塞进你的屁股里呢，天才？！”

En: Yeah, play it with your ass, COCKSUCKER! His voice echoes like thunder in the small room.
Zh: “是啊，用你的屁股听吧，王八蛋！”他的声音在小房间里仿佛雷鸣般回响着。

En: I'm sure we can find a tape player. It's not a problem, he repeats calmly in a hushed voice.
Zh: “我们肯定能找到一台录音机的，这不是问题。”他平静地低声重复着。

En: Okay, okay... easy, fellows. Give the guy a break. He's obviously one of god's special people.
Zh: “好了，好了……冷静点，伙计们。放那家伙一马吧，他显然是神特殊的孩子。”

En: Yeah? What about it?
Zh: “是吗？怎么了？”

En: Wh-why would you say that? He turns to Titus. "Ruby is just the next logical link in the investigation. If we can talk to her we can *eliminate* her as a suspect."
Zh: “你-你为什么要这么说？”他转向提图斯。“露比只是调查中的下一个逻辑链而已。如果我们能跟她谈谈，就可以*消除*她的嫌疑了。”

En: Oh, you need a scapegoat? The tension leaves Titus' voice. "Why didn't you just say so to begin with, buddy? Here, have Glen here!"
Zh: “哦，你要找个替罪羊吗？”提图斯的声音不再紧张。“你怎么不早说呢，伙计？来吧，把格伦带走吧！”

En: No, man, Eugene raises his hand. "Pin it on me and frame me good, mister Law!"
Zh: “别啊，兄弟，”尤金举起了手，“栽赃给我吧，好好地陷害我吧，法律先生！”

En: Stop it. This is a waste of everyone's time. She sounds agitated. "I think we have already established that you're not going to single anyone out."
Zh: “住手，这完全就是在浪费大家时间。”她听上去很激动。“我以为大家已经说好了，不会交出任何一个人。”

En: Sounds like you wanna push this steamin' pile of shit on *anyone* -- and now you've picked Ruby. Well, not on my watch you won't.
Zh: “听起来你是想把这个烂摊子推给*任何人*——现在你选中了露比。在我的地盘上，休想。”

En: Get *further* in there. Escalate.
Zh: 再*深入*一点，逐步升级。

En: Tilt your head from side to side, cracking your neck.
Zh: 左右歪头，扭扭脖子。

En: What the fuck is this? He gets up in your face. "You wanna fuck with me?!"
Zh: “这他妈是什么意思？”他站到你跟前。“你想跟我对着干吗？！”

En: I want to go over a few things regarding Ruby's disappearance again.
Zh: “关于露比的失踪，我想再确认一遍。”

En: Yeah, what?
Zh: “是啊，怎么了？”

En: De-escalate.
Zh: 逐步降级。

En: What are you looking at, copper? He cracks his neck.
Zh: “你看什么呢，条子？”他扭扭脖子。

En: Shift your centre of balance from one leg to another.
Zh: 把重心从一条腿移到另一条腿上。

En: His eyes narrow. His right hand starts tapping a quick little rhythm on his thigh: tap tap tap.
Zh: 他的眼睛眯了起来，右手开始在他的大腿上敲着一段紧凑的节奏：哒哒哒。

En: This guy's tough... get the eye-twitch going. You need to look *scary*.
Zh: 这家伙很强硬……他的眼睛开始抽搐了。你得看起来*唬人*一些。

En: *What do you think you're doing?* you hear, but his mouth isn't moving. It's the tension in his shoulders speaking.
Zh: *你以为自己在干什么呢？*你听到了这句话，但他的嘴并没有动，是他紧绷的肩膀说出了这句话。

En: What are *you* doing?
Zh: *你*在做什么？

En: *I can do this all night long*, his clenched fists say, their knuckles turning white. *I'm young. You're fifty thousand years old*.
Zh: *我没空跟你耗下去*，他握紧拳头说着，指关节都变白了。*我还年轻，你都有五万岁了*。

En: 'No, you're not. You're frail and your nerves are frayed. Old sadness weighs you down.' He refuses to budge.
Zh: “不，你不会。你的身体枯竭、神经衰弱、旧日的悲伤使你日益消沉。”他拒绝让步。

En: I'll go listen to the tape.
Zh: “我会去听磁带的。”

En: You do that, lawman.
Zh: “去吧，执法官。”

En: This is where you say your bit. A broad-shouldered man points at you with a beer can.
Zh: “要说什么就说吧。”一个肩膀宽阔的男人用啤酒瓶指着你。

En: Over here! She snaps her fingers.
Zh: “在这里！”她打了个响指。

En: We need to talk about the man hanged out back.
Zh: “我们得谈谈那个吊人。”

En: You sure took your time, huh? He looks to Titus for approval. "Waited for him to get real ripe and pretty for you?"
Zh: “你还真是悠闲，嗯？”他朝提图斯求证。“等到他烂透了，变得这么漂亮了才来？”

En: Oh, this is about him? He looks around. "A real looker, that one..."
Zh: “哦，你们是因为他来的？”他看了一圈。“那家伙，长得挺帅哦……”

En: Oh, he was a real pretty boy, hanging up there... Letting out that *pretty-boy smell*.
Zh: “哦，他可真是好看极了，挂在那里……散发着一股*美男的气味*。”

En: I can't for the life of me understand why you did it. He spreads his arms. "I mean... I would have just left him up there. You must really like cleaning up other people's shit."
Zh: “我怎么都想不通你为什么要这么做。”他展开双臂。“我是说……我应该就会把他留在那里。你一定很喜欢清理别人的烂摊子吧。”

En: Easy, boys... These janitors have a helluva job cut out for them. I mean, I wouldn't go in there for a million... He slaps his forehead.
Zh: “冷静点，兄弟们……这两个清洁工任务艰巨呢。我是说，我无论如何都不会靠近那里的……”他拍了拍前额。

En: Oh, this is about him? He looks around. "A real looker, that one..."
Zh: “哦，你们是因为他来的？”他看了一圈。“那家伙，长得挺帅哦……”

En: You're sure taking your time. Waiting for him to get ripe and pretty for you, huh?
Zh: “你还真是悠闲，等到他烂透了，变得这么漂亮了才来，嗯？”

En: Oh, he's a real pretty boy by now. REAL hot stuff! Glen nods along. "Letting out that *pretty-boy smell*."
Zh: “哦，他现在可变成一个真正的美男子了，很性感呢！”格伦不断地点头。“散发着一股*美男的气味*。”

En: TIME TO GO TO WORK IN THE SHIT FACTORY!!!
Zh: “该去破烂工厂上班了！！”

En: You might want to start asking your questions now. It's not going to get better than this.
Zh: “或许你现在就可以开始提问了，情况不会变得更好了。”

En: Questions? To hell with that. Get right up in the big dick's face -- physical confrontation.
Zh: 提问？见鬼去吧。站到那个混蛋的跟前——用肉体对抗他。

En: These guys are so macho they're ready to confess to first-degree murder. Ask if it was them.
Zh: 这些家伙大男子主义到足以承认一级谋杀了，问问是不是他们。

En: Wow... the RCM sent us some *big dick cops*.
Zh: “哇哦……RCM派了个*狠角色*过来。”

En: Yeah, look at the big dick on that cop. Can't tell a dick that big what to do.
Zh: “是啊，看看条子身上的狠劲儿。可不能对这种狠人瞎逼逼了。”

En: Yeah, gave them real nice big dicks.
Zh: “是啊，给他们点厉害尝尝。”

En: Yeah... Khm, anyways... He wraps it up.
Zh: “是啊……咳，总之……”他住了嘴。

En: Must be something in the water in Jamrock, he says thoughtfully.
Zh: “一定是加姆洛克的水里有什么东西，”他若有所思地说。

En: *Real* big dick cops... Look at them! Reckless, swinging in the wind.
Zh: “*真正的*狠角色……看看他们吧！冒冒失失，身陷囹圄。”

En: The coppernado is back... He smirks. "What do you want?"
Zh: “死条子回来了……”他假笑起来。“你想干什么？”

En: Look, the insane police officer is back. He takes a sip of his beer.
Zh: “看啊，那个疯子警官回来了。”他呷了一口啤酒。

En: The clowns are still hanging around... What is it now?
Zh: “小丑还在附近转悠呢……现在又怎么了？”

En: It's you again... He acknowledges you gruffly. "What is it?"
Zh: “又是你……”他粗鲁地向你打了个招呼。“怎么了？”

En: One lonesome clown... What a sad sight. He sighs. "What do you want?"
Zh: “一个孤单的小丑……看着就悲哀。”他叹了口气。“你想干什么？”

En: Engage eye-twitch.
Zh: 眼睛开始抽搐。

En: I didn't have what in me? To arrest her?
Zh: “没想到我还有什么本事？逮捕她？”

En: Yeah, you ruthless son of a bitch. He nods approvingly. "I certainly didn't have it in me -- I always let the pretty ones go. I'm soft like that."
Zh: “是啊，你这个残忍的混蛋。”他赞许地点点头。“我肯定是不行的——我总是会放美人一马，就是如此心软。”

En: Precinct 57th's finest scans the room, leaving the speaking to you: He trusts you -- maybe against his better judgement, but he does.
Zh: 57分局最好的警察扫视了一圈房间，把询问工作交给了你：他信任你——或许这违背于他做出的更好的判断，但他确实这么做了。

En: Open and close your fists.
Zh: 张开又握紧拳头。

En: He hunches forward. His right hand starts tapping a quick little rhythm on his thigh: tap tap tap.
Zh: 他隆起身子，右手开始在他的大腿上敲着一段紧凑的节奏：哒哒哒。

En: With all the abuse I've taken I'm still walking, talking and ready to get into this fight.
Zh: 尽管我遭受了那么多侮辱，但我仍能走路、说话并且随时可以投入这场战斗。

En: At my age you will be dead, you're impulsive. You'll get in a fight you won't win.
Zh: 到了我这个年纪，你注定会死去。你太冲动了，会卷入一场你赢不了的斗争。

En: In that time I have killed. More than once. I can feel it in me. *The ugly.*
Zh: 到了那个时候我一定已经开了杀戒，而且不止一次。我能感觉到那种*丑恶*在我体内。

En: Probably, the scars on his arms say. "I need to be like this to lead them -- something *you* would understand."
Zh: “大概吧，”他手臂上的伤疤说。“我必须得像这样去领导他们——*你*会明白的。”

En: *Good*, the twitch in his eye replies. *I've beaten a man so hard he doesn't move. Or maybe I kept his head underwater? Hard to say which, but you can feel the *ugly* in me too.*
Zh: *好极了*，他眼中的抽动回复道。*我曾经把一个人打到无法动弹，或者我把他的头按到水里了？很难说是哪一个，但你可能感受到我体内的*丑恶*。*

En: You failed here -- a weakness is a weakness, not a strength. Time to speak now. With words.
Zh: 你失败了——弱点就是弱点，不会变成优势。是时候用语言交流了。

En: With begrudging respect he leans back. The room around you is silent. Time to speak now. With words.
Zh: 他带着勉强的敬意向后靠了靠。房间周围一片寂静，是时候用语言交流了。

En: The moment ends. With begrudging respect the man leans back. Time to say something.
Zh: 那一刻结束了，男人带着勉强的敬意向后靠了靠。是时候开口了。

En: Do not explain or defend yourself, attack.
Zh: 不要解释或维护自己，进攻。

En: Or... don't get in his face *yet*. Wait. You may need to *establish* yourself in his eyes later. If you fail, doing this may give you a fresh chance!
Zh: 或者……*先*别惹他。等等，你或许得在他面前*证明自己*。如果你失败了，这么做可能会给你第二次机会！

En: So I've listened to the tape and I'm going to confront Klaasje with it.
Zh: “我已经听过录音了，我准备找卡拉洁核实一下。”

En: Do a headcount first -- connect these men to the tracks you saw in the yard -- chances are they're going to match.
Zh: 先清点人数——把这些人和你在院子里发现的脚印联系起来——很有可能会对上。

En: Titus, let's go over the night of the murder again.
Zh: “提图斯，我们再过一遍谋杀案发生的那一晚。”

En: The small man mimes blowing his brains out. You see his rat-face contorted in mock despair, his fingers touching his temple.
Zh: 这个小个子男人装模作样地朝自己的脑袋开了一枪。你看着他那老鼠脸因为假装绝望而扭曲着，手指还摸着自己的太阳穴。

En: There's laughter in the room. Try not to kill yourself this time.
Zh: 房间里充斥着笑声，这次可不要再作死了。

En: I did think that, yes -- that she's a *little* crazy...
Zh: “是的，我确实这么认为——她是*有点*疯狂……”

En: Crazy? What are you talking about? She was very lucid.
Zh: “疯狂？你在说什么？她非常清醒。”

En: Cut the bullshit. She told me the truth.
Zh: “废话少说，她把真相都告诉我了。”

En: This is a diversion. Stay on track.
Zh: 这是在转移话题。保持住你的节奏。

En: A whore, huh? A lot of that going around lately. From the mouths of *distressed* men. Whatever the case, you've hit a nerve. He's angry -- no, more than that -- he feels *betrayed*.
Zh: 一个荡妇，哈？最近有很多这样的人。从*痛苦的*男人口中说出来。无论如何，我们说到点子上了。他很生气——不，不仅于此——他感觉被*背叛*了。

En: She wasn't raped. The lieutenant's voice is beginning to betray his agitation. "The witness' statements were very clear."
Zh: “她没有被强暴。”警督的声音开始流露出他的不安。“证人的陈述非常明了。”

En: Lawman... he says through clenched teeth. "... I am at the end of my *goddamn* rope with you. I fucking told you not to push her!"
Zh: “执法官……”他咬着牙说到。“……我对你已经*忍无可忍*了。我他妈的告诉过你不要逼她！”

En: And you went and pushed her. Something breaks in him. He takes a step closer and says: "I am going to... fucking..."
Zh: “但你还是去逼供她了。”他有点不对劲儿，他上前一步说：“我他妈的……要……”

En: His hands become fists.
Zh: 他的手握成了拳头。

En: Goddamnit... you fucking questioned her, didn't you? I told you not to push her! I fucking told you!
Zh: “可恶……你他妈的去审问她了，是不是？我告诉过你不要逼她！我他妈告诉过你了！”

En: TITUS HARDIE! Her voice rings through the room like a warning shot.
Zh: “提图斯·哈迪！”她的声音如同一声警示枪响彻了整个房间。

En: Evrart *personally* sent me to take care of this. If this goes south we'll all be in the shit -- but you, Titus Hardie, are going to be buried. Am I understood?
Zh: “艾弗拉特*亲自*派我来处理这件事的。如果这事搞砸了，我们都要遭殃——但是你，提图斯·哈迪，很快就会待在墓里。听明白了吗？”

En: Success. Titus backs off. Fists down, everybody.
Zh: 成功。提图斯让步了，大家都放下拳头。

En: Goddamn *no one* tells you to put your fists down!
Zh: 该死的*没有人*告诉你要放下拳头！

En: The room is so quiet you could hear a pin drop. The rest of the cafeteria has gone quiet too.
Zh: 房间里静得连针掉在地上的声音都能听进，餐厅其它地方也都安静了下来。

En: Goddamnit... He clenches his fists. "I show you all kinds of kindness, let you snoop around in my town -- and then I ask you one thing..."
Zh: “可恶……”他紧握双拳。“我对你百般仁慈，允许你在我的城市里四处窥探——我只要求了你一件事……”

En: And what do you do? You go and push her anyway! Something breaks in him. He takes a step closer. "I am going to... fucking..."
Zh: “而你做了什么？你过去把她逼走了！”他有点不对劲儿，他上前一步说：“我他妈的……要……”

En: His hands become fists.
Zh: 他的手握成了拳头。

En: Now *that* is intriguing. You had me at Doorgunner.
Zh: *这*倒是很有趣了。你口中的那个门炮手。

En: This is their last play -- this tape. Their story is in tatters, a mess. It might be nice to listen to -- but at this point, you don't need to.
Zh: 这是他们最后一出戏——这盘磁带。他们的故事支离破碎，一片混乱。听起来不错——但到了这个份上，也没什么必要了。

En: Why should I care about the tape? You lied to me.
Zh: “我为什么要关心什么磁带？你骗了我。”

En: You don't care about *evidence*? His eye twitches. "The fuck are you a cop for then?"
Zh: “你不关心*证据*吗？”他的眼睛抽动着。“那你他妈当什么条子？”

En: Pigs, T -- they don't care about getting the truth, they care about getting convictions. They're fucking keeping score on their bulletin boards.
Zh: “死条子，提——他们不关心真相，只想要定罪。他们在自己该死的布告牌上记比分呢。”

En: I won't be on your bulletin board. If you don't listen to the tape we got *nothing* to talk about.
Zh: “我不会出现在你的布告牌上的。如果你不听录音，我们就*没什么*可谈的了。”

En: You think we go into this shit deaf and dumb? You RCM aren't the only ones who know how to bug people -- there's no university degree for that.
Zh: “你以为我们瞎吗？你们RCM不是唯一知道如何窃听别人的人——这可不需要大学学位。”

En: Actually there are a few -- cryptoanalyst, radio officer...
Zh: 其实还是有一些需要的——密码分析、通信官……

En: Actually, there are numerous degrees you can get in signals intelligence.
Zh: “其实，智能通讯方面还是有很多学位的。”

En: So you've bugged them? How?
Zh: “所以你窃听了他们？怎么做到的？”

En: Yeah, like you have one? Smartass... He looks to his left. There's a beer there that he forgot.
Zh: “是吗，那你有吗？白痴……”他看向左手边，那里有一瓶被他遗忘的啤酒。

En: Understood -- you've listened in on their communications. He takes a little note. "How long?"
Zh: “明白了——你监听了他们通讯系统。”他记了几笔。“有多久了？”

En: We have machines. He nods. "We're in logistics -- how do you think a harbour works? It's advanced stuff."
Zh: “我们有设备。”他点点头。“我们在物流系统——你以为港口是如何运作的？都是高级货。”

En: Since way before their chief started taking swing lessons.
Zh: “早在他们的老大学会挥杆前就开始了。”

En: Which one of you is doing this advanced radio work then?
Zh: “那么是你们中的哪一位在做这个高级的无线电工作呢？”

En: I think I get the picture.
Zh: “我想我明白了。”

En: It's not advanced, the heavy man wheezes. "You're just holed up in a coop all day, writing down what they say. It gets hot as hell in there."
Zh: “并不高级，”胖男人气喘吁吁。“你只是成天缩在笼子里，把他们说的话记下来。这笼子里热的要命。”

En: Don't put yourself down, Angus. It's important work. The chief picks his beer back up -- to offer a silent toast.
Zh: “别这么说自己，安格斯。这是很重要的工作。”头领拿起他的啤酒——无声地向他致敬。

En: Yeah, man, you're like a radio genius or something. Those notes are some in depth stuff. Indexes and shit.
Zh: “是啊，老兄。你就是个无线电天才。那些注释非常有深度，索引什么的。”

En: Beer.
Zh: 啤酒。

En: How long have you been listening to their communications?
Zh: “你监听他们的通讯系统多久了？”

En: Things got nice and quiet after that.
Zh: “从那以后，一切就平静下来了。”

En: Yeah -- try not to whack off to it.
Zh: “是啊——话别说的太早。”

En: Hold up, her face -- what did it look like?
Zh: “慢着，她的脸——什么表情？”

En: One third of humanity will be *culled* -- and you're worried about a single lambe?!
Zh: “三分之一的人类都会被*屠杀*——而你还在担心一个马屁精？！”

En: That was the *normal* thing to do. She was a flight risk. I'm a cop.
Zh: “这才是*正常*流程，她有逃跑的风险，而我是一个警察。”

En: Yeah, like when we let old Clancy's widow walk.
Zh: “是啊，就像我们放过老克兰西的遗孀一样。”

En: Or Denisse, the diner girl! The milk rack on that one... He says, almost elegiacally.
Zh: “或是德妮丝，那个餐厅的服务生！她的胸……”他几乎是哀伤地说着。

En: Can't say I wasn't sad to see her go -- our Klaasje there -- her face when your friend walked her out. Goddamn... He shakes his head.
Zh: “不得不说她离开的时候我真的很难过——我们的卡拉洁——你朋友送她出去时她的表情。该死……”他摇了摇头。

En: Like she was walking to the gallows.
Zh: “就好像她在走向绞刑架一样。”

En: He avoided looking her straight in the eye -- you would've too.
Zh: 他避开了她的视线——你也会如此。

En: Oh my god, she had a sad face? I'm so sorry I made her sad.
Zh: “哦天呐，她的表情很难过吗？我很抱歉让她伤心了。”

En: What? He doesn't understand.
Zh: “什么？”他不明白。

En: Goddamn you, Sunboy... He says quietly. "I guess there's nothing to do about it now. I just hope she can game her way through the system and come out the other way."
Zh: “该死的，阳光男孩……”他平静地说。“我想现在已经无能为力了。只希望她能在体制内找到出路，然后从另一条路走出来。”

En: Nothing to do about it now I guess. He nods slowly. "I just hope she can game her way through the system."
Zh: “现在已经无能为力了。”他缓缓点头。“只希望她能在体制内找到出路。”

En: Har-Mageddon is upon us. She's better off in the holding-pen once the Blood Rains come down.

En: The window might be closing. The more beers they get in them, the less cooperative they will be.

En: Let's try this again -- Kim, gimme your gun.
Zh: “我们再试一次吧——金，把你的枪给我。”

En: No, he whispers, not even looking at you. "Not going to happen."
Zh: “不行，”他低声说着，连看都没看你一眼。“不可能的。”

En: What happened? You got your ass handed to you. You should have made the gun work! How are you going to get out of this now?

En: Calm. Normal. Try to forget this *little hiccup*.
Zh: 冷静点，正常点。试着忘记这个*小小的挫折*吧。

En: Okay, enough about it then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好的，那就先到这里吧。”（总结。）

En: Yeah -- whatever.
Zh: “行啊——随便。”

En: Speak for yourself, buddy. He puts the beer down. "I've been running this outfit for ten years now. You shoulda seen Martinaise *before* we started. It was like Jamrock now."
Zh: “那是你自己的问题，伙计。”他放下啤酒。“我管理这里已经十年了，你真该看看在我们过来*之前*的马丁内斯。现在这里快跟加姆洛克差不多了。”

En: That's fucking right, no need to hide anymore -- I'm a proud Revacholian!
Zh: “太他妈对了，没必要再躲躲藏藏的了——我是个自豪的瑞瓦肖人！”

En: Why? You have a gun.

En: He repeats: "The *law* handled it, alright?"
Zh: 他重复着：“是*法律*解决的，对吧？”

En: All the while talking racist shit -- don't think we aren't watching, *fascha*. People here trust us. We're getting *complaints*.
Zh: “一直在说种族主义的鬼话——别以为我们不知道，*法斯卡*。这里的人都很信任我们，我们听到*抱怨*了。”

En: Well... The rest of that nancy stuff was really just there to make the gun thing more appealing. But it might work anyway.
Zh: 那么……其余那些稀奇的东西都只是为了让手枪事件更加吸引人。但无论如何，有可能行得通。

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Kim, I need your gun."
Zh: （转向警督。）“金，我需要你的枪。”

En: Why? You have your own gun.
Zh: “为什么？你自己有枪啊。”

En: This is not what he thinks it is -- this is a great idea.
Zh: 这不是他所想象的样子——这是个很棒的主意。

En: I'm afraid it's not what *you* think it is either.
Zh: 恐怕也不是*你*所想的那样。

En: Establish authority, yes! AUTHORITY! Feverish thoughts race through your mind.
Zh: 树立权威，没错！权威！狂热的想法掠过你的头脑。

En: This time it's going to work. You already ruled out the first, *bad* option that didn't.
Zh: 这次一定会成功的，你已经排除了第一个*糟糕*的选项。

En: Okay, this time... is FOR REAL. You have *two* bad options ruled out -- the odds are on your side!
Zh: 好吧，这次……是来真的了。你已经排除了*两个*糟糕的选项——胜算站在你这边！

En: Live in a bar. Sit on their fat asses like they own the place, while the neighbourhood goes to shit, corpses hang from trees, kids shoot up...
Zh: “住在酒吧里。坐在他们的肥屁股上，仿佛是这里的主人一样。与此同时周边环境变得一团糟，尸体被吊在树上，孩子们被枪击……”

En: EVERYONE SHUT UP!!! Titus's loud baritone echoes back from the mess hall walls and the entire cafeteria falls silent.
Zh: “所有人都闭嘴！！！”提图斯响亮的男中音回荡在餐厅的墙壁上，整个餐厅一片寂静。

En: Fuck you man! his eyes are fixed on Kim. "You didn't see the place before we got here. Fucking... graffito everywhere..."
Zh: “去你的吧！他的眼睛紧紧盯着金。你根本没见过我们来之前这地方的样子。到处都是……该死的涂鸦……”

En: Glen...
Hey, we don't have to fucking take this shit...
Zh: “嘿，我们根本没必要忍受……”

En: Kim, I'm only getting total garbage here. (Whisper) "Please help."
Zh: “金，我得到的回复根本没用。”（低声）“请帮帮我。”

En: The lieutenant nods and turns to address the room: "You know what? You guys *must* be the cops. Because this is what cops do, right?"
Zh: 警督点点头，转身朝着房间说：“知道吗？你们*一定*是条子吧。因为这就是条子的工作，对吧？”

En: Establish authority in their eyes.
Zh: 树立在他们眼中的权威。

En: Confronted him with drug trade.
Zh: 质问毒品交易案。

En: Strange reaction to bullet.
Zh: 对子弹的奇怪反应。

En: Evrart said cooperate.
Zh: 艾弗拉特说过要配合。

En: EIGHTH HARDIE
Zh: 第八个哈迪

En: Pointed out Titus' attitude.
Zh: 指出提图斯的态度问题。

En: Physical stand-off.
Zh: 肉体对峙。

En: Warned them of the mercenary tribunal.
Zh: 警告他们提防雇佣军审判。

En: First you tell me someone's been *raped*, and then you don't say *who*. That's bullshit! (Stomp your feet.)
Zh: “你先是告诉我有人被*强暴*了，却又不愿意说是*谁*。简直是废话！”（跺脚。）

En: I'm the only thing keeping this town from going to *hell* and you're not helping. (Point your finger.)
Zh: “我是唯一能让这座小镇免于陷入*地狱*的人，而你还不愿意配合。”（伸出手指。）

En: That's it? That's all I got?
Zh: 就这样？就这些？

En: Did you already try the gun thing? I hear the gun thing is excellent, and has great results.
Zh: 你已经试过枪的事了吗？我听说用枪挺棒的，而且效果很好呢。

En: You're probably right. The others are only there for filler -- to make the gun thing *pop*.
Zh: 也许你是对的。其他人只是为了充数——让枪的事更*显眼*。

En: If Kim was here, he would know what to say.
Zh: “如果金在场，他会知道应该说什么。”

En: You have *so* many bad options ruled out it's *insane*! There is no way in the world you won't do it this time!
Zh: 你排除了*那么*多糟糕的选项真是太*疯狂*了！这次什么都不能阻止你成功了！

En: Yeah, aren't you listening? It's done. Finito!
Zh: “是啊，你没在听吗？已经结束了。完结！”

En: Stop shitting your pants. You don't *need* to know, alright? We took care of it.
Zh: “别再丢人现眼了。你不*需要*知道，明白吗？我们已经解决了。”

En: You're not the law. I am the law!
Zh: “你不是法律，我才是法律！”

En: No, asshole -- *you're* nobody. *I'm* the law!
Zh: “不，混蛋——*你*就是个无名小卒。*我*才是法律！”

En: I guess we're both the law around here, if you start to think about it.
Zh: “如果你冷静下来想想，我们都是这里的法律。”

En: For god's sake, stop SHOUTING! The man behind the counter shouts. "Stop crashing into people in my establishment! Stop provoking those oafs!"
Zh: “看在老天爷的份上，别喊了！”柜台后面的人喊道。“别在我的店里撞人了！别再惹那群笨蛋了！”

En: Maybe you're right. Maybe I'm not the law after all. (To yourself.)  "Maybe everyone's been lying to me..."
Zh: “也许你是对的，也许我根本就不是法律。”（对自己说。）“也许大家都在骗我……”

En: What, now you're in some kind of *personal crisis* -- right in front of us? He seems taken aback by this sudden display of weakness. "Take this esoteric shit out of my bar!"
Zh: “怎么，现在你就当着我们的面——陷入某种*个人危机*了？”他似乎被这种突如其来的示弱吓了一跳。“少在我的酒吧里故作高深了。”

En: I assure you, there is no crisis, the lieutenant tries to salvage the situation. "We had questions, that's all."
Zh: “我向你保证，并没有什么危机，”警督试图挽救局面。“我们有些问题想问，仅此而已。”

En: For god's sake, stop SHOUTING! The man behind the counter shouts. "Stop breaking things in my establishment! Stop provoking those oafs!"
Zh: “看在老天爷的份上，别喊了！”柜台后面的人喊道。“别碰坏我店里的东西了！别再惹那群笨蛋了！”

En: They're not going to give you *anything*, can't you see?! I can't have a *police interrogation* in here. People are trying to eat...
Zh: “他们不会告诉你*任何东西*的，你看不出来吗？我不能让警察在这里做*审讯*了，客人们还要吃饭呢……”

En: Sounds like someone's dam broke.
Zh: 听上去好像有人的水坝坏掉了。

En: This doesn't concern you, cafeteria manager!
Zh: “这与你无关，餐厅经理！”

En: Shut up, barman!
Zh: “闭嘴，招待！”

En: I'm gonna tear your stupid hairy head off, Garte!
Zh: “我要把你愚蠢的长毛脑袋扯下来，加尔特！”

En: Wait, *what*?!
Easy, babes. What is this? Titus spreads his arms, smiling gently. "I can't have a slap-fight in my bar. It's embarrassing. Take it outside, will you?"\n
Zh: “冷静，宝贝。这算什么？”提图斯张开双臂，温柔的微笑着。“在我的酒吧里可不能互扇巴掌，这也太丢人了。要打出去打，行吗？”

En: Easy, babes. What is this? Titus spreads his arms, smiling gently. "I can't have a slap-fight in my bar. It's embarrassing."
Zh: “冷静，宝贝。这算什么？”提图斯张开双臂，温柔的微笑着。“在我的酒吧里可不能互扇巴掌，这也太丢人了。”

En: We are deep, *deep* in farce territory, the lieutenant whispers to you, taking a step closer to the door. "We need to cool off and re-engage, *later*."
Zh: “我们已经*深深*陷入闹剧的泥潭了，”警督低声对你说到，又朝着门口走近了一步。“我们需要冷静一下，*之后*重头再来。”

En: What is your...?
Of *course* it concerns me if you make this kind of...
Zh: “你这么做的话，*当然*关我的事……”

En: There is little left to salvage... Did I not tell you to pick the *right* option?
Zh: 几乎已经无力回天了……我不是告诉过你要选择*正确*的选项了吗？

En: Everyone can see you picked some wrong options. Next time you have to come up with better things to say.
Zh: 大家都能看出来你做了一些错误的选择，下次你必须想出更好的说辞。

En: Stop yelling about the god damn law in my establishment! the cafeteria manager interrupts with his voice raised to a squeak. "We get it, you're *all* the law!"
Zh: “别在我店里大喊该死的法律了！”餐厅经理用刺耳的声音打断了争吵。“我们明白了，你们*都是*法律！”

En: Sheesh... Titus wipes his brow. "Okay, we get it, no need to piss in my drink!" He lowers his voice. "I preferred Sylvie. What the fuck even happened to her?"
Zh: “真是够了……”提图斯擦了擦额头。“好吧，我们知道了，别在我的酒里下料！”他放低了声音。“我更喜欢塞尔维，她到底怎么了？”

En: Let's just all cool off, the lieutenant says, trying to salvage the situation. "We had some questions, that's all."
Zh: “大家都冷静一下，”警督试图挽救局面。“我们有些问题想问，仅此而已。”

En: And what exactly is it you've been doing that's so god damn special -- shitting yourself in front of us? He leans in. "Going around harassing kids on the street? Kids who've done nothing wrong?"
Zh: “而你真正做的事真的太他妈特别了——在我们面前吓尿了？”他靠过去。“到处骚扰街上的孩子，没有任何过错的孩子？”

En: *Fascha* -- that's short for fascist.
Zh: *法斯卡*——那是法西斯主义者的简称。

En: I haven't said anything racist!
Zh: “我没有说任何种族歧视的言论！”

En: (Lie.) "I don't even have a gun!"
Zh: （说谎。）“我甚至都没有枪！”

En: I don't even have a gun!
Zh: “我甚至都没有枪！”

En: I haven't harassed any kids!
Zh: “我没有骚扰过任何孩子！”

En: I'm not shitting myself!
Zh: “我才没有尿裤子呢！”

En: Of course you are. He takes a sip. "E, didn't Carly work in a diaper shop?"
Zh: “你当然有。”他抿了一口啤酒。“尤金，卡莉不是在尿布店工作吗？”

En: Of course you have! You've been calling people kipt left and right, inciting *race violence* in my town.
Zh: “你当然有！你一直在我的镇子上煽动*种族暴力*，到处说人是吉皮特。

En: Carly said they've been trying to set up a *race rally*, whatever the fuck that means. Trying to get the *kipts* out of Revachol, before the economy goes to shit...
Zh: “卡莉说他们一直试图组织一场*种族集会*，天知道那是什么意思。在经济陷入困境之前，设法让那些*吉皮特*离开瑞瓦肖……”

En: I'm not racist! All I care about is Revachol and it's people and if some foreigner thinks...
Zh: “我才不是种族主义者！我只关心瑞瓦肖和它的人民，如果某些外国人认为……”

En: That's a lie! There is no 'race rally'!
Zh: “那是个谎言！根本没有什么‘种族集会’！”

En: I'm not a racist. Just look at my partner!
Zh: “我才不是种族主义者，你看看我的搭档！”

En: Shit, boss, he actually *is* racist!
Zh: “妈的，老大。他*真的*是个种族主义者！”

En: Of course he's racist. Look at how fat he is.
Zh: “他当然是种族主义者了，看看他有多胖吧。”

En: The lieutenant turns to you. "Unless you had questions, we should get some air."
Zh: 警督转向你。“除非你还有别的问题，我们应该出去透透气了。”

En: Sure there is. Carly said they're bulk-purchasing confetti. Ribbons, too, and loudspeakers... He shakes his head. "And *fireworks*! Carly works at a carnival shop, you see."
Zh: “当然有了。卡莉说他们大量采购了彩纸、缎带还有扩音器……”他摇摇头。“还有*烟火*！卡莉在一家狂欢节商品店工作。”

En: Get the fuck out of here, you racist carnie. Titus points at the door. "There'll be no race rally in my town."
Zh: “滚出去，你这个种族歧视的小丑。”提图斯指着大门。“我的镇子决不允许有种族集会。”

En: What happened? the lieutenant half-whispers, "We got absolutely *wiped*. Let's go before it gets worse."
Zh: “发生什么事了？”警督小声说，“我们彻底被*打败*了。在情况变得更加糟糕之前，我们走吧。”

En: What's going on, are you a racist now? Is the rally real? Please don't set up any rally, it'll make you look awful!
Zh: 怎么回事，你现在是种族主义者了吗？集会是真的吗？请不要组织任何集会，那会让你看上去很糟糕的！

En: There is no rally, c'mon. Just ask questions, you need to put this race rally garbage in the rear-view mirror.
Zh: 拜托，根本没有什么集会。直接问问题吧，你得把种族集会这件事抛到脑后。

En: He's not racist and there is no rally. He turns to you. "There is no Carly either, it's a joke. Let's move on -- or get some air."
Zh: “他不是个种族主义者，也没有任何集会。”他转向你。“也没有卡莉这个人，这就是个笑话。我们继续询问——或者出去透透气。”

En: What happened? You got your ass handed to you. Always go with the gun! How are you going to get out of this now?
Zh: 发生什么事了？你这是自作自受。永远记得要随身带枪！你现在打算怎么脱身？

En: Get the fuck out of here, you race war asshole. We're not gonna march in your rally, stop trying to recruit us.
Zh: “给我滚出去，你这种族战争贩子。我们是不会参与你们的集会的，别再试图拉拢我们了。”

En: You got shredded to pieces there! Should've gone with the gun, what were you thinking?!
Zh: 你快被撕成碎片了！应该带着枪的，你是怎么想的？！

En: What are you talking about? Guns are the only thing you have! Waving them around old ladies' faces. Trying to gun down kids...
Zh: “你在说什么？你只剩下枪了！朝着老太太的脸上挥舞着它们，还试图枪杀一个孩子……”

En: Carly said they've been trying to set up a mass execution. He shakes his head in disdain. "Bulk-purchasing guns. Ammunition, too."
Zh: “卡莉说他们一直试图策划一场大规模处刑。”他轻蔑地摇了摇头。“大批量采购了枪支和弹药。”

En: I can believe that. He nods. "Carly works in a gun shop, you see."
Zh: “我相信你说的。”他点点头。“要知道，卡莉在一家枪械店工作。”

En: Oh my god, The Art -- they're using it against you. This is all a twisted phantasie!
Zh: 我的天呐，艺术——他们用来对付你了，这一切都是扭曲的幻影！

En: There's no mass-execution, officer. He looks at you, concerned. "Just ask questions and don't get sidetracked."
Zh: “没有什么大规模处刑，警官。”他担忧地看着你。“问问题就行，别跑偏了。”

En: Please don't set up a mass execution. You'll never be able to pull it off. The logistics alone are insane... Just ask questions. Be a normal cop.
Zh: 请不要组织大规模处刑，你永远不可能成功的。单是后勤保障就够疯狂的了……问你的问题，做个正常的警察吧。

En: So you keep saying -- and I do want to believe you, but it's getting harder and harder. He takes a sip. "E, didn't Carly say something?"
Zh: “你一直这么说——我真的很想相信你，不过现在越来越困难了。”他抿了一口啤酒。“尤金，卡莉没说什么吗？”

En: Yeah, Carly said you've been hanging around the day-care centre. In the bushes, man. By the jungle-gym. Tryna' set up some weird field trip to your station, that's in your home...
Zh: “这个嘛，卡莉说你一直在日托中心附近转悠。躲在灌木丛里，儿童攀登架旁边。打算带孩子们去做一些奇怪的实地考察，目的地是你家里……”

En: I can believe that. That rings true to me. He nods. "Carly works in the kindergarten, right?"
Zh: “我相信你说的，这听上去很真实。”他点点头。“卡莉不是在幼儿园工作吗？”

En: Oh my god, not this again! No! Your cheeks are burning red and you're sweating profusely...
Zh: 我的天哪，别再这样了！不要！你的脸颊涨红，还出了很多汗……

En: There is no Carly and there is no kindergarten, the lieutenant says to you, concerned you might not get it. "It's a joke and we're getting derailed."
Zh: “根本没有什么卡莉或者幼儿园。”警督对你说着，以防你理解不了。“这是个笑话，而且我们偏题了。”

En: Please stop hanging around the jungle-gym. Whatever you're doing there is making you look awful. And it's not helping you with these guys at all.
Zh: 请不要在儿童攀登架旁边闲晃了。无论你在那里做什么都会让你看起来很糟糕，而且这对你和这些家伙之间没有任何帮助。

En: Yeah, Carly said you've been hanging around the adult diaper shop. Browsing the aisles, too ashamed to ask for assistance.
Zh: “这个嘛，卡莉说你一直在成人尿布店附近转悠。只看不买，还羞于寻求帮助。”

En: Now *that* I can believe. He points down. "And then there's the matter of the floor being completely covered in faeces."
Zh: “*这个*我就信了。”他指着下面。“而且现在地板上全是排泄物。”

En: This Carly is a made up person. It's nothing but a mean joke. They just want to see how you wriggle out of it.
Zh: 这位卡莉是个虚构的人，不过是个刻薄的玩笑。他们只是想看看你打算如何脱身。

En: Yes -- shit, faeces, poop -- scatological jokes, the lieutenant concludes dryly . "If you don't have anything more to say, then we're going to go back to asking questions."
Zh: “是啊——粪便、排泄物、便便——污秽的笑话，”警督干巴巴地总结。“如果你没有更多想说的，我们继续回到提问环节吧。”

En: You don't have to be the butt of every joke. You're making the lieutenant look bad. Just stick to what you know. Guns.
Zh: 你不必成为每个笑话的笑柄，这让警督面上无光了。坚持你的观点，枪。

En: What? Who -- me? What is it?
Zh: “什么？谁？——我吗？这是什么？”

En: Yes -- you. Stop talking out loud, you sound like you're unravelling.
Zh: 是的——就是你。别再大声说话了，听上去感觉你要崩溃了。

En: What in the name of *fuck* are you doing?
Zh: “你*他妈*在干什么？”

En: But... The lieutenant looks worried, he whispers: "I don't want to give you my gun. Not after the last time."
Zh: “可是……”警督看上去很忧虑，他低声说：“我不想把我的枪给你，特别是经过上一次之后。”

En: But... The lieutenant looks worried, he whispers: "I don't want to give you my gun."
Zh: “可是……”警督看上去很忧虑，他低声说：“我不想把我的枪给你。”

En: The fuck is happening, Titus? What are they whispering about?
Zh: “这他妈是怎么回事，提图斯？他们在嘀咕什么呢？”

En: Trust me, Kim. I am merely flesh.
Zh: “相信我，金。我不过是具血肉之躯罢了。

En: There is no other way.
Zh: “没有别的办法了。”

En: I know what I'm doing. It's not my first day on the police-job.
Zh: “我知道自己在做什么，这不是我第一天当警察了。”

En: I'm not scared anymore. I know what I have to do.
Zh: “我不再害怕了，我知道自己的职责。”

En: Merely *what*...? He doesn't quite catch that last word. A brief moment passes in silence, then...
Zh: “不过*是什么*……？”他没听清后面的话，片刻的沉默过后……

En: The pressure in the room grows to a boiling point.
Zh: 房间里的压力上升至沸点。

En: Be careful -- it's loaded. He unholsters and gives you his firearm. It feels oddly light and buzzing in your hand. Like a funny toy.
Zh: “小心点——已经上膛了。”他解开枪套，把枪递给了你。它在你手中出奇的轻巧新奇，就像一个有趣的玩具。

En: Your fingers wrap around the handle, the index slides into the trigger loop. Do it. Go on!
Zh: 你的手指包裹着枪柄，食指滑进了扳机环。这是这样，开枪吧！

En: Good. Now put it in your mouth.
Zh: 很好，现在把它放进嘴里吧。

En: I want to turn back.
Zh: “我想回去了。”

En: There is no 'back'.
Zh: 没有‘回头路’了。

En: Detective. He thinks of putting his hand on your shoulder, then doesn't. "We don't have to demonstrate anything, let's just get back to the questions."
Zh: “警探。”他想把手放在你的肩膀上，但又放弃了。“我们不需要解释任何东西，直接问问题吧。”

En: Uhm... He hesitates. "And what *is* that?"
Zh: “呃……”他犹豫了一下。“那*是*什么？”

En: Silence. He tilts his head to the side, inspecting you. A brief moment passes, then...
Zh: 沉默。他歪着头打量你，过了一阵子之后……

En: Police job? He tilts his head to the side, as if inspecting you. A brief moment passes. In silence. Then...
Zh: “当警察？”他歪着头，似乎在打量你，过了一阵子之后……

En: Easy, Shanks... The leader gestures for him to stand down. "I want to see where this is going."
Zh: “冷静，尚克……”领头人示意他停下。“我想看看之后的发展。”

En: You will NEVER forget what happens in five seconds -- if you don't RESPECT me!
Zh: “你永远都无法忘记之后的五秒钟内会发生的事情了——如果你不尊重我的话！”

En: I'm going to kill myself now, you fucking whore. (Pull the trigger.)
Zh: “我准备自杀了，你这该死的婊子。”（扣动扳机。）

En: You better get ready to fucking respect me in: five, four, three...
Zh: “你他妈的最好放尊重一点：五、四、三……”

En: Give the gun back to the lieutenant.
Zh: 把枪还给警督。

En: But I want to point it at *them*.
Zh: 但我想用枪指着*他们*。

En: Put the barrel in your mouth.
Zh: 把枪管放进自己的嘴里。

En: *I want to point it at them*, phah! Quit your whining.
Zh: *我想用枪指着他们*，呸！别再发牢骚了。

En: *Everyone* knows how to do that! You're a special man. You're going to point it at *yourself*. Go ahead, show them.
Zh: *每个人*都知道要怎么做！但你是个特立独行的人，你要把它指向*自己*。快啊，给他们看看。

En: What are you doing?! The short cold barrel touches your lips. It tastes like iron and hell.
Zh: “你在干什么？！”短而冰冷的枪管触及你的嘴唇，感觉就像钢铁与地狱。

En: Genuine panic flies across the lieutenant's face.
Zh: 警督的脸庞掠过一抹真情实感的恐慌。

En: Fellas... The big guy glances over his shoulders. "You getting any of this?"
Zh: “伙计们……”大个子回头看了看。“你们能理解他在干嘛吗？”

En: These are my thoughts. This is my head.
Zh: “这就是我的想法，这是我的脑袋。”

En: What in the name of mother-fuck... He looks closer, squinting at you.
Zh: “你他妈的……”他凑得更近了，眯着眼睛看着你。

En: The fuck is he saying?
Zh: “他他妈的在说什么？”

En: Nothing. Give me the gun.
Zh: “没什么，把枪给我。”

En: Fellas. The cop is really threatening to off himself. This is insane.
Zh: “伙计们，这个条子竟然真的威胁要自杀，真是疯了。”

En: No. That is not what is happening. He turns to you. "This *show* is unnecessary. Give me my gun. Now."
Zh: “不，事实并非如此。”他转向你。“这段*表演*完全没必要，现在快把我的枪给我。”

En: He's desperately trying to offer a counter-narrative. There is none.
Zh: 他拼命想给出一个与之相悖的说法，但却无能为力。

En: I'm not fucking this case up! (Finger on the trigger.) "You *will* respect me!"
Zh: “我不会把这个案子搞砸的！”（手指放在扳机上。）“你们*会*尊重我的！”

En: He's not gonna off himself, c'mon!
Zh: “他不会是要自杀吧，拜托！”

En: Some insane shit.
Zh: “真是够疯狂的。”

En: No, a calm voice from the back of the room: "He'll do it. Go ahead, son -- three, two..."
Zh: “不，”一个平静的声音从房间后方传来：“他会这么做的。来吧，孩子——三、二……”

En: *Bratan*, we can't have any fun if you kill yourself. Painting the booth red with your brains sounds like a real blast now -- but what about later? There'll be nothing.
Zh: “*兄弟*，要是你自杀了我们该上哪儿找乐子去。”用你的大脑把包厢染红听上去的确非常有冲击力——但然后呢？什么都没有了。

En: No! Give it to Lieutenant Kitsuragi. It's not too late -- you can still spin this as some kind of tactic.
Zh: 不！还给曷城警督吧。现在还不算太迟——你仍然可以把它扭转为某种策略。

En: *Kisses... kisses... kisses...*
Zh: *亲一个……亲一个……亲一个……*

En: We'll solve the case. Look at me. Don't worry. He appears calm suddenly. "This *demonstration* is not helping. At all."
Zh: “我们会破案的，看着我，别担心。”他突然变得平静起来。“这番*表演*没有任何帮助。”

En: Yeah, 'cause I sorta *wanna* see that shit.
Zh: “是啊，因为我还有点*想看*呢。”

En: Inside the small mechanism, you can hear a spring tensing up...
Zh: 在这个小小的机械装置里，你能够听到弹簧绷紧的声音……

En: Two! One! Do it, get this *one* thing right.
Zh: 二！一！快点，做好*这件*事。

En: There will be no *spinning*. Do the *honest* thing! Pull the trigger!
Zh: 不要有什么*扭转*，只要做*诚实*的事。扣动扳机！

En: Acidic taste on your lips. Corrosive. Once as a child you put your tongue against the rail. It was winter. That was a long time ago...
Zh: 嘴唇尝到了一丝酸味，就像是腐蚀物。在一个冬天，年幼的你曾有一次把舌头贴在铁栏杆上。那是很久以前的事情了……

En: Don't. There's this *itch* in the middle of your skull, where you've never reached. Never scratched...
Zh: 住手。在你的头颅中间有一处你从未触及，无法触碰的*瘙痒*……

En: There's just this *itch*, deep under your skull, where you've never reached. Never scratched...
Zh: 在你的头颅中间有一处你从未触及，无法触碰的*瘙痒*……

En: Dark? The big guy bursts out laughing. "What the fuck was that, you carnies? We haven't seen RCM in four years and *this* shows up!"
Zh: “阴郁？”那个大个子突然爆笑出声。“那是什么鬼，你是个小丑吗？我们已经四年没见过RCM的人，直到*这家伙*出现了！”

En: You promised it would make sense, but It didn't make sense at all. He returns the gun to its holster and addresses the room: "Nothing to worry about here, people -- my partner likes to play these jokes. He's what you call *dark*."\n
Zh: “你承诺过这能说得通的，但它根本说不通。”他把枪塞回枪套，然后看着房间说：“大家，没什么好担心的——我的搭档就喜欢开这种玩笑。他就是你们口中的*阴郁*。”

En: Thank you. He returns it to his holster, then looks at the room: "Nothing to worry about here, people -- my partner likes to play these jokes. He's what you call *dark*."
Zh: “谢谢你。”他把枪塞回枪套，然后看着房间说：“大家，没什么好担心的——我的搭档就喜欢开这种玩笑。他就是你们口中的*阴郁*。”

En: I knew he wasn't gonna do it, though! I knew he'd pussy out!
Zh: “但我早就知道他不会这么做的！我就知道他是个懦夫！”

En: No, no. I still have my money on him. The old man pulls on his cigarette, slowly. "Sooner or later -- he has that look."
Zh: “不，不。我还是愿意相信他。”老人慢慢地吸了一口烟。“会有那么一天的——他有那副表情。”

En: Do not think your *failed suicide attempt* will win you any pity from these men -- do not expect them to forget it soon either.
Zh: “别指望你的*自杀未遂*能赢得这些人的同情——也别指望他们很快会把这件事抛之脑后。”

En: Again -- it was a dark joke. He turns to you. "Maybe we should go for a little walk, joker? Before we continue? Jogging around seems to have a positive effect on you."
Zh: “再次重申——这是个阴郁的玩笑。”他转向你。“不如我们去散散步，笑话大王。在我们继续询问之前？慢跑或许会对你有些积极的影响。“

En: Ha-ha everyone. Did you like my funny joke?
Zh: “哈哈大家，你们喜欢我这个有趣的玩笑吗？”

En: Yes. Let's go back to normal. Let's pretend this never happened.
Zh: “没错，我们恢复正常吧，一起假装这一切从未发生过。”

En: Yes! Get the serotonine running. Keeps those bad thoughts away.
Zh: 没错！来一段激情四射的慢跑吧，让那些不好的想法远离你。

En: The fucking whore left me, I know it.
Zh: “那个该死的婊子离开我了，我就知道。”

En: Ehm...
Do not think your *failed suicide attempt* will win you any pity from these men. Do not expect them to forget it either.
Zh: “别指望你的*自杀未遂*能赢得这些人的同情——也别指望他们很快会把这件事抛之脑后。”

En: Nothing. Just a minor misunderstanding. Let's return to questions -- officer?
Zh: “没什么，只是个小误会。我们继续提问吧——警官？”

En: So, uhm. Behold, the uhm... demonstration. (Give the gun back, this is getting too scary.)
Zh: “那么，呃，请看，呃……表演。”（把枪还回去，它看起来越来越吓人了。）

En: Thank you. He quickly returns the gun to his holster, then looks at the room. "So -- questions?"
Zh: “谢谢你。”他把枪塞回枪套，然后看着房间说：“那么——有问题吗？”

En: Yeah, I have one. The big guy bursts out laughing. "What the fuck was that, you nancies?"
Zh: “有啊，我有个问题。”那个大个子突然爆笑出声。“那他妈是什么鬼，你是个娘炮吗？”

En: He is grateful to have that gun back on his belt right now.
Zh: 他很庆幸现在那把枪又回到了自己的腰带上。

En: He is infinitely grateful to have that gun back on his belt right now.
Zh: 他无比庆幸现在那把枪又回到了自己的腰带上。

En: I need Kim and his gun here for my *demonstration*.
Zh: “我需要金和他的枪进行我的*表演*。”

En: You won't, man. His fucking balls ain't big enough!
Zh: “你才不会呢，老兄。他他妈的是个胆小鬼！”

En: The fuck are you talkin' about -- gun? He asks angrily. "No one's gonna let you wave a gun around here."
Zh: “你他妈说什么呢——枪？”他生气地问。“没人会允许你在这里舞刀弄枪的。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: So she's not from around here?
Zh: “所以她不是本地人咯？”

En: I see, okay. That's cool, that's cool. Another question?
Zh: “我明白了，很好。可以的，没关系。还有别的问题吗？”

En: I see. Another question?
Zh: “我明白了。再问个问题？”

En: He rubs his left temple. "If you must."
Zh: 他揉了揉左侧的太阳穴。“如果你一定要的话。”

En: Someone's been raped. She needs counselling -- we need to talk to her.
Zh: “有人被强暴了，她需要做心理咨询——我们得和她谈谈。”

En: You may have missed a spot there with the graffito.
Zh: “你们好像漏掉了一处涂鸦。”

En: As you look around this room full of sweaty men, swearing, drinking, spitting out tobacco.... does this look familiar?
Zh: 当你环视着这个满是男人的房间，他们浑身臭汗、咒骂着、喝酒、大嚼烟草…….这看起来眼熟吗？

En: It's not about who did it, it's about the victim. She needs help.
Zh: “重点不是谁干的，而是受害者。她需要帮助。”

En: I've asked this before -- why are you hiding the victim?!
Zh: “我之前就问过了——你为什么要把受害者藏起来？”

En: I get it, Titus. You guys really are *the authority* around here.
Zh: “我明白的，提图斯。你们就是这里的*权威*。”

En: Where have I seen this before?
Zh: 我之前在哪里见过这幅场景？

En: Titus... He looks at her.
Zh: “提图斯……”他看着她。

En: You don't know her.
Zh: “你不认识她。”

En: Huh?
So... (Scratch your head.) "What is your relationship with her?"
Zh: “那么……”（挠挠头。）“你跟她是什么关系？”

En: What is *your* relationship with her?
Zh: “*你*跟她是什么关系？”

En: You believe the place was called *Precinct 41* -- it was also filled with (almost exclusively) *men*, sitting on desks, talking shit and wasting time.
Zh: 你认为那个地方被叫做*41分局*——同样满是（几乎全是）*男人*，坐在桌子旁胡扯，消磨时间。

En: So, the, erm... the rape. When was that?\n
Zh: “那么，呃，那个……强暴，是什么时候的事？”

En: When did the rape happen?
Zh: “强暴是什么时候的事？”

En: Uh... so what was her relationship with the mercenary?\n
Zh: “呃……那她跟那个雇佣兵是什么关系？”

En: What was her relationship with the mercenary?
Zh: “她跟那个雇佣兵是什么关系？”

En: You must be -- you're just like real cops. Drinking beer and sitting around with your dicks in your hand.
Zh: “你们一定是——你们就像真正的警察似的。喝着啤酒，手抓裆部坐在那里。”

En: I think I... (Laugh nervously.) "I think I know her."
Zh: “我觉得我……”（局促地笑。）“我觉得我认识她。”

En: I think I know her.
Zh: “我觉得我认识她。”

En: Titus... He looks at her.
Zh: “提图斯……”他看着她。

En: I know her. He looks around and an uncommon silence fills the room.
Zh: “我认识她。”他环视四周，房间里鸦雀无声。

En: Relationship?! There was no fucking relationship! He raped her, that's their relationship!
Zh: “关系？！他们他妈的没有一点关系！他强暴了她，这就是他们的关系！”

En: Something is off here. His anger is... possessive.
Zh: 有点不对劲，他的愤怒……是因为占有欲。

En: You seen Apricot -- old Pidieu's daughter? asks Lieutentant McCoy. "Uh-huh," replies Torson. "The ass on that one..." McCoy shakes his head in appreciation. "A bit strange the old man named her Apricot, but, I mean -- who am I to judge? Wanna hit the kebab joint?"
Zh: “你见过爱普柯特吗——老佩迪鲁的女儿？”麦考伊警督问到。“嗯哼。”托森回答。“她的屁股真是……”麦考伊赞赏地晃着头。“虽然那个老头叫她爱普柯特有点怪，不过，我的意思是——我又有什么资格去说三道四呢？想去烤肉店吗？”

En: Speak for yourself, buddy. He puts the beer down. "We've been giving shit for *ten years*! Martinaise was a dump before we put this outfit together."
Zh: “说你自己去吧。”他放下啤酒。“我们已经奉献了*十年*了！在我们重整马丁内斯之前，这里就是个垃圾场。”

En: How well do you *know* her?
Zh: “你有多*了解*她？”

En: He leans in. "You got a problem with *beer* now?"
Zh: 他靠过去。“现在你又对*啤酒*有意见了吗？”

En: Oh, okay, cool, cool. How well though?
Zh: “哦，好吧。可以的，很好。但究竟有多了解呢？”

En: Okay. How well?
Zh: “好吧，有多了解？”

En: Titus is hiding nothing. She turns to the hulk of a man. "Titus..."
Zh: “提图斯没有任何隐瞒。”她转向那个魁梧的男人。“提图斯……”

En: A small twitch in the corner of Kim's mouth. He has a hunch about what *knowing* means.
Zh: 金的嘴角微微抽搐了一下，他对于所谓的*认识*有了某种预感。

En: You wanna *help* her, cop? Fine, I'm, gonna let you help her -- but you treat her with respect.
Zh: “你想*帮*她吗，条子？行啊，我就给你一个帮忙的机会——但你得给我放尊重点。”

En: Well enough, copper. We partied. She's been here for a few months. He crosses his arms.
Zh: “够了解的了，条子。我们一起派对，她是几个月前来这里的。”他抱起双臂。

En: Not quite there yet -- push on.
Zh: 还没到那一步——继续努力。

Zh: “行吧，好的。我们来谈谈吧，你想说什么？”

En: Do you now? she asks, narrowing her eyes. After a moment she shakes her head.
Zh: “是这样吗？”她眯起眼睛问了一句。过了一会儿，又摇了摇头。

En: Are you with him? (Point to the man with sunglasses.)
Zh: “你跟他是一起的吗？”（指着那个墨镜男。）

En: What are you -- the police -- doing here?
Zh: “你们——警察——在这儿干什么？”

En: He's an asshole.
Zh: “他是个混蛋。”

En: *You're* an asshole! He pauses. "You know what, maybe we're both assholes..."
Zh: “*你*才是个混蛋！”他顿了一下。“知道吗，或许我们都是混蛋……”

En: He seems like a cool guy.
Zh: “他看上去是个不错的家伙。”

En: Nothing, just wondering...
Zh: “没什么，只是想知道……”

En: The sooner you figure this hanging out, the sooner everyone can go home.
Zh: “你越早解决这起案件，大家就能越早回家。”

En: Of course I'm *with him*... She seems startled by her own tone. "Why do you ask?"
Zh: “我当然是*跟他一起的*……”她似乎被自己的语气吓了一跳。“你问这个干什么？”

En: Well, he's not. The man interjects -- his voice is stern. "He's a sack of shit barely kept together by crazy glue -- but at least he tries. Unlike you."
Zh: “唉，他不是。”男人插了进来——他的声音很严厉。“他就是个靠强力胶勉强拼凑起来的废物——但至少他努力了，不像你。”

En: Please, she looks at you, then him, then you again. "Let's not turn this into another *exchange*, okay?"
Zh: “求你了，”她看着你，然后是他，最后又转向你。“不要让它演变成另外一场*对骂*，好吗？”

En: Is he famous?
Zh: “他很有名吗？”

En: What? No. No, he's not... what are you talking about?
Zh: “为什么？不，不，他没有……你在说什么呀？”

En: Actually never mind. Don't tell me about it. It will just turn into another... She stops mid-sentence.
Zh: “还是算了吧，别告诉我。这只会变成另一场……”她说到一半停了下来。

En: I know this might sound weird, but are you... a firefighter?
Zh: “我知道这听起来很奇怪，但你是……消防员吗？”

En: Her brown eyes linger on you for a moment.
Zh: 她棕色的眼睛在你身上停留了一会儿。

En: No, Harry, neither of us are firefighters, she says with a heavy sigh, and falls silent.
Zh: “不，哈里，我们两个都不是消防员。”她长叹一声，陷入了沉默。

En: Did she just call you *Harry*? These people do *NOT* know you! You don't *have* a name.
Zh: 她刚刚叫你*哈里*了吗？这些人*不*认识你！你应该没*有*名字才对。

En: Did you just call me *Harry*?
Zh: “你刚才叫我*哈里*了吗？”

En: Yes, I called you by your name, what about it? She sounds slightly agitated and... sad.
Zh: “是的，我叫了你的名字，怎么了？”她听上去有些激动以及……悲伤。

En: My name is Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: “我的名字是拉斐尔·安柏修斯·柯斯托。”

En: How do you know... my name?
Zh: “你是怎么知道……我的名字的？”

En: God, everybody knows your name... She waves at you in a discarding motion. "Please go finish your investigation, so everyone can go home."
Zh: “天呐，大家都知道你的名字啊……”她挥手赶你离开。“请你快去完成调查吧，这样大家都能回家了。”

En: Is that... my name?
Zh: “那是……我的名字吗？”

En: I don't understand what this conversation is about anymore. She's trying to look elsewhere.
Zh: “我有点想不通这次谈话的意义了。”她试图将目光投向别处。

En: Nothing, ma'am.
Zh: “没什么，女士。”

En: She doesn't reply, but her eyes are still sad.
Zh: 她没有回答，但眼睛中仍然满是悲伤。

En: Honestly... Her sad eyes scan your face, looking for signs of deceit. "I don't care. I don't think anyone does anymore."
Zh: “说实话……”她悲伤的眼睛扫视着你的脸，试图寻找谎言的痕迹。“我不在乎，我想现在已经没有人会在乎了。”

En: I'm just looking out for...
Zh: “我只是在找……”

En: You look cute together.
Zh: “你们看起来很相配。”

En: And you look like you don't bathe -- on principle, the man with sunglasses interjects.
Zh: “而你看起来没洗过澡——原则上来说，”墨镜男插话道。

En: Goddamnit, you leave her alone! The man with sunglasses snaps at you. "Keep your weird bullshit to yourself and be professional for once, for fucks sake..."
Zh: “该死的，你别骚扰她了！”那个墨镜男怒道。“把你那些奇怪的屁话留着自己听吧，至少这次表现得专业一点儿，看在老天爷的份上……”

En: NO ONE! The man looks at her. "I'm just a man with sunglasses and you are..."
Zh: “没找谁！”男人看着她。“我就是个墨镜男，而你是……”

En: A horse-faced woman.
Zh: “一个马脸女人。”

En: You're a fucking asshole. She says this with quiet dignity. "Are we done here?"
Zh: “你真他妈是个混蛋。”她的话语带着一种沉静的尊严。“完事了吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 一言不发。

En: You have a blue uniform and mine is black... what does this mean?
Zh: “你穿的是蓝色制服，而我的是黑色……这是什么意思？”

En: Uh... she seems confused for a moment. "It means I'm a patrol officer, sir."
Zh: “呃……”她一时间似乎有些糊涂了。“意思是我是个巡警，先生。”

En: What was with that *sir*?
Zh: 那句*先生*是怎么回事？

En: She's obviously a bit uncomfortable in your company.
Zh: 她和你在一起时明显有点不自在。

En: A police woman.
Zh: “一个女警。”

En: Yes... Her voice is quiet. "A police woman who just wants to do her job, that's all," she says quietly and looks away.
Zh: “是的……”她的声音很安定。“一个只想做好本职工作的女警，仅此而已。”她平静地说着，然后移开了视线。

En: I just want to do my job, that's all, she says quietly and looks away.
Zh: “我只想做好我的本职工作，仅此而已。”她平静地说着，然后移开了视线。

En: Something about the man with sunglasses and you, the energy between you two, is making her uncomfortable.
Zh: 墨镜男和你之间有些渊源，你们之间的氛围，让她感觉有点不自在。

En: She's a woman, that's how women speak to men. With respect.
Zh: 她是个女人，这就是女人对男人说话的方式，尊重。

En: No...NO... NO!!! You're a feminist, remember?
Zh: 不……不……不！！！你是个女权主义者，记得吗？

En: Something is seriously wrong with you.
Zh: 你非常不对劲。

En: You called me *sir*. Why?
Zh: “你叫我*先生*。为什么？”

En: Well, I... uh... She looks extremely uncomfortable right now. "I guess I..."
Zh: “那个，我……呃……”她现在看上去非常不自在。“我想我是……”

En: Cut the shit. The words are addressed to you. "Every second you stand here running your mouth the case grows colder."
Zh: “少废话了。”这句话是对你说的。“你站在这里多费一秒钟的口舌，案件就会变得越来越悬。”

En: Did you hear that? Did you hear me sing? Did you like it?
Zh: “你听到了吗？听到我唱歌了吗？你喜欢吗？”

En: I bet you liked that, didn't you? Let's be honest, that was some first rate karaoke.
Zh: “我打赌你肯定喜欢，是不是？说真的，那是首一流的卡拉OK。”

En: That... that was pretty good, Harry. She pokes her companion with an elbow. "Wasn't it Jean?"
Zh: “那……那挺不错的，哈里。”她用胳膊肘捅了捅自己的同伴。“是不是，让？”

En: Uh, that was... He stares at you. "Yeah, that was absolute shit -- if you want my opinion. Drunken shit. Haven't wanted anything to end this badly since I had cluster headaches."
Zh: “呃，那真是……”他盯着你。“是啊，那真是太烂了——如果你要问我的话，一塌糊涂。自从患上丛集性头痛之后，我还从没这么希望某件事快点结束。”

En: Horrible, truly horrible, he continues. "I beg of you, don't ever subject anyone to this torture again. I mean seriously, you need to..."
Zh: “可怕，真的太可怕了。”他继续道。“求你了，别再让任何人遭受这种折磨了。我是认真的，你需要……”

En: Jean... she quietly interjects, giving you a compassionate side glance. "He's not right, don't make it worse and... and *I* really liked it."
Zh: “让……”她轻声插话，并给了你一个同情的眼神。“他说的不对，别再让事情变得更*糟*了……*我*真的很喜欢。”

En: I'm going to venture a guess. You're a nurse, right?
Zh: “我来猜猜看，你是个护士，对吗？”

En: Do you work for the animal control?
Zh: “你在动物管理中心工作吗？”

En: Are you by any chance... (Lean in real close.) "*working class*?"
Zh: “你有没有可能是……（凑得更近。）……*工人阶级*？”

En: She just looks at you and says nothing.
Zh: 她只是看着你，什么也没说。

En: Are you a nurse?
Zh: “你是护士吗？”

En: Actually, I wanted to address other matters.
Zh: “实际上，我想谈点别的。”

En: She nods in agreement.
Zh: 她点头表示同意。

En: Cut the crazy act, the man with sunglasses interjects angrily. "She's being polite enough by not telling you to fuck off."
Zh: “别发疯了，”墨镜男愤怒地插嘴。“她没有让你滚蛋就已经足够礼貌了。”

En: No, it's okay. She raises her eyes to meet your gaze. "I am not. Polite. I'm just tired... was there something else you wanted?"
Zh: “不，没关系的。”她抬起眼睛与你对视。“我不是，有礼貌，只是有点累了……你还有别的需要吗？”

En: No, no I don't. It doesn't look like she does -- the *police* uniform she's wearing is a dead giveaway.
Zh: “不，不我不是。”她看上去确实不像——她身上的*警察*制服就是个铁证。

En: Well... raising two children and half a husband on a patrol officer's wage. Yes, I guess I am working class.
Zh: “那个……靠一个巡警的工资养活两个孩子和半个丈夫。是的，我想我确实是工人阶级。”

En: Do you want to rise up and tear down the entire fucking system with me?
Zh: “你想站出来跟我一起摧毁整个该死的系统吗？”

En: Cool. (Give her a little wink.) I am too.
Zh: “真不错。（朝她眨眨眼。）我也是。”

En: Good to know.
Zh: “很高兴能知道。”

En: She merely nods in response.
Zh: 她只是点头回应。

En: Uh.... okay. Her tone doesn't suggest she's convinced.
Zh: “呃…….好吧。”她的语气听上去并不像是被说服的样子。

En: She's merely agreeing with you to reach the conclusion of this conversation faster.
Zh: 她认同你只是为了更快结束这段对话。

En: No. She sighs. "Okay. Sort of. But not with you. Like you are now. Don't take this the wrong way..."
Zh: “不。”她叹了口气。“好吧，有点，但不是跟你一起，就像你现在这样。别误会我的意思……”

En: Uh... okay. Yes, that was pretty good.
Zh: “呃……好吧，那挺好的。”

En: What? No. Nonononono! The man with sunglasses shakes his head stubbornly. "There are *universal* standards of good out there and that... that was just complete shit."
Zh: “什么？不，不是的！”墨镜男固执地摇着头。“全世界对于好的标准*普遍*相通，而那……那完全是一派胡言。”

En: Jean, she quietly interjects, giving you a compassionate side glance. "He's not right... treat him like you'd treat McCoy's little brother."
Zh: “让……”她轻声插话，并给了你一个同情的眼神。“他说的不对……像对待麦考伊的弟弟那样温和点。”

En: Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor John "Archetype" McCoy's younger brother Lance McCoy, although a man of 32 years, will mentally never surpass a six year old.
Zh: 二阶荣誉警督约翰“原型”麦考伊的弟弟兰斯·麦考伊，虽然是个32岁的成年男子，心理上永远不会超过六岁。

En: Hey, I'm standing right here!
Zh: “嘿，我就站在这里呢！”

En: Yes, and I'm wondering why? Don't you have a case to solve?
Zh: “是啊，我还在想为什么呢？你不是还有案子要解决吗？”

En: Yes, did you want something from me?
Zh: “是的，你是想从我这里得到什么吗？”

En: Wait... what? John? Lance? Who are these people?
Zh: 等下……什么？约翰？兰斯？这些人都是谁？

En: No idea. Just passing on information stored in your fractured neural cortex.
Zh: 不知道，只是把你断裂的神经皮层中储存的信息传递出来而已。

En: From within your mind-haze you hear the end of a sentence: "...and I'm not going to pretend like he's got a *learning disability*!"
Zh: 在你朦胧的思绪中，你听到了一句句尾：“……我也不会装出一副他有*学习障碍*的样子。”

En: I'm not handicappable.
Zh: “我不是残废。”

En: Jean, detective. She looks at both of you. "I don't understand what you're talking about."
Zh: “让，警探。”他看着你们俩。“我不明白你在说什么。”

En: Now he's got a problem with learning disabilities... is that supposed to be ironic?
Zh: “现在他又有学习障碍的毛病了……这是一种讽刺吗？”

En: Maybe it didn't go as well as I thought...
Zh: “也许事情没有我想的那么顺利……”

En: You think so?
Zh: “你是这么想的吗？”

En: It was very nice. Different, but good kind of different. I liked it.
Zh: “非常棒。与众不同，而且是一种很好的不同。我很喜欢。”

En: Sssssssshe's lying. Probably hated it. They all did.
Zh: 她在说谎。大概很讨厌它。他们都很讨厌！

En: Look, something is really bugging me. Are we or are we not from the same police station?
Zh: “听着，有些事情让我非常困扰。我们是不是同一个警局的？”

En: Can I actually help you with something? She looks at you apologetically.
Zh: “我能真正帮上你什么忙吗？”她略带歉意地看着你。

En: Whoa, how does she know your name? She probably heard it from somewhere. You have to be more careful. Names are *dangerous*.
Zh: 哇哦，她怎么会知道你的名字？可能是从什么地方听来的。你得小心点，名字是很*危险*的。

En: The name's Tequila. Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “龙舌兰，龙舌兰日落。”

En: It might be because you have the higher rank.
Zh: “可能是因为你的级别比较高。”

En: Yes, of course -- preposterous. I mean, you would *remember* if they were, right? Who forgets their squadmates? That's not possible.
Zh: 是啊，当然——简直荒谬。我的意思是，如果他们是的话你肯定会*记得*的，不是吗？谁会忘记自己的战友？那是不可能的。

En: Whoa, how does she know your name? She probably heard it from somewhere. You have to be more careful, karaoke-star. Names are dangerous.
Zh: 哇哦，她怎么会知道你的名字？可能是从什么地方听来的。你得小心点，卡拉OK之星，名字是很*危险*的。

En: Did she just call you *Harry*? These people do *NOT* know you! You don't *have* a name! Ha ha!
Zh: 她刚刚叫你*哈里*了吗？这些人*不*认识你！你应该没*有*名字才对！哈哈！

En: She actually *did*!
Zh: 她确实*这么做了*！

En: Whackathon? Shitshow? Pity party?
Zh: 暴击马拉松？狂欢？怜悯派对？

En: No, Harry... it's *not* your name, the man scoffs. "Your name is Baron Von Kikkenberg -- cool guy."
Zh: “不，哈里……这*不是*你的名字，”那人讥笑着，“你的名字是冯·基肯伯格男爵——酷毙了。”

En: You're the police right? Cool. So am I!
Zh: “你是警察对吗？太棒了，我也是！”

En: I don't... She looks around. "I don't know what to say."
Zh: “我不……”她向四周看了看。“我不知道该说什么了。”

En: Don't say anything, Judit.
Zh: “什么都别说了，茱蒂特。”

En: Wait, is... he police?
Zh: “等等，他……也是警察？”

En: Me? No. I'm just a man with sunglasses. I like wearing sunglasses inside. Sunglasses -- and a *fucking wig*.
Zh: “我吗？不，我只是个墨镜男罢了。我喜欢在室内戴墨镜。墨镜——还有一顶*该死的假发*。”

En: Okay.
Zh: 好吧。

En: You?
Zh: 你吗？

En: The woman in an RCM patrol officer's uniform winces as she notices you.
Zh: 那个穿着RCM巡警制服的女人注意到你后便缩到了一边。

En: Hold on, you're a patrol officer of the RCM?
Zh: “等等，你是RCM的巡警吗？”

En: Hold on, you are a cop with the RCM?
Zh: “等等，你是RCM的警察吗？”

En: Yes. She nods. "I am."
Zh: “是的。”她点头。“我是。”

En: A patrol officer is the lowest rank in the RCM, below lieutenant and sergeant.
Zh: 巡警是RCM中级别最低的一种，低于警督和警长。

En: As Lieutenant Kitsuragi explained, a patrol officer is the lowest rank in the RCM, below lieutenant and sergeant.
Zh: 根据曷城警督的解释，巡警是RCM中级别最低的一种，低于警督和警长。

En: I'm on a murder investigation. Are you the cavalry?
Zh: “我正在调查一桩谋杀案，你们是救兵吗？”

En: I'm definitely not the cavalry.
Zh: “我绝对不是什么救兵。”

En: ...after all you've done -- is what her tone implies.
Zh: ……在你折腾了这么久之后——她的语气这么暗示着。

En: No you're not, you're not the only cop in the world. She shakes her head and breathes out.
Zh: “不，你不是，你不是这个世界上唯一的警察。”她摇摇头，呼出一口气。

En: Something changes in her. It's pity. Pity comes over her.
Zh: 她有些变化。是怜悯，她心中的怜悯之意油然而生。

En: Have I wronged you? I've done that to a lot of people.
Zh: “我有冤枉过你吗？我对很多人都这么做过。”

En: No, you haven't wronged me. It's okay. She shakes her head and breathes out.
Zh: “不，你没有冤枉过我。没关系的。”她摇摇头，呼出一口气。

En: Of course he does... The man at the table says to himself.
Zh: “他当然有……”坐在桌旁的那个人自言自语道。

En: But you're not trying your best, are you? He says with a flash of anger.
Zh: “但你没有拼尽全力，不是吗？”他怒气冲冲地说。

En: I know.
Zh: “我知道。”

En: I'm a cop too.
Zh: “我也是警察。”

En: He's the real deal.
Zh: “他的确货真价实。”

En: He's the real deal, he has a badge.
Zh: “他的能力很出众，并且持有警徽。”

En: He's the real deal, he has a gun now.
Zh: “他的能力很出众，现在还有枪了。”

En: It's hard to tell whether he's sarcastic or sincere, but if you had to guess you would say the lieutenant is being sarcastic.
Zh: 很难说他是在讽刺还是真心实意的，但如果让你来猜，你会说警督这是在挖苦人。

En: Calm down, she says to the man, then turns to you.
Zh: “冷静点。”她对那个男人说，然后转向了你。

En: Then something changes in her. It's pity. Pity comes over her.
Zh: 她有些变化。是怜悯，她心中的怜悯之意油然而生。

En: Let's just do this by the book, okay.
Zh: “我们就照章办事，行吗。”

En: By the...? She pinches the root of her nose.
Zh: “照章……？”她捏了捏鼻梁。

En: This is gonna take *the hard* stuff too. Use as much as you can.
Zh: 它需要来点*硬的*，放得越多越好。

En: It's bad with those boots. Don't be stingy now.
Zh: 那双靴子已经够糟的了，别那么小气。

En: Alright now, chef. Light the stove up and boil them.
Zh: 来吧，大厨。把炉子点上，让它们沸腾吧。

En: Beautiful.
Zh: 漂亮。

En: A two course dinner of rotting flesh and hardened ceramic.
Zh: 一套双份主菜的晚餐，腐烂的人肉以及硬化的陶瓷。

En: Feels like you should've used vinegar too...
Zh: 感觉你应该把醋也倒进去的……

En: Far away, in the darkness of a makeshift morgue behind Station 41's lazareth, Dr. Nix Gottlieb cuts into the cold, dead feet of a murder victim. The veins are oddly black, he suspects a neurotoxin...
Zh: 远在41分局检疫所后面的临时停尸房中，尼克斯·戈特利布医生在黑暗里切开了被害人冰冷而僵硬的双脚。静脉呈现出怪异的黑色，他怀疑是一种神经毒素……

En: Just like beef stew.
Zh: 就像炖牛肉一样。

En: Whatever you had planned, you have to do it when the lieutenant's gone.
Zh: 不管你有什么计划，最好等警督走了之后再说。

En: You should taste some.
Zh: 你应该尝一尝。

En: No way, I'm not touching it.
Zh: 不可能，我不会碰它的。

En: Tip your finger in the pot and lick it.
Zh: 把手指在锅里蘸一下，然后舔一口。

En: The scorching hot 'stew' burns your finger and tastes absolutely vile.
Zh: 灼热的‘炖汤’烧伤了你的手指，味道可以说是绝对的恶心。

En: Yeah, maybe that's a good habit *not* to include in your roster. Anyways, the boots are coming along nicely.
Zh: 是啊，也许这是个‘不该’列入你名单的好习惯。总之，靴子的事进展的很顺利。

En: Rotten Human Meat-Ceramic Boot Soup? Nope, you really shouldn't.
Zh: 腐烂的人肉陶瓷靴汤？不，你不应该这么做。

En: Few more moments and the boots will be ready.
Zh: 不出一会儿，靴子就能准备妥当。

En: Your hands are protected from the heat, but the 'stew' tastes absolutely vile.
Zh: 你的手没有受到热度的伤害，但是‘炖汤’的味道实在是太恶心了。

En: You're tasting another human being. You shouldn't.
Zh: 你尝到了一个人类的味道。你不应该这样的。

En: Of course it tastes vile. Look at the effort you put in the culinary side of this? Not exactly fine dining, is it?
Zh: 味道当然恶心了。看看你在烹饪方面付出的努力？不算是很精致的饮食，不是吗？

En: You have awakened the *hunger*. There is no turning back now, better invest in a good cook book and stock up on salt.
Zh: 你唤醒了*饥饿*。现在没办法回头了，最好买一本好的烹饪书，储备一点盐。

En: Well, at least now you know.
Zh: 呃，至少现在你知道了。

En: This industrial gas-powered stove has been used to prepare food for many hungry hostel guests. There are several pots and pans on hand.
Zh: 这个工业燃气炉被用来为许多饥肠辘辘的旅社客人准备食物，上面有好几个汤锅和煎锅。

En: What are you planning to do, throw a dinner party? Please refrain from culinary experiments until after we've finished the investigation.
Zh: “你打算做什么，举办晚宴吗？在我们调查完成之前，请不要做任何烹饪实验。”

En: Okay, okay. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，好吧。”[离开。]

En: The stove is unmoved by your comings and goings.
Zh: 炉子并未因你的来来去去而受到影响。

En: The lieutenant said you'd need to boil the boots in acid to get the corpse smell out, but this huge pot, industrial dish soap, white vinegar, and patience might do the trick.
Zh: 警督说过你得把靴子放进酸里煮才能消除尸体的气味，但有了这个大汤锅，加上工业用洗涤精、白醋与一些耐心或许也能奏效。

En: Getting the corpse residue out of these boots is going to require patience. And also a huge pot full of boiling water, soap, and white vinegar.
Zh: 从靴子中祛除尸体的残留物需要相当的耐心，以及一个装满沸水、洗涤精和白醋的大锅。

En: Check out the cookware.
Zh: 查看厨具。

En: Check out the cleaning supplies.
Zh: 查看清洁用品。

En: Add water -- and the boots -- to the pot. Bring it all to a *nice* boil.
Zh: 加水——还有鞋子——入锅。*完全*煮沸。

En: Master chef out. [Leave.]
Zh: 大厨退场。[离开。]

En: A commercial pot draws your attention. It's very large. Gigantic, even. It could be used to make enough stew to feed an entire city. And also to boil a putrid pair of death-boots.
Zh: 一个商品锅引起了你的注意，它非常大，甚至可以说巨大，巨大到足以炖出满足整个城市的美味，也能煮下一双腐烂的死亡之靴。

En: There is a variety of soaps and bleaches in the cabinet to the left of the stove. There is also a bottle of white vinegar in the cabinet next to the fridge.
Zh: 炉子左侧的橱柜里有各种各样的洗涤精和漂白剂，冰箱旁边的柜子里还有一瓶白醋。

En: The boots are *really* disgusting. Pour some dish soap *and* the bottle of white vinegar into the pot.
Zh: 这双靴子*真的*恶心透顶，往锅中倒入一些洗涤精和白醋。

En: Soap! That's exactly what I need. Pour lots of dish soap into the pot.
Zh: 洗涤精！正合我意，往锅中倒入大量洗涤精。

En: The soap bubbles over the rim of the pot -- the water slowly comes to a boil.
Zh: 锅沿旁充斥着大量的肥皂泡——水慢慢地沸腾了。
