En: The strong smell of vinegar forces you to step away from the pot. The water slowly comes to a boil.
Zh: 浓烈的醋味迫使你离开了汤锅，水慢慢地沸腾了。

En: Wait...
Zh: 等等……

En: Strips of polymer fabric and human tissue separate from the lining of the boots. They float to the bubbling surface.
Zh: 长条的聚合物纤维和人体组织从靴子里剥离开来，它们漂浮在冒着泡泡的水面上。

En: Wait some more...
Zh: 再等等……

En: The boots look cleaner and cleaner. Those bits of human flesh are beginning to look *cooked*. You can smell it too.
Zh: 靴子看上去愈发干净了，星星点点的人肉碎末看起来也*熟透了*，你能够闻到。

En: A little more...
Zh: 再等等……

En: That's it, chef! The boots are as clean as they're going to get. Steam dense with the smell of strange meat disappears into the vent above the stove.
Zh: 好了，大厨！靴子已经焕然一新了。弥漫着奇怪肉味的蒸汽，消失在了炉子上方的通风口里。

En: Dump the sock and flesh stew -- and examine your new boots!
Zh: 倒掉袜子和炖肉——看看你的新靴子吧！

En: The boots look clean but still smell funky.
Zh: 这双靴子看着很干净，但闻起来仍然很诡异。

En: A pair of real beauties! The boots are shiny, hot and reek of vinegar. Just perfect.
Zh: 真是一双绝佳的艺术品！靴子火热又闪亮，还散发着阵阵醋味，完美极了。

En: Try boiling them again, this time with white vinegar.
Zh: 再煮一次试试，这次加点白醋吧。

En: Endure the stench. It has to come out eventually, right?
Zh: 忍耐一下恶臭，它最终会消散的，对吧？

En: The strong vinegar odour forces you to take a few steps back from the stove.
Zh: 浓烈的醋味迫使你从火炉旁退开了。

En: Wait *even more*...
Zh: 再等*一下*……

En: The whole kitchen now smells like white vinegar. But maybe your boots won't smell like a corpse?
Zh: 整个厨房都弥漫着白醋味，但也许你的靴子再也不会有尸体的味道了？

En: All right! Dump the water and sniff the boots.
Zh: 好极了！把水倒掉，然后闻一闻靴子。

En: A pair of real beauties! The boots are shiny, hot and reek of vinegar. Just perfect.
Zh: 一双绝佳的艺术品！靴子火热又闪亮，还散发着阵阵醋味，完美极了。

En: The delicious smells of cheap soap and vinegar waft up from the pot.
Zh: 廉价洗涤精和醋的香味从汤锅里飘了出来。

En: The bottom of the pot fills with shiny white goo.
Zh: 锅底布满了亮晶晶的白色粘稠物。

En: Dump the sock and flesh stew -- and examine your new boots!
Zh: 倒掉袜子和炖肉——看看你的新靴子吧！

En: The air is thick with anticipation.
Zh: 室内的气氛充满了期待之情。

En: The air is thick with anticipation.
Zh: 室内的气氛充满了期待之情。

En: The air is thick with anticipation.
Zh: 室内的气氛充满了期待之情。

En: The air is thick with anticipation.
Zh: 室内的气氛充满了期待之情。

En: Do you hear that? The most pathetic applause in the world. Made of pity. No one *liked* you -- they think you're a joke.
Zh: 听到了吗？这就是全世界最可悲的喝彩。仅仅是出于同情。从来就没人*喜欢*你——所有人都认为你是个笑话。

En: You feel a little dizzy. A little *unsteady*, suddenly.
Zh: 你感到一阵晕眩。突然有些*步伐不稳*。

En: Does someone feel like throwing up? I do. A little.
Zh: 有人想吐吗？我有点想吐了。

En: Yes. You can always do it in the evening. It will be less scary with a lot of people.
Zh: 你可以晚上再来唱嘛。人多的时候就不会显得这么可怕了。

En: He's not backing up from this. Standing up here is merely a practice run without paying him first.
Zh: 他在这方面是绝对不会让步的。如果不先付清账单，你站在台上就只能做彩排。

En: Thank you, Ancient Reptilian Brain.
Zh: 谢谢你，古老的爬虫脑。

En: There is silence now in the deep where the voice came from. It has receded -- to return only in dreams and nightmares.
Zh: 歌声那深沉的源头陷入了沉默。它已然隐退——只会在梦境与噩梦中再度回归。

En: This guy really carried you.
Zh: 这家伙真的帮了大忙。

En: Last words?
Zh: 最后还有什么要交待吗？

En: Thank you, Ancient Reptilian Brain.
Zh: 谢谢你，古老的爬行动物脑。

En: No reply. There is silence now in the deep where the voice came from. It has receded into you, to return only in dreams and nightmares.
Zh: 没收到回应。歌声那深沉的源头陷入了沉默。它已然隐退——只会在梦境与噩梦中再度回归。

En: Uhm... yes. I'm going to unplug the microphone, okay? He unplugs it.
Zh: “呃……好吧。我要把麦克风拔了，行吗？”他说完就直接拔了。

En: I'd like to dedicate this song to the RCM. Please don't fire me.
Zh: “我想把这首歌献给RCM，请不要解雇我。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't say anything -- but he smiles sadly.
Zh: 警督一言不发——但他的微笑中含着一丝忧伤。

En: Did you hear that? The most pathetic applause in the world. Made of pity. No matter the time of day, or how small the audience -- this is a *pitiful* applause.
Zh: 听到了吗？这就是全世界最可悲的喝彩。仅仅是出于同情。和演唱的时间无关，与在场的观众数量也无关——这就是*令人同情*的喝彩。

En: Say: "You *didn't* like it, huh?"
Zh: 直说：“你*不喜欢*吧？”

En: I'm up here singing my fucking HEART OUT and it's not *good enough* for you fuckers!?
Zh: “我在台上唱得撕心裂肺，你们这帮混蛋觉得还*不够劲儿*吗？！”

En: Fuck you! I gave it my fucking best. I gave it EVERYTHING and you SHIT on me?!
Zh: “操你们大爷！我都已经拼尽全力了。我都已经呕心沥血了，而你们居然还敢给老子起哄？！”

En: Your words echo in the karaoke mic. They sound sparse. The room is almost empty.
Zh: 你的话语通过麦克风在室内回荡。声音稀稀落落。餐厅里已经没几个人了。

En: Your words echo in the karaoke mic. People talk in the distance. A couple tries not to look at you.
Zh: 你的话语通过麦克风在室内回荡。人们在远处交谈着。有对情侣避开了你的视线。

En: Quit yellin' into the mic! You hear from the Union booth.
Zh: “别冲着麦克风喊！”声音是从工会包间传来的。

En: Uhm... I'm gonna unplug the mic now, okay?
Zh: “呃……那我这就把麦克风拔了，行吗？”

En: You hear -- or *think* you hear -- uncomfortable shifting around. A bit of laughter, maybe? No one's saying anything.
Zh: 你听到了——也可能是*认为*自己听到了——人群因不适而交换位置的声音。也许还有细微的窃笑声？观众们什么都没说。

En: You... liked it?
Zh: “你……喜欢吗？”

En: A voice from the back squeaks something. It's definitely laughter.
Zh: 后排传来了尖利的叫声。绝对是有人在笑。

En: You don't know what good singing is! This is *real* singing. I sang about *real* stuff.
Zh: “你根本不懂什么是*好歌*！这才是*真正的*歌声。我唱出的都是*真情实感*。”

En: Someone walks out of the room -- by the front door. Some woman. Is it because of you?
Zh: 有人离开了——还是从前门走的。是个女人。都是因为你吧？

En: I SANG ABOUT HOW I FEEL! Fuck all you, scum!
Zh: “我唱出的是自己的真实感受！操你们大爷，渣滓！”

En: Just stand there.
Zh: 原地不动。

En: That's it, I'm unplugging it. He presses stop on the tape carousel. You hear a little whine of feedback and then the mic dies in your hand.
Zh: “行了，我要拔了。”他按了下点唱机的顶部。在一丝轻微的反馈噪音之后，手中的麦克风便不再作声了。

En: Let's go, officer. The lieutenant closes his notes. "These people wouldn't know a good performance if it bit them in the ass."
Zh: “走吧，警官。”警督合上了笔记本“无论你怎么教训他们，这些人都分辨不出演出的好坏的。”

En: Detective Du Bois... He bows lightly. "It was downright *tragic*. Now let's go."
Zh: “杜博阿警探……”他稍稍点了点头。“真是彻头彻尾的*悲剧*，咱们还是走吧。”

En: Lieutenant Du Bois... he bows lightly. "It was downright *tragic*. Now let's go."
Zh: “杜博阿警督……”他稍稍点了点头。“真是彻头彻尾的*悲剧*，咱们还是走吧。”

En: Yes, he bows lightly. "It was downright tragic, detective."
Zh: “是啊，”他稍稍点了点头。“真是彻头彻尾的悲剧，警探。”

En: [Put down the mic.]
Zh: [放下麦克风。]

En: That's it. You're unpowered.
Zh: 到此为止了。你失去了动力。

En: I mean it, he thinks.
Zh: 我是认真的。他这样想着。

En: To him, being a cop in the RCM was truly expressed in that performance.
Zh: 在他看来，你的演唱把身为RCM警察的感受表现得淋漓尽致。

En: A lazy applause fills the room. You feel your hands shake as awareness of your body returns to you.
Zh: 室内响起了不情不愿的喝彩声。在身体恢复知觉之后，你才意识到自己的双手正在颤抖。

En: A rather lonely applause echoes through the mostly empty cafeteria. It's Garte. "Not bad," he tells you with an approving nod.
Zh: 一阵相当孤独的喝彩在空荡荡的餐厅内回荡。那是加尔特。“不错嘛。”他向你点头致意。

En:
I would often go there. To the tiny church there. The smallest church in Saint-Saëns -- though it once was larger.

How the rill may rest there. Down through the mist there. Toward the seven sisters -- toward those pale cliffs there

I would often stay there. In the tiny yard there. I have been so glad here -- looking forward to the past here

But now you are alone. None of this matters at all.
Zh: “我时常会去那个地方，圣桑最小的那座教堂。我不愿思念它过去的噢……辉煌。雾中的溪水涌向何方，流淌过七位姐妹身旁。那尽头是灰域之崖的噢……方向。我时常停留在那个地方，面对着小院四周的围墙。我曾如此沉醉于从前的噢……思量。可如今你我天涯各一方，一切再也无法回到老路上。”

En:
I would often go there. To the tiny church there. The smallest church in Saint-Saëns -- though it once was larger.

How the rill may rest there. Down through the mist there. Toward the seven sisters -- toward those pale cliffs there

I would often stay there. In the tiny yard there. I have been so glad here -- looking forward to the past here

But now you are alone. None of this matters at all.
Zh: “我时常会去那个地方，圣桑最小的那座教堂。我不愿思念它过去的噢……辉煌。雾中的溪水涌向何方，流淌过七位姐妹身旁。那尽头是灰域之崖的噢……方向。我时常停留在那个地方，面对着小院四周的围墙。我曾如此沉醉于从前的噢……思量。可如今你我天涯各一方，一切再也无法回到老路上。”

En:
I would often go there. To the tiny church there. The smallest church in Saint-Saëns -- though it once was larger.

How the rill may rest there. Down through the mist there. Toward the seven sisters -- toward those pale cliffs there

I would often stay there. In the tiny yard there. I have been so glad here -- looking forward to the past here

But now you are alone. None of this matters at all.
Zh: “我时常会去那个地方，圣桑最小的那座教堂。我不愿思念它过去的噢……辉煌。雾中的溪水涌向何方，流淌过七位姐妹身旁。那尽头是灰域之崖的噢……方向。我时常停留在那个地方，面对着小院四周的围墙。我曾如此沉醉于从前的噢……思量。可如今你我天涯各一方，一切再也无法回到老路上。”

En: The stage is all set up for your performance. Feels... silent. You can hear the pellets creak under your feet.
Zh: 舞台已为你的演出准备就绪。你感到……万籁俱寂，静得能听到脚下的光球在嘎吱作响。

En: What are you doing? I told you it was for customers only. I'm not turning on the karaoke carousel unless you pay your bills.
Zh: 你干嘛呢？我跟你说了这仅限顾客使用。在你把账单付清之前，别指望我会打开卡拉OK点唱机。”

En: Put your lips against the microphone -- test it.
Zh: 把嘴唇贴到麦克风上——测试一下。

En: Look around the room first.
Zh: 先观察一下室内的情况。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I'm ready, Garte. Hit play.
Zh: “我准备好了，加尔特，开始吧。”

En: Throwing in some dance moves.
Zh: 加入一些舞蹈动作。

En: The hand conundrum.
Zh: 手足无措。

En: Too close to the mic.
Zh: 离麦克风太近了。

En: Inspired by Arno van Eyck.
Zh: 受到阿诺·凡·艾克的启发。

En: Superstar cop.
Zh: 明星警探。

En: Dresscode: Insane silk dragon.
Zh: 着装要求：丝绸狂龙。

En: So, are you ready for your... *thing* now? Let me know when I should turn on the karaoke carousel.
Zh: “你准备好……*搞事*了吗？需要我打开卡拉OK的时候就说一声。”

En: Look, Kim, I'm going to sing karaoke!
Zh: “看好了，金。我要唱卡拉OK了！”

En: I can see that. The lieutenant steps away from the stage, ready for your performance.
Zh: “看到了。”警督走下舞台，准备欣赏你的表演。

En: The bar is full and buzzing with chatter. No one is paying you any attention, but still you feel your knees turn to noodles. Okay, now a couple is looking at you! Even worse...
Zh: 室内人声鼎沸。虽然没人注意到你，但你还是感到自己两腿发软。好嘛，突然有对情侣在看着你了！这下你更怯场了……

En: Immediately a loud feedback noise startles the room. You feel like an amateur. How are you supposed to hold the mic? Should you just *sing* into it? Where should you stand?
Zh: 一声刺耳的反馈噪音突然惊吓到了室内的所有顾客。你感觉自己特别业余。握麦克风的正确姿势是什么？是不是该直接对着它*开唱*？你到底应该站在什么位置？

En: It's still early here. Faint daylight is seeping into the dining hall. All the tables have been wiped pristine clean for the customers to come...
Zh: 现在时间还早，微弱的日光正慢慢渗入餐厅。所有的桌子都擦得干干净净，静待客人的光临……

En:
I would often go there. To the tiny church there. The smallest church in Saint-Saëns -- though it once was larger.

How the rill may rest there. Down through the mist there. Toward the seven sisters -- toward those pale cliffs there

I would often stay there. In the tiny yard there. I have been so glad here -- looking forward to the past here

But now you are alone. None of this matters at all.
Zh: “我时常会去那个地方，圣桑最小的那座教堂。我不愿思念它过去的噢……辉煌。雾中的溪水涌向何方，流淌过七位姐妹身旁。那尽头是灰域之崖的噢……方向。我时常停留在那个地方，面对着小院四周的围墙。我曾如此沉醉于从前的噢……思量。可如今你我天涯各一方，一切再也无法回到老路上。”

En: There are *some* people out there, but mostly -- a post meridian slumber has fallen on the premise. Not super lively. It would be wiser to perform in the evening, no? But the choice is yours...
Zh: 台下多少*有点*观众。但最主要的是——当前正值下午觉时间。这里的生意算不上兴旺。等到晚上再唱更明智一些，不是吗？但你说了算……

En: The dining hall has cleared up of customers for tonight. Even the kitchen is closed, and someone has turned down the lights. Maybe it's a good idea to do it in the dark like this? Almost after closing hours...
Zh: 餐厅已经清走了今晚的全部顾客。就连厨房也关门了。甚至连灯光都被调暗了。也许你应该在一片黑暗之中高歌一曲？快打烊了……

En: I want dedicate this song to whoever wrote me that fucking letter. I still love you. Thank you very much, asshole.
Zh: “无论是哪位给我写的那封信，这首歌都是献给你的。我仍然爱你。非常感谢，你个混球。”

En: I'd like to dedicate this song to my partner, Kim Kitsuragi. (Nod to the lieutenant.)
Zh: “我想把这首歌献给我的搭档金·曷城。”（向警督点头示意。）”

En: I'd like to dedicate this song to my partner, Kim, even though he's not here right now.
Zh: “我想把这首歌献给我的搭档，金。虽然他并不在场。”

En: I'd like to dedicate this song to Garte for letting me sing it. It's all shit, Garte.
Zh: “我想把这首歌献给加尔特，感谢他给了我演唱的机会。一切都太操蛋了，加尔特。”

En: You're sweating...
Zh: 你大汗淋漓……

En: No, you're not. The *bills*, remember?
Zh: “没门儿。你还记得*账单*的事吗？”

En:
I would often go there. To the tiny church there. The smallest church in Saint-Saëns -- though it once was larger.

How the rill may rest there. Down through the mist there. Toward the seven sisters -- toward those pale cliffs there

I would often stay there. In the tiny yard there. I have been so glad here -- looking forward to the past here

But now you are alone. None of this matters at all.
Zh: “我时常会去那个地方，圣桑最小的那座教堂。我不愿思念它过去的噢……辉煌。雾中的溪水涌向何方，流淌过七位姐妹身旁。那尽头是灰域之崖的噢……方向。我时常停留在那个地方，面对着小院四周的围墙。我曾如此沉醉于从前的噢……思量。可如今你我天涯各一方，一切再也无法回到老路上。”

En: Do you hear that? It's the most pathetic applause in the world, Harry. Made of pity. No one *liked* you.
Zh: 听到了吗？这就是全世界最可悲的喝彩，哈利。仅仅是出于同情。从来就没人*喜欢*你。

En: I'd like to dedicate this song to the pale nothing that will devour the world.
Zh: “我想把这首歌献给终将吞噬整个世界的灰域虚空。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't say anything, but gives you a quick smile, before turning away.
Zh: 警督一言不发，但在他转过身去之前，你看到了他脸上有笑容一闪而过。

En: He's incapable of blushing, but if he weren't -- he'd blush.
Zh: 他是不会脸红的人，如果他会脸红的话——此刻他一定会脸红的。

En: Good, good, the cafeteria manager intervenes to cut the moment short. "Are we ready? I want to unplug the microphone now."
Zh: “好了，好了。”餐厅经理打断了你的致辞。“准备好了吗？我都想把麦克风拔掉了。”

En: Yes, well... He clearly doesn't know how to respond -- your words caught him by surprise. "I'm just doing my job, that's all."
Zh: “好吧，呃……”他显然不知该如何回应——你的致辞完全出乎意料。“这是我的本职工作，仅此而已。”

En: I don't want to dedicate this song to anyone. I performed it for myself.
Zh: “这首歌不献给任何人。我仅为自己歌唱。”

En: The microphone amplifies your voice in an uncomfortable manner. Someone coughs. Most people have gone back to talking now.
Zh: 麦克风以令人不适的方式放大了你的声音。有人在咳嗽。大多数观众已经重新回到了他们的谈话中。

En: But uh, are you done now? Can I unplug the microphone? I'd like to unplug the microphone. He's ready for things to get back to normal.
Zh: “呃，你完事儿了吗？我能把麦克风拔掉了吗？我这就拔了啊。”他已经迫不及待地想要恢复正常了。

En: Thank you, Martinaise. [Leave.]
Zh: “感谢你，马丁内斯。”[离开。]

En: Evening will come, we will sew the white sail! Workers of the world, unite! [Leave.]
Zh: “夜幕即将降临，我们要亲手缝制白色的风帆！全世界的工人们，团结起来！”[离开。]

En: [Whip out your finger pistols, shoot in the air and leave.]
Zh: [以拔枪的气势伸出双手，向空气比划几下开枪的姿势，然后退场。]

En: It's been an honour. [Bow and leave.]
Zh: “这是我的荣幸。”[鞠躬离开。]

En: I'd like to dedicate this song to Contact Mike. Everyone else *sucks*.
Zh: “我想把这首歌献给一击即溃·迈克，其他人都*弱爆*了。”

En: [Say nothing. Leave enigmatically.]
Zh: [一言不发，神秘地退场。]

En: Someone's making coffee in the kitchen, but aside from that no one's really around yet. It would be a much better idea to perform in the evening, no?
Zh: 目前店内只有在厨房里煮咖啡的人，尚无顾客登门。也许等到晚上再唱更明智一些，不是吗？

En: They are going to hate you.
Zh: 他们会恨你的。

En: Just... just make sure you move, okay? Don't be stiff.
Zh: 你……一定要动起来，好吗？别傻呆着。

En:
I would often go there. To the tiny church there. The smallest church in Saint-Saëns -- though it once was larger.

How the rill may rest there. Down through the mist there. Toward the seven sisters -- toward those pale cliffs there

I would often stay there. In the tiny yard there. I have been so glad here -- looking forward to the past here

But now you are alone. None of this matters at all.
Zh: “我时常会去那个地方，圣桑最小的那座教堂。我不愿思念它过去的噢……辉煌。雾中的溪水涌向何方，流淌过七位姐妹身旁。那尽头是灰域之崖的噢……方向。我时常停留在那个地方，面对着小院四周的围墙。我曾如此沉醉于从前的噢……思量。可如今你我天涯各一方，一切再也无法回到老路上。”

En: Yes. Not bad at all. The lieutenant joins in. You feel your hands shake as awareness of your body returns to you.
Zh: “不错，非常不错。”警督也对你表示了赞赏。在身体恢复知觉之后，你才意识到自己的双手正在颤抖。

En: Thank you. I don't need big crowds, I just need my friends.
Zh: “谢谢你。我不需要太多观众，只要我的朋友们在场就行。”

En: Life is a fucking joke.
Zh: “人生就他妈是个笑话。”

En: Thanks for bearing with me. I'm gonna get back to work now.
Zh: “感谢你迁就我。我也该回去工作了。”

En: Okay. Yes. I'm going to unplug the microphone, so you can get off the stage now.
Zh: “好，好的。我这就要拔掉麦克风了，你可以退场了。”

En: Hands... Where do you put your *hands*?!
Zh: 双手……你的*双手*到底该放在什么地方？

En: Someone dims the lights as the music starts... Okay. Here we go...
Zh: 音乐响起，灯光也随之被调暗……很好。开唱吧……

En: Someone dims the lights as the music starts. A lump's in your throat suddenly...
Zh: 音乐响起，灯光也随之被调暗……突然间，你感到喉咙哽住了……

En: The tape clicks into the carousel and then the music starts...
Zh: 磁带滑入点唱机，音乐随之响起……

En: The tape clicks into the carousel and then the music starts... God, a lump's in your throat suddenly...
Zh: 磁带滑入点唱机，音乐随之响起……天呐，你感到喉咙突然哽住了……

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / KIM FIRST VISIT barks
En: Goddamned pinball.
Zh: “该死的弹球机。”

En: So the Whirling phone isn't working at all?
Zh: “飞旋旅社的电话完全不能用了吗？”

En: ... No one?
Zh: ……没人吗？

En: Really?
Zh: 真的吗？

En: Absolutely nothing?
Zh: 完全没有吗？

En: Just me, huh? Wow. Well... Good luck!
Zh: 只有我了，是吗？哇哦，好吧……祝你好运！

En: Just me, huh? Wow. Well... Good luck!
Zh: 只有我了，是吗？哇哦，好吧……祝你好运！

En: Really?
Zh: 真的吗？

En: Absolutely nothing?
Zh: 完全没有吗？

En: ... No one?
Zh: ……没人吗？

En: Still, there's definitely something there to check out... He turns the Eugene. "We need to have a look at that secret passage, boys."
Zh: “不过，那里绝对还有东西值得回去……”他转向尤金。“我们得去那条秘密通道看看，兄弟们。”

En: Through a secret passage.
Zh: “穿过一条秘密通道。”

En: I see. But what's back there, I mean -- storage rooms or shit?
Zh: “我明白了。但后面是什么，我是说——是储藏室之类的吗？”

En: ... a secret passage. That leads to the roof.
Zh: “……一条秘密通道，直通屋顶。”

En: I'll level with you, Titus. I've still got to check it out.
Zh: “跟你说实话吧提图斯，我还得去看一眼。”

En: Wild stuff, Titus. Stuff you're not prepared to believe.
Zh: “非常狂野的东西，提图斯，你还没做好准备接受的东西。”

En: You've not even been back there, have you? The big guy sighs at his beer. "Fuckin' clown. Honest to god, this shit is only funny in the circus."
Zh: “你甚至都没到后面去过，是吗？”大个子对着啤酒叹了口气。“该死的小丑。说真的，这玩意只有在马戏团里才好玩。”

En: The big man pinches the bridge of his nose. "Course you do, clown. Of fucking course. That's how you do it in the Revachol Circus Militia."
Zh: 那个大块头捏住自己的鼻梁。“当然了，小丑，太他妈当然了。你在瑞瓦肖马戏民兵团里就是这么干的。”

En: Cop, I'm gonna stop you right there. He pushes up his hat. "I'll save you the embarrassment -- I won't force you to tell me you've not been back there."
Zh: “条子，我要打断一下你。”他把帽子向上推了推。“免得你尴尬——我就不强迫你承认自己没去过后面了。”

En: While we cannot say for certain right *now*, we *will* find out.
Zh: “虽然*现在*我们还不能肯定，但我们*会*找到答案的。”

En: Sure, cops, sure... he turns the Eugene. "We need to have a look at that secret passage, boys."
Zh: “当然，条子，肯定的……”他转向尤金。“我们得去那条秘密通道看看，兄弟们。”

En: I don't know, but I'll find out.
Zh: “我不知道，但我会调查清楚的。”

En: Sure, cop, sure... he turns the Eugene. "We need to have a look at that secret passage, boys."
Zh: “当然，条子，肯定的……”他转向尤金。“我们得去那条秘密通道看看，兄弟们。”

En: You got it boss! Professional law enforcement will investigate.
Zh: “知道了，老大！专业执法人员会着手调查的。”

En: The big guy smiles. "You got something else to back this route up? Or is that it?"
Zh: 大个子笑了起来。“你还有别的东西能证明这条路径的可能吗？还是仅此而已了？”

En: The elevator is on the *outer wall* of the building. It's an old dumbwaiter -- used for moving pinball machines up and down from the workshop.
Zh: “电梯在大楼*外面*，是台旧升降机——用于从工坊里上下搬运弹球机。”

En: The big guy takes a long, considered gulp of beer. "And? How does she come into it?"
Zh: 那个大块头深思熟虑地喝了一大口啤酒。“然后呢？她是怎么进去的？”

En: Ruby could have gone up -- shot him -- come down -- all under seven minutes.
Zh: “露比完全可以上楼——朝他开枪——下楼——在七分钟之内完成所有。”

En: And I suppose you have a theory on that?
Zh: “我猜你已经有一套理论了？”

En: But she didn't! This is fucking stupid, Titus. This and the queer thing, all of it! The blond man looks around. "Why aren't more of you defending her? This is fucking stupid, Titus."
Zh: “但她没有！这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。这个还有姬佬的事，一切的一切！”金发男子看了一圈。“为什么你们都不替她说话？这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。”

En: Fuck you, man, I would never fuck my guys over like that. He squeaks with indignation. "Especially for some bird..."
Zh: “去你的吧，老兄，我永远不会这么坑自己兄弟的。”他气得哇哇直叫。“更别说是为了一只鸟……”

En: Which is why she didn't! This is fucking stupid, Titus. This and the queer thing, all of it! The blond man waves his beer around. "Why aren't more of you defending her? This is fucking stupid, Titus."
Zh: “所以她没有！这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。这个还有姬佬的事，一切的一切！”金发男子看了一圈。“为什么你们都不替她说话？这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。”

En: Thanks for the review, Titus. (Conclude.)
Zh: “感谢你的回顾，提图斯。”（总结。）

En: He sighs heavily. "Anything else you want, cop?"
Zh: 他重重叹了口气。“还有什么想问的吗，条子？”

En: Do you know what she's doing with Ulan frequencies?
Zh: “你知道她在用Ulan频率做什么吗？”

En: The what now? I've no idea. He looks around. "Boys?"
Zh: “你说什么？我听不懂。”他四周看了看。“兄弟们？”

En: She said she's... building a... His voice is very quiet. "A pale emitter."
Zh: “她说她在……做一个……”他的声音很安定。“灰域发射器。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: We were talkin' about radio equipment. She said she's doing Ulan frequencies and a pale-something. I don't know more.
Zh: “我们聊起了无线电设备。她说她在做Ulan频率和一个灰域什么的东西。我就知道这些了。”

En: This guy barely understands what he's talking about.
Zh: 这家伙完全不知道自己在说什么。

En: There you have it. Pale-something. Titus puts an end to it.
Zh: “就这样。灰域什么的。”提图斯结束了这个话题。

En: It's called a... radio, you believe. The gardener may have played you again when she stormed out. She has her own plan.
Zh: 你相信，它的名字叫……无线电。园丁摔门而出的时候，可能又耍了你一次。她有自己的计划。

En: Stop thinking about your lost gun, dammit. You'll get a heart attack.
Zh: 别再想你那把丢掉的枪了，该死的。你会得心脏病的。

En: I'll start there. One more question: what does Ruby look like?
Zh: “我会从那里开始。还有一个问题：露比看起来什么样？”

En: It feels like you'll get to know -- soon enough.
Zh: 感觉你会知道的——很快。

En: Boyish. Hair's red, dyed. She looks like a lorryman.
Zh: “有些男孩子气。红头发，不过是染的。她看上去就像个开货車的。”

En: Could this *complex operation* be the one Joyce asked you to look into? Probably, though they'll never open up about it. You'll have to keep investigating!
Zh: 这个*复杂的业务*会不会跟乔伊斯请求你调查的事情有关？也许吧，不过他们永远不会挑明的。你必须继续调查！

En: Of course! Ruby is the lady driver in this great big *Jam Mystery*! Probably. You'll have to keep investigating -- they'll never open up about it.
Zh: 当然！露比就是*神秘大堵車*队伍里的女司机！有这个可能。你必须继续调查——他们永远不会挑明的。

En: You know this already. It all fits together: Ruby, the missing Hardie, was present at the hanging and running the drug trade.
Zh: 这个你已经知道了。一切都很吻合：露比，失踪的哈迪兄弟，身处私刑现场，还在贩毒。

En: Oh no, he's stuck again. Please, someone -- just animate him. I got nothing.
Zh: 哦不，他又卡住了。拜托了，有谁能——给他鼓鼓劲儿，我已经无能为力了。

En: My wife left me and I'm completely alone.
Zh: “我的妻子离开了我，就剩下我孤独一人了。”

En: Nothing is happening. The pieces are there, but they remain unconnected.
Zh: 什么都没有发生。无数碎片散落在那里，却仍然无法将它们串联起来。

En: No, no -- we can do this. (Squint your eyes.)
Zh: 不，不——我们可以的。（眯起眼睛。）

En: Get a move on, asshole! Stop standing with your dick in your hand.
Zh: 快点，混蛋！别站着撸管了。

En: Sorry. You're not coming up with anything. Again, the pieces are there: she could have done it, somehow, something else, wala-wala-bing-bang -- it's just not coming together.
Zh: 抱歉，你什么都没想出来。又一次，碎片就在那里：很可能就是她干的，以某种手段，某种原因，巴拉-巴拉-啪-啪-就是拼凑不起来。

En: Your mouth is open. Close it. It's really, really going to help if you do.
Zh: 你的嘴巴是张开的，快合上。如果你闭上嘴巴，真的，真的就帮上大忙了。

En: No, no! He needs to say: "Five hundred Lears and I can't remember the first line!"
Zh: 不！他得说：“五百遍的李尔王，我还记不住第一行。”

En: Stop drooling. Run around or something. Hit yourself in the face. *Anything*. No one has any idea what to do with you if you just stand here.
Zh: 别再流口水了。兜个圈子，打自己的脸，*任何事*。如果你只是站在那里，没人知道该那你怎么办。

En: His wife left him. He needs to kill himself -- make her sorry she did.
Zh: 他的妻子离开了他，他得自杀——才能让她感到后悔。

En: Say something. If you say something you can jolt out of the stupor. Just one sentence. Here we go...
Zh: 说点什么吧。如果你说了什么，就能从晕厥中清醒过来。一句话就好，来吧……

En: I shouldn't have said that. I just wanted to hear the components of the theory...
Zh: 我不该那么说的，我只是想听听理论的构成……

En: I shouldn't have said that. I just wanted to hear the components of the theory...
Zh: 我不该那么说的，我只是想听听理论的元素……

En: I just want to hear the components of the theory.
Zh: 我只是想听听这个理论的构成部分。

En: Give up.
Zh: 放弃。

En: Walla-walla-bing-bang?
Zh: 巴拉-巴拉-啪-啪？

En: (Squint your eyes harder.)
Zh: （更用力地眯着眼睛。）

En: Why am I such an idiot?
Zh: 为什么我这么傻呢？

En: There we go. Your mind is a relaxed muscle. It's so nice and easy to give up, isn't it? On the downside -- you have no idea what you were supposed to do now. I could have *debriefed* you.
Zh: 好了。你的大脑现在是一块松懈的肌肉。放弃很容易，不是吗？缺点就是——你根本不知道现在应该干什么。我可以跟你*报告*一下。

En: No one knows.
Zh: 没人知道。

En: Still nothing -- but that's okay. This doesn't have to turn into some kind of meltdown. You're just a cop taking your time to present a theory.
Zh: 还是什么也没有——但是没关系，这并不会变成一场灾难。你只是个从容提出自己理论的条子。

En: I don't know what it means, but it felt like the most appropriate thing to say. That's what the *Witch Doctor* would say, at least.
Zh: 我不知道那是什么意思，但感觉是现在该说的话中最恰当的。至少，*巫医*也会这么说。

En: Please, please do not melt down again. We're so close to wrapping this up and getting out of here.
Zh: 拜托，拜托不要再垮台了。我们马上就能结束这一切，离开这里了。

En: Ehm... The lieutenant puts his hand on your shoulder. "Unless we have more business, we should..." He looks to the door.
Zh: “嗯……”警督把手搭在你的肩膀上。“除非我们有更多事务，否则我们应该……”他看着门。

En: Blissful idiot -- say something. You've stood there for too long.
Zh: 无忧无虑的白痴——快说点什么，你在那里站得太久了。

En: You're not an idiot. Don't say that. This is a really big mess and being a detective is hard.
Zh: 你不是白痴，别那么说。如今一切都是一团乱麻，而且当一名警探真的很不容易。

En: Go out and run circles around the building until you come up with something. Whatever you do, don't stand here anymore.
Zh: 走出去，绕着大楼跑一圈，直到你想出来为止。不管你做什么，都不要站在这里发呆了。

En: Why does Puzzle Face get to *not* melt down? You and I melted down like crazy, remember?
Zh: 为什么拼图脸*不会*垮台呢？你和我都快崩溃了，记得吗？

En: Right. Puzzle Face shouldn't be the cool guy when he fails. *I* was supposed to be that.
Zh: 没错。当他失败的时候拼图脸就不该是酷的那家伙了，酷的人应该是*我*才对。

En: It's that simple.
Zh: 就是那么简单。

En: Can't get through the door? You know what would help -- doing a *thorough* search of this room! Maybe something's hidden.
Zh: 进不了门？你知道什么能派上用场——把这个房间*彻底*搜查一遍！或许会有些隐藏的东西。

En: There's a *spooky* building West of this. Take a flashlight with you, search the basement. There are secrets -- magisterial, ancient secrets that may assist you.
Zh: 这里往西有座*闹鬼*的建筑。带上手电筒，检查地下室。里面有个秘密——权威的、古老的秘密或许能帮到你。

En: Hey! Did someone say something already? No? Yes? Who cares! Upstairs, in Klaasje's bedroom -- the window the bullet came through. Analyse that!
Zh: 嘿！有人说什么了吗？没有？有吗？谁在乎呢！楼上，在卡拉洁的卧房里——被子弹射穿的玻璃，分析一下！

En: Let's look at this as a learning experience. What are the components you need to implicate Ruby?
Zh: 不如把它当做一次学习经历。你还需要哪些元素才能给露比定罪？

En: Shush. I'm just more level headed than you. That's my thing. Let's look at this as a learning experience. What are the components you need to implicate Ruby?
Zh: 嘘。我只是比你更加冷静一点，我就是这样。不如把它当做一次学习经历。你还需要哪些元素才能给露比定罪？

En: He's *thinking*. Let the man think. The lieutenant defends you.
Zh: “他在*思考*呢，让他思考一会。”警督维护着你。

En: Also, you should say: I need to kill myself and it's all over!
Zh: 还有，你得说：我要自杀，一切都结束了！

En: No-no. He already said that, and nothing good came of it. Just do what the nice helpful guys said -- and *don't* do the bad things.
Zh: 不-不。他已经说过了，但是一点好处都没有。就照那些好心帮忙的人说的做——*不要*做坏事。

En: No-no! Nothing good will come of it -- he did the right thing not saying that the last time. Just do what the nice helpful guys said -- and *don't* do the bad things.
Zh: 不-不！这不会有什么好结果的——上次他没那样说是对的。就照那些好心帮忙的人说的做——*不要*做坏事。

En: Reporting for duty! A *thorough* search of this Union box may help unearth something *incriminating*! Pin it on her -- she's been here, right?
Zh: 报告！对这个工会包厢进行一次*彻底的*搜查可能会有助于找出一些*罪证*！推到她身上吧——她也来过这儿，对吧？

En: Five hundred Lears and I can't remember the first line.
Zh: “五百遍的李尔王，我还记不住第一行。”

En: Here you go: access to the roof, weapon, and motive. That's not too hard. And here is what you can do to make it even easier -- c'mon guys!
Zh: 就是这样：进入屋顶、武器和动机，也不算太难。还有能让你把事情变得更简单的方法——来吧伙计们！

En: I don't know what to say to that.
Zh: “我不知道该说些什么。”

En: Nah boss, he's just mentally handicapped. I've seen them do that.
Zh: “得了老大，他就是个智障。我见识过他们那一套。”

En: You know, I'm beginning to think this clown is squinting at me... He looks around. "...because he wants to start something."
Zh: “知道吗，我开始觉得这个小丑在眯着眼睛看我了……”他四下看了看。“……因为他准备开始搞事了。”

En: For starters, you could search the Whirling-in-Rags for hidden passages she could have used to get to the roof. There's a door in the *kitchen* I've had my eye on for a while...
Zh: 首先，你可以在褴褛飞旋里寻找她能用来爬上屋顶的秘密通道。*厨房*里的那扇门我已经注意好久了……

En: Smart move. The components are: access to the roof, weapon, and motive. Here is what you can do to make it easier -- c'mon guys!
Zh: 明智之举。元素包括：进入屋顶、武器和动机。还有能让你把事情变得更简单的方法——来吧伙计们！

En: That's sad, man -- but why are you squinting like that? The tattooed man leans closer. "Looks to me like he's about to get aggressive."
Zh: “真是令人难过，老兄——但你为什么要那样眯着眼睛看人？”纹身男靠得更近了。“在我看来，他要变得咄咄逼人起来了。”

En: Say: "Five hundred Lears and I can't remember the first line!!!"
Zh: 说：“五百遍的李尔王，我还记不住第一行！！！”

En: You should say: I need to kill myself and it's all over!
Zh: 你应该说：我要自杀，一切都结束了！

En: No, no. The lieutenant looks at you with slight worry. "That's his... concentration face."
Zh: “不，不。”警督略带担忧地看着你。“这是他……在集中精神的表情。”

En: He fires a nod of solidarity in your direction, as if to say: take your time. It's okay.
Zh: 他朝你释放了一个团结有爱的讯号，仿佛在说：慢慢来，没关系。

En: For starters, you could find out what's behind that mystery door in the kitchen. If it's a secret route, she could have used that to get to the roof!
Zh: 首先，你可以去调查厨房里那扇神秘的门背后是什么。如果那是条秘密通道，她很可能就是利用它爬上屋顶的！

En: You could crack Klaasje's last defences. Maybe she'll tell you something helpful?
Zh: 你可以攻破卡拉洁最后一道防线，或许她会告诉你一些有用的事？

En: Wow, you've been very thorough. There's nothing else to add -- just crack that Klaasje's defences. Maybe she'll tell you something helpful?
Zh: 哇哦，你想得真是周到。没有什么好补充的了——只要攻破卡拉洁最后一道防线，或许她会告诉你一些有用的事？

En: Hey! Lost gun right here! Lost and found!
Zh: 喂！失枪就在这里！丢掉之后又找回来了！

En: Say... Hard to remember with your brain in overload, but have you smoked lately? A cigarette may help you.
Zh: 比方说……你的脑袋超载了很难记起来，但是，你最近抽烟了吗？来根烟也许能帮到你。

En: YES! Huff and puff and *blow* this case wide open!
Zh: 是的！吞云吐雾，然后把这个案子*查个*水落石出！

En: Say... Hard to remember with your brain in overload, but have you smoked lately? A cigarette may help you.
Zh: 比方说……你的脑袋超载了很难记起来，但是，你最近抽烟了吗？来根烟也许能帮到你。

En: YES! Huff and puff and *blow* this case wide open!
Zh: 是的！吞云吐雾，然后把这个案子*查个*水落石出！

En: Never -- not until the case is solved. And then the next case. And the next...
Zh: 永不——直到这个案子解决，然后是下一个案子，再下一个……

En: And then when they're all empty, just move on. Unto the next can. Don't look back -- unless there was something you *missed*.
Zh: 等到把他们全部掏空，再继续前进，直到下一个罐子。不要回头——除非是你*错过*了什么。

En: Not until you *have* something on her. She said she's heard of you from Jamrock. That you're a human can-opener. That you play suspects against each other. Open them up, like cans.
Zh: “直到你*找出*她的把柄。她说她在加姆洛克听说过你，是个人形开罐器。说你会挑拨嫌犯们彼此针对，像开罐头一样，撬开他们的嘴。”

En: She's right. I won't.
Zh: “她是对的，我不会。”

En: Whack someone in my district? That doesn't curry much *favour* with Titus Hardie. She *has* to know that... He rubs his chin and looks out the window.
Zh: “想在我的地盘打人？这可不会让提图斯·哈迪有多少*好感*。她*必须*得知道……”他摸了摸下巴，望向窗外。

En: You had expressed -- on occasion -- your dislike of the mercenaries' presence in Martinaise, right?
Zh: “你已经表示过了——有时候你不喜欢雇佣兵出现在马丁内斯，对吧？”

En: It's the kind of chin-rubbing men -- often leaders -- do when they think of *punishment*.
Zh: 这是那种在思索*惩罚措施*时——通常都是领头人——就会摩挲下巴的人。

En: Okay -- they're connected by the winch. How, though? Is there a dumbwaiter back there?
Zh: “好吧——它们是由起吊机连接的。但是怎么运作的呢？后面有个升降机吗？”

En: There is a secret route in the kitchen that leads *straight* to the roof.
Zh: “厨房里有一条直通屋顶的秘密通道。”

En: A sudden flash of lightning in your neocortex -- the hostel-cafeteria is lit by its eerie blaze. Floor plans, bullet trajectories, webs of human emotion all channelled into a single thought...
Zh: 你的大脑皮层突然闪过一道闪电——旅社的餐厅被它可怕的火焰照亮了。建筑平面图，弹道轨迹以及人类情感的网络都被汇集至一个想法中……

En: *Or* she knew what happened, because she killed him.
Zh: “*或者*她很清楚发生了什么，因为是她杀的人。”

En: Let's not get dramatic.
Zh: 我们别太激动了。

En: *Not* so useful. Titus looks at Alain, then Eugene.
Zh: “*用不着*。”提图斯看着阿兰，然后看了看尤金。

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢你。”

En: A massive activity spike in the *amygdala copicum* -- the part cops use to pin murders on people.
Zh: *杏仁体*活跃度激增——警察用来确定谋杀罪犯的部分。

En: Fucking hell... The blond man is in some kind of anguish that makes him stare into his garlic bread bowl, intently.
Zh: “该死的……”金发男子陷入了某种痛苦之中，这让他目不转睛地盯着自己的大蒜面包碗。

En: Betrayal is not painless. Even a doubt of it hurts.
Zh: 背叛并非没有痛苦，甚至连怀疑都是痛苦的。

En: Why are you so sure Ruby *didn't* off him?
Zh: “你为什么这么肯定露比*没有*了结他？”

En: Maybe she killed him because she thought it would curry favour with you?
Zh: “也许她杀了他，是因为觉得这样就能讨好你？”

En: Yeah, some people have their shit together -- unlike you. What's wrong with that?
Zh: “是啊，有些人就会一起混——不像你。有什么不对吗？”

En: Yeah, some people have their shit together. What's wrong with that?
Zh: “是啊，有些人就会一起混。有什么不对吗？”

En: Because she was here *all night*. With us.
Zh: “因为她*整晚*都在这里，跟我们一起。”

En: So *that'*s what you were squinting at. He looks at you. "You were trying to come up a theory, weren't you? That she did it."
Zh: “原来你在寻思*这个*。”他看着你。“你想提出一个推测，是不是？说她是凶手？”

En: He *knows* he's avoiding the more serious implication -- and so does Titus.
Zh: 他*知道*自己避开了更严重的暗示——提图斯也是。

En: Yes, she's just got her *shit together*. That's how she knew.
Zh: “是的，她只是跟你们*一起混*。她就是这样知道的。”

En: Yeah... The man seems unsure of himself.
Zh: “是啊……”这个人似乎也很不确定。

En: She *organizes* things. She doesn't whack people. That's not the kind of person she is. She's a... talker.
Zh: “她搞*组织策划*，不伤人的，她不是那种人。她是个……用谈话解决问题的人。”

En: He gestures with his beer can -- so you haven't won him over yet! That bad boy goes down on the table only when he's completely focused on something else.
Zh: 他拿着啤酒罐做了个手势——这么说你还没能赢得他的好感！那家伙只有在完全专注于某些事情的时候才会把它放到桌子上。

En: Eugene wants a woman on the team so they can do their job. That must be *hard*. Half of Martinaise is female...
Zh: 尤金想要一个女人加入他们，以便他们完成工作。那一定很*困难*，马丁内斯有一半都是女性……

En: She was here all night? The lieutenant ignores the tattooed man. "11:30 to 12:15 -- she was here during *all* that time?"
Zh: “她整晚都在这里？”警督没有理睬那个纹身男。“从11:30到12:25——这段时间她*一直*都在？”

En: Would you prefer to go on trial with your friends -- as part of a lynch mob -- or alone, for committing murder?
Zh: “你想跟你的朋友一起受审吗——作为私刑帮派的一员——或者因为谋杀罪，单独受审？”

En: When Klaasje came downstairs -- Ruby appeared to *know* that something was wrong.
Zh: “卡拉洁下楼的时候——露比似乎已经*知道*出事了。”

En: Nah, man. That's just Ruby. She's got shit under control, the man explains. "That's her whole thing. That's why she's so good."
Zh: “哪有，老兄。那就是露比，一切都在她的掌控之中。”那人解释说。“她就是很有一手，所以她才那么厉害。”

En: Plus, man, it's like *female intuition* you know. Women talk to women. He peeks at Titus. "Which is sorta why we need someone on the team who they talk to."
Zh: “而且，老兄，就像*女人的直觉*一样。女人什么都会跟对方说。”他瞄了一眼提图斯。“所以我们才需要一个能跟他们交流的人。”

En: A more logical conclusion -- that you wish to avoid -- is that she knew what had happened, because she *did it*.
Zh: “一个更合乎逻辑的——也是你极力避免的结论——就是她很清楚发生了什么，因为是她杀的人。”

En: Wishful thinking has no place in a police investigation, the lieutenant tells Alain, his voice mild but firm.
Zh: “一厢情愿的想法在警方调查中是立不住脚的，”警督告诉阿兰，他的声音温和而坚定。

En: He makes a gruff gesture for you to continue.
Zh: 他比划了一个粗鲁的手势让你继续。

En: He's cobbling together shit so he can put her away. It's Cop 101.
Zh: “他在生编硬造好把她关进去。这是条子的必修课。”

En: Good one, Kim.
Zh: “说得好，金。”

En: Klaasje said Ruby *takes care* of things. You don't do that without a gun in Revachol.
Zh: “卡拉洁说露比会*料理*一切，在瑞瓦肖没有枪是不可能做到的。”

En: Well, let old Titus set your mind at ease then -- she didn't do it. She was here all night.
Zh: “行，就让老提图斯让你放宽心吧——她没有杀人，她整晚都在这里。”

En: Yeah, he was cobbling together shit. So he could put her away -- it's RCM 101.
Zh: “没错，他就是在生编硬造，好把她关进去——条子的必修课。”

En: How...? He looks sincerely curious.
Zh: “怎么会……？”他看上去真的很好奇。

En: Yeah -- exactly. Why not, man?
Zh: “没错——就是这样。为什么不呢，老兄？”

En: Just making an observation. Let's move on.
Zh: “只是观察一下而已，我们继续吧。”

En: Boss, but maybe... The blond guy interjects. "A single machine like that is worth over 500 reál."
Zh: “老大，也许……”金发男子插话。“像这样一台机器的价值就超过500雷亚尔。”

En: Yes. (Roll your eyes.) "Female intuition. That's what it was. You're right Eugene."
Zh: “是的。”（翻白眼。）“女人的直觉，就是这样，你说的没错，尤金。”

En: It wasn't that. I'm calling an end to this shit if you don't have anything better, cop.
Zh: “并不是那样。条子，如果你没有更好的说辞，我要叫停这一切了。”

En: Yeah... The man seems unsure of himself.
Zh: “是啊……”这个人似乎也很不确定。

En: Wishful thinking has no place in a police investigation, the lieutenant tells Eugene, his voice mild but firm.
Zh: “一厢情愿的想法在警方调查中是立不住脚的，”警督告诉阿兰，他的声音温和而坚定。

En: A more serious consideration -- that you wish to avoid -- is that she knew what happened, because she did it.
Zh: “一个更严谨的——也是你极力避免的结论——就是她很清楚发生了什么，因为是她杀的人。”

En: Through what looks like an abandoned pinball workshop.
Zh: “穿过一个看上去像是一间废弃的弹球工坊的地方。”

En: What else could you make out -- *how* recent? What size?
Zh: “你还认出了什么——*多久前*废弃的？什么尺寸？”

En: Yeah, with us, drinking. Near the stage there. He points to the karaoke stage.
Zh: “没错，跟我们一起，喝酒。就在舞台附近。”他指着卡拉OK的舞台。

En: The lieutenant opens his notes. "Sunday night 11:30 to 12:15 -- she was here *all* that time?"
Zh: 警督翻开自己的笔记本。“周日晚11:30到12:15——这段时间她*一直*都在？”

En: Recent. (Nod.)
Zh: “最近的。”（点头）。

En: Hmhm. People say there was a pinball arcade here, some time before the hostel -- what was it called, Theo?
Zh: “嗯。人们都说在旅社建成之前，这里曾是一家弹球游乐室——叫什么来着，特奥？”

En: No...
That key I found here. (Point at the window.) "Maybe it's for a secret route?"
Zh: “我在这儿找到的钥匙。”（指着窗户。）“也许能开启一条秘密路线？”

En: She didn't go out for fresh air -- for one smoke?
Zh: “她没有出去透会气——抽根烟吗？”

En: She didn't go to the bar -- for a drink?
Zh: “她没有去吧台——点杯酒吗？”

En: She didn't go to the toilet?
Zh: “她没有去洗手间吗？”

En: East Delta Pinball Arcade. The old man coughs. "*Weird* place -- went bankrupt."
Zh: “东德尔塔弹球游乐室。”老人咳嗽了几声。“*诡异*的地方——后来破产了。”

En: Okay, but... The man looks around. "How'd she get up? There's no room for a staircase in this building. *Or* an elevator, for that matter."
Zh: “好吧，可是……”男人看了一圈。“她是怎么上去的？这栋楼里没有安装楼梯，*或者*电梯的空间了。”

En: There's no fucking leadership challenge, here. A flash of rage; he calms himself. "Get your head out of your ass. This is serious."
Zh: “他妈的根本不存在什么挑战权威。”一阵愤怒之后他使自己冷静了下来。“别再异想天开了，这很严肃。”

En: Okay... The lieutenant steps in. "We've ruled out infighting. That's how this works: by exclusion. Remember, all we need to do is rule Ruby out too. That's all this is."
Zh: “好吧……”警督走了过来。“我们已经排除了内讧的可能，原理就是：排除法。记住，我们需要做的就是同样排除露比的嫌疑，就是这样。”

En: The window Klaasje gave for the time of death was From 11:30 to 12:15.
Zh: 卡拉洁给出的死亡时间窗口是从11:30到12:15。

En: There's a bit of truth in there for Titus to react so brashly. He does not like his authority questioned.
Zh: 提图斯的反应如此鲁莽是有一定道理的，他不喜欢自己的权威遭到质疑。

En: That's a lie.
Zh: 那是个谎言。

En: Maybe it's all part of a leadership challenge? Against you, Titus.
Zh: “也许这都是人们挑战权威的一部分？针对你的，提图斯。”

En: There's no fucking leadership challenge. A flash of rage; he calms himself. "Just when I thought you were taking this seriously, cop, you put your head in your ass."
Zh: “他妈的根本不存在什么挑战权威。”一阵愤怒之后他使自己冷静了下来。“正当我开始觉得你在严肃对待这件事的时候，条子，你又开始异想天开了。”

En: Man, now he's just throwing shit against the wall to see what sticks.
Zh: “老兄，他现在就是往墙上糊屎，看看什么东西能黏住。”

En: Yeah, everyone knows Glen loves guns.
Zh: “没错，人人都知道格伦喜欢枪。”

En: He thinks for a moment, then nods and says : "Good news is -- I'm still listening."
Zh: 他想了一会儿后点点头：“好消息是——我还在听。”

En: From there -- a door leads straight to the roof. You can just step outside.
Zh: “在那里——有一扇门直通屋顶。你可以直接出去。”

En: Fuck you... The man can't believe what he's hearing. "First he says she murdered him -- now she's a *f*g* too? It's a lie!"
Zh: “去你的吧……”那个男人不敢相信自己的耳朵。“他一开始说是她杀的人——现在她也变成*姬佬*了？一派胡言！”

En: What Kim said.
Zh: “金说过的。”

En: You mean to say she stayed put from 11:30 to 12:15? *All* that time?
Zh: “你是说她从11:30到12:15都一动不动地待在那儿？*一直*都在？”

En: In the *whole* 45-minute window -- she was with you *all the time*?
Zh: “*整整*45分钟的时间窗口——她*一直*都跟你在一起吗？”

En: You know that's not the case.
Zh: “你知道事实并非如此。”

En: Liberal, man? Ruby's like a hard core patriot. You'd have to be blind not to... He looks around. "Guys, I'm not the only one who *knew*, right?"
Zh: “自由主义者，老兄？露比是个铁杆爱国者。你们得是瞎了才会不知道……”他四处看了一圈。“伙计们，不是只有我一个人*知道*，对吧？”

En: She didn't tell you shit. He shakes his head. "If she did, it was lies."
Zh: “她什么都没有对你说。”他摇摇头。“就算她说了，也是谎言。”

En: It's scary, yes. She probably went to a University and learned it from a *liberal*.
Zh: “很可怕，确实。她可能是去上大学的时候，从一个*自由主义者*那里学来的。”

En: She just told us -- Ruby made her scared and she spilled the beans.
Zh: “她刚刚告诉我们——露比把她吓坏了，她说漏了嘴。”

En: That Ruby is queer as cabaret, now that I start thinking about it. I don't know I why didn't see it earlier... He looks out the window, pensively.
Zh: 他若有所思地看着窗外。“我仔细想想，那个露比确实就跟卡巴莱歌舞厅的姬佬似的。我不知道自己为什么没有早点发现……”

En: If that's the case then it's not right. He looks at Titus, then Alain. "But it's not the case -- right?"
Zh: “如果是这样，那答案就是否定的。”他看了看提图斯，然后阿兰。“但事实并非如此——对吗？”

En: There *are* many pieces that fit together that way, Eugene. Face it.
Zh: 很多碎片*都*已经能拼凑出那个答案了，尤金。坦然面对吧。

En: Well yeah... what do you expect? She wasn't a f*g, of course she had babe-posters everywhere. You should see *my* fucking shack. Tits literally all over the place.
Zh: “是啊……你还在期待什么呢？她不是个姬佬，所以当然会把美女海报贴的到处都是。你真应该去看看*我的*棚窝，到处都是大胸妹海报。”

En: Really, nothing.
Zh: “真的，没什么。”

En: Klaasje said she made advances on her -- and she thwarted them. She also threatened her to *end* her relationship with the deceased.\n
Zh: “卡拉洁说露比向她示好了——但她拒绝了。她还威胁说要她*结束*跟死者的关系。“

En: Klaasje said she made advances on her -- and she thwarted them.
Zh: “卡拉洁说露比向她示好了——但她拒绝了。”

En: It's supra-natural -- a reality-breaking event. And we just need to accept it.
Zh: “这很正常——一个冲击现实的事件。我们只能接受它。”

En: This complex operation is probably something illegal.
Zh: 这种复杂的操作很可能是非法的。

En: That close, huh? How fortunate. I'll take a thorough look at those prints myself before I believe that, cop.
Zh: “这么近嘛？真是幸运。在我相信这套说辞之前，我会亲自彻底检查一下那些脚印的，条子。”

En: That's probably for the best. Don't break your stride now.
Zh: 这可能是最好的结果了，不要打乱你的步调。

En: Alright, she took a fucking leak, okay? For one moment. *Maybe* went out too. She has a complex operation to run from her lorry. He points to the intersection. "She's a busy girl -- always has been."
Zh: “好吧，他妈的她去尿尿了，行吗？就一小会儿。*可能*还出去过，她要从货車里完成一个复杂的操作。”他指着十字路口。“她可是个大忙人——一直都是。”

En: She's not a ghost, she's gay. You'd have to be an idiot not to... He looks around. "Guys, I'm not the only one who knew, right?"
Zh: “她不是鬼，她是个同性恋。除非你是白痴才不……”他四处看了一圈。“伙计们，不是只有我一个人知道，对吧？”

En: This is *some* sordid shit. The big man rubs his chin. "It's also the kind of garbage our Miss Oranje puts out to cover her own ass."
Zh: “这些都是肮脏的污蔑”，大个子摩挲着自己的下巴。“就跟我们的奥兰治小姐为了掩盖自己而编造的鬼话一样。”

En: Sort of -- but *he* doesn't have to know that!
Zh: 差不多——但*他*不需要知道这些！

En: Alright -- she took a fucking leak, okay? For one moment. *Maybe* went out too -- she has an operation to run from her lorry. He points to the intersection. "We're not getting into what that *operation* is again, cop."
Zh: “好吧——他妈的她去尿尿了，行吗？就一小会儿。*可能*还出去过——她要从货車里完成一个复杂的操作。”他指着十字路口。“我们不要再谈具体是什么*操作*了，条子。”

En: They were about a week or two old.
Zh: “大概有一两个星期了吧。”

En: Recent. (Nod.) "A week or two."
Zh: “最近的。”（点头）。“一两个星期吧。”

En: And that's okay. Some are queerer than others, you can still be a Hardie. He glances at Glen. "But if you bring your own personal shit into our outfit..."
Zh: “那也没什么。有些人比别人更娘点，但是他们仍然可以成为哈迪兄弟的一员。”他瞥了一眼格伦。“但如果你把自己的私事带进来……”

En: Look who's finally speaking! Ratty's been uncharacteristically quiet since you started theorizing. Maybe he has something to gain from implicating her?
Zh: 看看谁终于开口了！自从你开始推测以来，小老鼠一反常态地沉默着。也许把她牵扯进来会给他什么好处？

En: And just 'cause she was gone for five minutes doesn't mean she *magically* got to the roof and shot him. He taps on his temple. "I've been through this -- it's not plausible."
Zh: “就算她离开了五分钟，也不意味着她能*神奇地*爬上屋顶朝他开枪。”他轻敲着自己的太阳穴。“我也考虑过——那是不可能的。”

En: Weird... how? You're wearing the pinball maker's coat.
Zh: 诡异……怎么说？你正穿着弹球机工匠的外套。

En: I also found footprints upstairs in the old workshop.
Zh: “我还在楼上的旧工坊里发现了脚印。”

En: This one's going into some kind of insane death spiral to preserve his version of the world.
Zh: 这个人正陷入了某种疯狂的死亡螺旋中，以坚守自己对世界的看法。

En: Just 'cause she was gone for five minutes doesn't mean she *magically* got to the roof and shot the merc. He taps on his temple. "I've been through this -- it's not plausible."
Zh: “就算她离开了五分钟，也不意味着她能*神奇地*爬上屋顶朝雇佣兵开枪。”他轻敲着自己的太阳穴。“我也考虑过——那是不可能的。”

En: Guess what. Remember that *key* I found here? (Point at the window.)
Zh: “你猜怎么着。还记得我在这里找到的*钥匙*吗？”（指着窗户。）

En: Don't take me wrong, I love to stare at broads on posters, but we're talking about a *murder* here.
Zh: “别误会了，我喜欢欣赏海报上的姑娘们，但我们现在正在讨论*谋杀*。”

En: I don't know, Glenny. Ratface leans across the desk. "Ruby's a little secretive, isn't she? Wasn't like she told you every little thing..."
Zh: “我不知道，格伦尼。”老鼠脸斜靠在桌子旁。“露比有点神神秘秘的，是不是？又不是说她会把每件小事都告诉你……”

En: Let's not get into it, it's needless sensationalism.
Zh: “我们别再继续下去了，这是完全没必要的无端猜测。”

En: No-no, he squints at you. "Let's get *into* it -- what did she say?"
Zh: “不-不，”他眯着眼睛看你。“*继续*说下去吧——她说了什么？”

En: The big man falls absolutely silent.
Zh: 大个子男人陷入了绝对的沉默之中。

En: All right. We're in, we got Ruby unaccounted for some time during the window. This was crucial. Now let's place her on that roof.
Zh: 很好。我们成功了，露比在窗口期的一段时间内行踪不明。这很重要，现在就能证明她在屋顶上了。

En: Behind his squinting eyes, dominoes are falling -- fast. It's all beginning to make sense to him now.
Zh: 在他眯起的双眼后面，多米诺骨牌正在飞快地——倒塌。现在他渐渐明白过来了。

En: He's been through it. That means *he's* suspected her too.
Zh: 他也考虑过，这说明*他已经*怀疑过她了。

En: Yeah but... The fat man looks up from the table, oblivious. "They're both girls."
Zh: “但是……”胖子不自觉地从桌子上抬起头，“她们都是女孩子啊。”

En: She didn't fuck you 'cause you're *forty* and you still live with your mom, Dennis. Light laughter sounds off in the room. It feels nervous.
Zh: “她是因为你已经*四十*了还跟你妈一起住才不愿意跟你上床的，丹尼斯。”房间里传来轻微的笑声，略显局促。

En: I did! I knew it! That's why she didn't fuck me at Fatty's birthday party.
Zh: “是的！我就知道！所以她在胖子生日派对那天才不愿意跟我上床的。”

En: C'mon Glen, she likes Monica's titties more than *you* do. The Mesque smiles. "Everyone knows which way the wind blows there."
Zh: “拜托格伦，她比*你*更喜欢莫妮卡的咪咪。”梅斯克人笑着。“人人都知道这说明什么了。”

En: Knew what? The man can't believe what he's hearing. "First he says she murdered him -- now she's a *f*g* too? It's a lie!"
Zh: “知道什么？”那个男人不敢相信自己的耳朵。“他一开始说是她杀的人——现在她也变成*姬佬*了？一派胡言！”

En: Glen here obviously likes men and he can't hack it. That's his thing and you do *not* want to get into it. Moving on.
Zh: 这个格伦显然喜欢男人，但他应付不来。那是他自己的事，而你也*不想*介入。继续吧。

En: What do you mean *weird*, Theo?
Zh: “你说*诡异*是什么意思，特奥？”

En: Ruby then threatened her -- and told her to end her relationship with the deceased.
Zh: “然后露比威胁了她——并告诉她结束跟死者的关系。”

En: Figures. He looks out the window. "But that Ruby *is* queer as cabaret, now that I start thinking about it. So there is some truth to it..."
Zh: “可以想象。”他看着窗外。“仔细想想，那个露比*确实*就跟卡巴莱歌舞厅的姬佬似的。所以还是挺合理的……”

En: She did tell us when we were close to arresting her.
Zh: “当我们准备逮捕她的时候，她这么对我们说了。”

En: Look, fucko -- Glen steps forward. "Ruby and I are pretty tight. She would have showed me the gun if she had one. She knows I love guns."
Zh: “听着，混蛋——”格伦上前一步。“露比和我关系很好的。如果她有枪，就一定会给我看的。她知道我喜欢枪。”

En: *If* it's true. He looks at Shanky, then Titus. "But it's not -- right?"
Zh: “*如果*是真的。”他看了看尚克，然后是提图斯。“但那不是——对吗？”

En: Good one.
Zh: “说得好。”

En: Yeah and they don't put her on the roof either. It's just mambo jumbo. He hasn't got shit.
Zh: “没错，而且他们也不能证明她在屋顶。一切都只是唬人的，他什么都没有。”

En: Those numbers were an *ass-pull*, man.
Zh: “那些数字完全是*胡编乱造*的，老兄。”

En: No, you can't. I know what you did here on the weekend. People talk -- and what they say doesn't sound like a science-cop. You're a madman.
Zh: “不，你做不到。我很清楚你周末在这里干了什么。人们都说了——而他们口中的你听上去完全不是个科学警察，你就是个疯子。”

En: Titus's gaze meets yours: "Yeah, so...?"
Zh: 提图斯对上了你的视线：“是吗，所以呢……？”

En: Nothing. They where just there.
Zh: “没什么，他们就在那里。”

En: The people on the posters -- were all women. One looked exactly like Klaasje.
Zh: “海报上的人——都是女人。有一个长得跟卡拉洁一模一样。”

En: Your *maybe* isn't good enough, cop. You found some key -- did it have *Ruby killed him* written on it?
Zh: “你的*也许*还不够好，条子。你找到了一把钥匙——上面写着*是露比杀了他*吗？”

En: I analysed it on the spot. Turns out I can do that.
Zh: “我当场验证了一下，事实证明我可以做到。”

En: No time to waste on this.
Zh: 没时间浪费在这个上面了。

En: Yeah? Like what?
Zh: “是吗？比如呢？”

En: Sex. She wanted sex from her.
Zh: “上床。她想跟她上床。”

En: Apparently she had a *thing* for Klaasje.
Zh: “她显然对卡拉洁有*好感*。”

En: *Definitely* lie. The truth is not credible.
Zh: *肯定*要撒谎，讲事实没人信你。

En: Guys, this is Revachol. Anyone could get a gun around here. *Anyone*.
Zh: “伙计们，这里可是瑞瓦肖。任何人都能弄到枪，*任何人*。”

En: Was run by a fella called... He thinks. "Nyflox something, who wore a yellow dust coat -- like the one you have on *right now*." He squints at you. "Weird guy. Never liked him."
Zh: “经营者叫……”他思索着。“耐弗洛斯什么的，穿着一件黄色的风衣——就像你*现在*穿的这件。”他眯着眼睛看你。“古怪的家伙，从来没喜欢过他。”

En: A thing? You mean... He tilts his head to the side and falls silent.
Zh: “好感？你是说……”他把脑袋歪到一边，沉默了。

En: Seventy-two *percent*! He picks up his beer. "There's a percentage and all -- where'd you get it from, your guys in the lab?"
Zh: “*百分之*七十二！”他拿起自己的啤酒。“那个百分比——你从哪儿弄来的，实验室里的人？”

En: Girls like girls too, Angus, the lieutenant explains. "Sometimes. This is one of those times -- she liked Klaasje."
Zh: “女孩也会喜欢女孩的，安格斯。”警督解释着。“有些时候。现在就是这个时候——她喜欢卡拉洁。”

En: There's a 72% chance that the bullet came from the roof.
Zh: “有72%的概率子弹是从屋顶射来的。”

En: I don't like guessing, cop. *No one* does.
Zh: “我不喜欢猜测，条子。*没有人*喜欢。”

En: Wouldn't hurt to have one of those in Martinaise. He nods. "What else have you got -- that puts her on the roof?"
Zh: “给马丁内斯来一个也无妨。”他点点头：“你还有什么——能证明她当时在屋顶上？”

En: Have you noticed the winch out back? On the outer wall of the Whirling?
Zh: “你注意到后面的起吊机了吗？就在飞旋旅社的外墙上？”

En: He thinks for a moment, then nods: "Good news is -- I'm still listening."
Zh: 他想了一会儿后点点头：“好消息是——我还在听。”

En: He knows they *had* to be recent for those lines to still read. This wasn't a failure.
Zh: 他知道日期*一定*很近才能读出那些字迹，这并不是失败。

En: Put the numbers out there. This *will* impress them.
Zh: 把数字写出来，这*会*令他们大开眼界的。

En: Yes. We have a ballistics lab in Couron we consult in cases like these.
Zh: “是的，在科戎我们有一个弹道实验室，可以为这类案件提供咨询。”

En: Yes. Big lab. Back in Jamrock. The... Jamrock Ballistics Lab.
Zh: “是的，很大的实验室，就在加姆洛克。名字是……加姆洛克弹道实验室。”

En: Ruby could have gone up -- shot him -- come down -- all under seven minutes.
Zh: “露比完全可以上楼——朝他开枪——下楼——在七分钟之内完成所有。”

En: It *is* a very comfortable coat for such an oldie...
Zh: 对于那样一个老人来说，这*是*一件相当舒适的外套……

En: See boss, they're contradicting themselves. Now they're saying it *wasn't* the roof. They can't put her there, it's all bullshit.
Zh: “看见了吗老大，他们自相矛盾了。现在他们又说*不是*屋顶了。他们不能证明她在上面，全是胡扯。”

En: He's *thinking* all right. Glen gets silence for an answer -- push forward with this theory.
Zh: 他还在*思考*。格伦得到了一阵沉默作为回答——加强推进这一理论。

En: Wouldn't hurt to have one of those in Martinaise. He nods. "Still -- all the labs in the world don't put her on that roof. How did she get there? Climb?"
Zh: “给马丁内斯来一个也无妨。”他点点头：“不过——世界上所有实验室都无法证明她在那个屋顶上。她是怎么上去的？爬上去？”

En: And then, boss... he shakes his head "...use us to cover it up? I mean, that's just fucked up."
Zh: “然后，老大……”他摇摇头。“……利用我们来掩盖这件事？要我说，这真是太荒谬了。”

En: A little thing -- Ruby had these posters in her truck. Old movie stars.
Zh: “小事——露比的货車里贴着很多海报，全是老的电影明星。”

En: Before I arrested Klaasje she told me some pretty *interesting* things about Ruby.
Zh: “在我逮捕卡拉洁之前，她告诉了我一些关于露比的非常*有意思*的事情。”

En: Klaasje told me some pretty *interesting* things about Ruby.
Zh: “卡拉洁告诉了我一些关于露比的非常*有意思*的事情。”

En: Fine. Don't impress them with ballistics.
Zh: 好吧，别再跟他们秀弹道学了。

En: She is part of the *homo-sexual underground*.
Zh: “她是*地下同性恋组织*的成员。”

En: The *what* now?
Zh: “你说*什么*？

En: You're going to have to be way more blunt here.
Zh: 你得更直接点。

En: Better not push it, back to other ideas.
Zh: 最好不要逼得太过了，回到其它的想法上去。

En: Titus, you're not *really* saying...
Zh: 提图斯，不是*真的*在说……“

En: C'mon, guys. She hangs out with you meatheads. This cannot come as a surprise.
Zh: “拜托，伙计们。她跟你们这群傻瓜混在一起，这也没什么好惊讶的吧。”

En: So this is why Shanky doesn't like her. Useful for you -- it makes him advocate against Ruby.
Zh: 所以这就是为什么尚克不喜欢她的原因。这对你来说很有帮助——他不会多费口舌维护露比。

En: Yeah -- no. Ruby's got more balls than a ball pit. You'd have to be an idiot not to... He looks around. "Guys, I'm not the only one who knew, right?"
Zh: “是啊——不对，露比的胆子比谁都要大。除非你是白痴才不会……”他四处看了一圈。“伙计们，不是只有我一个人知道，对吧？”

En: She did tell us right before we arrested her.
Zh: “在我们逮捕她之前，她的确这么说了。”

En: T, we're not seriously considering it, are we?! He almost gets up from his seat. "Ruby wouldn't do this. Why would she do something like this?"
Zh: “提，我们不会当真了吧，对吗？！”他几乎从座位上站了起来。“露比不会这么做的，她为什么要这么做？”

En: You don't need to *prove* anything. Doubt is enough for now -- and Titus *must* have some.
Zh: 你无需*证明*什么，目前为止怀疑就足够了——而提图斯*必须*要有一些。

En: I know what you've been thinking, Glen. We'll talk about it -- once the murder charges have been cleared.
Zh: “我知道你在想什么，格伦。我们会谈到的——等到谋杀指控被消除之后。”

En: I know. It's bewildering for me too, but that's what she said.
Zh: “我知道。我也觉得很困惑，但她就是这么说的。”

En: It's not *bewildering*, it's, like -- whatever, man. He looks around. "Guys, I'm not the only one who knew right?"
Zh: “这并不*困惑*，就像——随便吧，老兄。”他四处看了一圈。“伙计们，不是只有我一个人知道，对吧？”

En: Why not? You can't draw a straight line into Klaasje's window from any of the surrounding buildings -- not from what I know about Martinaise...
Zh: “为什么不呢？你不能从随便哪个楼上画一条线直通卡拉洁的窗户——据我所知在马丁内斯就做不到……”

En: All right, cop. He nods. "Keep talking. I'll tell you when I've had enough."
Zh: “好吧，条子。”他点点头。“继续说吧，我听够了会告诉你的。”

En: *Maybe* from the coast? But like I said, I've been too busy dealing with you idiots. So no. He shakes his head. "I don't think it was a sniper. It was close up."\n
Zh: “*也许*是从海边来的？但就像我说的，我一直在忙着跟你们这些白痴打交道。所以不可能。”他摇摇头。“我不觉得那是个狙击手，他离我们很近。”

En: *Maybe* from the coast? But like I said, I've been too busy dealing with you, copper. So no. He shakes his head. "I don't think it was a sniper. It was close up."
Zh: “*也许*是从海边来的？但就像我说的，我一直在忙着应付你呢，条子。所以不可能。”他摇摇头。“我不觉得那是个狙击手，他离我们很近。”

En: *Maybe* from the coast? I've been too busy with this shit to see if it's possible. So no. He shakes his head. "I don't think it was a sniper. It was close up."
Zh: “*也许*是从海边来的？我太忙了，都没时间考虑这件事。所以不可能。”他摇摇头。“我不觉得那是个狙击手，他离我们很近。”

En: You *do* agree the shot came from the roof, right?
Zh: “你*确实*同意子弹是从屋顶射过来的，对吧？”

En: You got his attention. Now don't ruin it by making *weird* claims. Remember: you can still mess this up.
Zh: 你引起了他的注意，现在不要发表任何*怪异*的声明毁掉这一切。记住：你还是有可能搞砸的。

En: Would you prefer to go on trial with your men  -- as part of a lynch mob -- or alone, for cold blooded murder?
Zh: “你想跟你的兄弟们一起受审吗——作为私刑帮派的一员——或者因为冷酷的谋杀罪，单独受审？”

En: Alone. I don't drag my men into shit with me.
Zh: “我自己一个人。我不会把兄弟们拖下水的。”

En: But she didn't! She would *never* do that. The blond man looks around. "Why aren't more of you defending her? This is fucking stupid, Titus."
Zh: “但她没有！她*绝对*不会这么做的。”金发男子看了一圈。“为什么你们都不替她说话？这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。”

En: If she used us... then that's a serious violation of the *Hardie code*, boss.
Zh: “如果她利用了我们……那就严重违反了*哈迪守则*，老大。”

En: Yeah, but that's what's so cool about her! The blond exclaims. "It's why we've been thinking..."
Zh: “是啊，但那就是她酷炫的地方！”金发男子叫嚷着。“所以我们一直在想……”

En: Titus, let's assume you killed him...
Zh: “提图斯，假设是你杀了他……”

En: You're on thin ice here, asshole. He points at you.
Zh: “你现在如履薄冰了，混蛋。”他指着你。

En: It's a thought exercise, Titus, he explains. "Think. You kill him. Get up there. Shoot him. Get down..."
Zh: “就当是次思维实验，提图斯。”他解释到。“想想吧，是你杀了他。去了上面，朝他开枪，下楼来……”

En: All right. He nods. "Keep talking. I'm getting a bit *curious* about some things myself."
Zh: “好吧。”他点点头。“继续说，我也开始对某些事情*好奇*起来了。”

En: T, we're not seriously considering it, are we?! He almost gets up from his seat. "Ruby wouldn't do this. Why would she do something like this?"
Zh: “提，我们不会当真了吧，对吗？！”他几乎从座位上站了起来。“露比不会这么做的，她为什么要这么做？”

En: I didn't say I'd *prove* she had the murder weapon -- just that we need to find her. (Move on.)
Zh: “我并没有说我能*证明*她持有凶器——只是需要找到她本人而已。”（继续。）

En: Like fuck she was!
Zh: “说得他妈的跟真的一样。”

En: It was her idea, right? The hanging?
Zh: “那是她的主意吧，对吧？把他吊起来？”

En: It's clear this one is protective of her.
Zh: 很明显这个人在保护她。

En: It's possible. Someone from their own unit could have taken the shot -- from a spot on the coast, as I said. The roof's still the *sanest* choice for me.
Zh: “有可能，枪也许是他们组织内部的人开的——就像我说的，从海边某个地方。对我来说，屋顶仍然是最*明智*的选择。”

En: An *exceptional* sniper could have done it.
Zh: “一名*杰出*的狙击手完全可以做到。”

En: Unless it was an exceptional, almost godly sniper.
Zh: 除非是个杰出的，近乎于神的狙击手。

En: That's quite the theory... He turns to Eugene. "We need to have a look at that secret passage, boys."
Zh: “非常大胆的推理……”他转向尤金。“我们得去看看那条秘密通道了，兄弟们。”

En: Phase III: Motive. The last component.
Zh: 第三阶段：动机。最后的部分。

En: The big one. Get this and they'll give her to you. Remember, don't piss him off. That never works.
Zh: 最重要的一个。搞定这个，他们就会把她交给你了。切记不要惹他生气，那样是不会成功的。

En: Forty three is pretty large for a girl, don't you think? He rubs his chin. "But then Ruby's what you call a *butch-girl* -- she's no daffodil."
Zh: “43码对女孩子来说有点太大了，你不觉得吗？”他摩挲着下巴。“但露比就是人们口中的*女汉子*——她可不是什么菟丝花。”

En: See boss, he's contradicting himself. Now he's saying it *wasn't* the roof. They can't put her there, it's all bullshit.
Zh: “看见了吗老大，他们自相矛盾了。现在他们又说*不是*屋顶了。他们不能证明她在上面，全是胡扯。”

En: Ah yes, of course. Good catch -- go ahead:
Zh: 是的，当然。干得漂亮——继续吧：

En: Wait -- that would go against the theory I'm presenting here.
Zh: 等等——那就会跟我现在的推理相悖了。

En: Yes, about size 43.
Zh: “是的，大概是43码。”

En: Recent. (Nod.) "Size about 43."
Zh: “最近的。”（点头）。“大概是43码。”

En: It's not 'why did she kill him?' -- it's 'why did she organize the coverup?'.
Zh: “不是‘她为什么杀了他？’——而是‘她为什么要安排掩盖死因？’”

En: And I suppose you have a theory about that too, cop?
Zh: “我猜你已经有一套理论了，条子？”

En: She could have just been covering up for *herself*, Titus.
Zh: “她很可能只是为了*掩饰*自己，提图斯。”

En: Ruby could have gotten to the roof from somewhere *inside* the Whirling.
Zh: “露比可能是从飞旋旅社*内部*的某个地方直达屋顶的。”

En: Think about it -- why go through all that effort? It was her idea, wasn't it? The hanging? You went along, but she suggested it.
Zh: “想想吧——为什么要费那么大的力气呢？这都是她的主意，不是吗？把他吊起来？你虽然配合了，但是她的提议。”

En: The little man squints, eyes beady. "She had, like, a fully formed plan and shit. Right when she came back downstairs..."
Zh: 小个子眯着眼睛，眼睛里闪着光。“她下楼的时候，就好像，已经有了一套完整计划似的……”

En: Really, Shanks? Klaasje wanted to talk to another girl, that's all. She was just the first one up there; *I* could've come up with that plan, if I'd been first.
Zh: “真的吗，尚克？卡拉洁只想跟另一个女孩谈谈，仅此而已。她不过是最先赶到的人；如果我最先到场，*我*也能想出那套计划。”

En: Time for a logics demonstration.
Zh: 是时候进行逻辑演示了。

En: Would you prefer to go on trial with your buddies? As part of a lynch mob? Or alone, for committing murder?
Zh: “你想跟你的朋友一起受审吗？作为私刑帮派的一员？或者因为谋杀罪，单独受审？”

En: Fuck you, man, I would never fuck my guys over like that. He squeaks with indignation.
Zh: “去你的吧，老兄，我永远不会这么坑自己兄弟的。”他气得哇哇直叫。

En: Glen. Titus looks grim. "I thought the same thing when she skipped town and left us in this shit."
Zh: “格伦。”提图斯看上去有些冷酷。“当她离开镇子，把我们留在这摊浑水里的时候我也是这么想的。”

En: She didn't either! She would never do that. The blond man looks around. "Why aren't more of you defending her? This is fucking stupid, Titus."
Zh: “她也没有啊！她绝对不会这么做的。”金发男子看了一圈。“为什么你们都不替她说话？这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。”

En: No -- but that doesn't prove anything.
Zh: “没有——但这证明不了什么。”

En: Easy now, Glen. He turns to you. "What he's is trying to say is -- people who don't have guns don't shoot people. You need a gun for that. And you can't prove she has one."
Zh: “冷静点，格伦。”他转向你。“他想说的是——没有枪的人是不能朝人开枪的，你得有把枪才能这么做。而你没办法证明她有枪。”

En: Shanky, let's assume you killed him...
Zh: “尚克，假设是你杀了他……”

En: I didn't do it, fucker! It wasn't *my* plan.
Zh: “不是我干的，混蛋！那不是*我的*计划。”

En: You probably *did* though...
Zh: “虽然很有可能*就是*你……”

En: Ooh! So he didn't rule her out completely? *And* she's skipped town. This is good.
Zh: 哦！所以他没有完全排除她的可能性？*而且*她还离开这里了。很好。

En: On this mere cameo. I get it. Moving on.
Zh: 只是客串一下，我明白了。继续。

En: Been thinking of getting *Whirling-in-Rags* tatted on my ass, boss.
Zh: “一直想把*褴褛飞旋*纹到我屁股上，老大。”

En: I don't know, cop -- why don't you find your *lost gun* first? The tattooed man bursts out laughing at his own joke.
Zh: “我不知道，条子——你为什么不先去找你那把*失枪*呢？”纹身男因为自己的笑话爆笑起来。

En: Easy now, Al. This isn't comedy hour.
Zh: “冷静点，兰。现在还不是喜剧时间。”

En: Titus, we're not seriously considering it, are we?! He almost gets up from his seat. "Ruby wouldn't do this... why would she do something like this?"
Zh: “提图斯，我们不会当真了吧，对吗？！”他几乎从座位上站了起来。“露比不会这么做的……她为什么要这么做？”

En: We've only been drinking here for, what? *Six years*? He looks around. "How much *do* we know?"
Zh: “我们不过是在这里喝了多久？*六年*的酒？”他四下看了看。“我们*能*了解什么呢？”

En: That looks antique -- a Belle-Magrave... He takes the gun, inspects it and hands it back to you. "It's inoperable. Where'd you get it?"
Zh: “那看上去像是个古董——一把美人侯爵……”他拿起枪检查了一下，然后还给你。“这枪没办法用，你从哪儿弄来的？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: It's just a thought experiment. Think, Shanky, you killed him. You got up there. Shot him. Got down...
Zh: “这只是次思维实验。想想吧，尚克，是你杀了他。去了上面，朝他开枪，下楼来……”

En: Maybe there's more.
Zh: “也许哪里还有更多。”

En: How much do you guys know about this place?
Zh: “你们对这个地方有多少了解？”

En: Nah. The only way up there is through Klaasje's room.
Zh: “得了，唯一能上去的路就是穿过卡拉洁的房间。”

En: It's just two doors. We're not giving you *Ruby* for that -- she's one of *us*.
Zh: “只是两扇门而已。我们不会因此就交出*露比*的——她是*我们*的人。”

En: There's a locked door in the kitchen -- and another on the roof. Ever been through those?
Zh: “厨房里有一扇锁着的门——屋顶上还有一扇。有进去过吗？”

En: He thinks for a moment, then nods and says : "Good news is -- I'm still listening."
Zh: 他想了一会儿后点点头：“好消息是——我还在听。”

En: *Horizontal*? I see they taught you well in your RCM school. My old man -- a hunter and a half blind one at that -- could've said that. Horizontal lines...
Zh: “*水平的*？我能看出你在RCM被教导得很好。我老爸——一个半瞎的猎人——也会这么说。水平线……”

En: Goes against your little theory, doesn't it? He rubs his chin. "Then again I've known few people in my life who own more than one pair of boots -- and occasionally do change them..."
Zh: “这跟你的小想法相悖了，不是吗？”他摩挲着下巴。“另外我这辈子也见过几个人拥有不止一双靴子——时不时也会换一下……”

En: Twenty, maybe thirty rifles, Titus. Also broken -- but still, there were too many. And there must be other caches too.
Zh: “二十，也许是三十支步枪，提图斯。虽说坏了——但数量还是太多了。而且一定还会有别的驻点。”

En: No...
There were prints there -- with horizontal lines.
Zh: “那里有带着水平线的——脚印。”

En: If she used us to cover her own shit up... well, that's a serious violation of the *Hardie code*, boss.
Zh: “如果她利用了我们给自己打掩护……这可是严重违反了*哈迪守则*的，老大。”

En: The man is silent.
Zh: 男人沉默着。

En: Which is why she didn't! She would never do that. The blond man waves his beer around. "Why aren't more of you defending her? This is fucking stupid, Titus."
Zh: “所以她没有！她永远不会那么做的。”金发男子摇晃着啤酒。“为什么你们都不替她说话？这太他妈的蠢了，提图斯。”

En: Eugene, let's assume you killed him...
Zh: “尤金，假设是你杀了他……”

En: He scoffs.
Zh: 他嘲讽地笑了。

En: No...
Well, they didn't fit with any of yours on the scene -- Ruby included.
Zh: “可是，它们和在场的任何人都不匹配——包括露比的。”

En: Recent. (Nod.) "But they didn't fit with any of yours on the scene -- Ruby included."
Zh: “最近的。”（点头）。“但它们和在场的任何人都不匹配——包括露比的。”

En: The lieutenant is glad he got the floor plan right there.
Zh: 警督很高兴自己拿到了平面图。

En: There's *another* door in the kitchen. He points West. "A locked *blue* door in the wall. I saw it this morning when I was waiting for you," he explains. "Ever been through that?"
Zh: “厨房里*还有*一扇门。”他指着西边。“墙上有一扇蓝色的*锁着*的门。我今天早上等你的时候看到的。”他解释着。“你进去过吗？”

En: There's a locked door on the roof. Leading *to* the roof. Ever seen that?
Zh: “屋顶上有一扇上锁的门，*通往*屋顶的。有见过吗？”

En: Think. You kill him. Get up there. Shoot him. Get down...
Zh: “想想吧，是你杀了他。去了上面，朝他开枪，下楼来……”

En: Would you prefer to go on trial with your friends -- as part of a lynch mob -- or alone, for committing murder?
Zh: “你想跟你的朋友一起受审吗——作为私刑帮派的一员——或者因为谋杀罪，单独受审？”

En: It was a joke.
Zh: “那是句玩笑。”

En: I've seen the winch, I'm not blind. He nods. "You saying it's part of the elevator she used to get up there?"
Zh: “我见过那台起吊机，我又不是瞎子。”他点点头。“你是说那是她上楼时搭乘的电梯的一部分？”

En: Sure. He scoffs. "Take your sorry ass out of here -- Ruby didn't use your lost gun to kill him."
Zh: “当然。”他嘲笑着。“快滚吧你这个可悲的家伙——露比没有用你弄丢的枪杀人。”

En: He looks tough but there is a nervous twitch in his dark green eye. This is all too close to home for him.
Zh: 他看上去很强硬，但在他深绿色的眼睛中有一丝紧张。这触及他的痛处了。

En: Boss... The big guy in the corner speaks up with a wheeze. "There's another on the roof next to her window. Me and Tibbs saw it when we... were replacing..."
Zh: “老大……”角落里的大块头气喘吁吁地说。“在她窗户旁边的屋顶上还有一扇门。我和提布斯修窗户的时候……看到了……”

En: You LOST your gun?! The blond man is taken aback. Not even laughing.
Zh: “你把枪弄丢了？！”金发男子吓了一跳，甚至没有一丝笑意。

En: The big man leans closer. "Tell me -- did you lose it *before* the murder?"
Zh: 大个子凑得更近了。“跟我说说——你是在谋杀*之前*弄丢的吗？”

En: What an idiot!
Zh: “真是个白痴！”

En: No.
Footprints. He takes a sip of his beer. "Recent?"
Zh: “脚印。”他抿了一口啤酒。“是最近的吗？”

En: You *didn't*. And Ruby didn't use your lost gun to kill him.
Zh: “你*没有*。而且露比也没有用你弄丢的枪杀人。”

En: Of course not. He scoffs. "And Ruby didn't use your lost gun to kill him."
Zh: “当然不是。”他嘲笑着。而且露比也没有用你弄丢的枪杀人。”

En: There's *another*, right on the roof, next to Klaasje's window. How about that? You been through that door?
Zh: “*还有*一扇门，就在屋顶上，卡拉洁的窗户旁边。怎么样？你进过那扇门吗？”

En: The tracks were recent -- but *not* worn down in the right foot like Ruby's. It's best to *omit* this pesky little polemic for now. Too *confusing*.
Zh: 足迹是最近的——但*没有*像露比的右脚那样有磨损的痕迹。现在最好*忽略*这个讨厌的小矛盾，过于*令人困惑*了。

En: Fuck, boss... The tattooed man realizes something. "This is why he was always asking for the other guy's gun. He doesn't *have* his own."
Zh: “该死，老大……”纹身男意识到了什么。“所以他才一直在要别人的枪，因为他*没有*自己的枪。”

En: If they're connected, she could've gone to the kitchen -- gone up -- shot him on the roof -- then come down. All in five minutes. How about that?
Zh: “如果它们是相通的，她就可以去厨房——上楼——在屋顶上朝他开枪——然后下来。全程5分钟搞定，你觉得如何？”

En: I'm on it, boss. Right when the law clears, me and Angus are going up there.
Zh: “我来吧，老大。条子和你说清楚了的话，我和安格斯就上那儿去。”

En: The local pawnshop sold my lost gun to a woman. Maybe it was her?
Zh: “本地当铺把我的失枪卖给了一个女人，也许就是她？”

En: The lieutenant leans closer to Eugene. "What if they're connected by the *winch*, Eugene? That is your, name, right -- Eugene?"
Zh: 警督凑近了尤金。“如果它们之间是用*起吊机*连接的呢，尤金？那是你的名字对吗——尤金？”

En: What if those two doors are connected, Eugene? Is that your name -- Eugene? The lieutenant leans closer.
Zh: “如果那两扇门是相通的呢，尤金？那是你的名字吗——尤金？”警督靠得更近了。

En: Right there, underneath the calm and the cool, a nervousness. He's thinking: Ruby could have fucked us all over. How did I not see it?
Zh: 就在那里，隐藏在平静与淡定之下的，是一丝紧张。他在想：露比很可能把我们都骗了，我怎么能没看出来呢？

En: There you go -- tatted on his ass. We know the place.
Zh: “这下你懂了——纹在他的屁股上。我们知道那地方。”

En: And even then -- a route doesn't put that bullet in the merc's head. A gun does that. And Ruby doesn't carry one.
Zh: “即便如此——一条通道也不能把子弹射进雇佣兵的脑袋，只有枪才行。然而露比并没有枪。”

En: Is it? It *proves* there are things about the Whirling-in-Rags that he doesn't know. He *must* see that.
Zh: 是吗？这*证明*了褴褛飞旋的某些事情他并不知情，他*必须*发现这一点。

En: Uhm....
Firmly? He shakes his head. "Try *shit on a stick*. All you've established is a *possible* route to the roof -- that you haven't found."
Zh: “证实？”他摇摇头。“不如说是*毫无根据*吧。你们能证实的只有一条*可能*通往屋顶的路线——甚至还没有找到。”

En: There's a door downstairs in the kitchen -- a locked *blue* door. Ever been through that?
Zh: “楼下厨房里有扇门——一扇上锁的*蓝色*大门。你进去过吗？”

En: Yeah, so what -- she's been here -- there's some key here -- so she killed him? It's a whack in the dark and you goddamn know it.
Zh: “是嘛，所以呢——她去过那里——这里有把钥匙——所以就是她杀的人？你很清楚这完全就是抓瞎！”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No you didn't. And Ruby didn't use your lost gun to kill him.
Zh: “你没有。而且露比也没有用你弄丢的枪杀人。”

En: Precisely.
Zh: “没错。”

En: Then why the fuck was he asking you for *your* gun, huh? When he tried shooting himself or some shit... Because he doesn't *have* his own!
Zh: “那他想开枪自杀的时候……为什么要问你要*你*的枪呢，嗯？因为他*没有*自己的枪！”

En: This was merely a thought exercise. He did not lose his gun. Let's move on.
Zh: “这不过是一次思维实验。他没有丢枪，我们继续吧。”

En: This man is smarter than he looks. We can work with this.
Zh: 这个人比他看上去要聪明，我们可以利用这一点。

En: That's a solid logic structure. Do something. This is bad.
Zh: 这是个很严密的逻辑结构。做点什么，情况不妙。

En: *Workshop Spare* actually. It was in *your* box. Ruby's been here, right?
Zh: “其实是*工坊备用*。它在*你的*包厢里。露比来过这里，对吧？”

En: It was in *your* box. I take it Ruby has been in here?
Zh: “它在*你的*包厢里，我想是因为露比来过这里吧？”

En: Hmmm... This one does not *like* Ruby. May prove useful.
Zh: 嗯……这个人不*喜欢*露比，可能会有用。

En: There were pinball machines in the workshop. Still operational.
Zh: “工坊里面有弹球机，还能用呢。”

En: (Hold out the bullet) "This is the bullet that did it -- 4.46 mm. The Belle-Magrave uses the same calibre."
Zh: （提起子弹）“就是这颗子弹——4.46毫米，跟美人侯爵是同一口径。”

En: The blond man looks at the mushroomed deathbringer in the evidence bag and says:
Zh: 金发男子看着证物袋中那个碎裂开花状的灵魂收割者说：

En: Yeah, the bitch is jacketed alright. 4 mm too...
Zh: “是啊，那玩意是被甲的。也是4毫米……”

En: Whoaa....
Well, god damn... His eyes follow the evidence bag back in your pocket. "It's not proof, but it's a possible murder weapon -- close to her. Too damn close."
Zh: “真是该死……”他的视线随着证物袋落进你的口袋。“这算不上证据，但可能是一件凶器——很接近她了，太他妈的近了。”

En: Of course.
Zh: 当然。

En: He squints. "You *have* been thorough. I'll give you that."
Zh: 他眯着眼睛。“我不得不说，你*做得*很彻底。”

En: Okay, I see your point -- there are guns lying around. He shakes his head. "Dammit, I thought we'd found all the old spots. Why was that still there?"
Zh: “好了，我明白你的意思了——到处都是枪。”他摇摇头。“该死，我还以为我们已经找到所有老地方了呢。为什么它还在那儿？”

En: Right -- what the fuck does that got to do with Ruby?
Zh: “好吧——那他妈跟露比有什么关系？”

En: We just missed one. Ruby doesn't know this place boss -- just these cops, diggin' up shit.
Zh: “我们漏了一个。露比不知道这个地方，老大——只有那些条子们，挖来挖去的。”

En: It's just two doors, he cuts in. "We're not giving him *Ruby* for that -- she's one of *us*."
Zh: “只是两扇门而已，”他插了一嘴。“我们不会因此就把*露比*交给他的——她是*我们*的人。”

En: He nods to it. "It's a god damn breechloader too -- find one that works and you got a military-grade weapon -- that shoots jacketed ammunition." He shakes his head.
Zh: 他点头同意。“还是个该死的后膛枪——找到一把能用的，你就拥有了军用级别的武器——它可以发射被甲子弹。”他摇摇头。

En: Nothing. I just thought we could bring some down. Get some new machines in here.
Zh: “没什么。我就是觉得我们可以搬几台下来，给这里弄些新机子。”

En: He sees it -- this is coming together -- he *must*.
Zh: 他意识到了——所有线索串在一起了——他*必须*。

En: It doesn't fit *well*, but it fits. And he knows it.
Zh: 虽然不*完美*，但也说得过去。而他很清楚这一点。

En: Time to really close the deal. Show them the bullet.
Zh: 是时候了结这一切了，给他们看看子弹。

En: Wow, man, can we? I mean...
Zh: “哇哦，老兄，我们能不能？我是说……”

En: And then put it *back?* Why? He points at the key. "It's damn interesting you found it there -- but it doesn't fit with Ruby. And you know it."
Zh: “然后再把它放*回去*？为什么？”他指着钥匙。“你在这里找到它真是太他妈的有趣了——但它跟露比没关系，你很清楚这一点。”

En: Tell you one thing: I liked how that goddamn *winch* came up again. That was nifty.
Zh: “告诉你一件事吧：我很高兴那台该死的*起吊机*又被旧事重提了，非常棒。”

En: That'd be awesome, I've beat that Royalist machine twenty times now.
Zh: “那真是太棒了，我已经打败保皇党的机器二十次了。”

En: Alright alright, we can get the barman to bring them down -- what else?
Zh: “好吧好吧，我们可以让酒保把它们搬下来——还有什么？”

En: Nah, man -- she'd totally wave her gun in *your* face if she were here right now. And you'd fucking PISS yourself.
Zh: “得了，老兄——如果她现在在这儿的话，绝对已经朝着*你*挥枪了。而你也绝对会吓尿裤子的。”

En: *Connected* -- with the *winch*, Eugene! She could've gone up -- shot him -- come down -- in well under five minutes.
Zh: “*相通的*——用那台*起吊机*，尤金！她就可以上楼去——朝他开枪——然后下来。全程5分钟搞定。”

En: What if the doors are *connected?* She could've gone up -- shot him -- come down -- in well under five minutes.
Zh: “如果门是*相通*的呢？她就可以上楼去——朝他开枪——然后下来。全程5分钟搞定。”

En: Yes, she *could* have. This is one *looooong* shot. He shakes his head, but the look in his eyes shows interest.
Zh: “是啊，她*可以*的，全凭*运气*而已。”他摇摇头，但眼神中透露着兴趣。

En: She could have made a copy.
Zh: “她可以做个复制品。”

En: There is a small -- 28% -- chance that shot came from *beyond* the roof.
Zh: “有很小的——28%的——几率那一枪是从屋顶*更远处*射来的。”

En: Yes, god damn it, I know what a 72% chance means. It means there's a 28% chance it *isn't* that. He thinks for a moment. "And 28% is no small chance either."
Zh: “是啊，该死的，我很清楚72%的几率意味着什么。这意味着还有28%的几率*不是*那样的。”他思考了一会儿。“但28%的几率也不小了。”

En: Just making sure we're on the same page. The lieutenant pretends to take a note. "It doesn't all fit. We're just sharing info -- candidly."
Zh: “只是想确认一下我们的想法是否一样。”警督假装记着笔记。“并不是完全一致。我们只能开诚布公地——分享情报。”

En: It doesn't *all* have to fit. The chance is still way smaller than it coming from the roof. And he knows it.
Zh: 并不需要*所有*事完全说通。几率仍然比子弹来自屋顶的可能性要小得多，而他也很清楚这一点。

En: It opens the kitchen door that leads to the roof. You didn't find it, but Ruby did. That's how she got up there.
Zh: “它能打开通向屋顶的厨房门。你没找到，但露比发现了。她就是这么上去的。”

En: It's just two doors, he agrees "We're not giving you *Ruby* for that -- she's practically a Hardie."
Zh: “只是两扇门而已，”他同意到。“我们不会因此就把*露比*交给你的——她基本上也算是个哈迪兄弟了。”

En: Now what the *hell* are we supposed to do with that?
Zh: “那我们*他妈的*应该那它怎么办？”

En: He's been fuckin' *eating* in the kitchen! The rat-faced man explodes. "The cop and the lardo don't have shit."
Zh: “妈的他一直在厨房里*吃东西*！”老鼠脸男人气炸了。“那个条子和肥猪什么都没有。”

En: He's trying to woo us with cop science. Forensics shit. I've heard this mambo jambo.
Zh: “他是想用条子的专业术语唬住我们，法医什么的。我听说过这类玩意。”

En: Yeah, but the mambo jambo he's saying goes *against* his theory A.
Zh: “没错，但他说的那些玩意跟他第一条理论完全*相悖*啊。”

En: Boss... The big guy in the corner speaks up with a wheeze. "There's another in the kitchen. A blue door. I've been there..."
Zh: “老大……”角落里的大个子气喘吁吁地说。“厨房里还有一扇，一扇蓝色的门。我看到过……”

En: It does?
Zh: “有吗？”

En: Yes. He pinches the root of his nose.
Zh: “是的。”他掐了一下鼻根。

En: Titus doesn't reply -- probably a good sign.
Zh: 提图斯没有回答——可能是个好兆头。

En: The ribbon was old. Faded. It'd been there for twenty years at least.
Zh: “丝带很旧，已经褪色了。它在那儿至少有二十年了。”

En: Goddamn it, cop -- are you kidding me? You know it was no goddamn Pinball Murderer!
Zh: “该死的，条子——你在开玩笑吗？你知道根本就没有什么该死的弹球机杀手！”

En: Next he's gonna kill himself -- again. The tattooed man looks at you with disdain.
Zh: “接下来他又要——自杀了。”纹身男用轻蔑的目光望着你。

En: Quit jumping to conclusions. Theo, he turns to the old man, "you took that key. Did it look like it was *recently* put there?"
Zh: “不要急于下结论，特奥。”他转向老人。“你拿了钥匙。它看上去像是*最近*被放在那里的吗？”

En: What if they're somehow connected to the murder -- a *Pinball Murderer*?
Zh: “如果它们跟谋杀案有关呢——一名*弹球机杀手*？”

En: He lost his partner, the binoclard. Cops can't function without their butt-buddies.
Zh: “他的搭档不见了，那个四眼仔。条子们没有他们的死党就不能正常工作了。”

En: You didn't find it, but Ruby did. That's how she got up there.
Zh: “你没找到，但露比发现了。她就是这么上去的。”

En: It opens the steel door in the kitchen. The one that leads upstairs, to the roof. This key... he looks at the hawthorn bush outside the window, "was right here -- with you -- all the time."
Zh: “它能打开厨房里的那扇铁门。从那里能通往楼上，直达屋顶。这把钥匙……”他望着窗外的山楂树丛。“就在这里——跟你在一起——始终如此。”

En: (Show them the antique rifle.) "There are weapons like this just lying around in Martinaise."
Zh: （向他们展示老式步枪）“在马丁内斯，这种武器藏得到处都是。”

En: Firmly? He shakes his head. "Firmly doesn't go well with *could've*. There's a route to the roof. Me and the boys need to check it out. That's what we've *established*."
Zh: “证实？”他摇摇头。“*可能*跟证实不是一回事。有条通往屋顶的通道。我和兄弟们需要去看看。那才是我们能*证实*的事情。”

En: So you like waving your guns in people's faces? Doesn't mean she does too.
Zh: “所以你喜欢在人们面前挥枪吗？这也不意味着她也会这样。”

En: There's a cellar under the bookshop. It was hidden there -- with others just like it.
Zh: “书店下面有个地窖，它就藏在那里——那里还有很多这种枪。”

En: Also, it has to be said -- this man would make a good RCM sergeant. Or maybe even lieutenant. Just don't let him anywhere near women.
Zh: 另外，不得不说——这个男人可以成为一名优秀的RCM警长，甚至是警督。只是不能让他靠近女人。

En: Goddamnit, we need to close that dump down for good.
Zh: “该死，我们得永久关闭那个垃圾场才行。”

En: Still. The key here is a strange coincidence -- and he knows it.
Zh: 然而，这把钥匙是个奇怪的巧合——而他很清楚这一点。

En: Just don't contradict yourself. If it doesn't sound like 'RUBY DID IT', maybe keep it to yourself?
Zh: 切记不要自相矛盾。如果它听起来没有*是露比干的*意思，还是不要说出来为好？

En: Don't say *funny* things. He looks calm, but these are serious accusations against -- against one of their own.
Zh: 别再发表*滑稽*的言论了。他看起来很平静，但这些毕竟是针对性的严重指控——针对的还是他们中的一人。

En: Also inoperable?
Zh: “也不能用吗？”

En: Yes. But there were *so* many. Twenty, maybe thirty rifles.
Zh: “是的。但是数量*太*多了。二十支，或许有三十支步枪。”

En: Not maybe. You *were*. He points at the lieutenant. "Both of you. I've done it myself, gone and piled every thread I find onto one theory. Doesn't work like that."
Zh: “不是可能，你*就是*。”他指着警督。“你们俩都是。我自己也想过，把找到的每一条线索堆积成一个理论。那样是行不通的。”

En: Or a *ghost*. The lieutenant adds. "It's never a ghost either -- don't worry, we've got better than that." He looks at you.
Zh: “或者是个*鬼魂*。”警督补充道。“从来都不是鬼魂——别担心，我们有比鬼更好的。”他看着你。

En: That still leaves a 72% chance it *did*.
Zh: “但仍然有72%的几率确实*如此*。”

En: He's been fuckin' *eating* in the kitchen! The rat-faced man explodes. "The cop and the lardo don't have shit."
Zh: “妈的他一直在厨房里*吃东西*！”老鼠脸男人气炸了。“那个条子和肥猪什么都没有。”

En: Boy, you really haven't given me a lot to work with here. You should explore better -- for now, just wing it.
Zh: 伙计，你真是没给我留下多少发挥余地。你应该探索得更深入些——现在，先即兴发挥吧。

En: Wait, did he say you don't have *big guns*?
Zh: 等等，他是说你不是什么*大人物*吗？

En: So...? The man shrugs and looks at you.
Zh: “所以呢……？”男人耸耸肩看着你。

En: So it had to come from a breech-loading rifle. Military-grade. He turns to you. "Not even you militia-monkeys have those."
Zh: “所以它肯定是来自一支后膛装步枪，军用级别的。”他转向你。“连你们这群民兵猴子都没有。”

En: I have analysed the bullet that killed him -- it was jacketed.
Zh: “我分析了杀死他的那颗子弹——它有包壳。”

En: Why? He shakes his head. "This doesn't make sense. It's damn *interesting*, but it doesn't fit well and you know it."
Zh: “为什么？”他摇摇头。“没道理啊。这他妈的很*有趣*，但根本就说不通。你自己也知道的。”

En: He doesn't wait for a response. "This goes against your everyone-has-a-gun theory. If it was a military-grade, how'd *Ruby* get it?"
Zh: 他没有等你回答。“这跟你人人持枪的理论相悖了。如果这是军用级别的武器，*露比*又是怎么拿到的呢？”

En: I'm just being up front with what I know.
Zh: “我只是把我所知道的如实说出来罢了。”

En: Right. Titus nods. "And that's mighty forthcoming of you, so let me extend you the same courtesy: she's connected. To you-know-what."
Zh: “好吧。”提图斯点点头。“你可真是慷慨，让我也回馈一下同样的敬意吧：她有些门路，你懂的。”

En: That's right.
Zh: “没错。”

En: He doesn't wait for a response. "This goes against your short-range theory. If the murder weapon was military-grade, how did *Ruby* get it?"
Zh: 他没有等你回答。“这跟你的近距射杀推理相悖了。如果凶器是军用级别的，*露比*又是怎么拿到的呢？”

En: Yeah, maybe I *was* jumping to conclusions.
Zh: “是啊，也许*是*我太急于下定论了。”

En: She could've just put it back there once she was done.
Zh: “她本可以用完就把它放回去的。”

En: Not maybe. You *were*. He points to himself. "I've done it myself -- gone and piled every thread I find onto one theory. Doesn't work like that."
Zh: “不是可能，你*就是*。”他指着自己。“我自己也想过——把找到的每一条线索堆积成一个理论。那样是行不通的。”

En: It's never a Sam Bo fighter and it's never a bullet-bending sniper.
Zh: “绝对不可能是萨拳战士，也不是能扭曲子弹的狙击手。”

En: Just a thought experiment. Let's go on. He nods for you to continue.
Zh: “不过是一次思维实验，我们继续吧。”他点头示意你。

En: You're right. I'm never solving this case, it's too *complicated*.
Zh: “你是对的，我永远都没法解决这个案子了，它太*复杂*了。”

En: Garbage. He crosses his arms. "It wasn't a Sam Bo artist, cop. I've been doing this for ten years -- let me give you a lesson. Boys?"
Zh: “屁话。”他抱起双臂。“那可不是什么萨拳武术，条子。我已经习武十年了——让我好好给你上一课吧。兄弟们？”

En: What are you gonna cry now? He squints at you, as if inspecting how close to crying you are.
Zh: “你现在是要哭了吗？”他眯起眼睛看着你，仿佛在检查你什么时候才能哭出来。

En: A skilled *Sam Bo artist* could have climbed the outer wall, like a spider...
Zh: “一位技艺精湛的*萨拳武术家*可以像蜘蛛一样，爬上外墙……”

En: No, he's not. He looks at you, as if making sure -- then at Titus again. "We're just under stress here. This case needs to be solved and fast, or bad things will happen."
Zh: “不，他没有。”他好像是在确认什么的看着你——而后又看向提图斯。“只是我们现在压力很大。这个案子需要尽快解决，否则会有不好的事情发生。”

En: I will boss. The man reaches in his overalls for a cigarette.
Zh: “我会的，老大。”那人从工作服中掏出了香烟。

En: Which is why we need to talk to her as soon as possible. There *is* grounds for a questioning here -- everyone sees that.
Zh: “因此我们需要尽快和她谈谈才行，*这是*非常合理的质询——明眼人都看得出来。”

En: Angie -- check out what's behind those doors when you get the time. It's probably nothing, but we need to know.
Zh: “安吉——等你有空的时候去看看那些门背后是什么。可能什么都没有，但我们得确认一下。”

En: Organized crime -- down in Jamrock probably. He's not being *too* forthcoming -- this is not a surprise.
Zh: 有组织的犯罪——很可能在加姆洛克。他不是*非常*乐于助人——这并不令人意外。

En: The two are not mutually exclusive. Get that through your fucking head. The man only slightly raises his voice.
Zh: “这两者并不互斥，让你的脑袋清醒一点。”那人只是稍微提高了嗓门。

En: So she *may* have access to semiautomatics. But that's a long fucking stretch of the imagination.
Zh: “所以她*有可能*会接触到半自动武器，但这需要相当丰富的想象力才行。”

En: What are you, a fucking cop now, Angie? You want to be a Hardie -- or you want to be a cop?
Zh: “怎么，你现在是个该死的条子了吗，安吉？你是想做哈迪兄弟——还是想当个条子？”
