En: There's another on the roof next to her window. Me and Tibbs saw it when we... were replacing...
Zh: “在她窗户旁边的屋顶上还有一扇门。我和提布斯修窗户的时候……看到了……”

En: No, just say you're honest with him, don't *challenge* him.
Zh: 不，就说你对他很诚实，不要*挑衅*他。

En: You don't know. Just be honest.
Zh: 你不知道，诚实就好。

En: Boss... The big guy in the corner speaks up with a wheeze. "There's this strange blue door in the kitchen. I've been there..."
Zh: “老大……”角落里的大个子气喘吁吁地说。“厨房里有一扇怪怪的蓝色铁门。我看见过……”

En: It's an old building. There may be a secret passage -- or even a network of passages.
Zh: “这是座很老的建筑了，可能会藏着一条秘密通道——甚至是一张通道网。”

En: Just because it's rare doesn't mean you can't get it.
Zh: “只是因为它很罕见并不意味着你无法得到它。”

En: Cop -- that's *exactly* what it means.
Zh: “条子——这*正是*它的真正含义。”

En: That wasn't strong.
Zh: 那并不强烈。

En: I *told* you not to say that. This is not a joking matter for him.
Zh: 我*告诉过*你不要这么说，这对他来说不是能开玩笑的事。

En: I don't. All I see is a cop who's about to kill himself -- again.
Zh: “没有，我看到的只有一个再次——想要自杀的条子。”

En: That's right, Gene. He nods. "Now, cop, you established a window of opportunity -- the only reason why you still have my ear. Don't waste it."
Zh: “没错，尤金。”他点点头。“现在，条子，你创造了一个机会窗口——这是唯一我还在听的原因，不要浪费了。”

En: That's one mystery down then. He nods. "An *architectural* mystery. It doesn't much concern Ruby, does it?"
Zh: “算是解决了一个谜团了。”他点点头。“一个*建筑学*谜团。这跟露比没多大关系，不是吗？”

En: Have we firmly established Ruby could have had access to the roof where the man was shot? (Move on.)
Zh: “我们不是已经证实露比可能进入那个人被枪杀的屋顶了吗？”（继续。）

En: He knows Eugene is more lieutenant material than he is.
Zh: 他知道尤金比自己更适合做警督。

En: Fuck off, man.
Zh: “滚蛋吧，老兄。”

En: You just showed him a breech-loading gun!
Zh: 你刚刚给他展示了一支后膛枪！

En: I've seen the winch.
Zh: “我见过起吊机了。”

En: On the side of the building. You can see it from the harbour. He points to his eyeballs. "You wanna be a lieutenant -- you gotta keep your eyes peeled."
Zh: “就在大楼侧面，尤金，从港口就能看到。”他指着自己的眼球。“你想当警督——就得仔细观察。”

En: You wouldn't believe the shit we have at the RCM.
Zh: “你不会相信RCM有多吊的。”

En: I don't have to *believe*. I know you ain't got shit. He squints at you.
Zh: “我没必要*相信*，我只知道你不怎么样。”他眯着眼睛看你。

En: What winch? Where's the winch?
Zh: “什么起吊机？在哪里？”

En: All right, I'll play along. Those two doors of yours -- the winch connects them? With some kind of... dumbwaiter?
Zh: “行吧，我可以配合。你说的那两扇门——是起吊机把它们连起来的？用某种……升降机？”

En: Still -- he's thinking about it now. And he knows *gunning up* has nothing to do with this.
Zh: 尽管如此——他已经在考虑这件事了，他也知道*武装起来*与此事无关。

En: C'mon guys, she's one of you. Of course she's carrying -- you all are.
Zh: “拜托，伙计们。她可是你们中的一员，当然会有啦——你们全都有。”

En: Shush now. He turns to you. "You got something else to back this route up? Or is that it?"
Zh: “先别说话。”他转向你。“你还有别的东西能证明这条通道吗？还是仅此而已了？”

En: Screw honest -- you just *showed* him a breech-loading gun!
Zh: 去他妈的诚实——你刚刚给他*展示*了一支后膛枪！

En: Nah... He taps his coat pocket. "This kind of heat is not easy to come by. It took us some time get *gunned up* to where we are now."
Zh: “得了……”他拍着外套口袋。“这种压力可不容易对付，我们花了好久才*武装起来*达到今天的成就。”

En: It's a *dumbwaiter*, not an industrial lift. How about I go instead of...
Zh: “那是个*升降机*，不是工业电梯。不如换我去……”

En: I just *showed* you a breechloader -- that any child could have.
Zh: “我刚刚给你*展示*了一支后膛枪——任何孩子都能拿到。”

En: Phase II: Murder weapon. Get a gun in her hand. If not that, then at least a shadow of a doubt (in the shape of a gun).
Zh: 第二阶段：凶器。把枪跟她联系起来。如果不行，那么至少提出一点质疑（跟枪联系起来）。

En: A broken old broomstick is what you showed me -- but point taken. He nods.
Zh: “你给我看的就是一根坏掉的旧扫帚——但我明白你的意思了。”他点点头。

En: He knows it's something. He's just not ready to say you knew more about Ruby than he does -- yet.
Zh: 他知道那是什么。他只是还没准备好——承认你比他更加了解露比。

En: I've already established Ruby's running drugs. She could've smuggled guns too.
Zh: “我已经确认了露比在交易毒品，说不定她也会走私枪支。”

En: Goddamn, that's just about your favourite topic isn't it? Titus slaps his thigh. "Every fucking five seconds!"
Zh: “该死，这正是你最喜欢的话题不是吗？”提图斯拍拍大腿。“你他妈每五秒钟就提一次！”

En: All I'm saying is -- from what I saw in her cabin she'd have *no trouble* getting a gun.
Zh: “我想说的是——从我在她車厢看到的情况来看，她应该能*毫不费力*地弄到一把枪。”

En: Fine, Titus -- I'm not getting into it, if it touches on your precious business.
Zh: “行了，提图斯——如果这涉及到你宝贵的生意，我就不多谈了。”

En: He probably just doesn't like her.
Zh: 他可能就是不喜欢她。

En: Trouble? This is Revachol -- even the roid-heads in the gym had a carbine. No one has *trouble* getting a gun. I still never saw her carry one.
Zh: “麻烦？这可是瑞瓦肖——就连健身房里的肌肉男都有卡宾枪。弄到枪一点也不*麻烦*。不过我从来没见到她拿过。”

En: Okay, cop -- she's got connections. He shakes his head slowly.  "I can see how she *could* have access to a gun -- that still doesn't mean she *did*."
Zh: “好吧，条子——她的确有些路子。”他缓缓摇头。“我知道她完全*可以*弄到一把枪——但这并不意味着她确实这么*做了*。”

En: He's about to blow his fuse.
Zh: 他快要发火了。

En: But a route, he forms a gun with his hand, "does not put that bullet in his head. A gun does that. And Ruby doesn't carry one."
Zh: “但一条路径，”他用手比划成枪的样子，“也不能把子弹射进雇佣兵的脑袋，只有枪才行。然而露比并没有枪。”

En: He's angry because he knows you're right. What he's saying only confirms your theory.
Zh: 他生气是因为他明白你是对的，他的话只能证实你的理论。

En: Let's go with that. Point is -- it's just a rusty winch, it doesn't put her on the roof.
Zh: “就先这样吧。问题是——那只是一台生锈的起吊机，并不能证明她就在屋顶上。”

En: That's all you needed. Doubt.
Zh: 这就是你所需要的，怀疑。

En: Kitten?
Zh: “猫咪？”

En: I've seen the winch, I'm not blind. He nods. "You sayin' she reeled herself up by her collar. Like some kind of..." he can't come up with an analogy.
Zh: “我看到起吊机了，我又不是瞎子。”他点点头。“你是说她用衣领把自己卷了上去，就像某种……”他想不出什么恰当的比喻。

En: And a little -- just a *little* -- worried.
Zh: 还有一点——只是*一点点*——担心。

En: He must be referencing some past case of theirs.
Zh: 他一定是在说他们过去的某个案子。

En: But what if you miss interesting information that way? The choice is ultimately yours, but...
Zh: 但如果你错过了有趣的信息呢？最终选择权在你手上，可是……

En: You were warned not to bring it up. Your best shot here is to just back out of it.
Zh: 你被警告过不要提起这件事，你最好的选择就是收回那句话。

En: Sure -- *normal*, he says without smiling.
Zh: “当然——*正常*，”他面无表情地说。

En: Titus, you have to see it. Things don't add up. I need to talk to her. (Move on.)\n
Zh: “提图斯，你得好好想想。事情有点说不通，我得和她谈谈。”（继续。）

En: Titus, you have to see it. Things don't add up -- we need to talk to her. (Move on.)
Zh: “提图斯，你得好好想想。事情有点说不通——我们得和她谈谈。”（继续。）

En: Silence. He looks around the room.
Zh: 沉默，他环视了一下房间。

En: The old man in the corner nods.
Zh: 角落里的老人点点头。

En: A very small nod -- and a trickle of tobacco spit on his lip.
Zh: 一个非常轻微的点头——他的嘴唇吐出一小滴烟草残渍。

En: This is the only opinion he cares for.
Zh: 这是他唯一关心的意见。

En: He's right, you know? The lieutenant notes dryly. "Great work, but the not shaking people's hands thing -- is a little odd."
Zh: “他说得对，知道吗？”警督干巴巴地记录着。“干得漂亮，但不跟人握手这件事——有点太奇怪了。”

En: I hear they have a shack where junkies sometimes crash. Time for you to step up.
Zh: “我听说他们有个窝棚，瘾君子们有时候会在那里嗑药。是你接手的时候了。

En: So she didn't go to tell Evrart.
Zh: “所以她没有去告诉艾弗拉特。

En: Yeah -- I see it. He puts his beer down. "There's one more thing I've been *wondering* about. Ever since you asked me where she is. Add it to your list of suspicions, if you want."
Zh: “是啊——我明白了。”他把啤酒放下。“我一直在*想*另外一件事。自从你问我她的行踪之后就开始了。如果你愿意，可以把她列入你的怀疑名单了。”

En: I don't *know*. He smiles a peculiar smile. "I don't know where she went. She just up and left. Got real scared too. Wouldn't tell me where -- however *hard* I asked. Wanna know why?"
Zh: “我不*知道*。”他笑得很怪异。“我不知道她去哪儿了，她那晚只露了个面就离开了。而且还很害怕，不愿意告诉我在哪里——不论我有多么*坚持*。想知道为什么么？”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: She was afraid I would tell you. He looks  you straight in the eye. "Maybe she was right. By now I probably would."
Zh: “她怕我会告诉你。”他直视着你的眼睛。“或许她是对的，现在我可能就告诉你了。”

En: And the hole in his heart.
Zh: “还有他心上的缺口。”

En: Asshole till the end, huh. Well, fuck you too, then! He doesn't sound angry, despite shouting.
Zh: “真是个彻头彻尾的混蛋，不是吗。行，去你妈的吧！”尽管大喊大叫着，他听起来并不生气。

En: She knew there's evidence on her -- and she knew we'd find it. This is typical suspect behaviour. Why fleeing is always incriminatory.
Zh: “她知道有不利于她的证据——她也说我们迟早会找到的。这正是典型的嫌犯行径，所以逃跑总是有罪的。”

En: You think she knew I'd find evidence against her?
Zh: “你认为她知道我能找到不利于她的证据？”

En: Walk away.
Zh: 走开。

En: Perhaps. He looks out of the window again. "Ask her if you find her. It won't be easy though. She made sure of that."
Zh: “也许吧。”他又望着窗外。“等你找到她再问吧。但这并不容易，她确保了这一点。

En: You're a smart cop, but you're a stupid person -- no one goes to tell Evrart anything. If he has to know, he has to know *fast*.
Zh: “你是个聪明的条子，却是个愚蠢的人——没有人会告诉艾弗拉特任何事。如果他必须知道，就必须*尽快*知道。”

En: We will start there. The lieutenant takes a quick note. "One more question: what does Ruby look like?"
Zh: “我们就从这里入手。”警督迅速做了个笔记。“还有一个问题：露比长什么样子？”

En: Sure -- there are some shithouses there. A cinderblock town. The fisher-folk there refuse to unionise, so that's one place we haven't looked.
Zh: “当然——那一片有几个棚屋，一座煤渣砖镇子。那里的渔民拒绝加入工会，所以那里是唯一我们没有考虑过的地方。”

En: What was she scared of?
Zh: “她在害怕什么？”

En: When did she leave?
Zh: “她什么时候离开的？”

En: Friday afternoon. When you first arrived. I got word the RCM was in town, then she came in to see me. Told me she was leaving. That's when we had our little... conversation.
Zh: “周五下午，你们过来的第一天。我听说RCM派人过来了，然后她就来找我：告诉我她要走了。然后我们就有了一小段……交流。”

En: Can you tell me where on the coast I should start looking?
Zh: “你能告诉我应该从海岸的什么地方开始找吗？”

En: I told you -- you.
Zh: “我告诉你——是你。”

En: Me, personally?
Zh: “我，一个人？”

En: Me? As in the RCM?
Zh: “我？一个RCM警官？”

En: No -- you. As in the cop with the sideburns and the disco clothes.
Zh: “不——你。一个留着鬓角胡、穿着迪斯科衣服的条子。”

En: Yes -- you, the cop with the sideburns and the disco clothes.
Zh: “没错——就是你，一个留着鬓角胡、穿着迪斯科衣服的条子。”

En: And a hole in his heart. That first.
Zh: 还有他心上的缺口，这是首要的。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: She said you have a funny taste in clothes. He scoffs. "And that you won't *stop*."
Zh: “她说你对服装的品味很奇怪。”他嘲笑着。“但绝不会*停下*你的脚步。”

En: What else did Ruby tell you -- about me?
Zh: “露比还告诉你了什么——关于我的事？”

En: Until you *have* something on her. She said she's heard of you from Jamrock. That you're a human can-opener. That you play suspects against each other. Open them up, like cans.
Zh: “直到你*找出*她的把柄。她说她在加姆洛克听说过你，是个人形开罐器。说你会挑拨嫌犯们彼此针对，像开罐头一样，撬开他们的嘴。”

En: You know, I don't usually do this -- I just measure things, but...
Zh: 要知道，我通常不会这么做——我只会估量东西，但是……

En: Of course. I should have known. Concentrate on tracking down Ruby.
Zh: 当然，我早该知道的。集中注意力追踪露比。

En: The gardener? But she went to tell Evrart.
Zh: “那个园丁？但她去跟艾弗拉特报信了。”

En: No. Her and Evrart -- they don't talk. They've never met. They have no relations. They live in different *galaxies*. Get that through your head.
Zh: “不。她和艾弗拉特——他们不会有交流的，他们从未见过，也没有任何关系，他们是生活在不同*星系*的人。记清楚这一点。”

En: Won't stop?
Zh: “不会停下？”

En: ...open Angus up like a can? Yes, he did. He nods. "Now, we can whine about it, whack him, or we can go on with our lives. I'm having a 'go on with our lives' kind of day, Al. How about you?"
Zh: “……像开罐头一样撬开安格斯？这倒是没错。”他点点头。“现在我们当然可以埋怨他、打击他，但也可以继续我们的生活。我的想法是*继续过生活*，兰，你呢？”

En: Fucking hell... The tattooed man shakes his head. "Titus, did he just..."
Zh: “该死的……”纹身男摇摇头。“提图斯，他刚刚是不是……”

En: It's not much, but it'll do. (Go!)
Zh: “虽然不多，但也足够。”（出发！）

En: It'll *have* to. He puts his hand out.
Zh: “*必须*足够。”他伸出手。

En: It's not an actual question.
Zh: 这不是一个真正的问题。

En: Don't forget the *funny* tie too.
Zh: “别忘了那条*趣味*领带。”

En: No wonder she's afraid. I have come to declare the ending of the Human Experiment.
Zh: “不怪她害怕了。我是来宣布人类实验结束的消息的。”

En: I have no idea why she'd be scared. I'm just a normal cop with regular thoughts in his head.
Zh: “我不知道她为什么要害怕。我只是个脑子里满是正常想法的普通警察。”

En: She probably knew me from my singing days.
Zh: “她可能从我还在唱歌的时候就认识我了。”

En: Why not? You could turn this thing into an actual police force. He extends his hand. "Afraid you would actually have to follow the *law*?"
Zh: “为什么不呢？你可以把兄弟会变成一个真正的警察机关。”他伸出手。“害怕你真得遵守*法律*吗？”

En: I'm sorry. God, why does everything flee at the sight of my shadow?!
Zh: “抱歉。神啊，为什么万事万物见到我的影子都要逃跑呢？！”

En: No one goes to tell Evrart anything. He knows what he has to know -- fast.
Zh: “没有人会去告诉艾弗拉特任何事，他很快就会知道——他需要知道的东西。”

En: He shrugs. "Lizzie needed some air."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“莉兹需要透透气。”

En: The coast is a dangerous place -- for *police*-men.
Zh: “海岸是个危险的地方——对*警务*人员来说。”

En: Like the RCM? He lets go of your hand. "You're a real police force all right -- of the Wild Pines. Everyone around here knows that. So watch your ass out there."
Zh: “就像RCM？”他放开你的手。“你当然算是真正的警察机关——听命于野松公司的。这里的每个人都心知肚明，所以你最好小心点儿。”

En: You said it would be nice to have a ballistics lab. Use ours -- turn this into a real police force.
Zh: “你说过要是有个弹道实验室就好了。用我们的吧——把这变成一个真正的警察机关。”

En: You know, when I first saw you limp in here I thought she was paranoid, or sniffing her own supply, but... He measures you up. "Now I'm not so sure."
Zh: “知道吗，当我第一次看到你醉醺醺地进来的时候，我还以为是她在疑神疑鬼，或者又嗑药了，但是……”他打量着你。“现在我不那么确定了。”

En: Silence. He nods.
Zh: 沉默，他点点头。

En: How can I forget. He nods at your tie.
Zh: “我怎么会忘记呢？”他朝你的领带点了点头。

En: How can I forget. He nods at your tie.
Zh: “我怎么会忘记呢？”他朝你的领带点了点头。

En: Not just granite -- tightly packed RCM sergeant material.
Zh: 不仅仅是花岗岩——妥妥的RCM警长材料。

En: Sure. The human experiment. That's real funny. He looks at you without smiling.
Zh: “当然了，人类实验，真有意思。”他面无笑意地看着你。

En: His grip is firm and reassuring. Like holding a piece of unpolished granite.
Zh: 他的握手坚实可靠，就像握着一块未经打磨的花岗岩。

En: It seems you have that effect -- especially on *women*. He looks at you.
Zh: “看来你有一种影响力——尤其对*女人*来说。”他看着你。

En: Shake it.
Zh: 跟他握手。

En: Yeah, that's real *funny*. She wasn't laughing though. He looks at you.
Zh: “是啊，真*有意思*。不过她并没有笑。”他看着你。

En: No.
Zh: 不是。

En: You should be a cop, Titus.
Zh: “你应该去做警察的，提图斯。”

En: Scared? You should be too. A strange fear creeps over you, a metallic taste in your mouth. Change the subject.
Zh: 害怕？你也应该如此。一种诡异的恐惧在你身上蔓延开来，你的嘴巴尝到一丝金属的味道。改变话题。

En: Do you have any clues on where Ruby went?
Zh: “你知道露比去哪儿了吗？”

En: She's not far. We know that much -- she didn't take her lorry. So she's on foot.
Zh: “离这不远，我们就知道这么多了——她没有开走货車，应该是步行的。”

En: It's not a threat -- it's a warning.
Zh: 这不是威胁——而是一个警告。

En: Is that true Kim? Am I a can-opener?
Zh: “这是真的吗，金？我是开罐器吗？”

En: Nah. We're good. He lets go of your hand.
Zh: “免了，我们好着呢。”他放开了你的手。

En: Evrart's file on you seemed thin on info. She must know from somewhere else. From Jamrock. Something *more*.
Zh: 艾弗拉特的档案里似乎没有多少你的信息。她一定是从别处知道的，从加姆洛克，以及*更多*。

En: Good fucking luck, man... She knows this place like the back of her hand. You'll never find her.
Zh: “祝你好运，老兄……她对这地方了如指掌，你永远找不到她的。”

En: Yeah, Al. He gives a sharp look. "And we won't either."
Zh: “没错，兰！”他狠狠地瞪了过来。“我们也不能。”

En: *Evrart* has a file on you, but that's bogus. What she knows comes from somewhere else -- from Jamrock. It must be *real* stuff.
Zh: *艾弗拉特*掌握了你的资料，但那完全是一派胡言。她掌握的情报来自别处——来自加姆洛克。一定是*真正的*情报。

En: You're all here. Who's out looking?
Zh: “你们都在这里了，谁在外面找人呢？”

En: She's not really a... The man stares into his beer. "Hardie candidate anymore, is she?"
Zh: “她不再是……”男人盯着他的啤酒。“哈迪兄弟候选人了，是吗？”

En: Right. That's the second one now who's trying to flee you.
Zh: 没错，那是第二个想要逃离你的人了。

En: She's not, Glen.
Zh: “她不是，格伦。”

En: You said it would be nice to have a ballistics lab. You could use ours.
Zh: “你说过要是有个弹道实验室就好了，用我们的吧。”

En: That will only piss him off. He's not going to yap about Evrart.
Zh: 那只会激怒他，他是不会对艾弗拉特大吼大叫的。

En: Anything else? *Anything*?
Zh: “还有别的事吗？*还有吗*？”

En: Could she have gotten this info from... Evrart?
Zh: “有没有可能她是从艾弗拉特那里……得到的情报？”

En: No -- he already is a sergeant. That's what you're feeling. He just wasn't sure what *you* are.
Zh: 不——他已经是个警长了，这就是你的感受。他只是不确定*你*是什么。

En: Just think it.
Zh: 想想就好。

En: The coast is a dangerous place for lawmen. Especially Mr. Can Opener here.
Zh: “对执法官来说海岸是个危险的地方，特别是这位开罐器先生。”

En: The coast is a dangerous place.
Zh: “海岸是个危险的地方。”

En: Have you looked for her?
Zh: “你找过她吗？”

En: Why not? He looks at your chest. "You look about ready to do something pretty crazy because some bird fucked you over."
Zh: “为什么不呢？”他看着你的胸口。“你看起来就像是准备做些相当疯狂的事情，就因为某只鸟惹到了你。”

En: You and I are going to dance in the moonlight under a billion stars.
Zh: 你和我将要在月光和亿万颗星星之下起舞。

En: Rich-boy law is a sham. He shakes his hand. "It only works for the Wild Pines lady. So keep your lab -- and watch your ass out there."
Zh: “富家子的法律根本形同虚设。”他摆摆手。“它只对野松女士生效。所以留着你的实验室吧——你也给我小心点。”

En: You are... insistent. He nods.
Zh: “你真的……很坚持。”他点点头。

En: Yeah, there was something else. She wouldn't tell me though. I could see she wanted to. It was *burning* on her lips: 'This cop, Titus, this cop, he...' But she was too scared.
Zh: “是啊，还有别的事情，但她不肯告诉我。我能看出她很想这么做，话都到她*嘴边*了：‘这个条子，提图斯，这个条子，他……’但她太害怕了。”

En: Why not? You suck on that gun like someone tore you a brand new asshole.
Zh: “为什么不呢？你吞枪的样子好像有人把你撕成了一个新的混蛋。”

En: A little -- on the coast.
Zh: “一点点——在海岸边。”

En: Where have you looked for her?
Zh: “你去哪里找她了？”

En: Sure. It was someone else. It appears we have a *ninth* Hardie boy.
Zh: 当然，是另一个人，看来我们有第九位哈迪兄弟了。

En: Where have you looked for her, more precisely?
Zh: “你去哪里找她了，说得确切一点？”

En: More precisely? On the *coast*. Past the water lock. He nods southwest. "She's not here, so I'm thinking she's there."
Zh: “更确切？就在*海岸*上面，水闸过去一点。”他朝西南方向点点头。“她不在这里，所以我想她会不会在那儿。”

En: *Who's* doing this looking? They're all here.
Zh: *谁*在找人？他们全都在这里。

En: And he still isn't. People aren't afraid of good cops in the way Ruby was afraid of you, he thinks, then turns back to his men.
Zh: 但他仍然不能确定你是不是好警察。人们不会像露比害怕你那样害怕一名好警察，他想着，然后转身面向他的兄弟们。

En: When are you gonna get it through your suicidal head? he scoffs. "I already *am* -- I just  wasn't sure you were."
Zh: “你什么时候才能用你有自毁倾向的脑袋想明白？”他嘲讽着。“我已经*是了*——只是不能确定你是不是。”

En: When are you gonna get it through your dumb head? He scoffs. "I already *am*, I just wasn't sure you were."
Zh: “你什么时候才能用你的笨蛋脑袋想明白？”他嘲讽着。“我已经*是了*，只是不能确定你是不是。”

En: The elevator is *outside* the building. It's an old dumbwaiter -- used for moving pinball machines up and down from the workshop.
Zh: “电梯在大楼*外面*，是台旧升降机——用于从工坊里上下搬运弹球机。”

En: I fear we just can't piece this puzzle together. At this point, the only sensible thing to do is get someone else to put the pieces together.
Zh: 我担心我们不能把这个难题拼凑完整。到了现在这个地步，唯一明智的举动就是让别人把碎片拼凑完整。

En: (Whisper) "Kim, you've had long enough to come up with a theory. What'cha got?"
Zh: （低声）“金，你已经有足够的时间想出一个理论了。结果呢？”

En: No, I want to do this all by myself!
Zh: 不，我想自己来！

En: Suit yourself! But remember, you're the one putting yourself through this hassle.
Zh: 随你便！但请记住，是你让自己经历了这些麻烦的。

En: But the lieutenant isn't here. What am I supposed to do without him?
Zh: 但是警督不在，没有他我该怎么办呢？

En: Easy! If the lieutenant isn't here, you should find him and attempt this again while he's with you.
Zh: 简单！如果警督不在，你应该去找他，趁他还和你在一起的时候再试一次。

En: I think it's best we just dive in, he whispers back, before turning to Titus and asking: "What makes you so sure Ruby *didn't* kill him?"
Zh: “我想我们最好直截了当点，”他同样低声回答，然后转向提图斯问到：“你为什么能这么肯定露比*没有*杀他？”

En: The lieutenant looks around the room, evidently enjoying the moment's respite. But before long, he puts his hand on your shoulder. "Unless we have more business, we should..." He looks to the door.
Zh: 警督环视了一圈，显然很享受这片刻的喘息。但不久后他就把手放在你的肩膀上。“除非我们还有更多事情，否则我们……”他望向大门。

En: It's that simple.
Zh: 就是那么简单。

En: Can't get through the door? You know what would help -- doing a *thorough* search of this room! Maybe something's hidden.
Zh: 进不了门？你知道什么能派上用场——把这个房间*彻底*搜查一遍！或许会有些隐藏的东西。

En: There's a *spooky* building West of this. Take a flashlight with you, search the basement. There are secrets -- magisterial, ancient secrets that may assist you.
Zh: 这里往西有座*闹鬼*的建筑。带上手电筒，搜查地下室。那里有秘密——能帮到你的权威而又古老的秘密。

En: Hey! Did someone say something already? No? Yes? Who cares! Upstairs, in Klaasje's bedroom -- the window the bullet came through. Analyse that!
Zh: 嘿！有人说什么了吗？没有？有吗？谁在乎呢！楼上，在卡拉洁的卧房里——被子弹射穿的玻璃，分析一下！

En: Reporting for duty! A *thorough* search of this Union box may help unearth something *incriminating*! Pin it on her -- she's been here, right?
Zh: 报告！对这个工会包厢进行一次*彻底的*搜查可能会有助于找出一些*罪证*！推到她身上吧——她也来过这儿，对吧？

En: For starters, you could search the Whirling-in-Rags for hidden passages she could have used to get to the roof. There's a door in the *kitchen* I've had my eye on for a while...
Zh: 首先，你可以在褴褛飞旋里寻找她能用来爬上屋顶的秘密通道。*厨房*里的那扇门我已经注意好久了……

En: For starters, you could find out what's behind that mystery door in the kitchen. If it's a secret route, she could have used that to get to the roof!
Zh: 首先，你可以去调查厨房里那扇神秘的门背后是什么。如果那是条秘密通道，她很可能就是利用它爬上屋顶的！

En: Wow, you've been very thorough. There's nothing else to add -- just crack that Klaasje's defences. Maybe she'll tell you something helpful?
Zh: 哇哦，你想得真是周到。没有什么好补充的了——只要攻破卡拉洁最后一道防线，或许她会告诉你一些有用的事？

En: You could crack that Klaasje's defences. Maybe she'll tell you something helpful?
Zh: 你可以攻破卡拉洁最后一道防线，或许她会告诉你一些有用的事？

En: Thanks guys. Let's get moving again.
Zh: 谢谢大家，我们再次行动起来吧。

En: Not just yet -- can't we review the components of the theory?
Zh: 先等等——我们不能回顾一下这个理论的组成部分吗？

En: Gladly.
Zh: 乐意之至。

En: Sure. Why not? Let's hear what some of our friends have to say.
Zh: 好啊，为什么不呢？让我们听听我们的朋友有什么想说的吧。

En: It's very unlikely to help, but sure. We can go to Theory Town.
Zh: 虽然不太可能有帮助，但是好啊，我们可以去一趟理论世界。

En: Got it. Now get me moving again, please.
Zh: 了解，现在请让我们再次行动起来吧。

En: Let's move.
Zh: 行动起来。

En: Sure thing. But before anything else -- can I venture to Theory Town?
Zh: 当然可以。但在此之前——我们能去一趟理论世界吗？

En: When are you gonna get it through your suicidal head? he scoffs. "I already *am* -- I just wasn't sure you were."
Zh: “你什么时候才能用你有自毁倾向的脑袋想明白？”他嘲讽着。“我已经*是了*——只是不能确定你是不是。”

En: When are you gonna get it through your dumb head? He scoffs. "I already *am*, I just wasn't sure you were."
Zh: “你什么时候才能用你的笨蛋脑袋想明白？”他嘲讽着。“我已经*是了*，只是不能确定你是不是。”

En: Okay, so this guy is cool -- he stands there like a statue. An *angry* statue. And he does *not* like you.
Zh: 好吧，所以这个人很酷——他像一尊雕像一样杵在那里，一尊*愤怒*的雕像，他*不*喜欢你。

En: Don't even try to win him over. You won't.
Zh: 不要考虑说服他了，你不可能成功的。

En: There's a joke you're not getting here. Could have something to do with the brain damage.
Zh: 你没明白其中的笑点，可能跟大脑的损伤有关。

En: Kim's cooler than you.
Zh: “金比你酷多了。”

En: I'm sure he's fucking *flattered*, but *Kim* is not part of this thought experiment. In this one -- *we're* partners.
Zh: “我敢说他一定是*受宠若惊*，但*金*不是思维实验的一部分。在这里——*我们*是搭档。”

En: The lieutenant is silent.
Zh: 警督沉默不语。

En: Okay, yes -- you get the joke. Leave it at that.
Zh: 那好吧——你听懂笑点了，就这样吧。

En: He didn't answer *your* question.
Zh: 他没有回答*你的*问题。

En: Who does this asshole think he is -- a *cop*?
Zh: 这混蛋以为自己是谁——一个*警察*？

En: Hear that? He wants you to say things. Say one!
Zh: 听见了吗？他想让你说点什么。说吧！

En: You know what? It must be because he's *buff*. He looks like he works out like he's got the devil on his back.
Zh: 知道吗？一定是因为他很*健壮*，他看着就像是在疯狂锻炼的样子。

En: Let's talk about the hanged man *again*.
Zh: “我们*再来*谈谈那个吊人吧。”

En: Okay, why not. Let's do the whole thing over again. We're not wasting time. There *is* no time!
Zh: “好啊，为什么不呢，我们重头再来一次吧。我们完全没有浪费时间，根本*就*没时间了！”

En: There's something strange about this guy. Figure it out.
Zh: 这家伙有点奇怪，弄明白。

En: Called your station.
Zh: 呼叫你的警局。

En: Bounced ideas off him.
Zh: 征求他的意见。

En: She's with him.
Zh: 她跟他是一起的。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: The man doesn't reply. He's too busy furiously squinting at you.
Zh: 男人没有回答，他正忙着疯狂地眯着眼睛看你。

En: Trying to get a good picture of your jacket, no doubt.
Zh: 毫无疑问，他是想好好欣赏一下你的夹克。

En: Can he even see through those glasses?
Zh: 他能透过那对镜片看见东西吗？

En: Again he provides no reply, instead seemingly trying to get a better look of your attire through his sunglasses.
Zh: 他还是没有回答，似乎是想透过他的墨镜更好地欣赏你的衣服。

En: Good luck, these look darker than welding glasses.
Zh: 祝你好运，那对镜片看起来比焊接眼镜还要深。

En: The lieutenant goes pale. 'No badge?' he mouths to himself silently.
Zh: 警督面色苍白。“没有警徽？”他默默地自言自语。

En: You look like shit. Your ruffled face reflects in the man's sunglasses.
Zh: “你看你那屎样。”你气恼的脸倒映在那个男人的墨镜上。

En: Okay then. See you around! [Leave.]
Zh: “那好吧，回见！”[离开。]

En: *"That's* the where you remember me from?"
Zh: “你是从*那个时候*认出我的吗？”

En: Now, what do you want? His tone is impatient.
Zh: “现在，你想干什么？”他的语气很不耐烦。

En: Cool shades. Are you wearing a disguise?
Zh: “墨镜很酷，你是在乔装打扮吗？”

En: Yes. It's a hobby of mine. He looks at you inquisitively.
Zh: “没错，个人的小爱好。”他揶揄地上下打量着你。

En: Got some questions for you. I'm a cop.
Zh: “我有几个问题想问你，我是警察。”

En: There's something about this guy that *matches* with a face in your head. A similar, but different face.
Zh: 这个家伙的某些地方与你脑海中的一张脸很*相似*。一张相似却不相同的脸。

En: The suit, the sunglasses, the haircut... he's *famous*. That's how you know his face.
Zh: 西装、墨镜、发型……他是个*名人*。所以你才会认得这张脸。

En: Are you famous or something?
Zh: “你是个名人还是怎么的？”

En: Not as famous as you, superstar. He says without the slightest hint of irony.
Zh: “远不及你的名气，大明星。”他的话不带一丝嘲讽。

En: Wait, I'm famous?
Zh: “等下，我很有名吗？”

En: Fine.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Oh yes, big time. He nods enthusiastically. "Who hasn't heard of the Far Out Son of Liver Failure?!"
Zh: “是啊，可有名了。”他热情地点头。“谁没听说过那个世外的肝功能衰竭之子呢？！”

En: No, not entirely. It's impossible to say what his eyes behind the shades are doing.
Zh: “不，也不完全是。”很难说他隐藏在墨镜后面的眼镜在看什么。

En: About *what?* You don't look like a cop... He inspects you. "You know what you look like?"
Zh: “关于*什么*的？你看起来并不像警察……”他打量着你。“你知道自己像什么样子吗？”

En: Like a prophet?
Zh: “像个先知？”

En: Like a revolutionary?
Zh: “像个革命者？”

En: Like a sack of shit?
Zh: “像一坨狗屎？”

En: Like a megastar?
Zh: “像个超级巨星？”

En: Yes. He nods. "A superstar cop. Of course. *This* again."
Zh: “没错。”他点点头。“明星警探。当然了，又来*这一套*。”

En: *Now* will you answer some questions for me?
Zh: “*现在*你能回答我的问题了吗？”

En: No, he says calmly, then just keeps staring at you.
Zh: “不行，”他平静地说着，然后便一直盯着你。

En: Don't you *have* to?
Zh: “你不是*必须*得这么做吗？”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: If I wasn't clinically depressed, I'd burst out laughing. He doesn't look amused. "But I'm gonna go with *no* right now."
Zh: “如果我没被确诊为抑郁症的话，我都要爆笑了。”他看上去并不开心。“但现在我的答案还是*不行*。”

En: Cause it's not my job. Why don't you go and fucking do yours and solve this damn hanging?
Zh: “因为这不是我的工作。为什么不去做你自己的事情，解决那个该死的上吊男。”

En: I don't know -- you tell me.
Zh: “我不清楚——你告诉我啊。”

En: Like Guillaume Le...
Zh: “就像纪尧姆·列……”

En: Like... Kras Mazov?
Zh: “就像……卡拉斯·马佐夫？”

En: Like a man down on his luck? I'm trying real hard here, man.
Zh: “就像个时运不济的人？我已经很努力了，老兄。”

En: Oh... He seems taken aback for a moment. "Well, go solve your case then. That would count as *trying hard*."
Zh: “哦……”他似乎吃了一惊。“那就去解决你的案子啊，那才能被称之为*努力*。”

En: Exactly. The man nods excitedly in approval.
Zh: “完全正确。”男人兴奋地点头表示赞同。

En: No, not like a revolutionary. He looks away.
Zh: “不，并不像革命者。”他移开了目光。

En: Not the prophet shit again... He looks away.
Zh: “别又是先知之类的玩意儿了……”他移开了目光。

En: NO! The man in the sunglasses raises his fist. "You will not start blabbering about that asshole."
Zh: “不！”墨镜男举起了拳头。“你可别再念叨那个混蛋了。”

En: Yeaaah... He's rubbing his chin as he drags out the *yeah*. "Sort of. Okay. I get the reference. Like after he got run over or something."
Zh: “是啊……”他摩挲着下巴，一边挤出长长的*啊*字。“有点吧，行了，我明白你的意思。就像他被車撞了似的。”

En: I don't know... He taps on his forehead, thinking. "A sad stack of shit? Next to someone far more proficient."
Zh: “我不知道……”他轻拍着前额思索着。“就像一坨悲剧的狗屎？站在一个远比你专业的人旁边？”

En: I don't know... He taps on his forehead, thinking. "A sad stack of shit?"
Zh: “我不知道……”他轻拍着前额思索着。“就像一坨悲剧的狗屎？”

En: No, he doesn't.
Zh: “不，他不是。”

En: Fine.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: The man in sunglasses nods.
Zh: 墨镜男点点头。

En: Yeah... it's still irony.
Zh: 没错……仍然是嘲讽。

En: Let's talk about the hypothetical 41 again.\n
Zh: “我们再来谈谈你提到的那个假想中的41分局吧。”

En: Let's talk more about that hypothetical Station 41 you mentioned.
Zh: “我们多谈一些你提到的那个假想中的41分局吧。”

En: Okay, the man says with a sigh. He's waiting for you to speak.\n
Zh: “行啊，”男人叹了口气说道，等待着你的发言。

En: Oh, the Hypothetical Four-One. Yeah, let's *fantasize* about that. He blinks aggressively. "I'm not busy, you're not busy, let's just play around!"
Zh: “哦，假想的41。行啊，我们来*幻想*一下吧。”他略带攻击性地眨眨眼。“我不忙，你也不忙，不如一起来玩玩吧！”

En: So what would our relationship be in this alternate universe?
Zh: “那么，在这个平行宇宙中，我们的关系会是怎样的呢？”

En: Do *you*... have a crime to solve?
Zh: “*你*有……案件要解决吗？”

En: Who else is in our imaginary police station?
Zh: “我们想象中的警局里还有谁？”

En: I can't imagine it anymore. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我再也想象不出来了。”（总结。）

En: Let's be crazy... let's say you and I are partners. How's that for a thought experiment?
Zh: “不如我们再疯狂一点……假设你跟我是搭档。这个思维实验如何？”

En: Like partners in crime, or...?
Zh: “像是犯罪搭档，还是……？”

En: No! he exclaims excitedly. "'Cause in this thought experiment we are police officers. In a police station. We don't do crimes -- we're not crime bros."
Zh: “不是！”他激动地惊呼。“因为在这个思维实验中我们是警察。在警察局里，我们不会犯罪——更不是犯罪同伙。”

En: You seem like a cool guy, I'd love to have you as my partner.
Zh: “你看起来是个不错的家伙，我很乐意你成为我的搭档。”

En: You seem like a bit of a drag. No offence, but I could do better.
Zh: “你看起来像个累赘。无意冒犯，不过我一个人更好。”

En: None taken, my friend -- none taken. He waves his hands. "Let's be honest, there's been some purely fictional talk in our imaginary station in regards of who'd even *be* worthy of your partnership..."
Zh: “我不介意，我的朋友——不介意。”他摆摆手。“老实说，在我们假想的警局里，曾经发生过一些谁*能*配得上你的纯属虚构的谈话……”

En: ... and the conclusion is that a man with your calibre should form his own one-man-policing-unit. He nods eagerly along. "Anyone else would just slow you down."
Zh: “……然后结论是，像你这般有才干的人应该组成一支单人警察部队。”他急切地点着头。“其他人只会扯你的后腿。”

En: Neither can I, partner. Neither can I... His grey eyes suddenly flash above the glass frames. They feel sad.
Zh: “我也不行，搭档。我也不行……”他灰色的眼睛突然从玻璃镜框上方闪过。看上去很悲伤。

En: It's a mere second, but it feels like you saw something... a gram of compassion in that sadness.
Zh: 虽然只有一瞬间，但好像你看到了什么……悲伤中带有一丝同情。

En: C'mon, stop it, she says quietly.
Zh: “拜托，别说了，”她平静地说。

En: Would you now?! Or would I be *cramping your style?* The man bites his lip, then waves his hand. "Never mind... *partner*."
Zh: “会吗？！还是我*束缚了你的风格*？”男人咬了咬下唇，然后摆手。“算了……*搭档*。”

En: Oh no, NO, NO! You see, I enjoy watching other, better cops solve crimes -- and let me tell you, it's been quite a privilege seeing you work...
Zh: “哦不！不，不！你要知道，我很喜欢看其他，更好的警察解决案件——我得告诉你，能欣赏你工作的英姿是我的荣幸……”

En: This isn't helping, she says, shaking her head and looking at the man with sunglasses disapprovingly.
Zh: “这样是没用的，”她摇摇头，不赞同地看着那个墨镜男。

En: You're not going to believe this, but... The man pauses for dramatic effect. "... police officers!"
Zh: “你不会相信的，但是……”男人停顿了一下，想要制造一些戏剧性效果。“……警官们！”

En: Yes sir, solving crimes, locking up bad guys and... *AND* get this... and *not* getting their drink on at two o'clock.
Zh: “是的先生，解决案件，抓捕坏人……*并且*听好了……*不*在两点钟的时候喝酒。”

En: Just some regular boring motherfuckers in suits and uniforms. Nothing spectacularly extravagant like you -- the Far Out Son Of Lung.
Zh: “就是一群穿着西装和制服的无聊混蛋。没有什么能比你更加放纵的了——世外的肺之子。”

En: Again?! I can't believe this shit... He shakes his head, looking like he really is having trouble believing this *shit*.
Zh: “又来？我真是不敢相信……”他摇摇头，看起来非常难以置信。

En: Who is the *Far Out Son of Lung*?
Zh: “谁是*世外的肺之子*？”

En: Oh, it's you, you eccentric genius. He winks at you sarcastically. "I mean with your unorthodox approach to police work it *has to* be you."
Zh: “哦，是你啊，怪里怪气的天才。”他讽刺地冲你眨眨眼。“我是说，像你这种以非正统的方式来执行警务的人，那*必须*得是你了。”

En: Want to tell me more about him? Or her?
Zh: “想跟我多谈谈他，或者她吗？”

En: Not even a little bit.
Zh: “完全不想。”

En: It's an urban myth, the lieutenant says quietly. "About an officer who is so far undercover he can't remember who he is. As I said -- just an urban myth. *You're* not the Son of Lung."
Zh: “这是个都市传说，”警督平静地说。“说的是一名警官卧底太久以至于忘记了自己是谁。就像我说的——这只是个都市传说。*你不是*什么肺之子。”

En: I'm never going to get this case solved.
Zh: “我永远无法侦破这个案件了。”

En: I knew it, Kim! I knew I was a superstar.
Zh: “我就知道，金！我就知道我是个超级巨星。”

En: It's happening! My hard work is paying off! I'm becoming famous!
Zh: “成功了！我的努力得到了回报！我要成名了！”

En: Hold on, who's Son of Liver Failure?
Zh: “等等，谁是肝功能衰竭之子？”

En: *Far Out* Son of Liver Failure -- the supercop who voluntarily enters an alcohol induced delirium to solve crimes.

Watch out for yourself, loser!

En: No.
Yes.

En: When that happens I suggest you take his advice, he adds bitterly.
Zh: “当这种情况发生时，我建议你接受他的建议。”他苦涩地补充道。

Jean, he said okay, give it a rest.

Khm... I have a bit of... (Point to you head.) "Memory trouble."

Zh: “是吗，可能不是吧。我不记得在哪里见过你了。”

En: Push it more. Harder. Be *more* intelligent.

Zh: “不，不。这该死的自杀卖艺人想要*骗*我，这对提图斯·哈迪可不管用。”他看着你的眼睛。“放下小提琴或者滚开，就这么简单。”

En: No, no. This fucking clown is trying to *get* to me. This ain't gonna work with Titus Hardie. He looks you in the eye. "Put the fiddle down or walk away. It's that simple."
Zh: “不，不。这该死的小丑想要*骗*我，这对提图斯·哈迪可不管用。”他看着你的眼睛。“放下小提琴或者滚开，就这么简单。”

En: Yeah, it fucking is, but whaddaya do -- say no to a nice piece of ass like that? He looks you in the eye. "I'd like to see you try, fiddle-man."
Zh: “没错，你他妈说对了，但你能怎么办——拒绝那么翘的屁股？”他看着你的眼睛。“我倒想看看你行不行，你这个王八蛋提琴手。”

En: Goooooooooood... lord... what is happening to me? (Look up.)
Zh: “天……呐……我是怎么了？”（向上看。）

En: No-no... The lieutenant intervenes -- to re-contextualize. "He's just playing his *viola da gamba*. Before becoming a detective my partner was a violinist. Many of us were..."
Zh: “不-不……”警督干预进来——又重新组织了语言。“他只是在演奏自己的*古大提琴*而已。在成为警探之前，我的搭档曾是个提琴手。我们中有很多人都是……”

En: You better not OD in our booth, cop. Make sure you get out before you keel over.
Zh: “你可别在我们的包厢里嗑药过量了，条子。在你倒下前一定记得出门。”

En: Did he just call you a *pussy-boy*?
Zh: “他刚才是不是叫你*娘娘腔*了？”

En: Yeah, you pull *incredible* pussy, boss. No one pulls pussy like you!
Zh: “是啊，你确实很吸引那些*难以置信*的娘们，老大。没人比你更能吸引娘们了！”

En: You hear that? He squints and points at you. "One more peep out of you and you're on the curb, fiddle man."
Zh: “你听见了吗？”他眯起眼睛指着你。“再看一眼你就要出局了，提琴手。”

En: Titus, pussy-boy... (Dramatically put the fiddle down.) "You and I both now where this is going."
Zh: “提图斯，娘娘腔……”（戏剧性地放下小提琴。）“现在你我都知道这是怎么回事了。”

En: First he tries to kill himself and now this. What next?
Zh: “他先是想要自杀，现在又来这一套。下一出又是什么？”

En: *More* fiddle playing! I can't believe this shit. What is this *mime* trying to do, *get* to me?
Zh: “*更多*的演奏！我真是不敢相信。这出*哑剧*是什么意思，*忽悠*我？”

En: I mean your sweet little plaything upstairs. (More fiddle playing.)
Zh: “我是说你楼上那个可爱的小玩物。”（变本加厉地演奏小提琴。）

En: He'll put the fiddle down. He turns to you. "Put it down, officer."
Zh: “他会把提琴放下的。”他转向你。“放下吧，警官。”

En: He's working really, really hard to spin this.
Zh: 他非常非常努力地想要扭转局面。

En: He gives you a stern sideways glance. "Time to move to more *earthly* matters. Like what really happened here -- detective?" He snaps his fingers under your nose.
Zh: 他严厉地斜了你一眼。“是时候做些更加*世俗*的事务了。比如这里到底发生了什么——警探？”他在你鼻子跟前打了个响指。

En: Like hell it is! I still can't believe he called you a *pussy-boy*, Titus. What does that mean?
Zh: “当然了！我还是不敢相信他会喊你*娘娘腔*，提图斯。那是什么意思？”

En: He gives you a stern sideways glance. "Time to move to more *serious* matters. Like what really happened between you and the man you hanged."
Zh: 他严厉地斜了你一眼。“是时候谈点更*严肃*的事情了。比如你和那个被你们吊死的男人之间究竟发生了什么。”

En: He's working really, really hard to spin this, but it's not easy.
Zh: 他非常非常努力地想要扭转局面，这并不容易。

En: No-no... The lieutenant intervenes to re-contextualize. "He's just playing his *viola da gamba*. Before becoming a detective my partner was a violinist. Many of us were..."
Zh: “不-不……，”警督干预进来，重新组织了语言。“他只是在演奏自己的*古大提琴*而已。在成为警探之前，我的搭档曾是个提琴手。我们很多人都是……”

En: I do *not* understand! This fiddle play totally fell apart. The concept was solid, but the delivery -- *tragically* off. You sounded like a madman, not a cop.
Zh: 我*不*明白！这出小提琴演奏彻底失败了。概念很明确，但传达的方式——简直*可悲*。你听上去像个疯子，而不是警察。

En: It can't be good, boss. He turns to you. "You got something more to say pussy boy man?"
Zh: “情况不妙啊，老大。”他转向你。“你还有什么想说的吗，娘娘腔？”

En: What happened Sunday night?
Zh: “周日那晚发生了什么？”

En: Klaasje came down. He points to the stairs. "She seemed really out of it. Drugged up -- even more than usual. Bug eyed and gurning, you know? Not in a *fun* way."
Zh: “卡拉洁下楼来了。”他指着楼梯。“她看起来很不在状态，嗑药了似的——甚至比平日里还要多。瞪着眼睛，怪模怪样的，明白吗？不是那种*寻欢作乐*的感觉。”

En: He doesn't think she did. Or at least he *hopes* she didn't.
Zh: 他认为她没有，或者至少他*希望*她没有。

En: The room falls quiet. So quiet you can hear Angus wheeze.
Zh: 整个房间陷入沉默。安静到你都能听到角落里安格斯的喘息声。

En: Fatty! The little guy hits Angus on the back of the head. A loud slap. "Say one more thing to the cops and I'll..."
Zh: “胖胖！”小个子拍了下安格斯的后脑勺，响亮的一声。“再跟条子说一句，我就……”

En: Outside in the midday sun -- ruins. The pavements are cracked and the benches peeling. Newspapers blow in the wind.
Zh: 正午的阳光下——废墟之中。人行道开裂，长凳褪色，报纸飘散在空中。

En: SHUT UP, ANGUS!
Zh: “闭嘴，安格斯！”

En: He was dead *before* you hanged him?
Zh: “他在你们把他吊上去*之前*就死了？”

En: Got it. He takes out his notebook. "Kill you -- because they don't like you. All because..."
Zh: “明白了。”他拿出笔记本。“杀了你——因为他们不喜欢你，都是因为……”

En: It wasn't that, it wasn't... the fat man says with a wheeze. "We didn't shoot him."
Zh: “不是那样的，不是……”那个胖子喘着粗气。“我们没有*开枪*。”

En: We didn't light him on fire! You hear the fat man wheeze. He's trying to get up.
Zh: “我们没有放火烧他！”你听到那个胖子呼哧呼哧地喘气，试图站起来。

En: It wasn't that. It wasn't... the fat man says with a wheeze. "We just couldn't get him down, okay?"
Zh: “不是那样的，不是……”那个胖子喘着粗气。“我们就是没办法把他放下来，明白吗？”

En: Write it down, Kim -- in Martinaise they just kill you because they don't *like* you.
Zh: “记下来，金——在马丁内斯，他们杀人的原因只是因为他们不*喜欢*你。”

En: It's cool, guys. You just hang them -- shoot them -- light them on fire. You don't care!
Zh: “没关系的，伙计们。你们只会吊死他们——射杀他们——放火烧他们。你们才不在乎呢！”

En: They just hang you -- shoot you -- light you on fire. They don't care!
Zh: “他们会吊死你——射杀你——放火烧你。他们才不在乎呢！”

En: I understand. You hang them -- you shoot them -- whatever! You can't even remember.
Zh: “我明白了。你们会吊死他们——射杀他们——随便什么。你们甚至都不记得了！”

En: They just hang you -- shoot you -- whatever! They can't even remember.
Zh: “他们会吊死你——射杀你——随便什么，他们甚至都不记得了。”

En: There's no need to hide it. You just hang people like in the dark ages -- make a display of the corpse.\n
Zh: “无需隐瞒了，你们就跟在黑暗时期一样吊死人——把尸体示众。”

En: They just hang you, like in the dark ages -- make a display of your corpse.
Zh: “他们只会吊死你，就跟在黑暗时期一样——把你的尸体示众。”

En: Yes, I understand, Alain -- that's your name, right? Alain. You'll *kill* us. He scratches in his notebook. "That's what they do in the Wild North."
Zh: “是的，我明白了，阿兰——这是你的名字，是吗？阿兰，你会*杀了*我们。”他在笔记本上潦草记了几笔。“他们在荒北就是这么干的。”

En: There we go. He jots down a note. "Don't come to the Wild North if you're not *white* enough."
Zh: “好了。”他草草记了个笔记。“如果你不够*白*，可千万别到荒北去。”

En: Just remember it's about more than Klaasje. It's about these men and Martinaise: their district, their *responsibility*.
Zh: 只要记住这不仅仅关于卡拉洁一人，而是关于这些人和马丁内斯：他们的地盘，他们的*责任*。

En: A wince. It's involuntary. "Bring that up *one more time* and you won't get to write that report."
Zh: 脸上不自觉地抽搐。“*再*提一次，你永远没有机会写那份报告了。”

En: Yeah, we liked it. It was fun, wasn't it guys? He looks around. "We had a great time."
Zh: “是啊，我们很喜欢。可真有趣，不是吗伙计们？”他环顾四周。“我们可开心了。”

En: ...because they *like* killing.
Zh: “……因为他们*喜欢*杀戮。”

En: Fuck you! *I'm* not from around. That's not why we...
Zh: “去你的吧！*我*不是本地人，那也不是因为这个我们才……”

En: ...because of some chick.
Zh: “……因为某个女人。”

En: ...because you're a *foreigner*.
Zh: “……因为你是个*外国人*。”

En: ...because you work for the wrong people.
Zh: “……因为你为错误的人工作。”

En: Good. I like this. This guy is learning.
Zh: 很好，我喜欢这样，这家伙学会了。

En: He'll get it. Go on.
Zh: 他会懂的，继续。

En: Outside in the nightlight -- quiet, dressed in sodium lamps. Torn and in tatters...
Zh: 外面的夜灯中——安静的，披着钠光灯的外衣。破烂不堪……

En: Outside under the rising sun -- tattered, and in ruins; the windows of the cafeteria aglow with her morning light.
Zh: 初升的朝阳下——破烂不堪的废墟之中；餐厅的窗户因为晨光而闪闪发亮。

En: Huh? The lieutenant raises his brow.
Zh: “嗯？”警督挑起眉毛。

En: Outside in the evening light -- ruined and old, shadows lengthen on the pavement. A distant gunshot.
Zh: 黄昏的光线下——残旧不堪的人行道上影子被拉长了。一发远距离的枪击。

En: You only manage to perform one more movement. An instinctive jerk to your left. Then -- no sound. No one screams. It's impossible to say where it comes from...
Zh: 你只来得及再做一个动作，本能地向左猛拉。然后——鸦雀无声，无人惊叫。无从判断它是从哪里来的……

En: Huh? He looks surprised.
Zh: “嗯？”他看上去很惊讶。

En: Really. I'm gonna start typing up my report. It's gonna say: in Martinaise they just kill you because they don't *like* you...
Zh: “真的。我要开始写报告了，里面会包括：在马丁内斯，他们杀人的原因只是因为他们不*喜欢*你……”

En: Convince Titus he's being manipulated? Bad idea. Bringing her up will do no good. You should know by now -- *Titus* will never falter.
Zh: 说服提图斯他被人玩弄了？不是个好主意，把她搬出来没有任何好处。你现在应该很清楚——*提图斯*绝不会动摇。

En: Convince *Titus?* he's being manipulated? You should know by now -- Titus Hardie will never falter.
Zh: 说服*提图斯*？让他知道自己被玩弄了？到了现在，你应该很清楚——提图斯·哈迪绝不会动摇。

En: To your right, you sense the air move. The lieutenant draws his firearm.
Zh: 你能感受到右手边空气的流动，警督拔出了他的枪。

En: That's it then. Case closed. (Look around.) "I'm going home."
Zh: “就这样吧，调查结束。”（环顾四周。）“我要回家了。”

En: But you know someone who *might*.
Zh: 但你知道谁有这个*可能*。

En: The lieutenant has put down his notebook -- his hand is resting on his holster. He gives you an imperceptible nod.
Zh: 警督放下他的笔记本——把手放在枪套上。他微微朝你点了点头。

En: That's it then. Case closed. (Look around.) "We're going home, Kim."
Zh: “就这样吧，调查结束。”（环顾四周。）“我们回家吧，金。”

En: One of his boys will!
Zh: 他的一个兄弟会的！

En: One of his boys will.
Zh: 他的一个兄弟会的。

En: You hear that, Angus! They're gonna kill me too!
Zh: “你也听到了，安格斯！他们也会杀了我们的！”

En: The most terrible fear. Bigger than any before.
Zh: 比以往任何时候都要强烈，最可怖的恐惧。

En: Too late. There is an awful ringing in your ears -- your body temperature spikes. You're burning all over -- with fear.
Zh: 太迟了。你的耳边响起可怕的嗡鸣声——体温飙升起来。你的全身都被恐惧——燃烧殆尽。

En: Go for it -- the lieutenant has his eyes set on the fat guy. Push Angus. We're almost there.
Zh: 加油吧——警督的视线锁定了那个胖子。逼迫安格斯，我们就快成功了。

En: You hanged a corpse to cover up the real cause of death -- the bullet in his head.
Zh: “把尸体吊起来是为了掩盖真正的死因——他脑袋里的子弹。”

En: Fat Angus. The powerful guy. Mr. All Muscle. The time has come -- put him in the pressure cooker.
Zh: 胖子安格斯。有力量的家伙。肌肉先生。是时候了——把他放进高压锅里。

En: You hanged a corpse to cover up the real cause of death -- what was it?
Zh: “你把尸体吊起来，来掩盖真正的死因——到底是什么？”

En: Suddenly there is an awful ringing in your ears -- your body temperature spikes. You're burning all over -- with fear.
Zh: 突然你的耳边响起可怕的嗡鸣声——体温飙升起来。你的全身都被恐惧——燃烧殆尽。

En: You just killed yourself. Like I said you would -- suicide... He shakes his silvery head; his calm voice is almost kind now.
Zh: “你是在自取灭亡，就像我说的那样——自杀……”他摇晃着银发的脑袋；他平静的声音现在听来竟有些亲切起来。

En: Goddamn right! Don't *come around* if you're not *from around*!
Zh: “太他妈对了！如果你不是*本地*人，就别*过来*！”

En: You hanged the corpse to cover up the real cause of death -- the bullet in his head.
Zh: “你把尸体吊起来，来掩盖真正的死因——他脑袋里的那颗子弹。”

En: Fine. I'll tell him. After a long walk along the *coast*. She walks off without looking back.
Zh: “行吧，我会告诉他的，在我去*海岸*边散完步以后。”她头也不回地走了。

En: Wait. Let's go back. I want to say something else...
Zh: “等等，我们回去吧。我还想说点别的……”

En: You hear that, Angus! They're gonna kill me too!
Zh: “你也听到了，安格斯！他们也会杀了我们的！”

En: I understand. You club them -- from behind -- you string them up and go back to drinking.
Zh: “我明白了。你会从背后——给他们一闷棍——把他们吊起来，再回去喝酒。”

En: They club you -- from behind -- string you up and go back to drinking.
Zh: “他们会从背后——给你一闷棍——把你吊起来，再回去喝酒的。”

En: Why the cover up?
Zh: “为什么要掩盖？”

En: We're not sure. Probably a bullet. The wound was difficult to see.
Zh: “我们也不确定，可能是颗子弹吧。很难看清伤口。”

En: It's cool, guys.  You drag them out back, light their corpses on fire and piss on them. You don't care!
Zh: “没事的，伙计们。你把他们拖出来，点燃他们的尸体，还在他们身上撒尿。你才不在乎呢！”

En: They drag you out back, light your corpse on fire and piss on you. They don't care.
Zh: “他们把你拖出来，点燃你的尸体，还会在你身上撒尿。他们才不在乎呢。”

En: You hanged the corpse to cover up the real cause of death -- what was it?
Zh: “你吊起一具尸体来掩盖真正的死因–是什么？”

En: Wait. Stop. Let's go back. I want to say something else...
Zh: “等等，停步。我们回去吧。我还想说点别的……”

En: Goddamn right, this is Uniontown! You work for the company -- we *will* kill you!
Zh: “太他妈对了，这里是工会之城！你为公司工作——我们*就会*杀了你！”

En: Me too.
Zh: “我也是。”

En: Fuck, Dennis, we don't kill you if you work for the company! Half the harbour works for the company...
Zh: “该死的，丹尼斯，就算你为公司工作我们也不会杀你的！半个港口的人都在为公司工作……”

En: We didn't kill him! We didn't even *hang him*, he was dead when... He takes a breath, wheezing.
Zh: “我们没有杀他！我们甚至没有把他*吊起来*。他早就死了……”他喘着粗气。

En: Work for the wrong company, he jots down, "and they execute you."
Zh: “为错误的公司工作，”他草草记录。“他们就会处决你。”

En: They kill you, he jots down, "because they think it's *funny*."
Zh: “他们要杀你，”他草草记录，“是因为他们觉得这*很有趣*。”

En: I'm sorry I made you guys fight.
Zh: “我很抱歉让你们起了争端。”

En: You only manage to perform one more movement. An instinctive jerk to your left. Then -- no sound. No one screams. It's impossible to say where it comes from...
Zh: 你只来得及再做一个动作，本能地向左猛拉。然后——鸦雀无声，无人惊叫。无从判断它是从哪里来的……

En: Lizzie. He turns to the fixer. "Your help is no longer needed here. Go tell Evrart."
Zh: “莉兹。”他转向调解人。“这里不需要你帮忙了，回去告诉艾弗拉特吧。”

En: It wasn't for your fucking entertainment, Dennis. She... He gets a hold of himself.
Zh: “这他妈不是给你找乐子的，丹尼斯。她……”他克制住了自己。

En: What do you mean, old man? What suicide?
Zh: “你是什么意思，老头？什么自杀？”

En: DENNIS! Titus roars. "Stand down or I'll beat your head in. Theo --" He points to the old man. "Take your hand off the belt -- this isn't '31. I've got this under control."
Zh: “丹尼斯！”提图斯咆哮道。“坐下，否则我就锤扁你的脑袋。特奥——”他指着老人。“把你的手从皮带上挪开——现在可不是31年了。一切都在我的掌握之中。”

En: So you *didn't* kill him. He was already dead.
Zh: “所以你们*没有*杀他，他已经死了。”

En: Yeah, let them know! The little guy slams his fist on the table. "We'll kill you dead."
Zh: “是啊，让他们知道！”小个子用拳头砸着桌子。“我们会杀死你的。”

En: What? What does that mean?
Zh: 什么？那是什么意思？

En: No. You see him shake his silvery head. His calm voice is almost kind now. "*I'm* not gonna do it. I'm too old for a shot like that now..."
Zh: “不。”你看到他摇晃着银发的脑袋，他平静的声音现在听来竟有些亲切起来。“*我*不会这么做的。我年纪大了，不适合开枪了……”

En: You caught the leader off guard. Now strike.
Zh: 你让领头人感觉猝不及防，乘胜追击。

En: Angie, where's your goddamn inhaler? You sound like you're dying.
Zh: “安吉，你那该死的呼吸器哪儿去了？你听起来快要死了一样。”

En: Look at Kim first.
Zh: 先看看金。

En: The man's fists under the table are balled -- you can tell from his neck and shoulders. He means it.
Zh: 那人放在桌下的手握成拳头——从他的脖子和肩膀就能看出来，他是认真的。

En: (Turn to Angus.) "Or what? You're gonna kill me like you killed him? FOR NO FUCKING REASON?"
Zh: （转向安格斯。）“不然呢？你要像杀死他那样杀了我？没有任何理由？”

En: It wasn't that. It wasn't... the fat man says with a wheeze. "We didn't hit him, okay?"
Zh: “不是那样的，不是……”那个胖子喘着粗气。“我们没有杀他，明白吗？”

En: I left it home. I can't get it, I'm too fucked... He grabs his chest. "I'm sorry."
Zh: “落在家里了。我也没办法去拿，我太难受了……”他抓着自己的胸口。“我很抱歉。”

En: We didn't light him on fire! You hear the fat man wheeze. He's trying to get up. "No one pissed on him either!"
Zh: “我们没有放火烧他！”你听到那个胖子呼哧呼哧地喘气，试图站起来。“也没人对着他尿尿！”

En: That's it, that's the weak one -- you flushed him out. Now go in for the...
Zh: 就是他，最脆弱的那个人——你把他拎出来了，现在继续……

En: Officer, you *will* be next if you don't shut up. The old man reaches for his belt, but his voice is strangely calm.
Zh: “警官，再不闭嘴下一个*就是*你了。”老人伸手去够皮带，但他的声音出奇的平静。

En: (Turn to Theo.) "What happens if I keep talking? You gonna *kill* me too -- in this bar?! FOR NOTHING?!"
Zh: （转向特奥。）“如果我继续说下去会如何？你也要*杀了*我吗——在这个酒吧里？没有任何理由？”

En: Why are you so fucking FAT, Angus?! Lizzie snaps at him. "Now it's all pointless, because of *you*. You wasted my time. I told you, Titus --" she turns to him. "I told you to just give her up."
Zh: “你他妈为什么那么胖，安格斯？”莉兹厉声斥责。“都是因为*你*现在一切都没有意义了，还浪费了我的时间。我说过，提图斯——”她转向他。“我告诉过你别管她的。”

En: Steel yourself, push on. Just ignore Theo.
Zh: 振作起来，更进一步，只要无视特奥。

En: Firearm! A Glace 08, or a .38 calibre pistol -- either is small enough for you to have missed.
Zh: 火器！一把格莱斯08，或是一把.38口径的手枪——每把都小巧到躲过了你的视线。

En: He's on to you. He knows what you're trying to do...
Zh: 他盯上你了，他知道你想干什么……

En: Girls plural? There's another girl? Two of them? Take note of this. They'll probably say more about her later.
Zh: 女孩们？还有另一个女孩吗？一共两个人？记录下这一点。他们之后可能还会提起她。

En: Cause the girls asked us to. They were in some shit.
Zh: “因为是女孩们请我们这么做的，她们惹上大麻烦了。”

En: Another nod.
Zh: 又一次点头。

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点了点头。

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: If Klaasje didn't kill him -- why the cover up?
Zh: “如果不是卡拉洁杀的他——为什么还要掩盖？”

En: So, *who* killed the merc then? Any leads?
Zh: “那么，会是*谁*杀的雇佣兵呢？有任何线索吗？”

En: So there *was* a wound -- you should try looking for it one more time, before you send the body away.
Zh: 所以那里*有*一处伤口——在你把尸体送走之前，应该再去看一次。

En: At least you'll know what to look for now -- when you get around to doing the autopsy.
Zh: 至少你现在有头绪该找什么了——当你准备做尸检的时候。

En: The coroner and his assistant are wrapped in blue: hands covered in blue gloves, mouths covered by blue masks, bodies by blue aprons. The coroner wipes his brow, scalpel still in his hand. "Get the light. Shine it in his mouth," he tells his assistant. The coroner squats, to better see as the light illumines the darkness inside. "Well, shit. Can't blame them for missing that. Get the saw, Alan. This just got fun."
Zh: 验尸官和他的助手穿了一身蓝：手上是蓝色的手套，脸上是蓝色的口罩，身系蓝色的围裙。验尸官擦了擦额头，手中还握着手术刀。“灯光，照进他的嘴里，”他对助手说。验尸官蹲下身，以便看清被灯光照亮的黑暗。“哦，该死，不怪他们错过了。把锯子拿来，艾伦。开始有趣起来了。”

En: It looked like she'd redosed after something went down. I've seen that look before, she was scared. I knew someone had died.
Zh: “看起来她好像是在出事后又嗑药了。我以前见过那种表情，她很害怕。我就知道是有人死了。”

En: How did you know?
Zh: “你怎么会知道？”

En: What happened then?
Zh: “之后呢？”

En: I've done this job for ten years. I've seen it before. It's the politician in the motel room with the dead hooker scenario -- only in reverse.
Zh: “我干这行已经十年了，以前就见过这种表情。完全就是汽車旅馆里政客与死掉妓女的情景再现——只是这次反过来了。”

En: Did *she* kill him?
Zh: “是*她*杀的人吗？”

En: Did *she* kill him?
Zh: “是*她*杀的人吗？”

En: Cop, I have no idea -- the girl says she didn't...
Zh: “条子，我真的不知道——女孩说不是她……”

En: Good analogy, boss. The rat-faced man snickers.
Zh: “好比喻，老大。”老鼠脸对着男人窃笑。

En: You don't get to talk yet, Shanky. He points at him. "You're still on the bench -- and you keep taking it easy too, Angus." He turns back to you.
Zh: “还轮不到你说话呢，尚克。”他指着他。“你还在坐冷板凳呢——还有你也别紧张，安格斯。”他又面向了你。

En: That's okay with the fat man, still wheezing there. He couldn't speak if he wanted to.
Zh: 那个胖子没事，还在喘气呢。就算他想说话也说不出口。

En: We went upstairs. Sure as day the merc was dead. And there was a bullet hole through the window. Fucking... He scratches his chin. "Dirty sheets and bottles everywhere."
Zh: “我们上楼去，那个雇佣兵确实死了，窗户上有个弹孔。该死的……”他挠了挠下巴。“到处都是脏床单和酒瓶。”

En: Tibbs patched the window. And the corpse -- we hanged.
Zh: “提布斯补好了窗户。至于那具尸体——我们吊起来了。”

En: He means they'd been fucking?
Zh: 他的意思是他们在上床吗？

En: Does he? His closed fist is shaking... it's *you* who's in control. Let them have their moment.
Zh: 是吗？他紧握的拳头颤抖着……现在是*你*在掌控全局，让他们冷静一会儿吧。

En: (Whisper) "Kim, we did it."
Zh: （低语）“金，我们做到了。”

En: The lieutenant gives a smile only you can see.
Zh: 警督露出一个只有你能看见的笑容。

En: You're in. He's all yours. Questions!
Zh: 你成功了，他现在任你差遣了。发问吧！

En: Whose idea was it to hang him anyway -- hers?
Zh: “到底是谁出的主意要把他吊起来的——她的吗？”

En: Who's Tibbs -- the eighth Hardie?
Zh: “提布斯是谁——第八个哈迪兄弟？”

En: Who's *Tibbs*?
Zh: “*提布斯*是谁？”

En: Nah, he's my brother. He's in the window replacement business.
Zh: “不，他是我兄弟，是做窗户修理生意的。”

En: My brother. He's in the window replacement business.
Zh: “我兄弟，他是做窗户修理生意的。”

En: Tibbs -- that's short for...
Zh: 提布斯——是个简称……

En: Short for Tiberius?
Zh: “提比乌斯的简称？”

En: Back to cop questions.
Zh: 回到警察问题上去。

En: Yeah. He nods. "Good man."
Zh: “没错。”他点头。“挺不错的家伙。”

En: Bet their father's named Atticus Hardie -- Lucretia Hardie would be their sister. Anyway.
Zh: 我打赌他们父亲的名字是阿蒂库斯·哈迪——还有卢克丽霞·哈迪是他的姐妹。

En: Yes. No more small talk.
Zh: 是的，别再闲聊了。

En: You may have noticed our girl's in some shit of her own.
Zh: “你可能已经发现我们这位女孩已经惹上一身腥了。”

En: Thank you for this, Titus. I'll go talk to her -- for the last time. (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢你，提图斯。我会去跟她谈谈的——最后一次。”（总结。）

En: I do -- one of those mercenary buddies of his could've done it. They got guns. Training. Years of bad blood, probably. Or it could've been someone else from Krenel... He pauses to think.
Zh: “的确——他的任何一个雇佣兵同伙都能做到。他们有枪，训练有素。可能是多年的不和。或者还有可能是科奈尔的其他人……”他停下来思索着。

En: She told me some people were after her -- from Oranje. She wouldn't say more.
Zh: “她告诉我有人在找她——奥兰治来的人。然后就不愿意多说了。”

En: Yeah, I told you she's not what she seems. She's had special training -- for something shady.
Zh: “是啊，我告诉过你她不是她看上去的样子，她受过特殊训练——为了某种阴暗的目的。”

En: I didn't notice anything -- what kind of *shit* are we talking about?
Zh: “我根本没注意到——你指的是什么样的*麻烦*？”

En: The *can't show up on police radar* kind. There are people after her. From the Old Old World -- where she came from.
Zh: “*不能让警察知道*的那种，有来自旧世界的人在追查她——她就是从那儿来的。”

En: These people -- who are they?
Zh: “这些人——他们是谁？”

En: They're powerful. He looks out the window. "Connected to the Moralintern. She's clearly afraid for her life -- says if she showed up in your systems, she'd be ghosted away."
Zh: “他们很强大。”他看向窗外。“跟国际道德伦理委员会也有关系。她显然是在担心自己的生命安全——说如果她出现在你们的系统里，很快就会消失了。”

En: And why would you help someone like that? By taking on a murder?
Zh: “你为什么要帮助这样一个人呢？揽下谋杀罪？”

En: Why would help someone like that out?
Zh: “为什么你要帮助这样一个人呢？”

En: Why *would* I? He shrugs. "I guess we abide all sorts of runaways and losers here. It's a Martinaise thing."
Zh: “我为什么*要*？”他耸耸肩。“我想是因为这里包容了各种各样的流浪者和失败者，马丁内斯就是这样。”

En: Yeah -- she wouldn't. She's fucked if she shows up on police radar.
Zh: “是啊——她不会的。如果她被警察发现，就完蛋了。”

En: That's all he knows -- that's all she's told him.
Zh: 他就知道那么多了——她就告诉了他那么多。

En: Oh, you meant *that* kind of training -- like a spy? He scratches his chin. "Maybe. The kind of people who are after her... I guess she'd have to be."
Zh: “哦，你是指*那种*特训——间谍之类的？”他挠挠下巴。“可能吧，那些追查她的人……可能她真的是。”

En: Why *would* I? He looks you in the eye: "I guess we abide all sorts of runaways and losers here. It's a Martinaise thing."
Zh: “我为什么*要*？”他直视着你的眼睛。“我想是因为这里包容了各种各样的流浪者和失败者，马丁内斯就是这样。”

En: Not yet. Just some ideas. She says the shot came from outside, behind the window somewhere. So that's a clue.
Zh: “没有，只是一些想法而已。她说枪声是从外面，从窗户后面什么的地方传来的。这也算条线索吧。”

En: What are you thinking?
Zh: “你有什么想法？”

En: I'm thinking -- someone's past caught up with them. Either hers or his.
Zh: “我在想——是某个人的过去找上门来了，不是她的就是他的。”

En: I mean the people after Klaasje. Maybe the shot missed. Maybe it was meant for *her*?
Zh: “我是说在追查卡拉洁的那些人。或许是他们失手了，或许目标其实是*她*。”

En: Got it. Tell me something else.
Zh: “明白了，还有别的吗。”

En: Yeah, sure. You don't need my clues. You're doing a great job on your own. He shakes his head.
Zh: “是啊，当然，你不需要我来提供线索。你自己就能做得很好了。”他摇了摇头。

En: Hers -- you mean...
Zh: “她的——你是说……”

En: She's got one of those *chequered* pasts. The shot could have missed. Could've been meant for her?
Zh: “她有一段*跌宕起伏*的过去。或许是他们失手了，或许目标其实是*她*？”

En: I like that. The young guy nods. "Been thinkin' the same thing myself."
Zh: “我喜欢。”年轻人点点头。“我自己也是这么想的。”

En: And you had ideas about *his* past too?
Zh: “你对*他的*过去也有想法吗？”

En: Tell you what I'd do -- check out the coast for vantage points. Maybe consult with a ballistics buddy of mine -- that's what *I'd* do. If I wasn't too busy doing this clown dance with you.
Zh: “告诉你我会怎么做吧——去海岸查看射击位置。也许可以找我做弹道分析的朋友咨询一下——*我*是这么想的，如果我不是忙着跟你一起耍猴戏的话。”

En: Hers?
Zh: “她的？”

En: In a manner of speaking.
Zh: “可以这么说。”

En: Remember the two girls? He may be talking about the other one.
Zh: 还记得那两个女孩嘛？他可能指的是另一个人。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Earlier you said the *girls* asked for your help. Was this the other girl?
Zh: “早些时候你说*女孩们*向你寻求帮助。是另一个女孩吗？”

En: That's right. He blinks. "It was her idea to hang him -- I liked it, for political reasons. It sent a good message."
Zh: “没错。”他眨眨眼睛。“把尸体吊起来就是她的主意——出于政治原因，我觉得很不错。这能传达出一个好的讯号。”

En: We had... help. From another girl. It was her idea to hang him -- and I liked it, for political reasons. It sent a good message.
Zh: “我们有人……帮忙，就是另一个女孩。把尸体吊起来就是她的主意——出于政治原因，我觉得很不错。这能传达出一个好的讯号。”

En: Who is she -- this *other* girl? Do I know her?
Zh: “她是谁——*另一个*女孩？我认识她吗？”

En: No, he shakes his head, "you're not gonna be on a first name basis with this person."
Zh: “不。”他摇头。“你跟她不会有多熟悉的。”

En: Okay -- *where* is she?
Zh: “好吧——那她在*哪里*？”

En: Okay -- I get it. Stonewalled.
Zh: “好吧——我明白了。拖延战术。”

En: Glad you understand that.
Zh: “很高兴你能理解。”

En: You're not getting to her, he says calmly. "This person is affiliated with the Hardie boys. It's Klaasje you want to talk to."
Zh: “你接近不了她的，”他平静地说。“她是哈迪兄弟的人，你要找的人是卡拉洁。”

En: Hah! Here we go. Jigsaw pieces falling into place...
Zh: 哈！开始了，拼图碎片逐渐拼上了……

En: You do that. He grabs his beer and swirls it in his hand, then thinks of something. "Hey, cop -- before you go."
Zh: “去吧。”他拿起啤酒在手里转了转，突然想起了什么。“嘿，条子——在你走之前。”

En: She... He looks up. "Klaasje came to Martinaise to hide -- many of us did. This is where you wash up when there's nowhere left to go. The Union takes you in -- now, she *refused* that protection, but..."
Zh: “她……”他抬起头。“卡拉洁来马丁内斯避难——我们中很多人都是这样。当你无处可去的时候，这里是你重新开始的地方。工会庇护了你——现在她却*拒绝*了这份保护，但是……“

En: But you would *still* prefer if we didn't take her away?
Zh: “但你*还是*不希望我们把她带走？”

En: Suddenly the wind picks up outside. You hear it rattling the large windows in their frames. It carries newspapers, circles the Whirling-in-Rags in a warm column...
Zh: 外面突然起风，你能听到风把窗框里的玻璃震得咔哒咔哒响。它卷着报纸，把褴褛飞旋包裹在温暖的漩涡中……

En: Good bye, Titus. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见，提图斯。”[离开]

En: Still, after all this headache...
Zh: 尽管如此，在所有头痛之后……

En: That's right. If we didn't take care of the people who end up here, this place would just be a couple of ruins and some cargo containers.
Zh: “没错。如果我们不去照顾那些沦落至此的人，这个地方也就只剩下一片废墟和集装箱了。”

En: We'll take that into account. The lieutenant slides his notebook into his coat pocket. He turns to leave.
Zh: “我们会考虑的。”警督把笔记本塞进上衣口袋，转身离开了。

En: A story that fits the occasion, of course! I invented a wonderful *allegory* for all this...
Zh: 一个符合时宜的故事，当然！我为此编造了一个绝妙的*故事*……

En: I do not understand. They're just stupid, probably. Too stupid to understand the arguments. Maybe the lieutenant could do it for you.
Zh: 我不明白，或许是他们太蠢了，蠢到理解不了这段争论。或许警督可以帮你。

En: (Whisper to the lieutenant.) "Kim, I can't keep doing this. You take the wheel."
Zh: （低声对警督说。）“金，我没办法再继续下去了。你来接手吧。”

En: The lieutenant inclines his head, only slightly. "Thanks for your time, Titus. Case closed." He takes out his notebook and writes a few words. "Let's go, officer."
Zh: 警督微微歪着头。“谢谢你的配合，提图斯。调查结束。”他拿出笔记本写了几个字，“我们走吧，警官。”

En: Huh? He looks surprised.
Zh: “嗯？”他看上去很惊讶。

En: Really. We're ready to begin our report. I've already started, actually. He turns back to his notebook, and writes as he speaks:
Zh: “真的，我们准备好要写报告了。实际上，我已经开始了。”他又翻开笔记本，边说边写着：

En: Yeah, let them know! The little guy slams his fist on the table. "We'll kill you dead."
Zh: “是啊，让他们知道！”小个子用拳头砸着桌子。“我们会杀死你的。”

En: In Martinaise they just kill you because they don't *like* you...
Zh: “在马丁内斯，他们杀人的原因只是因为他们不*喜欢*你……”

En: Funny how your sis didn't get it.
Zh: “奇怪你妹妹怎么没有。”

En: Tell it to the bossman, bozo.
Zh: “跟老大说去吧，笨蛋。”

En: The big boss has it covered.
Zh: “大老板都搞定了。”

En: Your fucking feet, man...
Zh: “你该死的脚，老兄……”

En: Goddamn bindlestiffs waltzing in here...
Zh: “该死的流浪汉在这里打转……”

En: Play a round of biscuit, Angie!
Zh: “给我们表演一轮吃小饼干吧，安吉！”

En: Did you fucking shit in your boots?
Zh: “你是在自己的靴子里拉屎了吗？”

En: Fuck it, I'm seeing Monica tonight.
Zh: “去他的，我今晚要去见莫妮卡。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Return where?
Zh: “回去哪里？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: To his opportunities in Occident, Sur-la-Clef. Still... He breathes in and keeps his lungs filled for a moment, before letting it out. "His coming and going brings some life to the village."
Zh: 回到充满机遇的西方世界，苏拉菲。不过……”……他深吸一口气，充盈着肺部，过一会才呼出来。“他的来来回回给村子带来了生机。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Or is it just money, I don't know... He stares at the bar.
Zh: “或者只是金钱，我不知道……”他盯着吧台。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: His earpiece?
Zh: “他的耳机？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Yes, you know those tiny speaker-microphones that fancy security guards sometimes wear.
Zh: “没错，你知道高级安保人员有时候会戴的那种小型麦克风。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: And he believed you?
Zh: “他就相信你的话了？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Why shouldn't he?
Zh: “为什么不信呢？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: A bit too dramatic, don't you think? He says it like it's an aesthetic question. "If you really must do it then I'd go with something else... some other substance."
Zh: “有点太戏剧性了，你不觉得吗？”他说得好像这是个美学问题。“如果你非要这么做的话，那我就选别的……别的内容。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Well, here's to you! He raises a glass, before taking a sip of his drink, froth grazing his mouth.
Zh: “好吧，这杯敬你！”他举起杯子，还没喝进去，泡沫先蹭到了他嘴上。

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: But then again -- you're a cop, it definitely fits the genre. He looks at his hands folded on a table. "*I* fit the genre, we all do... moulded by fiction like we're characters in some bad novella."
Zh: “不过话又说回来——你是个警察，绝对符合题材。”他看着自己交叠在桌子上的双手。“*我*也一样，我们都是……被小说塑造成了某些三流中篇故事里的角色。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: I don't know what I'm doing here. I just go wherever life takes me.
Zh: “我不知道自己在这里干什么，完全是随波逐流。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Human psychology is definitely lazy, don't you think?
Zh: “人类心理学绝对是怠惰的，你不觉得吗？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY

Zh: “为什么不呢？你难道不想听听我们是如何想要摧毁最后残存的意义，瑞瓦肖人民最后紧握的东西，男人与女人的意义吗？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Or the true symbols of security, mother and father and their everlasting marriage... *Everything* will be constantly shifting and moving under our rule, the future will belong to a *circus* of identities, just spinning around, surreal and unreal...
Zh: “或是安全感真正的符号，父母和他们永恒的婚姻……在我们的统治下，*一切*都会不断转换变化。未来会像*马戏团*一般，超现实与非现实交织在一起，令人头晕目眩……”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: You won't even know who you are anymore.
Zh: “你甚至都不会知道自己是谁了。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: But I like knowing who I am.
Zh: “但我喜欢知道自己是谁。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Does it have anything to do with disco?

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Other questions, I had other questions, really! (Try to back off.)
Zh: “还有别的问题，真的，我还有别的问题要问！”（尽量后退。）

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: The smoker starts laughing and leans back against the counter: "Sure thing, officer, what do you want to know next?"
Zh: 吸烟者倚在吧台边，笑了起来：“当然了，警官，接下来你想知道什么呢？”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: Yes, we listen to a lot of disco. Some say we *engineered* disco to spread our vision of a vertiginous, ever-changing society, where all there is a razzle-dazzle of gold!
Zh: “没错，我们也会听很多迪斯科舞曲。有人说我们*设计*迪斯科，是为了传播我们对于令人眩晕，变幻无穷的社会的愿景。在那里，所有的一切都是让人眼花缭乱的金子！”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: We are going to *change* the family unit with all this razzamatazz and finger-dancing, he wiggles his hands, "and with mysteries, of course, the mysteries are also of sexual nature, very esoteric."
Zh: “我们会用趣味活动和手指舞去*改变*家庭模式。”他扭动着双手，“还有神秘，当然，神秘的性之奥秘，非同寻常。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: No, this is not me.

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY
En: You can't just get into it, you have to be born into it -- one is either already *in* the homo-sexual movement or forever excluded from it.
Zh: “你不能仅仅浅尝即止，必须融入你的骨血中——一个人要么已经*投身*于同性恋活动，要么就永远地被排斥在外。”

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY

ConvTitle: WHIRLING F1 / SMOKER ON THE BALCONY

Zh: “妙哇！”吸烟者如同一只准备跳跃的动物一样爬向你。“妙哇，这正是我们想要的！谁知道呢，也许过去的你*就是*个同性恋，也许这一切都被*压抑*了……”他用手环抱着你。
