Zh: “它*可以*打开厨房的门——那扇蓝门。”他看着你手中的钥匙。“上面写着‘工坊备用’——也许里面是间工坊？”

En: One can't see into the Union box from the yard *outside* -- that hawthorn is too thick.
Zh: 从院子的*外面*是看不见工会包厢的——那棵山楂树太茂盛了。

En: Look at Glenny and his girlfriend!
Zh: “看看格伦尼和他的女朋友！”

En: Why did she put it *back*? After she took it?
Zh: “她为什么要把它放*回去*？在她拿走之后？”

En: What's this weirdo cop doing now, huh? Window-shopping?
Zh: “那个怪人警察现在在干什么，哈？浏览沿街橱窗吗？”

En: I think he's trying to find dirt on us, in our box. It's what they do, Shanks. Did you know policemen are sometimes called *pigs*?
Zh: “我猜他是想在我们的包厢里拱土。他们就是这么做的，尚克。你知道警察有时也会叫做*猪头*吗？”

En: Nothing, as always. By now you're almost certain there may be a sparrow's nest on one of the branches.
Zh: 一如既往，并无区别。但现在你几乎能够确定其中一根树枝上藏着一个麻雀窝。

En: Go ahead, Glen.
Zh: “来吧，格伦。”

En: Wow, no. I hadn't heard that.
Zh: “哦不，我还没听过呢。”

En: Yeah -- because they like to sniff around in *dirt*. The tattooed man gives you a look.
Zh: “是啊——因为它们喜欢在*泥地*里嗅来嗅去。”纹身男看了你一眼。

En: Boss, I don't want to make excuses for her, but... He stops.
Zh: “老大，我不是想给她找借口，不过……”他停了下来。

En: It's close enough to the latch up there -- one can slide it open and just take it. Surely not a coincidence.
Zh: 这里离门栓很近——近到谁都可以拉开门栓拿走它。这肯定不是巧合。

En: Off yourself -- make it up to me, cop. I still got my money on that.
Zh: “请便吧——记得补偿我，条子。我的钱还押在上面呢。”

En: Yes. The nest of a common house sparrow. Passer domesticus.
Zh: 是的，这是一只普通麻雀的窝，家雀。

En: Wild theory, cop-man. The worst thing is -- I like it. She's been in this box plenty of times. This would give her access to the workshop route.
Zh: “狂野的理论，条子老哥。糟糕的是——我很喜欢它。她已经来过包厢很多次了，这让她找到了前往工坊的通道。”

En: He's just trying to sound clever. He has no idea what you're looking for -- nor do you, yourself.
Zh: 他只是想听上去聪明点儿。他根本不知道你在找什么——就连你自己也不知道。

En: Maybe this is how Ruby got to the roof that night? Using this key.
Zh: “也许那晚露比就是这样爬上屋顶的？用钥匙？”

En: There's a yellow ribbon tied to one of the branches -- light yellow, faded with time. A tiny splash of colour in the blackness of the thicket. Hanging from it -- a bronze key.
Zh: 一条黄丝带系在了其中一根树枝上——淡淡的黄色，随着时间褪去了。但也给漆黑的灌木丛增添了一抹色彩。上面挂着的——是一柄铜钥匙。

En: If he says it's there, it's there. The lieutenant takes his glasses off to clean them -- then puts them back on.
Zh: “如果他说是这里，那就是这里。”警督取下了眼镜擦了擦——然后又戴了回去。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: (Look at the key in your hand.) "I wonder what doors does it open?"
Zh: （看着你手中的钥匙。）“我很好奇它是开哪扇门的？”

En: Huh? The big guy looks behind him.
Zh: “哈？”大个子往后看去。

En: It's worth a try.
Zh: 值得一试。

En: I need your guys to hand the key to me.
Zh: “我需要你们这些家伙把钥匙给我。”

En: And *I* need the fatty to get his feet amputated 'cause the smell is killing me -- we can't always get what we want.
Zh: “那*我*需要胖胖把他的脚锯掉，因为我实在受不了那股味道了——我们不可能想要什么就有什么吧。”

En: Never even seen it. Someone must have hidden it there before this room became our place.
Zh: “从来没见过它。一定是有人在这间包厢专属于我们之前就把它藏在这里了。”

En: Didn't even know it was there... The man looks at the key in your hand -- then around the room. "Boys?"
Zh: “我根本不知道它在那里……”男人看了看你手中的钥匙——又看向房间里的人。“兄弟们？”

En: Sorry, Fucko! Looks like you're gonna have to go bush-diving. Good fucking luck with that -- the hawthorn's got a bitch of a bite!
Zh: “抱歉了，混蛋！看来你得去灌木丛潜水了。祝你好运——那棵山楂树可扎人了！”

En: I'm gonna enjoy the sight of you in the bushes out there...
Zh: “我很乐意欣赏一下你在灌木丛里的样子……”

En: I don't know about that... The tattooed man yawns and settles more comfortably on the bench. "I'm comfortable here... don't think any sliding would really help right now."
Zh: “不知道呢……”纹身男打了个哈欠，更舒适地窝在长椅上。“我现在就很舒服了……不想再挪窝了。”

En: Can you let me slide by so I can grab the thing?
Zh: “你能让我过去拿个东西吗？”

En: The kitchen -- the only part of the building you haven't thoroughly searched yet. It's worth a try!
Zh: 厨房——是这里你唯一没有彻底搜查过的地方，值得一试。

En: Titus, can you hand the key to me please?
Zh: “提图斯，能请你把钥匙给我吗？”

En: I'm not your janitor, cop. He glances at the window. "I don't even know what you're talking about. There's nothing there."
Zh: “我可不是你的门童，条子。”他瞥了一眼窗户。“我甚至不知道你在说什么，那里什么都没有。”

En: It's an older building -- who knows? He shrugs.
Zh: “这是栋老房子了——谁知道呢？”他耸了耸肩。

En: Does anyone know why this key was hanging right outside the Union box window?
Zh: “有谁知道为什么这把钥匙就挂在工会包厢的窗户外面吗？”

En: The kitchen -- the only part of the building you haven't thoroughly searched yet.
Zh: 厨房——是这里你唯一没有彻底搜查过的地方。

En: There is a silence around this man's words -- unlike Titus, they're *afraid* of him. That's the type of respect he commands.
Zh: 男人说话时周围一片沉默——不像提图斯，他们*害怕*他。这就是他所赢得的尊重。

En: It's an older building -- who knows? He shrugs.
Zh: “这是栋老房子了——谁知道呢？”他耸了耸肩。

En: With a loud thud the old man stands up -- pushes the window open -- grabs the key from the hawthorn branch and slides it across the table to you.
Zh: 老者砰地一声站了起来——推开了窗户——从山楂树枝头抓下钥匙，从桌子上滑给了你。

En: The key is brass. 'Workshop, Spare' is etched into its bow. The old man closes the window and sits back down in silence.
Zh: 钥匙是黄铜的，‘工坊，备用’的字样刻在上头。老者关上窗户，沉默地坐了下来。

En: C'mon, man. We were just having some fun! Where's the harm in...?
Zh: “拜托老兄。我们只是在找点乐子罢了！有什么害处呢……？”

En: I'm tired of listening to your shit.
Zh: “我听够你的鬼话了。”

En: It's an older building -- who knows? He shrugs.
Zh: “这是栋老房子了——谁知道呢？”他耸了耸肩。

En: Sorry, Fucko! These *guys* won't help you. Looks like you're gonna have to go bush-diving. The hawthorn's got a bitch of a bite!
Zh: “抱歉了，混蛋！这些*家伙*可不会帮你。看来你得去灌木丛潜水了，那棵山楂树可扎人了！”

En: It's an older building -- who knows? He shrugs.
Zh: “这是栋老房子了——谁知道呢？”他耸了耸肩。

En: Take the key.
Zh: 拿走钥匙。

En: Don't thank me. The old man takes out his pack of chewing tobacco. "I don't give two shits about your key."
Zh: “别谢我。”老者拿出一包嚼烟。“我根本不在乎你的钥匙。”

En: Thank you. (Nod to the old man.)
Zh: “谢谢你。”（朝老者点点头。）

En: No, it's not.
Zh: 不，并不是。

En: You're right. The door looks positively *immutable*. You could never get it open using force.
Zh: 你是对的，这扇门根本*无法撼动*，用武力是打不开它的。

En: You immediately feel *drawn* to the colour. Blue is for mystery.
Zh: 你立刻就被它的颜色*吸引*了，蓝色代表着神秘。

En: You see a heavy steel door with a prominent dimple lock. It's painted blue.
Zh: 眼前是一扇厚重的蓝色铁门，上面挂着一把显眼的凹槽锁。

En: The key fits the dimple lock. It takes a bit of effort to turn it after all these years, but then -- the lock clicks.
Zh: 这把钥匙与凹槽锁完全匹配。毕竟这么多年过去了，转动它还得费些功夫。然后——锁开了。

En: It could be connected to the barred door upstairs.
Zh: 这扇门可能通往楼上那扇被封住的门。

En: Try to push on the door.
Zh: 试着推门。

En: Touch the door.
Zh: 摸一下门。

En: The door does not budge.
Zh: 大门纹丝不动。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You do? The lieutenant regards you with patient scepticism. "It's a door in the back of the kitchen. Why do you care where it leads?"
Zh: “是吗？”警督向你投来充满耐心与怀疑的目光。“这不过是厨房的后门。你为什么要关心它通往哪里？”

En: I wonder where this door leads.
Zh: “我想知道这扇门通往哪里。”

En: Your words fail to even echo against the door's implacable steel.
Zh: 你的话语传到了这扇门上，那无法撼动的钢铁材质甚至湮灭了回声。

En: It's part of the Whirling-in-Rags. There's something *about* this place that makes me want to know.
Zh: “它是褴褛飞旋的一部分，这里的*某种*东西让我很好奇。”

En: Out of duty. We may find something pertinent to the investigation.
Zh: “责任使然。我们也许能发现与本案有关的东西。”

En: He's not bothered by your eccentricity. He seems genuinely intrigued himself.
Zh: 他并不介意你的反常行为。他也对此感到好奇。

En: The cobalt blue surface feels rough to touch. The stainless steel door is flush with its frame on every side.
Zh: 钴蓝色的表面手感粗糙。这扇不锈钢门严丝合缝地嵌在门框之中。

En: The key fits the dimple lock. It takes a bit of effort to turn it after all these years, but then -- the lock clicks.
Zh: 这把钥匙刚好能插进这把锁里。经过多年的闲置，拧动钥匙稍微费力了一些。不过——锁还是打开了。

En: Pull on the door -- hard.
Zh: 拉这扇门——使劲。

En: The steel doesn't even tremble.
Zh: 铁门纹丝不动。

En: Put your weight into it.
Zh: 利用体重再加把劲。

En: Give up.
Zh: 放弃。

En: Now you're hanging from the handle. The door remains immobile.
Zh: 现在你整个人都挂在了门把手上，门还是纹丝不动。

En: Try the 'Workshop Spare' key on the door.
Zh: 试试‘工作间备用’钥匙。

En: Old cobalt paint. Rough on the fingers. Forty, fifty years since this was painted, maybe?
Zh: 老旧的钴蓝颜料，粗糙的触感，刷上去已经有四五十年了？

En: A challenge!
Zh: 一个挑战！

En: Okay -- it was a *challenge* and you failed. What now, loser?
Zh: 好吧——本次*挑战*你失败了。然后呢，失败者？

En: So do I. The lieutenant touches the lock. "It's locked -- and sturdy. The paint job looks old. From *before* the cafeteria."
Zh: “我也是。”警督摸了摸锁。“锁上了——而且很坚固。油漆看起来很旧了，在餐厅之前就漆上了。

En: We're not getting past it without a key. We should ask the bartender... He corrects himself. "The cafeteria manager."
Zh: “没有钥匙我们是进不去的，不如去问问酒保……”他又纠正了自己。“餐厅经理。”

En: It leads to a side-building, adjacent to this one. The old building next to this, half ruined. Whatever is behind it must be older.
Zh: 它连着相邻的侧楼。就是旁边那栋老楼，塌了大半了。那后面一定是什么更有年头的东西。

En: I am drawn to its cobalt blue.
Zh: “我被它的钴蓝色吸引了。”

En: The winch, outside, in the back yard. Remember? No? Your *fingers* do!
Zh: 起吊机，外面，在后院。还记得吗？忘了吗？你的*手指*可没有！

En: There was a winch outside, on the roof. Like that of a small *elevator*.
Zh: “外面有架起吊机，就在屋顶上。就像一台小型*电梯*。”

En: Hmm... Well, if there was a *winch*, I suppose we could look into it. As a... side-investigation.
Zh: “嗯……好吧。如果真有一架*起吊机*，的确可以去调查一下。就当是个……分支调查。”

En: Yes, a mini side-investigation.
Zh: “没错，一次小小的分支调查。”

En: He looks at you, then at the door. "Garte is the person to ask about this -- the cafeteria manager."
Zh: 他看着你，然后看着门口。“加尔特才是我们要去询问的人——那个餐厅经理。”

En: It *is* quite pretty. I suppose we could look into it. As a... side-investigation.
Zh: “*它*很漂亮，我们的确可以调查一下。就当是个……支线调查。”

En: No, the door is a *mega*-investigation. I'm calling it... Behind the Blue Door.
Zh: “不，这扇门需要*超级调查*。我给它命名为……蓝门之后。”

En: No, the door is a *mega-investigation*.
Zh: “不，这扇门需要*超级调查*。”

En: The door and the main investigation will merge into a *stereo*-investigation.
Zh: “这扇门和主调查将会合并成为一个*联合*调查。”

En: If you say so. He looks at the door, then you. "Garte is the person to ask about this -- the cafeteria manager."
Zh: “既然你都这么说了。”他看了看门，又看向你。“加尔特才是我们要去询问的人——那个餐厅经理。”

En: It's hardly worth a *title*... He looks at the door, then you. "Anyway, Garte is the person to ask about this -- the cafeteria manager."
Zh: “它并不值得一个专门的*标题*……”他看了看门，又看向你。“总而言之，加尔特才是我们要去询问的人——那个餐厅经理。”

En: Eccentric. But okay, I suppose we could look into it. As a... side-investigation.
Zh: “有点怪。但行吧，我们的确可以调查一下。就当是个……分支调查。”

En: Hmm... Yes, I suppose it's worth seeing if we can get in. Just to be thorough. As a... side-investigation.
Zh: “嗯……是的，如果我们能进去的话，的确可以去看看。周全一点也好。就当是个……分支调查。”

En: You're right. It's not important. Let's go.
Zh: “你说得对，这不重要。我们走吧。”

En: It's hardly a side-investigation. You already have a name for it.
Zh: 你已经有一个名字了。

En: Hardly. He looks at the door, then you. "Anyway, Garte is the person to ask about this -- the cafeteria manager."
Zh: “很难说。”他看了看门，又看向你。“总而言之，加尔特才是我们要去询问的人——那个餐厅经理。”

En: Dust rises before you like mist. A tomb? Haunted by regal spirits from distant ages...
Zh: 尘埃在你眼前如同雾气般升腾，一个墓室？寄居着来自遥远时代的皇室魂灵……

En: No. Smells like engine grease and cut wood. A workshop.
Zh: 不对。闻起来像是机油和木头的味道，是一个工作间。

En: The darkness before you smells like engine grease and cut wood...
Zh: 你面前的黑暗闻起来像是机油和木头的味道……

En: I'll be back, door. Mark my words. [Leave.]
Zh: “我还会回来的，门。记住我的话。”[离开。]

En: The door stands silent, unmoved by your words.
Zh: 这扇门安静地伫立着，对你的话置若罔闻。

En: This door is *connected* to the one upstairs. But how do we get through?
Zh: “这扇门跟楼上的那扇是相通的，但我们该怎么过去呢？”

En: Have you tried using the key? The lieutenant nods toward the lock. "I suspect that will work."
Zh: “你试过用钥匙吗？”警督冲着锁点点头。“我认为这样应该行得通。”

En: There must be a key somewhere, the lieutenant says. "Probably somewhere in the Whirling? It's not *too* important. We can get in and out using the roof door."
Zh: “这附近一定有钥匙。”警督说。“大概就在飞旋旅社里？但也不是*很*重要，我们可以从屋顶的门进出。”

En: The door stands silent, unmoved by your words. You still feel like you should look into it if you get the chance...
Zh: 这扇门安静地伫立着，对你的话置若罔闻。你还是认为如果有机会的话，还是调查一下为好……

En: The key fits the dimple lock. It takes a bit of effort to turn it after all these years, but then -- the lock clicks.
Zh: 这把钥匙与凹槽锁完全匹配。毕竟这么多年过去了，转动它还得费些功夫。然后——锁开了。

En: Hey. Can I borrow your gardening gloves?
Zh: “嘿，我可以借用一下你的园艺手套吗？”

En: No.
Zh: “不行。”

En: I have a cadaver situation. I would really appreciate it.
Zh: “我有一具尸体等着处理，我会很感激的。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: She doesn't say anything.
Zh: 她什么也没说。

En: My answer is no.
Zh: “我的答案是否定的。”

En: C'mon Lizzie. The big man takes a sip of beer. "We're generous people here in Martinaise. With *property*."
Zh: “得了莉兹。”那个大块头喝了一口啤酒。“在马丁内斯，大家对自己的*财产*都很慷慨的。”

En: Fine. She hands you her pair of yellow gardening gloves.
Zh: “好吧。”她递给你一双黄色的园艺手套。

En: Wow -- really? A political moment? Now?
Zh: 哇哦——认真的吗？一场政治运动？现在？

En: Thank you comrade. Property is theft.
Zh: “感谢你，同志。拥有私产等于偷窃。”

En: You shouldn't have assisted me like that. I am your enemy. Nature is struggle.
Zh: “你不应该这样帮助我的，我是你的敌人。天生就是抗争关系。”

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢你。”

En: I didn't deserve this handout. You should've forced me to *hustle* harder.
Zh: “我不应该得到这份施舍，你应该迫使我更*强硬*一点的。”

En: Don't thank her -- thank me.
Zh: “别谢她——谢我呀。”

En: Yeah, don't you forget -- T did you a favour, popo.
Zh: “是啊，你可别忘了——是提帮了你一把，条子。”

En: Don't thank her -- thank me.
Zh: “别谢她——谢我呀。”

En: You're right. I shouldn't have.
Zh: “你说得对，我不应该的。”

En: Vulgar idiot, she shakes her head.
Zh: “低俗的白痴。”她摇了摇头。

En: Another day -- hustlin' and grindin' in the free world!
Zh: 又一天——在自由的世界里奔波并煎熬着。

En: Your understanding of the worker's struggle is about one century old, she's thinking.
Zh: 她在想，你对于工人斗争的认知大概还停留在一个世纪以前。

En: It's not her. She's not a Hardie girl. Definitely.
Zh: 不是她，她不是哈迪姐妹，一定不是。

En: Her entire character has shifted. This young woman is cold as ice.
Zh: 她的整个性格都转变了，这位年轻的女士变得如同寒冰般冷漠。

En: Uhm...  you're sure Easy Leo mentioned something about a lawyer Elizabeth someone... but you're too *dumb* to remember what it was.
Zh: 呃……你很肯定老好人利奥提到了一个名叫伊莉莎白的律师……但你*蠢*到记不起具体是谁了。

En: She shut you down -- because you don't have enough backbone.
Zh: 她阻止了你的发言——因为你没有足够的勇气。

En: A very minor victory.
Zh: 一次小小的胜利。

En: A left jab, right in the looker.
Zh: 一记左刺拳正中对面。

En: You should.
Zh: “你应该的。”

En: No, Glen -- they aren't. Her tone is cold and uninvolved.
Zh: “不，格伦——她们不是。”她的语气冰冷又不近人情。

En: This didn't change her opinion of you.
Zh: 这并没有改变她对你的看法。

En: You're far from home, *lieutenants*... This isn't a district known for its love of self-proclaimed militiamen.
Zh: “你们都离家太远了，*警督们*……这里可不是一个以拥护志愿民兵而闻名的地区。”

En: Detective Disorientated. She smiles coldly. "Are you still wondering where you are? This is Martinaise, in case you've forgotten. I advise you not to overstay your welcome."
Zh: “迷途的警探。”她冰冷地微笑着。“还在想自己身在哪里吗？这里是马丁内斯，以防你忘记了。我劝你不要在这里逗留太久。”

En: Officer Discotheque. She smiles coldly. "Still wondering if we listen to disco? We don't, by the way. We don't listen to disco anymore."
Zh: “迪斯科舞厅警官。”她冰冷地微笑着。“还在想我们还会听迪斯科吗？没有了，顺便说一句，我们已经不听迪斯科了。”

En: Detective Skin-Colour. She smiles coldly. "I'm still black as you can see. Do you have any more keen-eyed observations?"
Zh: “肤色警探。”她冰冷地微笑着。“正如你所见，我仍然是个黑人。你还有什么独到的见解吗？”

En: I guess it's fair to say you guys have it on life support.
Zh: “公平地说，你们这些人的确将其视作生命之源。”

En: Let me handle this, the woman says to the crowd in the mess hall before turning to you...
Zh: “交给我来处理吧。”在转向你之前，那个女人对着食堂里的人群说……

En: Her entire character has shifted. This young woman is cold as ice.
Zh: 她整个性格都转变了，这位年轻的女士变得如同寒冰般冷漠。

En: Wait, and you are...?
Zh: “等等，你是……？”

En: Enough. Let's talk seriously.
Zh: “够了。我们认真谈谈。”

En: I mean no trouble, I'm here to do my job, that's all.
Zh: “我不是来找麻烦的，只是工作使然，仅此而已。”

En: You're right, lieutenant, she did seem friendlier sitting on that corner.
Zh: “你说的对，警督。她坐在那个角落里时确实显得更友好些。”

En: I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. I've been watching you. It feels like she's waiting for a reaction of some sort.
Zh: “我是码头工人工会的法律顾问，我一直在观察你们。”她好像在等你做出某种反应。

En: Not a muscle moves in her face, but her eyes trace yours, stern and perceptive.
Zh: 她脸上的肌肉没有一丝动作，但她的眼睛紧紧地盯着你，目光敏锐。

En: You are looking for Titus Hardie, who you think has information on a murder the RCM is investigating. You want to interview him. She points to the man standing in the middle of the room.
Zh: “你在找提图斯·哈迪，你认为他掌握了RCM正在调查的一起谋杀案的信息。你想要审问他。”她指着站在房间正中的男人。

En: That's Titus. Talk to him. But know this: I'll be keeping an eye on you. No strong-arming, nothing official. The district of Martinaise does not recognize your authority to make arrests.
Zh: “那就是提图斯，去跟他说话吧。但要记住：我会一直盯着你的。不得使用武力，这不是正式的审问。马丁内斯地区并不承认你有实施抓捕的权力。”

En: Be straight with me, what kind of music *do* you listen to?
Zh: “老实告诉我，你*会*听什么类型的音乐？”

En: Are you Lizzy -- Elizabeth -- Miss Beaufort?
Zh: “你是莉兹——伊莉莎白——博福特小姐吗？”

En: What if I want to talk to *you*, not Titus?
Zh: “如果我是想和*你*谈谈，而不是提图斯呢？”

En: What are you going to *do* to me?
Zh: “你打算对我*做*什么？”

En: Maybe we'll talk later. [Leave.]
Zh: “之后有机会再聊。”[离开。]

En: I should talk to Titus then! [Leave.]
Zh: “我要去跟提图斯谈谈了。”[离开。]

En: What are we going to *do* to you? She starts laughing. It's a cold laughter, devoid of joy.
Zh: “我们打算对你*做*什么？”她开始笑起来，然而是没有一丝笑意的冰冷笑声。

En: Hmh... The lieutenant is not satisfied with the approach.
Zh: “嗯……”警督对这种方法有些不满。

En: *Why* did you say that? Did you forget you're a cop... again?
Zh: 你*为什么*要那么说？你难道……又忘记自己是个警察了吗？

En: The Union isn't going to *do* anything to you. It is not a crime syndicate, it is a labour organization.
Zh: “工会并不打算对你*做*什么。这不是什么犯罪集团，只是个劳工组织。”

En: I suggest not wasting time on trivial pleasantries and focusing on why you are actually here -- Titus Hardie, she points to the tall man by the table.
Zh: “我建议不要把时间浪费在无聊的寒暄上了，而是专注于你来这里的原因——提图斯·哈迪，”她指着桌子旁边那个高个子男人。

En: Even though she has excellent control over her self, something moved behind her eyes, in the way she stands, in her face...
Zh: 即便她能很好的掌控自己，但她的眼底，她站立的姿势，她的脸上都显示出了一些动摇……

En: You caught her off guard. Push her some more.
Zh: 你说得她措手不及，再逼近一步。

En: What you want is of no significance, *officer*. Don't test your authority -- in Martinaise you are no one.
Zh: “你想要什么并不重要，*警官*。不要测试你的权威——在马丁内斯，你什么都不是。”

En: She looks as if pondering whether to answer or not... but then shakes her head slightly.
Zh: 她看上去好像在考虑是否要回答你的问题……但随后她轻轻地摇了摇头。

En: Dance music -- basically disco? I know all about it.
Zh: “舞曲——基本就是迪斯科？我可是行家。”

En: You're right. I don't know anything about anything. What is anodic music?
Zh: “你说的对，我什么都不知道。什么是阳极音乐？”

En: Okay, let's put on a new record. (Conclude.)
Zh: “行，我们放上一张新唱片吧。”（总结。）

En: Let's.
Zh: “来吧。”

En: Why are you so aggressive?
Zh: “你为什么这么咄咄逼人呢？”

En: No, she steadies her breathing. "Not disco. Disco is *undead*."
Zh: “不，”她稳住了呼吸。“不是迪斯科，迪斯科是*不死*的。”

En: It's beyond the horizon for you.
Zh: “它超出你的认知水平了。”

En: The hell it is.
Zh: “才不是。”

En: You know, maybe you're right.
Zh: “知道吗，也许你是对的。”

En: Look, we're not discussing your taste in music anymore, officer. Do you have questions for my clients or not?
Zh: “听着，关于你音乐品味的讨论到此为止，警官。你到底有没有问题要问我的客户？”

En: What do you mean *us guys*?
Zh: “你说的*我们这些人*是什么意思？”

En: Hjelmdallermen.
Zh: “赫姆达尔人。”

En: A lot of people out there appreciate a good disco breakdown.
Zh: “喜欢迪斯科舞步的大有人在。”

En: I. Don't. Care.
Zh: “我。不。在乎。”

En: Okay, stick with your opinion.
Zh: “行吧，固执己见吧。”

En: I will. It doesn't look like you changed her mind.
Zh: “我会的。”看来你并没能改变她的观点。

En: Aggressive? You make your living enforcing violence. These people are just *dock workers*.
Zh: “咄咄逼人？你以实施暴力为生，而这些人只是*码头工人*罢了。”

En: Get sober. Her expression stiffens. "Do your *job*. Ask your questions and get the hell out of Martinaise."
Zh: “清醒点吧。”她的表情僵硬了。“完成你的*工作*，问你的问题，然后滚出马丁内斯。”

En: Listen, you Moralintern lackeys. You're a mob, enforcing the unlawful privatization of Revachol. Twenty fat men in the Occident are stealing it all -- and you're their body guards.
Zh: “听着，你这个国际道德伦理委员会的走狗。你就是一个暴徒，强制执行瑞瓦肖的非法私有化。二十个奥西登肥猪就把它们全部偷走了——而你就是他们的保镖。”

En: I don't need privatization paeans from a rent-a-cop. Her expression stiffens. "Just do your *job*. Ask your questions. Then get out of Martinaise."
Zh: “我不需要听一个业余警察高唱私有化赞歌。”她的表情僵硬了。“完成你的*工作*，问你的问题，然后滚出马丁内斯。”

En: You will never be able to experience it the way she does. She's even a bit sad about it; then the feeling passes.
Zh: 你永远不可能有她那般的体验。她甚至为此有些难过了；然后那种感觉过去了。

En: She means it's part of the future you won't experience.
Zh: 她的意思是那是你无法体验的将来。

En: What if I want to, it's in my mind for some reason...
Zh: 如果我很想呢，不知为何它一直在我的脑海中……

En: Yeah, I don't care about that.
Zh: 是啊，我才不在乎呢。

En: There is nothing a determined man could not achieve.
Zh: 世上无难事，只怕有心人。

En: Sure, why be interested in the world around us. Get back to boring, brutal detective work.
Zh: 是啊，为什么要对身边的世界如此好奇呢。继续回到无聊，残酷的警探工作中去吧。

En: You're far from home, *officer*... This isn't a district known for its love of self-proclaimed militiamen.
Zh: “你离家太远了，*警官*……这里可不是一个以拥护志愿民兵而闻名的地区。”

En: You're far from home, *lieutenants*... This isn't a district known for its love of self-proclaimed militiamen.
Zh: “你们都离家太远了，*警督们*……这里可不是一个以拥护志愿民兵而闻名的地区。”

En: You seem a little different today. Less hospitable.
Zh: “你今天看起来有点不一样，没那么好客了。”

En: You're far from home, *officer*... This isn't a district known for its love of self-proclaimed militiamen.
Zh: “你离家太远了，*警官*……这里可不是一个以拥护自称的民兵而闻名的地区。”

En: She's right, you're basically an epidermochromatic police officer.
Zh: 她说的对，你基本上就是披了一层警官的外衣而已。

En: I knew you were not a gardener!
Zh: “我就知道你不是园丁！”

En: You lied to me! You're *not* a gardener!
Zh: “你骗了我！你根本*不是*园丁！”

En: I mean no trouble, just here to do my job, that's all.
Zh: “我不是来找麻烦的，只是工作使然，仅此而已。”

En: I was just making an observation. I was reacting to stimuli.
Zh: “我只是观察一下，对刺激做出反应。”

En: I just wanted to know what kind of music you listen to. I was just curious!
Zh: “我只是想知道你听什么样的音乐，就是好奇而已！”

En: I just wanted to know more about the place before I check it out. That's normal.
Zh: “我只是想在调查前多了解一下这个地方，这很正常。”

En: That's right, this is an epidermochromatic police officer. Deal with it.
Zh: “没错，我就是披了一层警官的外衣。面对现实吧。”

En: You're the gardener!
Zh: “你是那个园丁！”

En: So let's get to it -- you're looking for Titus Hardie. You think he has information that will help you. Maybe he does. She points to the man on her right.
Zh: “我们说正事吧——你在找提图斯·哈迪。你认为他掌握了可以帮助你的信息。也许确实如此。”她指了指右边的男人。

En: It doesn't matter if you recognize our authority. We will make an arrest if we have to.
Zh: “无论你承不承认我们的权威性都不重要。如果有必要，我们就会逮捕他。”

En: No, I'm not a gardener, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “不，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。

En: That's right, I'm not a gardener, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “当然，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。

En: Sure, reacting to stimuli... I'm not a gardener, by the way, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “当然，对刺激做出反应……顺便说一句，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。

En: Sure, you were *curious*... I'm not a gardener, by the way, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms.
Zh: ”当然，你很*好奇*……顺便说一句，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。

En: Of course, you were just doing your *job*... I'm not a gardener, by the way, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “当然，你只是*工作*使然……顺便说一句，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。

En: Yes, extremely normal... I'm not a gardener, by the way, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “是啊，非常正常……顺便说一句，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。

En: Of course, and I'm just an epidermochromatic counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union, she says without blinking an eye.
Zh: “当然，那我也只不过是披了一层码头工人工会法律顾问的外衣而已。”她眼睛都不眨地说。

En: What's your role in all this?
Zh: “你在这中间扮演了什么角色？”

En: Obviously I'm a lawyer. A legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. She crosses her arms. "I would urge you to consider what you say to my clients."
Zh: “很明显我是个律师，码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。“我希望你好好考虑一下要对我的客户说的话。”

En: Like I already told you, I'm a legal counsellor. She crosses her arms. "Do you have hearing problems?"
Zh: “我已经告诉过你了，我是一个法律顾问。”她抱起双臂。“你的听力有障碍吗？”

En: I knew you were not the gardener!
Zh: “我就知道你不是园丁！”

En: You're not a gardener!
Zh: “你根本不是园丁！”

En: That's right, I'm not a gardener, I'm a legal counsellor for the Dockworkers' Union. It feels like she's waiting for a reaction of some sort.
Zh: “没错，我不是园丁。我是码头工人工会的法律顾问。”她好像在等你做出某种反应。

En: She says nothing; her glare speaks for her.
Zh: 她一言不发；她那双怒火中烧的眼睛说明了一切。

En: I've got nothing to say to you. Why are you wasting your time? She crosses her arms.
Zh: “我跟你没什么好说的。为什么要在这浪费时间呢？”她抱起双臂。

En: Get a grip Glen. She went to law school.
Zh: “克制一点吧格伦，她念的是法学院。”

En: So fucking what? Lots of models are actually really smart people, fuckwad!
Zh: “那他妈的又怎样？很多模特实际上都是聪明人呢，混蛋！”

En: Could this be the *Miss Beaufort* that Easy Leo mentioned? The one Mr. Evrart sent to law school?
Zh: 这难道就是老好人利奥所说的*博福特小姐*吗？艾弗拉特先生送去法学院的那个？

En: Easy Leo told me about you, he likes to talk a lot.
Zh: “老好人利奥跟我说起过你，他很健谈。”

En: Hardie? Not yet, I had another question.
Zh: “哈迪？先别急，我还有一个问题。”

En: No. Talk to Titus.
Zh: “不了，去跟提图斯说吧。”

En: You're not here to chat up the legal counsel, you're here to question these men.
Zh: “你来这不是为了跟法律顾问搭讪的，你是来审问这些人的。”

En: You set the pace and the topic of these conversations here. Establish that.
Zh: 你决定好了这段对话的节奏和主题，现在开始吧。

En: He said Mr. Evrart sent you to law school?
Zh: “他说艾弗拉特先生把你送去了法学院？”

En: He said you were in debt to Mr. Claire.
Zh: “他说你对克莱尔先生有所亏欠。”

En: He said you're the Union fixer. You fix things.
Zh: “他说你是工会的调解人，你能解决问题。”

En: A man like Easy Leo could have said anything. Do not be restrained, sire.
Zh: 老好人利奥那样的人什么话都说的出来。无需拘束，大人。

En: He said you're Mr. Evrart's *Doux et Sucré* Death Machine.
Zh: “他说你是艾弗拉特先生的*糖衣*炮弹。”

En: He said no such thing. She accidentally raises her voice. "This isn't about me."
Zh: “他没有这样说过。”她不小心提高了嗓门。“那不是在说我。”

En: I represent the Union and these men here. She points to the crew around the table. "Don't make this personal."
Zh: “我是工会和这些人的代表。”她指着桌子周围的人们。“不要牵扯个人。”

En: I am a *legal counsel*, she intones. "Don't make this personal."
Zh: “我是一名*法律顾问*，”她拔高了声调，“不要牵扯个人。”

En: Calm down everyone. Let's stay professional.
Zh: “大家都冷静下来，专业一点。”

En: Dirty tactics, officer.
Zh: 下作的手段，警官。

En: You will not lose out on anything good by not saying it.
Zh: 缄口不语不会让你失去任何好东西。

En: Oh yeah, you *get it*.
Zh: 噢耶，你*明白*了。

En: Not only do you *get it* -- you know it.
Zh: 你不仅仅是*明白*了——你懂了。

En: Hjelmdallermen is a reference to the *Northern* themed action-adventure series, Man From... Actually, this is less than helpful right now. I'll just get out of the way.
Zh: 赫姆达尔人引用自一部以*北境*为主题的动作冒险系列小说，来自……实际上，这在目前没有多大帮助。我先走一步了。

En: Sorry.
Zh: 抱歉。

En: Oh, you're a hjelmdaller-head?
Zh: “哦，你是个赫姆达尔的粉丝吗？”

En: No. It doesn't look like she's into popular adventure-fantasy.
Zh: “不是。”她看上去并不像是会喜欢流行冒险幻想故事的人。

En: You were specifically asked *not* to say that -- and then you said it anyway. You junior delinquent! But you *still* did!
Zh: 你被特别要求*不要*这么说——但你还是说了。这是次失职，但你*依然*这么做了。

En: Mhm... A gruff humph from the table. "Goddamn right it is."
Zh: “哼……”一声粗哑的哼声从桌子上传来。“太他妈对了。”

En: If anything, it is the RCM who *do* things to people... She shakes her head. "But we digress."
Zh: “如果真有事的话，也是RCM对人们*做了*什么……”她摇了摇头。“但我们跑题了。”

En: Do dock workers spy on the Police? You were on that corner all day. Now we know why. He smiles: "Just dock workers..."
Zh: “码头工人在监视警察吗？你们一整天都坐在那个角落里。现在我们知道为什么了。”他笑着：只是码头工人而已……”

En: Hmh. So you were spying on us. And now you represent murder suspects -- *just dock workers*.
Zh: “哼。所以你在监视我们，现在又变成了谋杀嫌犯的代理人——*只是码头工人*而已。”

En: I guess you're not a winner after all.
Zh: “我想你终究不是赢家。”

En: No one is emotional here. Her expression stiffens further. "Do your *job*. Ask your questions -- then leave."
Zh: “这里没有人感情用事。”她的表情毫无变化。“做你的*工作*，问你的问题——然后离开这儿。”

En: I'm being aggressive? She raises her eyebrows. "Listen, you Moralintern lackey, you're part of a mob, enforcing the unlawful privatization of Revachol. Twenty fat men in the Occident are stealing it all -- and you are their body guard."
Zh: “我在咄咄逼人？”她扬起眉毛。“听着，你这个国际道德伦理委员会的走狗。你就是一个暴徒，强制执行瑞瓦肖的非法私有化。二十个奥西登肥猪就把它们全部偷走了——而你就是他们的保镖。”

En: We've talked to Evrart Claire. We know who these men are. The Union's *militant* wing.
Zh: “我们已经跟艾弗拉特·克莱尔谈过了。我们很清楚这些是什么人，工会的*激进分子*。”

En: Fuck yeah, the tall, broad shouldered man takes a sip of his beer.
Zh: “没错，”那个身材高大、肩膀宽阔的男人呷了一口啤酒。

En: The world needs a financial buffer zone. No need to get emotional.
Zh: “这个世界需要一个金融缓冲区，没必要感情用事。”

En: The privatization is not unlawful, it's *cool* and *funny*. Maybe you're just not historic individuals?
Zh: “私有化并不违法，反而又酷又有趣。或许你们只是不符合历史进程的那些个体？”

En: I don't know where you heard that, but it's wrong. The RCM is principled and *strong*, unlike you socialists.
Zh: “我不知道你是从哪儿听来的，但你错了。与你们这些工人主义者不同，RCM是*坚强*而有原则的。”

En: I liked that. Good start. Let's take it a step further -- armed uprising. What are the Union's plans?
Zh: “我喜欢这个，是个良好的开端。不如我们更进一步吧——武装暴动。工会在策划什么？”

En: So ask what you came to ask -- or get back to your commanders.
Zh: “问你想问的——不然就回去向你的长官复命吧。”

En: Yeah, let's do that. Let's ask those questions.
Zh: “好啊，就这么办吧。我们来询问吧。”
